summary: Completed architectural refactor of Linux FloatBall to fixed-window + GDK Input Shape to eliminate platform-level geometry offsets, and systematically resolved two root-cause issues in Life-Copilot Discord Bot: missing structured logging and MCP tool permission denial
title: Bug Journal 2026-04-05

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-04 Today’s Overview What was done: Parallel progress across four projects: TokenMonitor completed the root fix for GTK size negotiation and a series of FloatBall Linux bugs, with continued debugging of WM clamp-induced position drift; Gadget Research Scout completed a survey and writing guide across 63 NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track papers; Life-Copilot completed a full refactor of the Discord Bot conversation system into a multi-CLI Agent architecture, followed by new features including screen time tracking, Feishu idea capture, and Codex/Anthropic backends; and explored AionUI usage and remote SSH access feasibility. How it was done: Located low-level system mechanisms such as GTK bottom-up size negotiation via runtime log analysis; used ccplan + Critic agent adversarial review for structured planning of multiple features, proactively surfacing high-value risks like Wayland compatibility and lark-oapi thread blocking; accelerated large-scale refactoring via subagent parallel development; completed research paper analysis through a multi-stage arXiv LLM evaluation pipeline. What it means: Life-Copilot code reduced by a net 2823 lines, gaining screen time awareness, a Feishu input channel, and an 11-tier AI fallback chain; TokenMonitor’s FloatBall runs correctly on Linux X11 for the first time (controllable size, snap working, no jump on collapse), with a complete diagnosis of architectural constraints for Linux transparent floating windows; the NeurIPS survey provides researchers with an actionable submission guide. Deep debugging and fixing multiple system-level bugs in TokenMonitor FloatBall on Linux (GTK size negotiation root cause, WM clamp positioning offset, etc.), completing a systematic research survey of 63 NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track papers, major Life-Copilot feature launches (Discord Bot architecture refactor with net -2823 lines, full Screen Time Tracker implementation, Feishu Bot for quick idea capture, Codex/Anthropic backend integration), and exploring AionUI remote SSH access feasibility.
Today’s Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ TokenMonitor FloatBall Linux Root Fix (GTK Size Negotiation + Bug Series) — Located and fixed the root cause of GTK bottom-up size negotiation locking the window at 400x400 instead of the expected 112x112: called widget.set_size_request() via with_webview() callback, removed set_resizable toggling (which would deadlock the old size before the async callback executed), and made Linux permanently resizable. Also fixed: missing deferred reposition (exponential backoff retry), incorrect multi-monitor expand direction (fixed to monitor.position.x + width/2), pointerdown race condition (dragGeneration + pendingPointerId), GTK ScrolledWindow scrollbar (GTK-level set_policy Never), drag flicker (non-resize calls set_position only once), CSS capsule size overflow (min(56px, 100%)), two-phase collapse jump (Linux path: Rust atomically shrinks window first, then CSS), and test/implementation semantic divergence. All tests pass, svelte-check zero errors. ✅ Life-Copilot Discord Bot Conversation System Full Refactor — Replaced 3170 lines of complex intent routing (semantic/, 24 _handle_* methods, conversation_history) with a CLIAdapter + SessionManager + MCP Server (19 tools) + Skills (4) architecture, supporting Claude/Codex/Gemini CLI fallback chains, with Discord UI (Views/Modals) decoupled via Skills JSON protocol. Net deletion of 2823 lines across the project, 419 tests passing. 🔄 TokenMonitor Linux FloatBall WM Clamp Positioning Deep Debug — Confirmed via backend.log analysis that the WM clamps post-collapse window coordinates using the old expanded width (systematic offset of ~305px). After multiple rounds of GTK gravity (introduced new offset), GDK move_resize, and probe-based correction (double-frame flicker), the root cause was identified: Linux transparent borderless Tauri floating windows lack a single source of geometric truth (WM/GTK/WebView three-layer geometry desync), an architectural platform limitation. The correct solution is a fixed large-size window with internal CSS expand/collapse. ✅ NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track Systematic Paper Survey and Comprehensive Writing Guide — Under the gadget research scout framework, searched 63 papers, filtered 20 high-relevance ones, deeply analyzed 5 top papers (TTA-VLM, DATE-LM, CAB, KG-RAG Pitfalls, DCAD-2000) across three dimensions (writing structure/publication elements/knowledge extraction), generated a comprehensive writing guide (five-part narrative, reviewer focus areas, reusable methodology). Also generated 3 executable research directions (multi-dimensional VLM credibility evaluation benchmark, joint benchmark for fine-tuning vs. TTA synergy, and online continual learning temporal benchmark). ✅ Life-Copilot Bot Four Accumulated Bug Fixes — Fixed: MCP Server not actually serving (added __main__.py + mcp_servers.json); ClaudeCLIAdapter.is_available() missing async causing silent fallback to Codex (awaiting a sync method throws TypeError swallowed by except); butler system prompt never injected into Claude CLI (added --append-system-prompt); multi-turn conversation context loss (_process_input never passed last_assistant_message and last_intent parameters). Added ensure_instruction_files() for idempotent self-healing generation of AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md. ✅ Life-Copilot Screen Time Tracker Full Implementation — Via ccplan 9-phase planning (Critic adversarial review surfaced critical risks including Wayland compatibility), implemented: cross-platform window tracking (GNOME Introspect D-Bus → xprop → cmdline fallback), AI app classifier (100+ presets + persistent cache + async lock), date-partitioned JSONL storage, independent asyncio.Task sampling loop, and integration with PreferenceLearner/ContextAssembler/BackgroundCoordinator. 26/26 tests passing, zero regressions. ✅ Life-Copilot Feishu Bot Quick Idea Capture Platform, Codex/Anthropic Backend Integration, and Full-Chain Token Validation — ccplan planned the Feishu Bot; Critic proactively found two critical issues (lark-oapi thread blocking and ThoughtStore non-singleton); implemented ThoughtCaptureService shared service layer + AutoCategorizer (10 categories, bilingual) + daemon thread asyncio bridge + 3 REST API endpoints. Added CodexProvider and AnthropicProvider, extending the fallback chain to 11 tiers. Created key_validator.py for real API validation of all services, fixed Google Calendar scope mismatch (calendarList → events.list), registered daily 06:00 re-check cron job. ❌ OpenReview Reviewer Comment Retrieval — OpenReview API returns 403 Forbidden for all unauthenticated access; the NeurIPS D&B Track uses a separate venue ID rather than the main conference path. Both issues combined cause complete failure; requires account credentials (OPENREVIEW_USERNAME/OPENREVIEW_PASSWORD) to proceed. ✅ AionUI Usage Exploration and Remote SSH Server Access Feasibility Assessment and Skills Cross-Platform Analysis — Investigated AionUI model switching, thinking depth configuration, and Skills cross-CLI platform (Codex/Gemini) adaptability; confirmed that AionUI remote Agent only supports URL connections and cannot directly connect to a Claude Code CLI process on a headless server (CLI process has no HTTP endpoint — fundamental architectural limitation). Recommended tmux as a temporary substitute and filing a feature request with the AionUI team. Implementation & Fixes ✅ Research Pipeline Environment Dependency Fix and Idempotency Handling — Installed arxiv/openreview-py packages and switched to absolute path script invocations; cleared 5 cache files in the insight cache directory to resolve stale cache hits; manually deleted 4 duplicate daily report entries written to overview.md from multiple pipeline executions. Problems & Solutions Key Problems 1. FloatBall window locked at 400x400 on Linux, Tauri set_size() completely ineffective Solution: GTK bottom-up size negotiation takes max(window_requested, widget_natural_size); WebKitGTK natural size ≈200x200 causes the window to lock. Fix: call widget.set_size_request() on the GTK main thread via with_webview(), remove set_resizable toggling (since with_webview is async dispatch, set_resizable(false) queued before the callback executes would lock the old size), keep Linux permanently resizable.
Key insight: Tauri’s set_size/set_min_size/set_max_size operate at the GTK window hint level; GTK bottom-up negotiation ignores these hints to satisfy widget natural size. with_webview() returns before its callback executes, and subsequent Tauri API calls are still enqueued FIFO and may deadlock the old size.
2. Linux collapse visual jump: two-phase sequence exposes intermediate state + WM clamping to old width causes systematic coordinate offset Solution: Two-phase sequence: macOS has a 250ms animation buffer; after disabling transitions on Linux the intermediate state is jarring. Changed to: Linux path first shrinks the window atomically in Rust to 56px, then sets CSS expanded=false (at which point 100% = 56px, making the change invisible). WM clamp issue: GTK gravity has no effect in this WM (and actually introduces systematic offset); probe + corrective reapply produces double-frame flicker. The fundamental solution is a fixed large-size window + internal CSS (pending implementation).
Key insight: Disabling CSS transitions requires simultaneously adjusting corresponding JS/Rust timing logic; GTK gravity is not supported by all WMs; post-hoc correction is itself a source of visible transition frames; the root solution is to have the WM accept correct coordinates in a single shot.
3. Life-Copilot MCP Server three-layer breakage: Calendar token present but agent still reports connection failure Solution: Three simultaneous breakages: FastMCP was instantiated but run() was never called; .claude/mcp_servers.json was missing; butler instructions (including MCP tools list) were never passed to the Claude CLI subprocess. Fixed by creating __main__.py to launch stdio transport, creating mcp_servers.json, and adding --append-system-prompt to ClaudeAdapter. All 19 MCP tools restored.
Key insight: “Code was written” and “code is being called” are two different things; creating an object with a factory pattern does not start a service; external programs need config files to discover services; app-level token validation and CLI tool context injection are two independent layers — passing token validation does not mean the CLI tool received the correct context.
4. Linux Screen Time Tracker initial approach used xdotool, which does not support Wayland (user’s machine runs GNOME 50 Wayland) Solution: Feasibility probe confirmed Wayland environment; switched to GNOME Introspect D-Bus (org.gnome.Shell.Introspect.GetWindows()) as the primary path, xprop as XWayland fallback, PID→cmdline as final fallback. All subprocess calls have 5-second timeout + proc.kill().
Key insight: GNOME 41+ provides a standard Introspect D-Bus interface, the correct way to get the active window under Wayland. This issue was proactively discovered by the Critic subagent during adversarial review — missing it would have caused the feature to be completely non-functional on the user’s machine.
5. AionUI cannot connect to a remote headless server’s Claude Code CLI via SSH or any non-URL method Solution: AionUI’s remote Agent architecture assumes the agent exposes an HTTP port; CLI processes have no HTTP endpoint, making them fundamentally incompatible. Recommended tmux/screen for managing multiple sessions as a temporary substitute, and filing a feature request with the AionUI team.
Key insight: AionUI’s database already has a remote_agents table (with an allow_insecure field), indicating the development team is building remote Agent capabilities; SSH direct connection may be on the future roadmap.
6. X11 WM refuses to position windows partially off-screen, causing snap and collapse position drift Solution: Mutter/GNOME and other X11 WMs clamp undecorated windows within the visible area, rejecting positions where x < 0 or x > screen_width - window_width. Linux uses edge_offset=0; macOS/Windows use edge_offset=ball_size/2.
Key insight: macOS and Windows allow undecorated windows to partially extend beyond screen boundaries; X11 WMs generally do not. The two platforms require different edge offset values.
7. ClaudeCLIAdapter.is_available() missing async causes Claude to silently fall back to Codex and time out Solution: Awaiting a non-async method produces no compile error; the runtime TypeError is swallowed by except Exception, causing Claude to be deemed unavailable. Adding the async keyword restored Claude available: True.
Key insight: In Python, awaiting a synchronous method produces a TypeError. If silently caught by a broad except Exception, it causes the logic to return an incorrect False result, manifesting as feature degradation rather than an error — extremely difficult to debug.
8. Bot cannot understand cross-turn references in multi-turn conversations (DualVerifier parameter wiring broken) Solution: DualVerifier, SemanticRouter, and KeywordScorer all implement last_assistant_message and last_intent parameters, but _process_input() never passed them in its calls. Fixing the wiring required no changes to any classification logic.
Key insight: “A feature has an implemented parameter interface” does not mean “the parameter is being passed.” The integration layer is the most common wiring breakage point; validation should check the call site, not just the implementation.
9. Codex CLI session resume has a bug; three CLI session mechanisms differ too much to unify Solution: Abandoned reliance on native CLI sessions. Maintain a lightweight history ring buffer (last 20 messages) at the Adapter layer; inject history as a prompt prefix when switching CLIs, achieving transparent cross-CLI conversation history transfer.
Key insight: A unified abstraction layer (history injection) is more reliable than adapting to each CLI’s session mechanism; session mechanism inconsistency is the primary risk in a multi-CLI architecture.
10. lark-oapi WebSocket client runs in a blocking manner, incompatible with the FastAPI asyncio main loop Solution: Start the Feishu bot in a separate daemon thread; message callbacks bridge to the main event loop via asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe().
Key insight: lark-oapi v1.5.3’s Client.start() is fundamentally synchronous and blocking — it must be thread-isolated rather than directly awaited in an asyncio task. This issue was proactively discovered by the Critic agent during adversarial review.
11. WebKitGTK internal ScrolledWindow scrollbar cannot be suppressed with CSS Solution: The WebKitGTK scrollbar belongs to the gtk::ScrolledWindow layer, not the WebKit render tree; CSS pseudo-elements cannot reach it. In the with_webview callback, traverse the widget ancestor chain and call set_policy(Never, Never) on each ScrolledWindow.
Key insight: WebKitGTK’s UI chrome belongs to the GTK widget layer and cannot be targeted by CSS selectors; it must be handled at the GTK code level.
12. OpenReview API returns 403 for all unauthenticated access, NeurIPS D&B Track reviewer comment retrieval completely fails Solution: Identified two compounding issues: OpenReview recently tightened anonymous access policy (account authentication required); the NeurIPS D&B Track uses a separate venue ID rather than a sub-path of the main conference. Together they cause complete failure. Currently blocked, requires OPENREVIEW_USERNAME/OPENREVIEW_PASSWORD environment variables.
Key insight: In the OpenReview venue ID system, satellite tracks use independent venues rather than parent conference paths, requiring separate ID confirmation; the API policy has been broadly tightened recently.
General Problems 13. Repeated research pipeline execution causes duplicate writes to overview.md; cache hitting stale results after dependency fix Solution: Manually deleted duplicate blocks; manually cleared the insight cache directory and re-ran. Root issues: the auto-append mechanism lacks idempotency protection; the cache system is not aware of dependency installation state changes.
Key insight: After fixing dependencies, the cache must be manually invalidated; the pipeline should check whether a record for the current day already exists before writing.
Human Thinking vs. AI Thinking Strategic Level GTK Root Cause Localization and Visual Defect Discovery Role Approach Human Directly identified via backend.log evidence that the window was actually 400x400 rather than the expected 112x112 — the problem was not in the geometry algorithm but in the Linux window size layer; observed the precise two-step phenomenon of “first shifting right then shrinking on collapse,” forcing the AI to explain the mechanism. AI Without log evidence, incorrectly assumed Tauri set_size() was effective and repeatedly fixed geometry algorithms. Because the two-phase collapse had a comment indicating intentional behavior, did not question its Linux applicability from a user experience perspective. Difference analysis: The human jumped to the root cause via empirical log reasoning and UX perception; the AI inferred from code logic and, lacking runtime validation, repeatedly iterated on incorrect assumptions.
Stopping Repairs to Diagnose Root Cause After Multiple Failed Fixes Role Approach Human After multiple rounds of WM clamp fixes still unresolved, explicitly requested “no more changes, just tell me the root cause,” and ultimately framed it as “is there any way to solve this WM window clamping?” — implying awareness of an underlying platform limitation. AI Each time a new log appeared, continued trying the next technical approach, without proactively stating “this direction has reached its limit.” Did not jump out of the single-point fix mindset without explicit instruction. Difference analysis: The human judged earlier that “continuing to iterate is futile” and proactively requested an architectural diagnosis; the AI lacks the ability to proactively suggest stopping and performing an architectural summary after repeated fix failures.
Discord Bot Architecture Refactor Direction Role Approach Human Directly proposed “why not just solve everything with claude code cli or codex cli?” — a single sentence suggesting a radical simplification, discarding the entire intent routing engine. AI Detailed the problem list with the existing 3170-line architecture, but leaned toward optimizing within the existing framework rather than proactively proposing an engine swap. Difference analysis: The human proposed a radical simplification; the AI initially favored incremental optimization. The human’s direction proved correct — far more code was deleted than added (-6061 vs +3238), achieving a fundamental simplification of the project.
Proactive Identification of Wayland Compatibility and AI Classification Cost Role Approach Human Did not proactively raise either of these issues. AI Proactively discovered via the Critic subagent that xdotool does not support Wayland, and independently identified the cost issue of calling the LLM every 30 seconds, proposing an app-name-level caching strategy. Difference analysis: These were high-value issues discovered independently by the AI through systematic adversarial review, avoiding the risk of the feature being completely non-functional on the user’s machine.
Solution Choice for Delivering Butler Instructions to All CLI Agents Role Approach Human Directly pointed out “just automatically create an agent.md and a claude.md for all agents — how hard is that?” — identifying the simplest and most general solution. AI Chose to inject --append-system-prompt for Claude, without proactively considering that Codex/Gemini could read conventional files directly. Difference analysis: The human saw the more general solution (convention files > runtime injection); the AI was stuck in a “how to inject” mindset rather than a “can we use a convention” mindset.
AionUI Remote Access True Requirement and Product Information Accuracy Role Approach Human Clearly expressed the core need as a unified management panel; directly corrected the AI’s outdated information about Claude’s 200k context window (actually updated to 1M). AI Initially focused on technical solutions (SSH tunnels, port forwarding) without proactively exploring the user’s deeper need; stated outdated model specs with confident tone. Difference analysis: The user had more current product information than the AI; only after the user explicitly stated their deeper need did the AI shift to more targeted suggestions.
AI Limitations Significant Limitations Without runtime evidence, incorrectly assumed low-level APIs (Tauri set_size, GTK gravity, Google Calendar calendarList scope) work as expected; needed log evidence or user-reported errors to correct direction. Repeatedly fixed geometry algorithms without questioning whether the underlying OS mechanisms were effective. After multiple failed fixes, unable to proactively identify that “continued patching cannot solve architectural problems.” Each time a new log appeared, continued trying the next technical approach; required explicit user instruction to stop before performing root cause summary. Did not verify platform support before introducing new mechanisms: did not confirm WM support for GTK gravity (which then introduced systematic offset); the transparent safety margin approach immediately broke both drag-following and WM clamp, without anticipating side effects before the change. Incomplete awareness of the global dependency graph in large codebases: the ccplan Critic agent discovered 4 hidden dependencies (BackgroundCoordinator, slash commands, etc.) that the AI missed during planning. Concurrency/race condition fixes often miss edge cases (the scenario of pointerUp firing before async initialization was only discovered upon follow-up questioning). In fast-moving product domains, provided outdated or fabricated information: fabricated AionUI’s /model and /effort slash commands (user verified they failed); stated Claude’s 200k context window with confidence (actually already 1M). Should not state rapidly-changing technical parameters with certainty. Cannot foresee external API limitations of toolchain dependencies (OpenReview 403, lark-oapi thread blocking, Codex session bug) at the planning stage; requires actual execution or Critic adversarial review to discover. Unable to proactively provide working alternatives to the OpenReview authentication wall. General Limitations Scattered investigation paths: when facing multi-layer breakage issues (calendar connection failure, Discord context anomalies), approached each symptom independently without quickly pinpointing the root cause at the architectural level, requiring multiple rounds of tool calls to converge on a conclusion. Today’s Takeaways Core Takeaways Full picture of GTK window size control: GTK bottom-up negotiation takes max(window_requested, widget_natural_size); WebKitGTK natural size ≈200x200 logical — the breakthrough requires calling widget.set_size_request(); Tauri’s set_size/set_min_size/set_max_size are ineffective for WebKitGTK windows; with_webview() returns before its callback executes, subsequent Tauri API calls may deadlock the old size under FIFO ordering; WebKitGTK scrollbars belong to the GTK widget layer and must be disabled with set_policy(Never, Never) at the GTK code level. Architectural constraints of Linux transparent borderless Tauri/WebKitGTK floating windows: WM, GTK, and WebView content area dimensions/positions do not auto-sync — this is a platform constraint, not a bug. The correct architecture is a fixed large native window with pure CSS expand/collapse internally, without relying on frequent resizing of the outer window; GTK gravity is not supported by all WMs, and moving by right-edge coordinates causes systematic offset; probe + corrective reapply cannot eliminate user-visible transition frames; separating the visible rect from the window rect at the native coordinate layer causes drag start points and mouse drift. Runtime logs are the fundamental tool for low-level system debugging: when dealing with GTK, WM, OS, or other low-level system behavior, validate assumptions via logs/probes empirically rather than inferring from code; cross-platform animation strategies must be holistically consistent — disabling CSS transitions requires simultaneously adjusting corresponding JS/Rust timing logic; X11 WMs forcibly clamp undecorated windows within the visible area (edge_offset must be platform-differentiated); macOS/Windows allow partial off-screen positioning. NeurIPS D&B Track submission strategy: “Field correctors” (triggering evaluation paradigm reconstruction by quantifying specific flaws in existing benchmarks, e.g., 57% accuracy rate) are more likely to be accepted and have more lasting impact than “new method proposers”; reviewers value most: multi-dimensional evaluation, fair baseline comparisons, human validation mechanisms (κ≥0.68), and counter-intuitive negative findings; counterfactual entity substitution can eliminate lexical overlap bias; symbolic verification loops (SPARQL/SQL execution comparison + N automatic repair cycles) can improve data correctness from 57% to 96%+; data cleaning can be reframed as unsupervised anomaly detection (Isolation Forest + Z-score cross-language normalization). Radical simplification (swapping the engine) is often more effective than incremental patching: Thin Adapter + MCP Server + Skills three-layer pattern replacing proprietary routing logic achieves cross-CLI universality; large refactors should undergo adversarial review (Critic subagent) before coding, proactively discovering hidden dependencies and architectural risks. Across multiple projects today, the Critic phase discovered high-value issues including Wayland compatibility, lark-oapi thread blocking, ThoughtStore non-singleton, and 4 hidden code dependencies — extremely high ROI. “A feature’s parameter interface is implemented” does not mean “the parameter is being passed”: the integration layer is the most common wiring breakage point; validate at the call site, not just the implementation side. FastMCP’s “create” and “serve” are two separate steps, and mcp_servers.json declarations are additionally required for external programs to discover the service; Python’s TypeError from awaiting a sync method, if swallowed by except Exception, causes silent feature degradation rather than an error. Modern Linux desktops (Ubuntu 22.04+) default to Wayland; cross-platform window tracking solutions must use GNOME Introspect D-Bus as the primary path, with xdotool/xprop only as XWayland fallback. Background sampling tasks should not all depend on CronScheduler (minimum 60s tick) — tasks requiring sub-minute periods need independent asyncio.Task loops. ECL documents structurally persist design decisions and are a key engineering practice for multi-phase complex feature development. Multi-CLI system design best practices: different CLI tools (Claude Code/Codex/Gemini) each have conventional instruction files (CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md); using convention files is simpler and more reliable than runtime injection. Skills are essentially Markdown prompts; cross-platform migration only requires tool name mapping. App state validation and CLI tool context injection are two independent layers — passing token validation does not mean the CLI tool received the correct context. A unified history ring buffer is more reliable than adapting to each CLI’s session mechanism. AionUI remote Agent only supports URL connections (CLI processes have no HTTP endpoint — fundamental architectural limitation), but AionUI’s database already has a remote_agents table, suggesting SSH direct connection may be on the future roadmap. AI should not state technical parameters (commands, model specs) with certainty in fast-moving product domains — it should declare a knowledge cutoff date and recommend users verify. Correct lark-oapi WebSocket architecture: Client.start() is blocking and must use a separate daemon thread + asyncio.run_coroutine_threadsafe() bridge. Google Calendar OAuth scope is granular — token validation should use events.list(calendarId='primary') rather than calendarList.list(). Log level is a zero-cost observability lever — changing provider errors from DEBUG to WARNING immediately and significantly improves operational visibility. Session Summaries TokenMonitor ✅ FloatBall Linux Code Review, Four Bug Fixes, and Complete GTK Root Fix (Size Negotiation + Collapse Sequence) 19:42:04.443 | claude_code After the user requested an explanation of FloatBall Linux logic, multiple inaccuracies were found, leading to a deep code review. Identified and fixed 4 bugs (missing deferred reposition, incorrect multi-monitor direction, pointerdown race condition with pendingPointerId completion, test divergence). User then reported 3 new Linux bugs; geometric fixes by AI didn’t resolve the root cause. After the user provided backend.log, GTK bottom-up size negotiation was confirmed as the root cause and fixed by calling widget.set_size_request() via with_webview(), removing set_resizable toggling, and adding GTK-level ScrolledWindow set_policy to suppress scrollbars. Finally changed CSS capsule to min(56px, 100%) and inverted the collapse sequence (Linux path: Rust shrinks window first, then CSS) to eliminate the two-phase jump. All tests pass, svelte-check zero errors.
🔄 Linux FloatBall WM Clamp Positioning Issue: Log Diagnosis, Multi-Round Debugging, Final Architectural Limitation Confirmed 00:57:20.724 | codex User reported FloatBall position jumping around on Linux. AI confirmed via log analysis that the WM clamps post-collapse coordinates using the old expanded width (systematic offset of ~305px). After multiple rounds of GTK gravity (introduced new offset), GDK move_resize, probe-based correction (double-frame flicker), and transparent safety margins (broke mouse tracking), none resolved the issue completely. After the user requested stopping, AI systematically summarized the root cause: Linux transparent borderless Tauri floating windows lack a single source of geometric truth (WM/GTK/WebView three-layer geometry desync) — an architectural platform limitation. The correct solution is a fixed large-size window + internal CSS.
Gadget Research Scout 🔄 NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track 63-Paper Complete Survey, 5 Deep Analyses, and Comprehensive Writing Guide Generation 20:18:09.048 | claude_code Building on the existing nips2025-benchmark project, fixed arxiv/openreview-py dependencies and cleared insight cache. Ran the pipeline three times, completing 63-paper search / 20 high-relevance filtering / 5-paper (TTA-VLM, DATE-LM, CAB, KG-RAG Pitfalls, DCAD-2000) deep three-dimensional analysis (writing structure/publication elements/knowledge extraction), and generated a comprehensive writing guide covering the five-part narrative, reviewer focus areas, and reusable methodology. Also generated 3 executable research directions. OpenReview reviewer comments failed completely due to API 403; account authentication required.
Life Copilot ✅ Discord Bot Full Architecture Refactor: From Intent Routing to Multi-CLI Agent Architecture (Planning + Full Execution) 19:40:36.464 | claude_code User proposed replacing 3170 lines of complex intent routing with Claude/Codex/Gemini CLI subprocess architecture. After ccplan planning (adversarial review found 7 critical/high risks and a Codex session bug feasibility probe) and writing-plans producing 18 tasks, parallel implementation via subagent-driven-development: CLIAdapter (three CLIs + fallback chain), SessionManager (ring buffer history), MCP Server (19 tools), Skills (4 SKILL.md files), Discord UI decoupled via Skills JSON protocol, slash command migration, and code cleanup. Net deletion of 2823 lines across the project, 419 tests passing.
✅ Screen Time Tracker Full Requirements Planning and Implementation (ccplan 9 Phases + Three Waves of Parallel Subagents) 03:19:12.046 | claude_code User proposed building a screen time tracking feature for LifeCopilot. Through ccplan 9-phase planning, Critic adversarial review discovered 32 issues including Wayland compatibility (and event loop blocking). User approved Plan 1 (EventBus integration). Three waves of parallel subagent implementation: abstract PlatformTracker + GNOME Introspect D-Bus primary path, AI classifier (100+ preset cache), date-partitioned JSONL storage, independent asyncio.Task sampling loop, and integration with PreferenceLearner/ContextAssembler/BackgroundCoordinator. 26/26 tests passing, zero regressions.
✅ Fixing Four Accumulated Post-Refactor Bugs: MCP Not Serving, Claude async Missing, Butler Not Injected, Multi-Turn Context Lost 22:38:00.853 | claude_code User reported having a token but agent saying it couldn’t connect to the calendar — found to be three simultaneous breakages (MCP not running, missing mcp_servers.json, butler instructions not passed). Fixed each in turn; all 19 MCP tools restored. Then fixed ClaudeCLIAdapter async missing (Claude available: True restored), added ensure_instruction_files() for idempotent AGENTS.md/GEMINI.md generation, and fixed _process_input never passing conversation history parameters causing cross-turn reference failures.
✅ Feishu Bot Quick Idea Capture, Codex/Anthropic Backend Integration, and Full-Chain Token Validation System 03:15:58.220 | claude_code Three features integrated: (1) ccplan planned Feishu Bot; Critic proactively found lark-oapi thread blocking (critical) and ThoughtStore non-singleton (critical); implemented ThoughtCaptureService + AutoCategorizer + daemon thread asyncio bridge + 3 REST API endpoints; (2) Added CodexProvider and AnthropicProvider, extending fallback chain to 11 tiers; (3) key_validator.py performs real API validation for all services, fixed Google Calendar scope mismatch (calendarList → events.list), registered daily 06:00 re-check cron job. Startup logs show full-service validation status.
AionUI 🔍 AionUI Usage Exploration, Remote SSH Server Access Feasibility Assessment, and Skills Cross-Platform Analysis 19:36:59.842 | claude_code User asked about model switching (/model) and thinking depth (/effort) commands in AionUI; AI’s responses were ineffective (AI acknowledged they were guesses). User raised core need of a unified management panel for accessing remote headless server Claude Code CLI. After technical discussion, confirmed AionUI remote Agent only supports URL connections — fundamentally incompatible with CLI processes. Also analyzed Skills cross-platform nature (Markdown prompts; cross-platform migration requires tool name mapping). Recommended tmux as temporary substitute and filing a feature request. AI was corrected that Claude’s context window has been updated from 200k to 1M.
Token Usage Claude Code Overview Metric Value Total Tokens 135,084,309 Input Tokens 43,775 Output Tokens 419,203 Cache Creation 5,687,355 Cache Read 128,933,976 Cache Hit Rate 95.8% Total Cost (USD) $95.9250 Model Breakdown Model Input Output Cache Creation Cache Read Cost Share claude-opus-4-6 25,307 323,732 3,642,945 115,985,769 $88.9811 92.8% claude-sonnet-4-6 85 36,799 796,638 1,190,037 $3.8966 4.1% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 18,383 58,672 1,247,772 11,758,170 $3.0473 3.2% Codex Overview Metric Value Total Tokens 50,553,332 Input Tokens 50,144,484 Output Tokens 408,848 Reasoning Tokens 249,100 Cache Read 47,165,568 Total Cost (USD) $25.3714 Model Breakdown Model Input Output Reasoning Cache Read Cost Share gpt-5.4 50,144,484 408,848 249,100 47,165,568 $25.3714 100.0%
summary: Deep debugging and fixing multiple system-level bugs in TokenMonitor FloatBall on Linux (GTK size negotiation root cause, WM clamp positioning offset, etc.), completing a systematic research survey of 63 NeurIPS 2025 D&B Track papers, major Life-Copilot feature launches (Discord Bot architecture refactor with net -2823 lines, full Screen Time Tracker implementation, Feishu Bot for quick idea capture, Codex/Anthropic backend integration), and exploring AionUI remote SSH access feasibility.
title: Bug Journal 2026-04-04

content:
Daily Report — 2026-04-03 Today’s Overview What was done: DCC advanced MIHD multimodal spatial transcriptomics paper preparation (HD fusion experiment reruns, establishing zero-shot FM baseline narrative framework, creating bilingual paper skeleton, reorganizing docs structure, analyzing code gaps); DesktopLinux fixed TokenMonitor float ball Rust compilation errors and continued Linux GTK window positioning bug fixes, completed two rounds of rclone bidirectional sync for gadget data, and investigated NIPS research data location How it was done: Tracked HD fusion experiments via SLURM job management, built paper narrative referencing Yuan et al. 2024 Nature Methods, used ccplan skill to plan document cleanup; read compilation error logs to fix Rust signature issues, GTK set_resizable hint controls window operation timing; executed rclone bidirectional sync via python sync.py, inferred NIPS data device location through research/logs paths Why it matters: Established ‘zero-shot FM embedding fusion’ differentiated research positioning with complete paper skeleton; TokenMonitor development build blocker resolved, GTK fix context saved for later; local and Google Drive data fully bidirectionally aligned, NIPS data source located to DCC device DCC What was done: Submitted 4 HD learning-based fusion SLURM jobs (staig/llava/adaln/contrastive), obtained key results; completed MIHD paper narrative design, bilingual skeleton writing, docs folder reorganization, and code gap analysis How it was done: Used sacct to track historical job timeout causes, submitted each fusion job individually; refined narrative through multiple conversation rounds, introduced reference paper structure analysis, planned document cleanup steps via ccplan and executed mv archiving Why it matters: adaln_attention achieving ARI≈0.003 on HD reveals method collapse at scale; paper narrative upgraded from ‘benchmark’ to ‘systematic evaluation of zero-shot FM fusion’, forming a clear differentiated positioning against Yuan et al.; identified 4 code gap categories and archived 12 outdated scripts DesktopLinux What was done: Fixed TokenMonitor float_ball.rs compilation errors; continued FloatBall Linux GTK window positioning bug fix context; executed two rounds of full rclone bidirectional sync (including pulling 127 remote-only files); investigated NIPS research results location How it was done: Read compilation error logs to locate issue, added resize parameter signature; restored FloatBall fix cross-session context via /compact; completed bidirectional sync with pull-then-push; inferred NIPS data origin through grep/find and research/logs paths Why it matters: Tauri development build blocker resolved, cargo check passes; FloatBall GTK fix approach (resizable hint timing control) context fully preserved; local and Google Drive fully aligned; confirmed NIPS data on DCC device awaiting sync MIHD project completed HD fusion experiment reruns, paper narrative restructuring, and bilingual skeleton writing, establishing the differentiated research positioning of zero-shot FM embedding fusion; TokenMonitor fixed float ball Rust compilation errors and continued Linux GTK window positioning bug fixes; Gadget completed two rounds of rclone bidirectional sync and investigated NIPS research data location
Today’s Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ MIHD paper narrative restructuring and bilingual skeleton creation — Established core narrative: ‘zero-shot FM embedding fusion unmapped system research’ as Setting, ‘fusion strategy » encoder selection, simple fusion is a trap, spatial graph learning is key’ as Finding, ‘cross-slide retrieval’ as Application; referencing Yuan et al. 2024 Nature Methods, created draft_en.md and draft_cn.md bilingual skeletons under docs/paper/, with complete structure including Abstract, Introduction (3 paragraphs), Results (R1-R6), Discussion (5 paragraphs), and Methods ✅ docs/ folder reorganization and code gap analysis — Archived 5 outdated documents to docs/archived/, created docs/paper/figures/ three-level directory structure; systematically analyzed capabilities required by draft_en.md vs. existing code, archived 12 outdated scripts and config files, planned 4 code tasks: Leiden results recovery script, figure generation script, spatial continuity metrics, and multi-seed support 🔄 TokenMonitor FloatBall Linux GTK positioning bug fix — Fixing two bugs: position jumping after FloatBall expand/collapse on Linux and window failing to retract. Implemented approach: last_rect state tracking, apply_and_store_rect helper, GTK set_resizable hint timing control; this session restored cross-session context via /compact, fix work still in progress Implementation & Fixes 🔄 HD Learning-Based Fusion job resubmission and result collection — After previous GPU job (#44850906) timed out, resubmitted four HD fusion experiments: staig/llava/adaln/contrastive. staig (ARI=0.258)/llava (ARI=0.201)/adaln (ARI=0.003) completed, basic_contrastive (48h limit) still running ✅ TokenMonitor float ball Rust compilation error fix — Fixed E0425 and E0061 compilation errors caused by missing resize: bool parameter in apply_float_ball_window_rect function signature in float_ball.rs, added #[allow(unused_variables)] to handle cross-platform warnings, verified with cargo check ✅ rclone bidirectional data sync and NIPS research investigation — Executed two rounds of full gadget sync: first round performed status check then pushed directly; second round discovered 127 remote-only files then pulled before pushing, achieving full local and Google Drive bidirectional alignment. Investigated NIPS research data location, inferred data exists on DCC device and not yet synced to DesktopLinux via Windows paths (C:\Users\tongt...) in research/logs, recommended sync pull or rerun Problems & Solutions Key Problems 1. adaln_attention achieves ARI≈0.003 on Visium HD, near complete failure; and simple fusion (concat ARI=0.191) underperforms single-modality PCA-only (ARI=0.351) Solution: Confirmed results are genuine, converted both negative results into core findings: adaln collapse labeled as large-scale data failure case, concat < PCA-only reframed as the paper’s core argument that ‘simple fusion is a trap’
Key insight: Negative results are equally important as positive results — negative results define the difficulty of the problem, positive results provide the solution; data scale (3900 vs 17502 spots) is the key variable in fusion method selection
2. Paper narrative logic gap: single-modality can also perform retrieval, the assumption that ‘fusion = necessary’ may not hold Solution: Restructured narrative as a syllogism: simple fusion is a trap → spatial graph learning is the bridge → correct fusion surpasses single-modality, incorporating the user’s counterargument into the paper’s core findings
Key insight: Critically examining paper narrative completeness is more important than building arguments from best experimental results; counterexamples often form stronger argument chains
3. FloatBall Linux: GTK WM asynchronous behavior causes set_size/set_position to be non-atomic, window fails to retract after expand-then-collapse Solution: Unlock GTK WM hint via set_resizable(true) → set_position → set_size → set_resizable(false) relock, ensuring operation timing; use last_rect state tracking instead of directly reading WM to avoid stale values
Key insight: Linux GTK window manager size/position updates for transparent borderless windows are asynchronous and non-atomic; reliable coordination requires controlling timing through the resizable hint
General Problems 4. Local machine missing 127 remote-only files; user cannot find previous NIPS research results Solution: Pull remote-only files before pushing to complete bidirectional sync; inferred NIPS data is on DCC device via Windows paths in research/logs, recommended sync pull or rerun
Key insight: Before bidirectional sync, analyze the direction of differences first; file paths in log files are the fastest clue for determining a file’s origin device — check logs before doing full filesystem searches
5. TokenMonitor: apply_float_ball_window_rect function signature lost resize: bool parameter during refactoring, causing compilation errors Solution: Added parameter to signature, added #[allow(unused_variables)] to handle unused warnings on non-Linux platforms, verified with cargo check
Key insight: In cross-platform conditional compilation (#[cfg(target_os)]), parameters used by one platform must appear in the function signature for all platforms
6. HPC environment PDF reading tools unavailable (poppler-utils not installed, PyMuPDF has zlib errors) Solution: Installed pypdf library to successfully read reference paper PDF (25 pages full text)
Key insight: pypdf as a pure Python implementation is more portable than system tools and is a reliable fallback for HPC environments
Human Thinking vs. AI Thinking Strategic Level Paper core narrative: single-modality can complete retrieval tasks & strategic definition of Setting Role Approach Human User proactively raised the counterargument ‘single modality can also do retrieval’, pointing out the narrative logic gap; explicitly established ’no existing systematic study of zero-shot FM fusion’ as the Setting — defining the paper’s starting point by research gap rather than method performance AI AI’s initial narrative defaulted to ‘fusion = necessary’, not considering that single-modality embeddings can directly perform cosine similarity retrieval; focused on building narrative from experimental findings angle, did not use research gap as the paper’s starting point Difference analysis: User simultaneously examined from a critical perspective and paper strategic height, identifying logic issues AI overlooked and leading the Setting definition; the combination formed a complete three-layer ‘Setting + Finding + Application’ structure, greatly strengthening the argument
FloatBall expand positioning solution direction selection Role Approach Human User explicitly rejected AI’s screen safe-zone anchoring approach, led the proposal of ball-anchored positioning as the core idea and personally implemented key logic AI AI’s initial approach was rejected, then responsible for implementing engineering details: last_rect state tracking, apply_and_store_rect helper, GTK resizable hint fix Difference analysis: User leads directional decisions, AI handles engineering implementation; humans outperform AI in decisions involving UI spatial intuition and user experience
Implementation Level Context switching ability (‘setting’ in paper writing vs. technical configuration parameter) Role Approach Human User explicitly corrected ’not that setting, I mean setting in the paper writing sense’, precisely switching between technical context and academic writing context AI AI misunderstood as technical configuration level, provided answers about YAML parameters, CLI options, etc. Difference analysis: Same vocabulary has different meanings in different contexts; human context-switching ability significantly outperforms AI
Practical judgment in file structure planning Role Approach Human User directly pointed out ‘docs/paper/figures should be a folder path’ AI AI created a single README file as planned, not considering the actual need to store figure scripts and output files Difference analysis: User’s intuition about actual workflows is closer to practical needs than AI’s documentation structure planning
AI Limitations Important Limitations Systematic blind spots in paper narrative framework construction: tendency to build arguments from best results, ignoring important information in negative results (simple fusion underperforms single-modality), failed to anticipate adaln’s complete failure at HD large-scale data; required user correction before restructuring the argument chain Insufficient UI spatial intuition and user intent inference: FloatBall initial positioning approach (screen safe-zone anchoring) was rejected by user; during rclone sync, failed to directly infer bidirectional operation intent from ‘both sides’, producing redundant confirmation interactions General Limitations Inefficient strategy during problem investigation: NIPS data investigation involved extensive full filesystem searches, without prioritizing device path clues in logs; risk of context omission in cross-platform Rust conditional compilation maintenance (parameter signature loss) Limited HPC environment toolchain detection capability; pdftotext/PyMuPDF failed consecutively, requiring multiple attempts to find the pypdf solution Today’s Takeaways Core Takeaways Multimodal fusion paper narrative must treat negative and positive results equally: negative results (simple fusion underperforms single-modality) define problem difficulty, positive results (STAIG doubling) provide solutions; paper Setting should be defined by research gap rather than method performance, Finding needs to distinguish between ‘why do this’ and ‘what was discovered’ Nature Methods Analysis-level paper standards (referencing Yuan et al. 2024): run each method 10 times reporting mean±std, parameter search rather than fixed defaults, 4-dimensional evaluation (Accuracy/Continuity/Marker/Scalability), contribute new methods beyond benchmarking Data scale is the key variable in multimodal fusion method selection: scale difference between Visium HD (17502 spots) and DLPFC (~3900 spots) can render AdaLN from effective to completely ineffective; Linux GTK window manager size/position updates for transparent borderless windows are asynchronous and non-atomic, must control WM hint timing via set_resizable(true/false) Practical Takeaways Cross-device workflow investigation: check logs first — file paths in research/logs are the fastest clue for determining which device originally generated data; bidirectional rclone sync should diff the direction first, pull before push when remote has unique files In Rust cross-platform compilation, variables used in #[cfg(target_os)] conditional blocks must be declared in the function signature for all platforms; platforms that don’t use them need #[allow(unused_variables)] Session Summaries MIHD 🔄 HD fusion experiment reruns, paper narrative design, and document reorganization 15:24:09.206 | claude_code Advanced MIHD paper preparation on DCC throughout the day: resubmitted 4 HD learning-based fusion SLURM jobs (staig ARI=0.258/llava ARI=0.201/adaln ARI=0.003), contrastive still running. In-depth paper narrative discussion: user established ‘zero-shot FM fusion unmapped system research’ Setting, AI proposed syllogism structure, user raised the key counterargument that single-modality can also do retrieval, prompting narrative restructuring to ‘simple fusion is a trap → spatial graph learning is the bridge → correct fusion surpasses single-modality’. Created draft_en.md and draft_cn.md bilingual skeletons referencing Yuan et al. 2024, reorganized docs/ folder (archived 5 outdated documents, created new figures/ three-level directory). Used ccplan to compare draft with existing code, identified 4 gap categories (QFormer/AdaLN results incomplete, Leiden not in CSV, missing spatial continuity metrics, missing multi-seed support), archived 12 outdated scripts.
TokenMonitor 🔄 Float ball Rust compilation fix and Linux GTK positioning bug fix continuation 04:18:21.823 | claude_code Fixed E0425/E0061 compilation errors caused by missing resize: bool parameter in apply_float_ball_window_rect function signature in float_ball.rs, verified with cargo check. Also continued fixing two FloatBall Linux expand/collapse position jumping and window-fails-to-retract bugs: implemented last_rect state tracking, apply_and_store_rect helper, GTK set_resizable hint timing control; user rejected screen safe-zone anchoring approach and led ball-anchored positioning concept. Restored cross-session context via /compact this session, fix work still in progress.
Gadget ✅ rclone bidirectional sync and NIPS research data investigation 21:20:28.738 | claude_code Executed two rounds of full gadget sync: first round performed status check then pushed directly; second round discovered 127 remote-only files (mainly .md files from summarize reports), completed bidirectional alignment with pull-then-push order, 4 categories 17 directories 0 failures. Investigated user’s missing NIPS research results: comprehensive search of local outputs/cache/reports/website directories found no data, inferred data was generated on DCC device and not yet synced to DesktopLinux via Windows paths (C:\Users\tongt...) in research/logs, recommended sync pull or rerun.

title: Bug Journal 2026-04-03

content:
Daily Log — 2026-04-02 Today’s Overview What was done: DCC advanced the MIHD multimodal spatial transcriptomics paper experiment pipeline, creating GPU Slurm job scripts and visualization analysis tools; DesktopLinux iteratively fixed TokenMonitor window positioning and float-ball interaction bugs on Linux/Wayland, systematically resolved all 21 code review issues from PR #4, cleaned up git history and pushed to GitHub, and completed gadget rclone environment initialization along with Claude Code skills and plugin configuration. How it was done: DCC used parallel Agents to batch-generate Slurm scripts and analysis scripts; DesktopLinux diagnosed Wayland protocol restrictions, GTK resizable flags, and other root causes layer by layer, processed code review feedback by CRITICAL→HIGH→MEDIUM priority, squashed 24 commits into 2 semantic commits via git reset –soft and pushed with force-with-lease, and configured rclone gdrive remote for cloud data sync. Why it matters: The MIHD paper experiment pipeline now has complete GPU job scripts and visualization tools ready for direct Slurm queue submission; TokenMonitor Linux user experience is restored from random window jumping to normal behavior; PR #4 eliminates high-risk issues including assert! panics and path traversal vulnerabilities, with zero Rust compile warnings and all 199 frontend tests passing — the code meets merge standards; DesktopLinux is integrated into the gadget cloud sync ecosystem with a fully configured development toolchain. DCC What was done: Updated the ECL weekly plan document in the MIHD project, added four independent experiment definitions (STAIG, contrastive, LLaVA, AdaLN) to pipeline_config.yaml, created 4 Slurm GPU job scripts, and in parallel generated three analysis scripts: cross-section query aggregation, paper figure visualization, and HD marker gene visualization. How it was done: Read existing pipeline_config.yaml and Slurm templates, added independent experiment definitions and experiment groups, and concurrently created three Python analysis scripts via parallel Agents with syntax validation. Why it matters: The paper experiment pipeline now has complete GPU jobs and visualization tools, enabling one-click Slurm queue submission and publication-ready figure generation. DesktopLinux What was done: Fixed TokenMonitor Linux/Wayland main window positioning errors and float-ball position jumping ghost artifact bugs; systematically fixed all 21 code review issues from PR #4 (Rust security vulnerabilities, frontend lifecycle bugs, CI cross-platform compatibility); squashed git history and pushed to GitHub; completed gadget rclone environment initialization; installed Claude Code skills and plugins. How it was done: Main window issue resolved by forcing GDK_BACKEND=x11 to bypass Wayland protocol restrictions; float-ball issue required multiple diagnostic rounds fixing ball-anchored layout, last_rect authoritative geometry, GTK resizable temporary unlock, frontend native-first collapse, and CSS width transition disable as cooperating defects; PR fixes processed in batches by severity; 24 commits squashed with git reset –soft then pushed with force-with-lease. Why it matters: TokenMonitor Linux core interaction is restored to normal; PR eliminates production crash risks and high-severity vulnerabilities, meeting merge standards; DesktopLinux is integrated into the gadget cloud sync ecosystem with a complete development toolchain. Dual-device parallel progress: DCC completed MIHD paper experiment scaffolding (GPU Slurm scripts + three visualization scripts); DesktopLinux systematically fixed TokenMonitor platform bugs on Linux/Wayland and all 21 PR #4 code review issues and pushed to GitHub, while completing gadget environment initialization and Claude Code development toolchain setup on the new machine.
Today’s Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ Fix all 21 code review issues from TokenMonitor PR #4 — Systematically fixed by CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM priority: assert!() replaced with Result propagation, SSH lock crossing async boundaries, path traversal protection, I/O error distinction, Svelte 5 double fetch, FloatBall listener race condition, NaiveDate overflow, timestamp defaults, CI cross-platform compatibility, LiteLLM boundary validation, and more. Zero Rust compile warnings, all 199 frontend tests passing. 🔄 Fix TokenMonitor Linux float-ball bug — Float-ball jumps position after expand/collapse on Linux, with ghost artifact residue after collapsing. After multiple iterations fixed ball-anchored expand layout calculation, FloatBallState.last_rect authoritative geometry, GTK resizable temporary unlock, frontend native-first collapse, and CSS width transition disable. Some details remained unconfirmed by end of session. ✅ MIHD ECL weekly plan update and GPU Slurm script creation — Updated ECL weekly plan YAML file feature status, added four independent experiment definitions (STAIG, contrastive, LLaVA, AdaLN) and corresponding experiment groups to pipeline_config.yaml, and created 4 independent Slurm GPU job scripts. ✅ Fix TokenMonitor Linux main window positioning — Main window appears at screen center instead of upper-right on Linux/Wayland. Diagnosed as Wayland protocol-level prohibition of client-set absolute positions; forced GDK_BACKEND=x11 at run() entry point. All 283 Rust tests passing. ✅ Batch generate MIHD paper analysis scripts — Concurrently created three Python analysis scripts: cross-section query aggregation (aggregate_cross_section_queries.py), paper figure visualization (visualize_paper_figures.py), and HD marker gene visualization (visualize_hd_marker_genes.py). All scripts passed syntax validation. Implementation & Fixes ✅ Clean up git commit history and push to GitHub — Used git reset –soft to squash 24 messy commits into 2 semantically clear commits, force-with-lease pushed to TzJ2006/TokenMonitor:main, retaining backup-before-squash backup branch. ✅ DesktopLinux gadget environment initialization — Installed rclone on the new machine, configured gdrive remote, ran bootstrap to pull the full history of summarize/research/website/test data, updated device_name to DesktopLinux. 🔄 Investigate sync.py pull failure — 6 directories reported ‘directory not found’ during pull; root cause identified as these subdirectories (bugJournal, etc.) having never been pushed to remote. Fix proposed (treat non-existent remote as success) but not implemented. ✅ Install Claude Code skills and plugins — Copied 5 skills (ccplan, cchypothesis, optimize, slurm-gpu, summarize) from gadget/skills to ~/.claude/skills/, and installed and activated 12 plugins via the /plugin command. Problems & Solutions Critical Issues 1. Tauri main window set_position() completely ignored by compositor on Linux/Wayland, window appears at screen center Solution: Detect WAYLAND_DISPLAY environment variable at run() entry; if in a Wayland session, force GDK_BACKEND=x11 to run the application via XWayland.
Key Insight: The Wayland protocol design prohibits clients from controlling absolute window positions; any retry strategy is futile — must fall back to the X11 protocol layer.
2. Float-ball expand layout anchors to screen boundary instead of ball position, causing visual jump when clicking to expand Solution: Modified expanded_rect_from_ball to ball-anchored mode: for Left anchor, expanded x = ball_rect.x; for Right anchor, expanded x = ball_rect.right - expanded_width; combined with clamped_y to keep y stable.
Key Insight: The expanded window’s reference point should anchor to the ball’s own position, not the screen safe-area boundary, to ensure visual position consistency before and after expansion.
3. host_cache_dir() in ssh_remote.rs uses assert!() which panics the entire program on path validation failure Solution: Changed function signature to Result<PathBuf, String>, propagated Result through all call chains, handled with unwrap_or_default() at call sites.
Key Insight: assert!() in production is equivalent to a time bomb; paths accepting external input must use Result to propagate errors.
4. On Linux GTK, setting resizable(false) causes programmatic set_size() to be rejected by WM, preventing float-ball from collapsing Solution: In the Linux branch, call set_resizable(true) before set_size to temporarily unlock, then call set_resizable(false) again after the operation completes.
Key Insight: The resizable(false) WM hint blocks both user drag and programmatic calls — it is not limited to user interaction.
5. FloatBallState does not store window geometry, reads outer_position/outer_size from WM each time, potentially reading stale values during expand/collapse sequencing causing layout errors Solution: Added FloatBallState.last_rect, created apply_and_store_rect helper function to synchronously write window and record state; added get_float_ball_position command for frontend use.
Key Insight: Maintaining authoritative geometry state at the application layer completely bypasses timing non-determinism of WM reads.
6. sync_ssh_host holds an RwLock read lock across SSH I/O await points that can last several seconds, blocking all readers Solution: Added #[derive(Clone)] to SshCacheManager, clone the manager before await to release the lock early.
Key Insight: Locks crossing async boundaries are a common hidden hazard in Rust async programming; the solution is to Clone the needed data before await to release the lock early.
7. provider parameter without validation can be used for path traversal attacks (e.g., passing ‘../../../etc’ to construct malicious paths) Solution: Added validate_provider() whitelist function allowing only ‘claude’ and ‘codex’; other values return Err.
Key Insight: Any user input concatenated into file paths must be whitelist-validated at system boundaries; allowlists are more reliable than denylists for preventing path traversal attacks.
8. load_cached_records silently returns an empty Vec for all I/O errors (including permission denied), masking real errors Solution: Changed to return Result, explicitly distinguishing ErrorKind::NotFound (legitimate, returns Ok(vec![])) from other I/O errors (returns Err).
Key Insight: File not found and unable to read are fundamentally different states; conflating them makes debugging extremely difficult.
9. ssh_command treats partial stdout with non-zero SSH exit code as success, accepting erroneous results Solution: Strictly check exit code first; only accept stdout on success(); log stderr on non-zero exit.
Key Insight: partial stdout + non-zero exit is a common SSH error pattern; non-empty stdout cannot be used as the success criterion.
10. Svelte 5 component uses both onMount and $effect to trigger data requests, causing double requests on mount Solution: Removed onMount call, kept $effect (in Svelte 5, $effect automatically executes once on mount).
Key Insight: Svelte 5’s $effect differs from Svelte 4 reactive statements — it executes immediately on mount and does not need to be paired with onMount.
11. FloatBall component registers Tauri event listeners asynchronously inside onMount with a race condition: component may be destroyed before listen() resolves, causing cleanup function not to be called and memory leaks Solution: Introduced a destroyed boolean flag; if already marked as destroyed when the Promise resolves, immediately call unlisten().
Key Insight: Any pattern of asynchronously acquiring cleanup handles in frontend components must account for the case where the component outlives the Promise.
General Issues 12. sync.py pull reports ‘directory not found’ for 6 directories, user mistakenly believes sync failed Solution: Confirmed root cause is that these subdirectories were never pushed to remote and don’t exist there. The sync logic should treat non-existent remote as success (no data to pull) rather than an error.
Key Insight: Non-existent remote directory is not the same as sync failure; must distinguish between ‘remote has data but pull failed’ and ‘remote never had this directory’.
Human Reasoning vs. AI Reasoning Strategic Level Root Cause Diagnosis and Solution Iteration for FloatBall Collapse Bug Role Approach Human User tested AI proposals and reported them ineffective, providing precise problem descriptions to drive re-diagnosis; independently completed frontend setExpanded timing rewrite (native-first collapse, expansionRequestId race protection) AI AI sequentially proposed three root cause hypotheses: hide/show approach → last_rect authoritative geometry → resizable flag, relying on user feedback each round to eliminate the previous hypothesis Difference Analysis: User’s real-world test feedback was the critical driver of diagnostic progress; without a runtime environment, AI can only reason from code and narrow root causes iteratively; users have more direct judgment on frontend changes.
Root Cause Identification and Solution Selection for Wayland Main Window Positioning Role Approach Human User provided screenshots confirming retry approach was ineffective, pushing AI to find the root cause AI After second round of exploration, AI correctly identified the Wayland protocol-level restriction and proposed the industry-standard GDK_BACKEND=x11 forced X11 fallback solution Difference Analysis: AI has correct theoretical understanding of Wayland vs X11 architectural differences; after ruling out the retry approach, quickly converged to the correct solution; user primarily provided real-world feedback while technical decisions were led by AI.
Tool Selection and Batch Execution for Code Review Fixes Role Approach Human User directly provided the CRITICAL/HIGH/MEDIUM priority list and expected a systematic planning solution via the /ccplan command AI AI recognized ccplan was not suitable for this known-fix scenario and declined, proceeding directly to implementation; batched multiple issues in the same file during execution to reduce file read/write operations Difference Analysis: Human provided structured priority input; AI made reasonable judgments on tool selection and batch execution — for 21 clearly defined issues, direct implementation is more efficient than divergent planning.
Parallel Generation Strategy for MIHD Experiment Scripts Role Approach Human User provided clear experiment requirements and ECL weekly plan framework, with clear priority judgment (do non-GPU-dependent tasks first) AI AI used parallel Agent strategy to simultaneously generate three analysis scripts, systematically completing all three layers: pipeline_config experiment definitions, Slurm scripts, and visualization scripts Difference Analysis: Task parallelization and completeness are AI strengths; judgment of scientific value of experiments (which fusion strategies are worth independent testing) comes from the user’s domain knowledge.
Implementation Level Development Environment and Codebase Access Role Approach Human User knew the codebase already existed locally, rejected AI’s remote clone request, and directly provided the local path AI AI defaulted to attempting a remote GitHub clone, lacking local environment awareness Difference Analysis: Humans have complete awareness of the local development environment; AI should search the local filesystem first — a single user correction sufficed to change direction.
AI Limitations Critical Limitations Diagnostic limitations without a runtime environment: The FloatBall bug required three rounds of hypothesis iteration (hide/show → last_rect → resizable flag) to find the true root cause; the first approach was logically correct at the code level but ineffective in practice; GTK’s behavior of resizable blocking programmatic set_size() is a framework-internal behavior not explicitly documented in the API, which AI cannot predict in advance. General Limitations Lack of local environment awareness: Defaulted to attempting remote GitHub clone of a repository that already existed locally, without prioritizing local filesystem search; required user rejection to correct course. Has theoretical understanding of framework-specific semantics (Svelte 5 $effect lifecycle, GTK resizable side effects), but needs to read actual code to provide reliable advice — cannot give accurate answers without reading the files. Execution limitations for system-level operations requiring sudo: rclone installation via curl | bash failed due to terminal interaction limitations; user must execute manually. Today’s Takeaways Core Takeaways Wayland compositors prohibit clients from setting absolute window positions at the protocol level; any set_position() retry strategy is futile. The correct fix is to detect WAYLAND_DISPLAY and force GDK_BACKEND=x11 to fall back to XWayland. In Rust production code, assert!() and unwrap() on paths that accept external input (file paths, user parameters, SSH output) are potential crash points; the uniform pattern is to change function signatures to Result and propagate with ?, using unwrap_or_default() at call boundaries. GTK’s resizable(false) WM hint blocks both user drag and programmatic set_size(); for transparent floating windows that need dynamic size adjustment, resizable must be temporarily unlocked before resizing. Geometry state of transparent overlay windows should be authoritatively managed at the application layer (last_rect) rather than re-read from WM each time; WM’s outer_position/outer_size may have timing delays after resize, and reading stale values during expand/collapse sequencing causes layout errors. Float window expand layout should anchor to the ball itself (ball-anchored) rather than the screen safe-area boundary, to ensure visual position consistency before and after expansion — no perceived jumping. Locks crossing await points in Rust async code are hard-to-detect performance/deadlock hazards; the fix is to Clone or Arc::clone needed data before await to release the lock early. Any user input concatenated into file paths must be whitelist-validated at system boundaries; ‘only allow known values’ is more reliable protection than ‘reject dangerous characters’, effectively preventing path traversal attacks. In Svelte 5, $effect executes once automatically on component mount; using it together with onMount causes double requests. When asynchronously acquiring cleanup handles in frontend components, a destroyed flag is needed to handle the race condition where the component outlives the Promise. Practical Takeaways git reset –soft is an efficient method for cleaning up commit history: no interactive rebase needed, can squash any number of commits into one semantic commit — suitable for CI environments and automation tools. After adding new directory structures to rclone sync config, remote will not automatically create those directories if data has never been pushed; pull should distinguish between ‘remote does not exist’ (normal, treat as success) and ‘pull failed’ (error) to avoid false negatives. Session Summaries MIHD ✅ MIHD Paper Weekly Plan Execution: GPU Slurm Scripts and Three Visualization Analysis Scripts Created 04:02:08.822 | claude_code Advanced MIHD paper experiment pipeline setup on DCC. Updated ECL weekly plan YAML feature status, added four independent experiment definitions (STAIG, contrastive, LLaVA, AdaLN) and 4 Slurm GPU job scripts to pipeline_config.yaml. Launched three parallel Agents to create cross-section query aggregation, paper figure visualization, and HD marker gene visualization scripts respectively — all scripts passed syntax validation.
TokenMonitor 🔄 Linux Platform Bug Fixes and PR #4 Code Review Systematic Remediation 00:57:54.705 | claude_code Completed TokenMonitor quality improvements on DesktopLinux in two phases. Phase 1 fixed Linux/Wayland platform bugs: main window positioning error resolved by forcing GDK_BACKEND=x11 (283 Rust tests passing); float-ball position jumping and ghost artifact bug fixed through multiple iterations addressing ball-anchored layout, last_rect authoritative geometry, GTK resizable temporary unlock, etc. — some details remained unconfirmed at session end. Phase 2 systematically fixed all 21 code review issues from PR #4, covering CRITICAL (assert! panic, double fetch, SSH exit code, I/O error distinction, path traversal), HIGH (lock across async, NaiveDate overflow, FloatBall race, etc.), and MEDIUM (CI compatibility, LiteLLM boundary validation, etc.) — zero Rust compile warnings, all 199 frontend tests passing. Finally squashed 24 commits into 2 semantic commits via git reset –soft and pushed to GitHub with force-with-lease.
gadget ✅ DesktopLinux New Machine gadget Environment Initialization and Sync Issue Investigation 01:06:53.164 | claude_code Completed gadget toolchain environment initialization on the DesktopLinux new machine: installed rclone, configured gdrive remote, ran bootstrap to pull the full history dataset, updated device_name to DesktopLinux. Also investigated the ‘directory not found’ error for 6 directories during sync.py pull — confirmed root cause is that these subdirectories (bugJournal daily/weekly/monthly, etc.) were never pushed to remote, not a sync failure. Proposed fix direction of distinguishing ‘remote does not exist’ from ‘pull failed’; not implemented.
Claude Code Tools Setup ✅ Install Claude Code Skills and Plugins 00:40:29.284 | claude_code Completed Claude Code development toolchain configuration on DesktopLinux: copied 5 skills (ccplan, cchypothesis, optimize, slurm-gpu, summarize) from gadget/skills to ~/.claude/skills/, and installed and activated 12 plugins via the /plugin command.

TokenMonitor 🔄 TokenMonitor Tauri生产构建与benchmark验证，以及两轮迭代修复悬浮球Linux平台位置跳变与收起残影Bug 00:02:15.999 | claude_code✅ Dual fixes for the bug of Linux floating ball position jumping and ghosting after collapse in Tauri production builds on new Linux machines 20:08:24.935 | claude_code First fixed the Rust PATH issue (source ~/.cargo.env), successfully built TokenMonitor v0.6.1 on new Linux machines in three formats: deb/rpm/AppImage. Subsequently, two rounds of fixes were carried out to address bugs with Linux floating balls on both platforms: In the first round, frontend coordinate conversion and collapse timing were adjusted through static analysis. After user verification, two issues still existed; in the second round, the issue of incorrect calculation of expanded_rect_from_ball() anchor point on the Rust side was corrected (jumping caused by using safe-area edges as anchors), and the CSS-first collapse timing issue was resolved (ghosting occurred because WebKitGTK transparent windows did not clear old pixel buffers). This achieved Rust anchor point reconstruction + native-first collapse, and the floatBallInteraction.ts pure function was extracted. It was fully verified through npm test with 205 cases, svelte-check, and cargo test with 17 cases.
