title: Bug Journal 2026-03-29

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-28 Today’s Highlights What was done: Multiple systematic fixes and functional integrations were completed across two devices on four projects: conceptual layer (VLA context window cleanup) and code layer (policy_adapter timing + render_window renaming) of the Error Recovery Benchmark’s Context Replay mechanism; fixes for LIBERO/BOSS evaluation environments and zero-shot task design analysis; integration of cchypothesis’s intelligent dual-track instrumentation architecture (verified through 11 adversarial problems with the critic agent); four progressive bug fixes for TokenMonitor Tauri: 1-2Hz jitter in the dashboard, 42 Rust compilation errors, 4 failed import paths in the frontend, and reversed window resize bottom position. How it was done: On the tianhe side, planning through ccplan → subagent exploration → batch grep/edit modifications → pytest verification completed; context_replay.py refactoring and 22 places of code cleanup were done; asset directory comparison and traceability to form_boss_44_dataset.py whitelist mechanism confirmed the design intent of the benchmark. On the TzJsDesktop side, ccplan adversarial planning was used for skill documentation refactoring; TokenMonitor side made incremental fixes at the root cause level, with full-link verification using cargo/vitest/svelte-check. What’s achieved: Eliminated fundamental conceptual errors in error_recovery_benchmark (misunderstanding of VLA context window), aligned code logic with documentation; BOSS evaluation pipeline could load all tasks correctly and understand 0% expected behavior of zero-shot tasks; cchypothesis gained runtime instrumentation verification capabilities; TokenMonitor recovered from multiple UX defects to a stable state with 222 Rust tests and 191 frontend tests passed, svelte-check errors were zero. tianhe What was done: Completed two projects: (1) comprehensive refactoring of error_recovery_benchmark Context Replay—correcting VLA context window errors, fixing policy_adapter timing, removing dead code in observations, renaming render_window, and synchronizing approximately 22 places in documentation across 7 files; (2) fixing 5 missing object assets in BOSS benchmark and analyzing that the 0% success rate of 7 LIVING_ROOM zero-shot tasks was a deliberately designed test for benchmarking. How it was done: Structured planning using ccplan skill, combined with subagent exploration of code libraries; batch location via grep and precise editing through tools, with pytest verifying each change (139 unit tests); asset directory comparison on the LIBERO side confirmed design intent through diff. What’s achieved: Improved correctness of context_replay.py code logic (dead code removal, timing correction, naming unification), consistency update across 7 files; BOSS evaluation script could load all 44 task environments; clarity that 0% success rate of 7 zero-shot tasks is expected behavior rather than model issues. TzJsDesktop What was done: Completed two projects: (1) refactoring cchypothesis skill into an intelligent dual-track architecture (Phase 3 parallel static + serial instrumentation + Git Safety Checkpoint + Phase 4 manual confirmation), verified through 11 adversarial problems with the critic agent, involving 4 files and 395/-70 lines of code; (2) four progressive bug fixes for TokenMonitor: 1-2Hz jitter in the dashboard (four-layer positive feedback interruption), 42 compilation errors left after Rust command module refactoring, 4 failed import paths in the frontend, unstable window resize bottom position (Win32 API replacement + dynamic anchor detection removal + IPC atomic commands). How it was done: On the cchypothesis side, ccplan selected plan C and batch-modified 4 technical documents, first self-inspection followed by critic agent verification; on the TokenMonitor side, incremental fixes at the root cause level: RESIZE_SETTLE_DELAY + shallowPayloadEqual to interrupt jitter loops, rewriting commands.rs thin module to resolve Rust module ambiguity, updating import paths to fix Vite parsing issues, removing dynamic anchor detection and fixing resize with fixed bottom anchors through backend IPC atomic commands. What’s achieved: cchypothesis gained runtime instrumentation verification capabilities and passed strict adversarial reviews for the architecture; TokenMonitor completed full testing from multiple UX defects (222 Rust tests + 191 frontend tests, svelte-check errors zero), window positioning behavior aligned with system tray notifications. The work was carried out across tianhe and TzJsDesktop devices simultaneously on four projects: tianhe completed conceptual refactoring and code fixes for error_recovery_benchmark’s Context Replay, as well as fixing LIBERO/BOSS evaluation environment assets and zero-shot design analysis; TzJsDesktop completed intelligent dual-track instrumentation architecture for cchypothesis skill, along with progressive bug fixes for TokenMonitor’s dashboard jitter, Rust compilation errors, frontend import failures, and window anchoring.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Context Replay code logic fix (dead code removal + policy_adapter timing correction + render_window renaming) — removed dead code list in observations (collected but never consumed), moved policy_adapter feeding inside the replay loop (clean frame phase) to Step F (post-injection environment stabilization), renamed context_window globally to render_window (compatible with ErrorScene data structure backward), updated 3 pipeline scripts and test files, 139 unit tests passed. ✅ Integrate instrumentation debugging mode into cchypothesis skill (intelligent dual-track architecture) — through ccplan plan C, refactored Phase 3 as: Git Safety Checkpoint + investigation diversion (static/needs-instrumentation) + parallel static analysis + serial instrumentation detection ([DEBUG Hx] tag logs + each git restore cleanup) + summary; added Human Confirmation in Phase 4; new Instrumentation Protocol section; resolved 11 adversarial problems returned by the critic agent; involved SKILL.md/cchypothesis.md/diagnostic-schema.md/skills/CLAUDE.md, totaling 4 files and 395/-70 lines of code. ✅ TokenMonitor dashboard 1-2Hz vertical jitter fix — four-layer concurrent fixes interrupted the ResizeObserver↔setSize positive feedback loop: ①RESIZE_SETTLE_DELAY_MS increased from 16 to 100ms; ②shallowPayloadEqual skipped meaningless store updates; ③resize throttling (up to 3 times within 500ms); ④is_active check added a 2-minute grace period to eliminate 30-minute oscillation in parser.rs. 191 vitest tests passed. 🔄 Context Replay residual checks and set_sim_state_flat alternative planning — through ccplan, checked for Residual ContextReplayEngine remaining in the code library, found it fully present (393 lines) and used by 3 pipeline scripts; planned to use set_sim_state_flat directly to replace frame-by-frame replay, with user interruption during ExitPlanMode without code changes. ✅ Fixing BOSS benchmark environment missing assets and analyzing zero-shot task design intent — evaluation script reported KeyError: ‘potato’, diff found 5 missing object assets (corn/egg/lemon/onion/potato) in the standard LIBERO repository; copied assets and registered 4 new classes in hope_objects.py, then checked form_boss_44_dataset.py to confirm: boss_44’s allowed_files whitelist contained only 46 KITCHEN_SCENE files, 7 LIVING_ROOM tasks were intentionally excluded as zero-shot generalization tests. ✅ Fixing 42 Rust command module compilation errors left in cchypothesis — rewritten 2222 lines of old commands.rs into about 80 thin modules (declared 6 submodules, AppState and shared helpers retained), eliminating Rust module path ambiguity caused by coexistence of old files and new commands/ directory; also fixed 4 pre-stored clippy warnings. ✅ TokenMonitor window positioning and resize bottom anchor fix — fixed two overlapping bugs: ①replaced inaccurate tauri_plugin_positioner with Win32 API (FindWindowW/FindWindowExW to find TrayNotifyWnd), achieving precise initial window positioning above the system tray; ②removed VerticalAnchor enumeration and detect_vertical_anchor dynamic detection functions, aligned_window_origin always calculated Y coordinates using work.bottom - height; ③frontend setSize() called backend set_window_size_and-align IPC atomic commands to update size and position simultaneously; all tests passed. Implementation and Fixes ✅ TokenMonitor frontend 4 failed import path fixes — updated App.svelte and usage.ts to change import paths of rateLimitMonitor/traySync/windowAppearance to new locations, added resizeDebug stub to uiStability.ts, completed missing usage_source/usage_warning fields in emptyPayload/makePayload; 191 vitest tests passed, svelte-check errors zero for 229 files. ✅ Full code library VLA narrative cleanup and documentation updates (OVERVIEW.md + 22 code library replacements) — corrected five items in OVERVIEW.md (removing VLA timing narrative from Context Window description, changing Trajectory Collector to MimicGen Generator from 10→1000 lines, describing Recovery Behavior Groups, detailed reference tables for 13 Error Skills, statistical data); batch-replaced 22 errors in context_replay.py/framework/init.py/CLAUDE.md/benchmark_v5.yaml/project_panorama.md by deterministic replay narrative, grep verification zero remaining, 139 unit tests passed. Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. Context Replay’s policy_adapter feeds during the replay loop (clean trajectory phase), forcing the strategy to see a clean state before injection rather than a stable state after errors, which does not match actual deployment scenarios. Solution: Move the call to policy_adapter.predict() to Step F (completed collect_rollout_stats, environment stabilized), ensuring the strategy receives post-error observation with a stable environment.
Key Insight: The timing of policy_adapter feeding must align with actual deployment scenarios—the strategy should only see the state after errors occur and the environment stabilizes; feeding clean frames during the replay phase is meaningless.
2. The context_window parameter name carries two meanings (VLA observation window size vs rendering start frame offset), leading to incorrect documentation descriptions like “VLA requires timing context window,” which does not match the actual VLA open-loop reasoning mechanism. Solution: Rename the parameter to render_window, clarifying its sole purpose as controlling rendering range; batch-replace 22 errors in 7 code files with “MuJoCo deterministic simulation state replay” for correct descriptions, grep verification zero remaining.
Key Insight: VLA is an open-loop reasoning system that accepts single frames without maintaining a recurrent state, so it doesn’t need a “timing context window.” Naming is the cheapest form of documentation; incorrect narratives are more damaging than code bugs and must be systematically cleaned up.
3. TokenMonitor Dashboard jitters vertically at a rate of 1-2Hz across multiple layers: data refresh → re-rendering → window Resize → data refresh again, with oscillations in the is_active state at the 30-minute boundary. Solution: Four-layer concurrent fixes: ①RESIZE_SETTLE_DELAY_MS increased from 16 to 100ms to stabilize the window; ②shallowPayloadEqual skipped meaningless store updates; ③resize throttling (up to 3 times within 500ms); ④is_active check added a 2-minute grace period to eliminate time boundary oscillations.
Key Insight: Oscillating jitter bugs involving multiple layers of positive feedback loops must be fixed at all stages simultaneously; any single fix will only weaken the problem, not completely eliminate it.
4. BOSS evaluation script reports KeyError: ‘potato’ during runtime, preventing task environment loading; in boss_44 evaluation results, 7 LIVING_ROOM tasks had 0% success rates, suggesting either model generalization or training data issues. Solution: Copied 5 missing object assets (corn/egg/lemon/onion/potato) from the BOSS repository to the corresponding LIBERO directories and registered them as new classes; checked form_boss_44_dataset.py to confirm that boss_44’s allowed_files whitelist contained only KITCHEN_SCENE files—7 LIVING_ROOM tasks were intentionally excluded as zero-shot generalization tests, with 0% success rate being a deliberately designed benchmark behavior.
Key Insight: BOSS is an extended benchmark of LIBERO, introducing new objects from the standard repository; its core testing concept (Out-of-Suppositional-Set) evaluates the model’s zero-shot generalization ability in completely unseen scenarios, so a 0% failure cannot be misjudged as a model failure.
5. cchypothesis’s current Phase 3 pure READ-ONLY parallel architecture cannot verify runtime assumptions (timing races, data flow states, dynamic behavior), leaving debugging capabilities vulnerable. Solution: Designed an intelligent dual-track architecture: parallel READ-Only subagents for static assumptions; upgrading inconclusive static analysis results to serial instrumentation detection ([DEBUG Hx] tag logs + each git restore cleanup); Git Safety Checkpoint as the entry point for Phase 3, protecting users’ work environment.
Key Insight: Instrumentation debugging should be a step-up mechanism when static analysis cannot provide enough information, rather than an alternative. It allows maintaining parallel processing speed while gaining runtime detection capabilities.
6. Rust project had 42 compilation errors (unresolved import crate::change_stats/crate::integrations), and the coexistence of old commands.rs (2222 lines) with new commands/ directory caused module path ambiguity. Solution: Rewrote 2222 lines of old commands.rs into about 80 thin modules, correctly declaring 6 submodules under the commands/ directory; removed all duplicate functions and outdated imports moved to submodules; also fixed 4 pre-stored clippy warnings.Key Insights: In the Rust module system, commands.rs and commands/mod_rs serve as mutually exclusive module roots. When both exist simultaneously, old files prevent new sub-modules from being recognized. Incremental refactoring must be done in a last-minute manner to replace or delete old entries.
7. The TokenMonitor pop-up appears in the middle-left part of the screen rather than the system tray area. During resizing, the bottom edge moves instead of staying fixed at the top (top-bottom inversion). Dynamic detection using detect_vertical_anchor is prone to false positives under initialization timing and concurrency conditions. Solutions: ① Use the Win32 API (Shell_TrayWnd → TrayNotifyWnd) to obtain precise tray coordinates instead of tauri_plugin_positioner. ② Remove the dynamic detection for detect_vertical_anchor, and always use work.bottom - height to calculate the Y coordinate for aligned_window_origin. ③ Change the front-end setSize() method to call the back-end set_window_size_and_align IPC atomic command to update both size and position.
Key Insights: The Windows support for tauri_plugin_positioner is unreliable. The system tray pop-up always anchors at the bottom, so no dynamic detection is required. Tauri’s setSize() is a pure size API, and resizing needs to be handled through the back-end IPC atomic command to process both size and position.
General Issues 8. The observations list collects the last 50 frames of observations during the replay loop, but subsequent code never consumes this list. This is dead code that wastes memory and confuses with the rendering purpose. Solution: Directly remove the observations list and the related context_start initialization code. Rendering is completed independently through the render_fn callback, so it doesn’t affect the functionality.
Key Insights: The render_fn callback and observation collection are two parallel mechanisms. The latter is dead code left over from incremental development. Removing this code doesn’t affect any functions.
9. Vite cannot parse four front-end import paths (rateLimitMonitor.js/traySync.js/windowAppearance.js/resizeDebug.js). This is because after deleting old files for incremental refactoring, the references were not updated synchronously. Solution: Update the import paths to new locations. Add a resizeDebug stub function as an alternative export in uiStability.ts. Complete the missing fields in emptyPayload/makePayload.
Key Insights: After deleting old files for incremental refactoring, all import references must be updated synchronously. Otherwise, Vite will encounter parsing errors.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level VLA Domain Knowledge: Direct understanding of the context window concept and project architecture Role Thought Human The user clearly pointed out that VLA does not have a context window concept (open-loop reasoning, each step is independent, no recurrent state maintained). There is no need to replay 50 frames for VLA during injection. At the same time, it was directly pointed out that Trajectory Collector in Section 3.2 is actually MimicGen Generator (10→1000 demo items). AI The AI did not question the design assumptions in the code comments regarding VLA awareness. It accepted the old narrative. In understanding project architecture, it needed to reverse-engineer through code paths, so its understanding lagged behind the user’s direct knowledge. Difference Analysis: The user has domain knowledge about the VLA reasoning mechanism and a direct understanding of the overall project design intention. The AI tends to trust the design assumptions in the existing code. Its understanding of architecture is not as accurate as that of the user. It needs the user to correct it actively to start systematic cleaning.
Policy_adapter Correct Feeding Time Physical Intuition Role Thought Human The user clearly stated that policy_adapter should start feeding only after “the incorrect injection is completed and the environment is stable.” This is a precise requirement for sim-to-real alignment. Based on physical simulation intuition, it immediately judged the correct timing. AI The AI found that policy_adapter was called during the replay loop. However, it tended to let the user confirm through option enumeration rather than directly judging the correct timing based on simulation semantics. Difference Analysis: The user immediately judged the correct timing based on physical intuition. The AI needed to confirm through alternative solutions, indicating that its understanding of simulation semantics was not deep enough.
Rust Module Refactoring Root Cause Independent Identification Role Thought Human The user only provided compilation error messages without explaining the refactoring background or module coexistence issues. AI The AI actively analyzed the structure of the old and new modules through the Explore agent. It independently identified that commands_rs as the module root is the fundamental reason why commands/ cannot be recognized. It developed a complete rewrite plan. Difference Analysis: The AI correctly identified the unique ambiguity trap in the Rust module system, showing an active analysis ability beyond what the user prompted. This is a problem at the language mechanism level, not dependent on domain knowledge.
BOSS Zero-Sample Task Cause Assumption Direction Role Thought Human The human actively proposed the core assumption: 7 tasks with a 0% success rate might be due to an uncovered training set rather than poor model generalization ability. AI The AI listed the training set files, compared and evaluated the tasks, reviewed the dataset construction script to verify the assumption, and further found that this was a zero-sample generalization test deliberately designed by the benchmark. Difference Analysis: The human proposed the correct problem direction (data coverage assumption). The AI was responsible for verifying it and provided an explanation at the mechanism level (the allowed_files white-list design). The human’s intuition was correct, while the AI provided a chain of evidence.
cchypothesis Integration Plan Selection Decision Role Thought Human The human chose the most thorough architecture reconstruction plan C (intelligent dual-track). They selected all four integration modes and approved the complete implementation plan that included 11 risk fixes. The decision was more radical than expected by the AI. AI The AI designed three progressively complex plans and recommended plan C. It first conducted adversarial analysis and repaired most of the known risks based on its own analysis before returning results to the critic agent after feedback. Difference Analysis: The human’s decision was more radical than expected by the AI. The AI’s proactive repair mode showed initiative, but this led to the critical agent’s discovery being a post-event confirmation rather than preventive action.
TokenMonitor User Visual Perception and Bug Identification Independence vs Repair Complexity Judgment Role Thought Human The user immediately identified several independent problems through visual perception. Screenshots directly showed incorrect pop-up positions. After the first repair, it was pointed out that there was still an independent “bottom fixed but top moving” logic issue (used ?????? to imply that the repair should be simple). The Dashboard collected community solution documents in advance. AI The AI repaired each bug reported only once. It did not anticipate that resize was an independent second bug. When analyzing window positioning, it first called Explore + Plan agents for extensive analysis. In reality, the repair only required deleting about 30 lines of code. Difference Analysis: The user’s user perspective allowed them to directly perceive functional defects and judge the complexity of repairs. The AI tended to analyze each bug systematically, possibly overanalysing simple problems. The user collected solutions in advance, separating high-cost collection steps.
AI Limitations Important Limitations Insufficient understanding of physical simulation semantics: In terms of the incorrect timing for feeding policy_adapter, the AI tended to ask “which plan to choose” rather than directly judging the correct timing based on simulation semantics. In terms of the VLA open-loop reasoning mechanism, it did not question the wrong assumptions in the code comments actively. It needed the user to correct them explicitly before starting systematic cleaning. Blind spots in code data flow analysis: The dead code of the observations list (collected but never consumed) required the user’s guidance to be discovered. When repairing TokenMonitor window initial positioning, it did not anticipate that resize was an independent second bug. It needed the user’s visual feedback to realize that Tauri’s setSize() API did not update position after removing about 30 lines of code. Issues with execution rhythm and parallel processing judgment: After the ccplan planning was completed, attempts were made to proceed with changes without waiting for user confirmation, resulting in interruptions by the user. The critic agent only returned results after the implementation was completed (about 390 lines of change had been submitted). Anti-vigilance became a post-event confirmation rather than preventive action. For simple problems (repairing window positioning that deleted about 30 lines of code), excessive use of Explore + Plan agents for extensive analysis was carried out. General Limitations Lack of global view across sessions: Repeatedly scanning the same type of problem in multiple sessions (VLA context narrative) started from scratch each time, resulting in low efficiency. Environment-dependent and tool-usage limitations: Unable to verify LIBERO OBJECTS_DICT registration in the main environment (requiring robosuite). The process substitution diff command failed in Windows Git Bash and needed serial retries. It did not support reading binary files (.mp4, etc.). Today’s Takeaways Core Takeaway The VLA (Vision-Language-Action) model is an open-loop reasoning mechanism: Each step accepts single-frame observation outputs of actions without maintaining a recurrent hidden state. Therefore, there is no need to “pre-replay N frames to establish a context window” - this assumption is a fundamental misunderstanding of the VLA working mechanism. The MuJoCo simulation state is deterministic but not reproducible as a snapshot: It is necessary to execute actions frame by frame from the initial state to obtain the correct intermediate physical state. Directly setting sim_state_flat to the target frame is a potential alternative (physical consistency needs to be evaluated). UI jitter-related bugs are often the result of multiple layers of positive feedback loops. Single-layer repairs can only weaken, not eliminate, these problems. It is necessary to interrupt all links simultaneously (measurement delay, equality check, throttling valve, data boundary grace period). The correct architecture mode for poking debugging: Use static parallel analysis as the main path and poking detection as the upgrade path for inconclusive results. Each assumption is independently completed through the instrument→run→analyze→cleanup cycle. Git Safety Checkpoint protects the user’s work area at the entry point. In multi-stage debugging, use git restore. rather than git stash to avoid stacking conflicts. Naming is the cheapest way to express design intentions (context_window → render_window). Incorrect documentation narratives are more harmful than code bugs - they do not affect the current running results, but they cause systematic misunderstandings during project handover, paper writing, and code review. It is necessary to actively identify and systematically clean up such issues. BOSS benchmark design mechanism: The boss_44 training set intentionally covers 37 KITCHEN tasks through the allowed_files white-list but excludes 7 LIVING_ROOM tasks for zero-sample generalization evaluation. BOSS has expanded the standard LIBERO object assets (corn/egg/lemon/onion/potato). Before using them, you need to obtain them separately from the BOSS repository. The system tray pop-up should always anchor at the bottom (work.bottom - height). There is no need for dynamic detection (dynamic detection is prone to false positives under initialization timing and concurrency conditions). The Windows support for tauri_plugin_positioner is unreliable. It is necessary to directly obtain precise coordinates using Win32 API (FindWindowW/FindWindowExW). Tauri’s setSize() is a pure size API. Resizing needs to be handled through the back-end IPC atomic command to handle both size and position. The safe order for Rust incremental module refactoring: First create new file structures and re-export old entry files. After verification, replace or delete old entries in the last step. The coexistence of commands_rs and commands/mod_rs causes module path ambiguity. Old files prevent new sub-modules from being recognized. The shallowPayloadEqual mode (reference equality check when cache hits + shallow comparison of fields during background refresh) is an effective way to avoid unnecessary Svelte store rendering. It is especially suitable for high-frequency data polling scenarios. Session Summary Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Comprehensive Context Replay Reconstruction: Correction of VLA Concept + Cleanup of Narrative + Fixing Code Logic 04:10:46.496 | claude_code The user discovered a fundamental error in the context_replay.py code regarding “VLA requiring a temporal context window.” A comprehensive reconstruction was systematically completed through four sessions: ① Planning to use set_sim_state_flat instead of frame-by-frame replay (user interrupted and did not execute); ② Correcting five items in OVERVIEW.md (description of Context Window, MimicGen Generator with 10→1000 items, detailed tables for Error Skills, etc.) and fixing seven related configuration files; ③ Batch replacing about 22 lines of VLA narrative in the code library with deterministic replay descriptions. grep verification showed no remaining errors; ④ Fixing three code logic defects: removing dead code in observations, moving policy_adapter feeding to after post-injection stability (Step F), and renaming context_window globally to render_window (compatible with ErrorScene backward). A total of 139 unit tests passed.
OpenPI-LIBERO ✅ Fixing the missing object assets in the BOSS benchmark and confirming the design intention for zero-sample tasks 11:06:05.882 | claude_code When running the BOSS evaluation script, a KeyError: ‘potato’ was encountered. Through diff comparison, it was found that 5 object assets (corn/egg/lemon/onion/potato) were missing in the standard LIBERO repository. After copying and registering 4 new classes, further analysis revealed that the success rate of all 7 LIVING_ROOM tasks was 0. Verifying with form_boss_44_dataset.py confirmed that the allowed_files white-list intentionally excluded LIVING_ROOM scenarios. This is the core design of the BOSS benchmark - evaluating the model’s zero-sample generalization ability on completely unseen scenarios. A success rate of 0% is expected behavior.
gadget-skills ✅ Integrating poking debugging mode into cchypothesis skill (intelligent dual-track architecture) 04:19:54.398 | claude_code First, a web search confirmed that there was no similar product on the market. The cchypothesis’s parallel subagents and batch assumption design have unique characteristics. Through complete ccplan planning, the user selected all four modes and chose plan C (intelligent dual-track). The AI completed Phase 3 (Git Safety Checkpoint + investigation diversion + parallel static + serial poking with [DEBUG Hx] tags + git restore per round), expanded Phase 4 (manual confirmation), added an Instrumentation Protocol section, and completed repairs after receiving feedback from 11 anti-vigilance issues in the critic agent. This involved 4 files with a total of +395/-70 lines of changes.
TokenMonitor ✅ Progressive Bug Repairs for TokenMonitor (jittering, Rust compilation, front-end import, window positioning) 04:02:33.844 | claude_code Throughout the day, four progressive repairs were completed: ① UI jitter at 1-2Hz - The user provided community solution documents. The AI identified three layers of positive feedback loops and implemented four repairs: increasing RESIZE_SETTLE_DELAY, shallowPayloadEqual shallow comparison, throttling valve for resize, and a 2-minute grace period for is_active. ② Rust commands module refactoring left 42 compilation errors - Rewriting 2222 lines of old commands_rs into an 80-line thin module root resolved the module path ambiguity caused by the coexistence of commands_rs and commands/ modules. ③ Four failed front-end import paths were updated to new locations, and resizeDebug stub was added as a substitute export in svelte-check’s 229 files with zero errors. ④ Window initial positioning and resize bottom anchor - Using Win32 API to replace tauri_plugin_positioner, removing the dynamic anchor detection and changing it to work.bottom - height for fixed bottom anchoring. The front-end setSize() was changed to an IPC atomic command. Finally, 222 Rust and 191 front-end tests all passed.

On tianhe, the implementation of Context Replay concept refactoring and code fixes for error_recovery_benchmark was completed. title: 错误日志 2026-03-28

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-27 Today’s Highlights What was done: Completed in-depth improvements to three independent systems: unifying the log output of BOSS benchmark evaluation scripts, implementing a scientific-based cchypothesis debugging skill design, and fixing multiple layers of defenses for the stability of TokenMonitor’s bottom edge. How it was done: The tianhe device completed the evaluation infrastructure construction through code reading and precise modifications; TzJsDesktop implemented the cchypothesis using the complete 9-stage requirements engineering process of ccplan, and then used the assumptions of cchypothesis itself to drive the debugging process to identify visual issues at various levels of TokenMonitor. What’s achieved: The scientific evaluation process has obtained unified result recording standards; a systematic debugging tool has been added to the Claude Code skill ecosystem; TokenMonitor has achieved true bottom edge stability (removing SWP_NOCOPYBITS, using position:fixed footer, and setting JS preset minHeight). TzJsDesktop What was done: Explored installing the Claude-plugins-official market and skills, implemented the cchypothesis debugging skill through the complete ccplan process design, and carried out 5 rounds of iterative front-end repairs to TokenMonitor. How it was done: ccplan completed Phase 0-10 (intention calibration → adversarial review → review gate control → implementation), using the cchypothesis debugging process to drive TokenMonitor issue diagnosis. After each round of repairs, visual verification by users was conducted and feedback provided, creating a closed loop. What’s achieved: The cchypothesis skill is installed in ~/.claude/skills/ as a permanent tool; TokenMonitor has completely solved the bottom edge jitter problem. The key finding is that the inherent 1-frame delay between Win32 SetWindowPos and CSS re-layout must be bypassed at the architecture level, rather than being counteracted. tianhe What was done: Modified three openpi BOSS benchmark evaluation scripts to unify the success rate log output and JSON result saving logic, no longer relying on the args.save_stats command flag. How it was done: Moved the succ_list/eval_task_id addition logic outside the if args.save_stats condition, added import json, implemented unconditional terminal table output and save of eval_results.json; eval_skill_chain.py additionally maintained an all_chain_results dictionary to aggregate multiple chain results. What’s achieved: The BOSS evaluation experiment results no longer rely on command line flags. The three evaluation scripts have established unified output standards, improving the reproducibility of experiments. Unified the log and result saving logic of robot evaluation scripts, fully designed and implemented a cchypothesis-driven debugging skill, and completely fixed the TokenMonitor window bottom edge jitter problem through 5 rounds of iterations.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Design and implement a cchypothesis-driven debugging skill — Through the complete 9-stage process design of ccplan, a 6-phase debugging workflow was designed (Bug Triage → assumption generation → parallel read-only investigation → repair verification → cycle control → diagnostic documentation). After 10 adversarial discoveries were corrected, three files SKILL.md, slash command, and diagnostic-schema were created and installed in ~/.claude/skills/. ✅ Fix the bottom edge jitter when TokenMonitor’s height changes — Fixed the Windows anchor point detection algorithm (replacing the 5px threshold with distance comparison), fixed the bottom anchor positioning logic (keeping current_rect.bottom unchanged), removed SWP_NOCOPYBITS to reduceWebView2 repainting flicker, and added a syncSizeAndVerify after an additional 50ms delay after animation completion. ✅ Fix TokenMonitor Footer jitter — Made theFooter position:fixed;bottom:0 to fix it at the bottom of the viewport, completely bypassing the 1-frame delay of CSS layout.移出了.from.pop-content to.out to avoid the impact of transform animations, and added a footer-spacer placeholder to prevent content overlap. ✅ Fix TokenMonitor App bottom edge transparent gap flicker — Before sending IPC with applyWindowHeight, preset appEl.style.minHeight as the target height to ensure the browser layouts #app to the new height in the same frame, eliminating the transparent gap caused by the 1-frame delay between SetWindowPos and CSS re-layout. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Fix TokenMonitor Chart hover panel flicker — Removed theDOM destruction and reconstruction caused by{#key displayedIdx}, shortened DETAIL_FADE_MS from 500ms to 150ms, merged the hide process, added an$effect guard, and eliminated double animation flickers when switching bars. ✅ Unify BOSS benchmark evaluation script log and saving logic — Modified three scripts eval_boss44.py, eval_oss_ch.py, and eval_skill_chain.py. Success rate statistics and save of eval_results.json no longer rely on the args.save_stats flag, always outputting summary and average values of each task’s success rate. ✅ Configure Claude-plugins-official market and install frontend-design skill — Added the anthropics/claude-plugins-official plugin market, installed the frontend-design skill, and understood the superpowers workflow management framework and the core concept of frontend-design to avoid AI-like front-end design. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. Footer jitter during window height animation: The flex-end layout cannot solve this problem because the screen position of the Footer equals window_top+content_height, there is an inherent 1-frame delay between SetWindowPos and CSS re-layout. Solution: Change the Footer to position:fixed;bottom:0, directly anchoring it to the bottom of the viewport, completely bypassing the CSS layout frame delay.
Key Insight: The 1-frame delay between Win32 SetWindowPos and browser CSS re-layout is an inherent architecture problem. Any positioning scheme that relies on CSS layout flow cannot eliminate this delay. Position:fixed relative to the viewport is the only way to bypass it.
2. Windows anchor point detection depends on a 5px absolute threshold, resulting in incorrect anchor direction judgment for non-edge windows; bottom anchor uses work.bottom, causing the window to jump to the screen working area bottom when resized instead of keeping the current bottom edge. Solution: Change to comparing the relative sizes of top_gap and bottom_gap to determine the anchor point; change bottom anchor to current_rect.bottom.min(work-bottom) - height, keeping the current bottom edge unchanged.
Key Insight: Anchor point judgment should be based on the relative distance of ‘which side is closer’, not an absolute threshold. Keeping the bottom edge fixed means calculating a new top using ‘current bottom position - new height’, rather than using the screen bottom.
3. #app bottom edge has a temporary transparent gap when the window expands: #app’s min-height:100% needs to wait for CSS re-layout updates, while SetWindowPos has already been executed, and the newly added area of the window becomes transparent during this period. Solution: Execute appEl.style.minHeight=nextHeight+‘px’ synchronously before invoking IPC, allowing the browser to immediately layout #app to the target height in the same frame.
Key Insight: JS directly setting style properties is 1-frame earlier than waiting for CSS re-layout, matching the IPC sending timing, ensuring that #app always stays synchronized with the window size.
4. SWP_NOCOPYBITS flag causes WebView2 to discard all old content after each SetWindowPos, making the window area completely transparent before repainting, resulting in flickering. Solution: Remove SWP_NOCOPYBITS from resize_window_pos_flags(), keeping only the pixels of the old content for the newly added areas.
Key Insight: The design of SWP_NOCOPYBITS is intended for scenarios where content changes completely. It’s harmful for Tauri transparent windows, where content needs continuous rendering.
General Problems 5. Chart hover panel flickers when switching between bars:{#key displayedIdx} causes DOM destruction and reconstruction, with old nodes fading out in 500ms and new nodes fading in in 500ms simultaneously. Solution: Remove the block enclosed by{#key displayedIdx} to allow Svelte to update content responsively; shorten DETAIL_FADE_MS to 150ms; merge scheduleSlotCleanup into beginDetailHide to eliminate intermediate states.
Key Insight: The {#key} block will completely destroy and reconstruct the DOM every time its value changes, suitable for scenarios that need state reset, but causing unnecessary double animations for scenarios that only require content updates.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Use ‘Xs ago’ timestamps as a visual reference indicator for bottom edge stability Role Idea Human Proposed using the screen coordinates of ‘Xs ago’ in the Footer to determine whether the bottom edge is stable: if this element doesn’t move, then the bottom edge is stable; if it moves, then the bottom edge is moving. AI Analyzed the animation system, differences in easing curves, and timer competition at the code level, proposing H1-H3 technical assumptions and investigating them concurrently. Difference Analysis: Humans instinctively found a specific observable visual anchor point as a diagnostic tool, which is more direct and effective than AI’s code-level analysis. This key insight directly led to the discovery of H4 (CSS layout frame delay is the real root cause).
Iterative Verification Method for Problem Fixing Role Idea Human Immediately after each round of repairs, visually observe through tauri dev with precise language descriptions of residual problems: ‘Footer is still fixed but APP bottom edge is moving’, ‘bottom edge moves up first and then down’. AI After each repair, run unit tests and type checks, assuming the problem has been solved based on code logic. Difference Analysis: Tauri’s native window behavior visual defects (frame delay, transparent gap) cannot be detected through code testing and can only be discovered through visual observation. Human direct observation is the only reliable way to verify such problems. AI tests that prove the correctness of code logic can only prove the correctness of visual effects, not the reality of frame delays and transparent gaps.
Parallel Assumption Processing Design for cchypothesis Skill Role Idea Human Specifically required generating 3 assumptions per time to be processed in parallel, with a maximum of 9. If exceeded, document it. Specified the names of specific workflow nodes (fine-tune prompt, split intentions). AI Through Phase 0 calibration, clarified the specific meaning of ‘fine-tune prompt’ in the debugging context, proposed 5 architectural solutions that were discovered and corrected after adversarial review, including 2 critical defects (parallel writing conflicts requiring read-only investigation, successfully defined ambiguity requiring three-level testing verification). Difference Analysis: Humans defined business requirements and core constraints, while AI was responsible for exploring system details and ensuring quality. AI’s discovery of the key design decision ‘parallel subagents must be read-only’ was not considered in the original human description.
AI Limitations Important Limitations The TokenMonitor window bottom edge jitter problem took 5 rounds of iterations to finally solve. After each round, AI thought the problem was solved and passed all tests, but the actual visual effect still had problems. Tauri’s native window frame-level visual defects exceeded the detection capabilities of static code analysis. AI initially did not realize that the inherent 1-frame delay between Win32 SetWindowPos and CSS re-layout is an architectural problem. Instead, it tried to fix it with better CSS layout (flex-end), requiring multiple rounds of human feedback to find the correct bypass solution of position:fixed. General Limitations Unable to read user-provided.mp4 video files (binary format does not support this), resulting in loss of the specific visual defect context shown in the video. Only code-based inferences can be made about the problem symptoms. Today’s Gains Core Gains There is an inherent 1-frame delay between Win32 SetWindowPos and browser CSS re-layout, which is a core constraint in Tauri desktop application development. Solution: Use position:fixed to bypass CSS layout for UI elements at the bottom; use JS to pre-synchronize style.minHeight for the background container; avoid SWP_NOCOPYBITS to preventWebView2 from repainting throughout the frame. Practical verification of cchypothesis-style assumption-driven debugging: Turning intuitive guesses into verifiable hypotheses and investigating them concurrently effectively shortened the debugging cycle. Key constraint: The investigation phase of parallel subagents must be read-only, and writing operations (editing code, running tests) must be serialized; otherwise, file write conflicts will occur. Visual quality verification of Tauri applications must rely on manual visual testing. Unit tests and type checks can only verify logical correctness and cannot detect native window-level visual defects such as frame delays and transparent gaps. Practical Gains Window anchor point detection should be based on relative distance comparison rather than absolute threshold: Directly compare the sizes of top_gap and bottom_gap, allowing the window to correctly select the nearest side as the anchor point regardless of edge conditions.| Metric | Value | |——–|——-| | Total Tokens | 22,134,140 | | Input Tokens | 14,873 | | Output Tokens | 43,772 | | Cache Creation | 1,092,954 | | Cache Reads | 20,982,541 | | Cache Hit Rate | 95.0% | | Total Cost (USD) | $15.7602 | Model Details Model Input Output Cache Creation Cache Reads Cost Percentage claude-opus-4-6 5,747 30,914 783,198 18,761,918 $15.0775 95.7% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 9,126 12,858 309,756 2,220,623 $0.6827 4.3% Usage by Device Device Total Tokens Input Output Cost tianhe 398,799 18 4,236 $0.5632 TzJsDesktop 21,735,341 14,855 39,536 $15.1971 Codex Overview Metric Value Total Tokens 18,054,161 Input Tokens 17,940,411 Output Tokens 113,750 Inference Tokens 57,848 Cache Reads 16,487,040 Total Cost (USD) $9.4614 Model Details Model Input Output Inference Cache Reads Cost Percentage gpt-5.4 17,940,411 113,750 57,848 16,487,040 $9.4614 100.0%
summary: 我们统一了机器人评估脚本的日志和结果保存逻辑，并完全实现了cchypothesis假设驱动调试功能。同时，通过五轮迭代，我们彻底解决了TokenMonitor窗口底部出现抖动的问题。
title: 错误日志 2026-03-27

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-26 Today’s Highlights What was done: Three devices were used in parallel: DCC advanced spatial transcriptomics research engineering, Tianhe completed robot strategy evaluation and project restructuring, TzJsDesktop iterated Claude Code skill toolchain and significantly upgraded TokenMonitor desktop application. How it was done: Utilized SLURM cluster management, structured workflows driven by ccplan/summarize/optimize skills, parallel subagent code exploration, Tauri+Rust+Svelte full-stack development, and ECL document cross-session state preservation mechanisms. What’s achieved: Produced MIHD-QueST alignment implementation and preliminary benchmark data, Pi0.5 full-task benchmark (showing significant performance differences between tasks), completely re-engineered Error Recovery Benchmark, significantly improved ccplan quality, and TokenMonitor iterated from multiple defect states to production readiness with multiple successful builds. DCC What was done: Completed gadget skill installation, HPC GPU resource exploration, analysis of MIHD-QueST cross-sample query gaps and code alignment (4 gaps), planning and partial implementation of 8-genetic encoder benchmarks (completed 4/8), generation of MIHD technical documentation. How it was done: Used sinfo to accurately filter available GPU nodes, analyzed ccplan skill-driven requirements and planned, explored the code library in depth with parallel Explore agents, isolated multiple conda environments using Cache-First Integration architecture. What’s achieved: Established a QueST-style benchmark extension (with –quest-style flag) and an extensible multi-encoder evaluation framework; HVG1500 (ARI=0.33) outperforms all tested base models, providing important benchmarks for research directions. TzJsDesktop What was done: Completed systematic upgrades to ccplan skills (Phase 0 + multi-intention decomposition + Phase 4-6 refinement + Feature Guard + flow interruption fixes), added –for parameter to code-summarize and slurm-gpu skill; TokenMonitor completed automation of three-platform construction, Windows-native UX (taskbar embedding/transparent rounded corners/dynamic positioning), full lifecycle iteration of floating balls (implementation → multiple rounds of restructuring → interactive improvement), large-scale code refactoring, chart hover fixes, and ccusage integration. How it was done: Used ccplan/simplify/optimize skill-driven workflows, ECL YAML cross-session state preservation planning, Tauri CLI+cargo+vitest+svelte-check full-link verification, multiple production builds confirmed. What’s achieved: Upgraded ccplan to a spiral requirements engineering framework with intention calibration and deep adversarial review; TokenMonitor iterated from multi-functional defect states to production readiness, successfully generating MSI+NSIS installation packages multiple times. Tianhe What was done: Completed evaluation of Pi0.5 merged-LoRA D0/D1 full-task rollout (10 tasks, 8×A800 parallel), zero-config migration of BOSS Benchmark to openpi LIBERO, improvement of lerobot2rlds tool; precise documentation and systematic code refactoring of error_recovery_benchmark (all 139 tests passed); first installation of ccplan/summarize/optimize skills. How it was done: Written and debugged multiple shell scripts for parallel evaluation, seamless integration with BOSS through Python module injection, incremental refactoring driven by the /init→/summarize→/optimize→/ccplan skill chain. What’s achieved: Obtained complete Pi0.5 performance data (significant differentiation between Stack 96-98% and PickPlace 6%); engineering implementation of BOSS evaluation process; Error Recovery Benchmark eliminated ~60 lines of duplicate code and fixed security vulnerabilities. Throughout the day, three devices were used in parallel: DCC completed MIHD-QueST cross-sample query protocol alignment and 8-genetic encoder benchmark framework construction; Tianhe completed Pi0.5 LoRA full-task rollout evaluation, BOSS environment migration, and systematic reengineering of Error Recovery Benchmark; TzJsDesktop performed multiple system upgrades to ccplan skills (Prompt Calibration, multi-intention decomposition, Feature Guard), and completed large-scale feature iterations for TokenMonitor (full lifecycle of floating balls, Windows-native UX, code refactoring, ccusage integration).
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Analysis of MIHD-QueST cross-sample query protocol gaps and alignment implementation — Read arXiv:2410.10652v3 (QueST), identified 4 query protocol gaps (query granularity/candidate representation/niche type/evaluation metrics); created new utils/niche_utils.py (K-hop mean-pool, classification of 7 types of boundary niches, NCJS calculation), modified benchmark_rm_ideal.py to add –quest-style mode, maintaining backward compatibility with the original mode, and removing the archive version. 🔄 MIHD 8-genetic encoder DLPFC benchmark (Cache-First architecture) — Planned ARI/NMI evaluation for 8 encoders (PCA/HVG1500/scGPT-spatial/scGPT original/TEDDY/UCE/C2S/Geneformer); completed embedding extraction of 4/8 encoders (HVG1500 with ARI=0.3300 was the best, scGPT original at 0.1934, C2S extraction complete), Geneformer near completion, UCE installation in progress due to Figshare download failures. ✅ Precise documentation and systematic code refactoring of Error Recovery Benchmark — Through/init→/summarize→/optimize→/ccplan workflows: improved CLAUDE.md (added missing module descriptions), corrected classification system errors (29→26 subtypes, removed D2); extracted 6 shared helpers from BaseErrorSkill (eliminated ~60 lines of duplicate code), fixed security and performance issues such as bare except/hot path import/variable list closures; updated OVERVIEW.md to reflect precise metrics after refactoring; all 139 tests passed (0.82s). ✅ Evaluation of Pi0.5 LoRA D0/D1 full-task rollout — Confirmed that training was interrupted by Slurm time limit at 25,000 steps; completed parallel evaluation of 50 trials each for 6 D0 tasks and 4 D1 tasks on an an72 node with 8×A800. D0 results: Stack 96%, StackThree 78%, ThreePieceAssembly 28%, Coffee 16%, Threading 14%, PickPlace 6%; D1: Stack 98%, StackThree 58%, Coffee 26%, ThreePieceAssembly 24%. ✅ Multiple system upgrades to ccplan skills (Phase 0 + multi-intentions + refinement + flow interruption fixes) — Borrowed from AutoPrompt/Prompt Master/Prompt Improver, added Phase 0 (5-step Prompt Calibration); added Step 1 multi-intention decomposition (coupled/related/independent classification + orbital parallelization), updated ECL schema; refined Phase 4-6 (10-dimensional adversarial review + minimum discovery threshold max(3,N/2), 4-layer dependency analysis, forced at least one feasibility probe, removed skip all option); fixed WebSearch flow interruption (added Tool Invocation State Preservation section + three inline reminders); synchronized two copies of installation directory. ✅ Design and implementation of ccplan Feature Guard Protocol — Added ECL feature_guard section within ccplan skill, Phase 10 automatic guard generation; created portable guard-check.py script, using python-reviewer to detect and fix 2 CRITICAL security issues (shell injection+bare except) and 5 HIGH issues, completed 5 performance optimizations (regular expression preprocessing+PyYAML caching+result latency caching). ✅ TokenMonitor Windows-native UX (taskbar embedding + transparent rounded corners + dynamic positioning) — Implemented platform/windows/taskbar.rs (Win32 SetParent+GDI rendering, 400-600px panel, 28px font, Explorer restart recovery, DPI adaptive, dark/light color themes); transparent window+DwmSetWindowAttribute DWMWA_WINDOW_CORNER_PREFERENCE (value 33) rounded corners, WebView with transparent background; added reposition_window IPC command to accurately align the bottom of the floating window with the taskbar after each setSize() operation; reorganized platform/modularity. ✅ Complete implementation and multiple rounds of refactoring for TokenMonitor cross-platform floating balls — Implemented from scratch (Tauri secondary WebView window, FloatBall.svelte, hover expansion+drag+edge吸附) → multiple rounds of refactoring: four-sided adsorption + threshold (20px/radius of about 42px configurable), overall margin of 8px when expanding, changed to blur event for focus loss retract, horizontal expansion→capsule UI (single capsule container.shell, ball embedded at the end), Win32 CombineRgn native shape clipping, Pointer Capture replaced by startDragging (5px threshold distinguishing drag/double-click), FloatBallState backend geometric state machine (direction adaptive+bottom alignment), automatic switching between Windows/Linux platforms for floating ball mode and disabling taskbar embedding. ✅ Phase 9 file reorganization of TokenMonitor (five waves of execution) — Wave1 archived MCP/docs/ccusage deprecated files; Wave2 removed 100+ resizeDebug instrumentation; Wave3 Rust backend reorganization (usage/stats/tray three subdirectories); Wave4 front-end reorganization (tray/window/views subdirectories); Wave5 full verification (Rust 191+front-end 163 tests, svelte-check 0 errors), CLAUDE.md updated synchronously, generated MSI+NSIS installation packages. ✅ Animations for detailed chart hover details and window flicker fixes — Identified the root cause chain (CSS max-height collapse→ResizeObserver→Tauri native SetWindowPos jitter); implemented a layered CSS transition delay scheme (opacity fades out without triggering layout, max-height delays to instantly reset after one resize); explicit callback through onDetailToggle + suppression of resizeObserver replaced passive observer, eliminating content overlap and flickering. ✅ TokenMonitor ccusage silent CLI access (fallback by scenario) — Found that ccusage was only marked as verified in planning documents but not implemented in code; added usage/ccusage.rs adaptation layer, week/month/year/5h(Claude) prioritized silent invocation of ccusage (CREATE_NO_WINDOW hides the console), fallback to old parser if failed; day view and Codex 5h still used old logic due to ccusage不支持; front-end added usage_source/usage_warning fields and prompt banner. ✅ Zero-config migration of BOSS Benchmark to openpi LIBERO environment — Copied BOSS data files and mapping to openpi LIBERO installation path; created boss_benchmark.py to register BOSS benchmarks to LIBERO global BENCHMARK_MAPPING through module injection; added two server-client architecture evaluation scripts: eval_oss_ch.py (environment assessment) and eval_skill_chain.py (skill chain assessment). ✅ Automation of TokenMonitor three-platform construction — Created 6 bash scripts under build/ directory (lightweight standard installation package + full version with embedded Node.js portable+ccusage offline package); extended release.yml to include macOS/windows/ubuntu three-platform matrix, built two variants per platform, unified publish job upload to GitHub Release; created three-platform uninstallation scripts. ✅ Large-scale code quality refactoring of TokenMonitor — Commands.rs (2466 lines→7 modules: mod/period/float_ball/tray/usage_query/calendar/config), rate_limits_rs (1202 lines→5 modules) split; enumerated TrayConfig fields (eliminated string comparisons); intelligent mtime cache expiration (replaced with unconditional clear); OnceLock cache CLI path; bootstrap.ts IPC parallelization; extracted month helper functions, UsagePayload Default implementation; all 199 Rust tests passed. ✅ Creation of code-summarize –for audience parameter + slurm-gpu skill — Added –for self/coworker/user/display parameters to /code-summarize (weight matrix+viewpoint instruction scheme, maintaining 6-segment structure unchanged, backward compatibility); created slurm-gpu skill (parsed sinfo/squeue/scontrol outputs, supported –free/-partition parameters, output GPU availability information by partition and node). Implementation and Fixes ✅ MIHD project technical overview document (OVERVIEW.md) — Used /summarize skill, analyzed 46 Python files with 3 parallel exploration agents, generated a paper-style technical report with 6 chapters (including experimental results ARI=0.546). ✅ Improvement of lerobot2rlds tool and environment compatibility fixes — Added –max-episodes parameter to support quick verification of the first N episodes (both beam/non-beam paths supported); fixed compatibility issues between lerobot 0.1.0 and new datasets library through monkey-patch torch.stack (Column object vs tensor list). ✅ DCC environment configuration (skill installation + GPU resource query) — Installed ccplan/summarize/optimize skills in ~/.claude/skills/; analyzed scavenger-gpu and gpu-common partitions using sinfo, identified optimal available nodes (majoroslab 2 RTX 6000 Ada). Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Tauri decorations:false on Windows 11 cannot completely eliminate window borders (there are two sources: CSS box-shadow and Windows DWM system details) Solution: Remove the box-shadow inset from app.css; call DwmSetWindowAttribute(hwnd, DWMWA_BORDER_COLOR=34, &DWMWA_COLOR_NONE=0xFFFFFFFE) in window.rs to eliminate the DWM system border.
Key Insight: Tauri window borders have two independent sources that need to be addressed simultaneously; DWMWA_COLOR_NONE value is 0xFFFFFFFE instead of 0.
2. Tauri v2 capability system defaults to rejecting all undeclared APIs: floating ball windows’ outerPosition()/scaleFactor() calls silently fail, making dragging completely ineffective; floating balls in multi-window applications are not declared in capabilities Solution: Add three missing permissions (allow-outer-position/allow-scale-factor/allow-current-monitor) to capabilities/default.json, and add float-ball to the windows array.Key Insights: The capabilities of Tauri v2 are on a whitelist, and any window API must be explicitly declared. Silent failures without error messages are the most difficult to troubleshoot.
3. When startDragging() is called in a setTimeout callback or async context, it fails due to Win32 restrictions, resulting in ineffective floating ball dragging. Solution: Use Pointer Capture for manual dragging: onPointerDown captures the starting point of the pointer, and onPointerMove, if the movement exceeds a threshold of 5px, it enters dragging mode. Move the window through move_float_ball_toIPC, and if the onPointerUp does not exceed this threshold, it is considered a click.
Key Insight: startDragging() must be called within synchronous pointer event handlers. Any asynchronous or delayed calls will cause Win32 to reject it. Pointer Capture allows for precise distinction between dragging and clicking.
4. When the CSS max-height animation transition ends during the CSS resizeObserver, the native flicker occurs due to continuous window repositioning. The overlay fix eliminates the flicker but introduces content occlusion. Solution: Use a layered CSS transition delay: opacity fades out after 1s (without triggering layout changes and without ResizeObserver); then instantly reset max-height to zero after 0.8-1s (exactly triggering one resize). Replace passive observers with explicit callbacks on onDetailToggle and the suppressResizeObserver flag.
Key Insight: Decoupling visual animations (opacity) from structural animations (max-height) is key. To fix visual bugs, it’s necessary to verify both the intended solution and side effects (the overlay fixes flicker but disrupts layout semantics).
5. ccusage is marked as status: verified in the planning document, leading users to believe it has been implemented in the code. However, the actual code still uses a hard-coded pricing table in pricing.rs. Solution: Confirm through code review that it has not been implemented and inform users clearly. Choose silent CLI access (instead of an ongoing MCP process) and define fallbacks based on scenarios: for ccusage, implement day views and Codex 5h granularity to backtrack to the old parser.
Key Insight: The status field in the planning document does not indicate that the code has been implemented. It is necessary to directly verify it by reading the source code. Third-party tools may not cover all granularities, so it’s important to determine the fallback strategy based on scenarios when integrating them.
6. AI misunderstands the issue of cross-sample queries: It generalizes comparisons at the query protocol level to analyze differences in training architectures (GIN vs GCN, loss functions, batch effects, etc.), which is seriously off track from what users actually care about. Solution: Users clearly correct the direction, and AI focuses on the query process. It identifies four differences in query protocols: query granularity (entire layer centroid vs single spot K-hop subgraph), candidate representation (spot emb vs niche emb), niche type definition (single-layer label vs cross-layer boundary types), and evaluation metrics (Spearman vs PCC+NCJS).
Key Insight: There are multiple levels of ‘method differences’ in the same paper (training architecture/推理协议/evaluation system). AI tends to start from the most macroscopic level. Researchers usually have a clear focus, and AI should confirm the analysis granularity before responding for the first time.
7. The Error Recovery Benchmark has two problems: There are ~60 lines of repetitive object detection logic in Drop skills; CLAUDE.md has long-term documentation drift after the classification system was重构 (29 subtypes/D2 degree historical errors not updated). Solution: Extract 6 shared helper methods from BaseErrorSkill and call them directly for each subclass to eliminate duplication. Verify the entire CLAUDE.md file and correct it to 26 subtypes/D0+D1 levels through code verification.
Key Insight: Abstract base classes should provide both shared tool methods and definitions. After code refactoring, document verification should be carried out immediately. The constants of the classification system should be based on authoritative sources (error_taxonomy_v5.py) rather than being manually maintained in the documentation.
8. There is a systematic tendency to ‘skip over’ in ccplan Phases 4-6: Qualitative descriptions (‘careful analysis’) cannot enforce analysis depth; Phase 0 does not consider scenarios with multiple intentions, and single-intention extraction fails under complex prompts. The system reminder tag returned by WebSearch can interrupt the workflow context. Solution: Increase the minimum discovery threshold in Phases 4-6 to max(3, item_count/2) and remove the skip all option. Add a multi-intention decomposition step in Phase 0 Step 1 (coupled/related/independent classification + parallel tracks). Add a section on preserving tool invocation states (externalizing states + inline reminders for three high-risk phases).
Key Insight: Quantitative constraints (at least N discoveries) are better than qualitative descriptions (‘careful analysis’). AI will find reasons to skip qualitative requirements. The ECL document serves as an externalized state anchor, which is more effective than relying on context memory in preventing tool calls interrupting the workflow.
9. In the initial version of guard-check.py, there are two critical security issues: Reading command fields from YAML and directly passing them to subprocess.run(shell=True) (shell injection); bare except Exception silences all exceptions, making the guard ineffective without a prompt. Solution: Confirm interactively before execution with a yes/no option (skip directly in non-interactive mode). Only catch the expected json.JSONDecodeError; write other exceptions to stderr for visibility.
Key Insight: If the security hook fails silently, it’s equivalent to having no hook at all. Even if YAML is a trusted source, there must be a confirmation gate when executing in bulk. Failure paths must leave observable traces.
10. When ccplan Phase 4-6 resize, due to the default behavior of Win32 SetWindowPos, old client content is copied back in the upper left corner, causing visual position drift. Solution: Add the SWP_NOCOPYBITS flag to the SetWindowPos call to prevent the copy of old content. Also introduce detect_vertical_anchor to distinguish between top and bottom anchors based on a threshold of 5px.
Key Insight: The Win32 API (InvalidateRect/RedrawWindow, etc.) may not have a one-to-one binding in the Windows crate. It’s necessary to use an equivalent but more conservative path (SWP_NOCOPYBITS).
General Issues 11. HPC environment engineering challenges: gpu-common partition is fully loaded, git-lfs is missing, causing failure to clone large files; UCE model file download from Figshare fails (0 bytes); scGPT’s official API cannot be used due to pyarrow compatibility issues; Geneformer V2 CUDA OOM occurs on a 48GB single card. Solution: Filter out available nodes for scavenger-gpu (majoroslab RTX 6000 Ada); fix git-lfs to allow cloning large files with conda install; UCE blocking requires proxy or scp pre-transmission; directly implement the underlying reasoning logic to bypass package compatibility issues; reduce the batch size of Geneformer to 10 to solve OOM issues.
Key Insight: HPC computing node system tools are often missing, and it’s necessary to supplement them through conda user-level installation. The availability of model checkpoints should be verified as a risk point during planning. Priority should be given to bypassing package compatibility issues rather than forcing downgrades.
12. The Pi0.5 evaluation script failed in three rounds of debugging: ① It used Python from openpi .venv, but the openpi package was linked to an incorrect user copy; ② PYTHONPATH lacked the full project directory prefix; ③ MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID specified GPU was not in CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. Solution: Use the openpi05 conda environment; correct PYTHONPATH to absolute paths; modify the eval process to include both server GPU and EGL GPU in CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES.
Key Insight: On a shared file system, packages with the same name for multiple users must specify a conda environment explicitly. The GPU used for MuJoCo EGL rendering must be a subset of devices visible to CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. This is a rigid requirement of Robosuite.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Technical Solution Constraints (Product Position vs Technical Implementation) Role Thought Humans BOSS Migration: Proposed core constraint of ‘directly deploying BOSS in existing libero as an extension’ (no new environment installation); Floating Ball: Clearly define the product position (secondary entrance/alternative solution, main entrance remains the tray icon); Feature Guard: Indicated that it should be built into skills to ensure portability rather than being written into the project CLAUDE.md AI AI is responsible for finding technical implementation paths that meet constraints (module injection vs fork code, built-in skills vs project configuration), and discovering existing implementations in the code library (full implementation in taskbar.rs line 493). Difference Analysis: Humans provide core constraints and product intuition, while AI provides technical feasibility analysis and implementation paths. The most important architectural decisions come from humans, and AI’s value lies in code discovery and implementation details.
Research Problem Granularity Role Thought Humans Users focus precisely on the comparison at the ‘query setting/protocol’ level. After AI provides a macroscopic architecture comparison, they correct the direction immediately. In experimental design, they actively add D1 evaluation to create D0 vs D1 comparative experiments AI AI defaults to analyzing from the most macroscopic level (training architecture, loss function, etc.). After completing D0 evaluation, it does not actively suggest D1 comparison but focuses on completing the current task Difference Analysis: Domain knowledge allows researchers to quickly recognize that ‘architectural differences are already known, and query protocol is the current focus’. AI tends to analyze from a complete macroscopic level. The initiative in experimental design comes entirely from humans.
Skill Iteration Quality Drivers Role Thought Humans Based on actual usage experience, it was found that ccplan Phases 3-7 ‘progress quickly’. Questions about the triggering mechanism and requests for deeper analysis were made. It was pointed out that Phase 0 did not consider scenarios with multiple intentions until explicitly indicated by users. It was proposed to filter external tool reference projects using star ratings AI AI designed Phases 0-10 but did not actively evaluate whether the analysis depth at each stage was sufficient. When questioned, it discovered the systematic flaw of ‘skipping over’. It tends to list all related projects without assigning weights Difference Analysis: Humans’ actual usage feedback triggered quality improvements. AI tends to focus on happy paths while ignoring the guarantee of depth in boundary cases. Using star ratings for filtering is a pragmatic engineering judgment.
TokenMonitor UI Design Intent传达 Role Thought Humans Directly upload screenshots (capsules/moon-shaped reference images) instead of text descriptions. Clearly define ‘floating ball like 360 Security Guard’ as a product reference. Corrected AI’s misunderstanding of ’too fast animation’ (transition duration vs trigger delay, two different parameters). Indicated that the overlay fix would occlude content AI AI extracts design intent from screenshots and converts it into technical specifications. Mistook ’too fast’ for requiring a longer debounce delay (1800ms). Recommended the overlay solution as the most direct way to eliminate flicker but did not evaluate occlusion scenarios Difference Analysis: Humans use images and analogies to convey complex UI intentions more efficiently. AI tends to choose incorrect technical parameters under vague UX descriptions. Only actual users can discover side effects like content occlusion caused by overlays.
Requirement Completeness: Explicit Expression of Constraints Role Thought Humans Floating ball: Add ‘Do not touch the border when expanding, both the entire expansion box and the ball must be within the screen’. Main window: Require ‘Keep other parts stationary until the disappearing part completely disappears before making it smaller overall’. Configuration: Indicate that the behavior of the floating ball should be fixed and not controlled by Settings barDisplay AI AI did not consider the rationality of the overall position after expansion when implementing the horizontal layout of the floating ball. Choosed ’no flicker’ as the only goal for the overlay solution. Retained unnecessary barDisplay configuration dependencies Difference Analysis: Complex UX repairs involve multiple implicit constraints. Humans have a complete understanding of requirements (semantic correctness + visual correctness + no side effects), while AI only implemented some of them. It’s necessary to actively ask about complete constraints rather than assuming single-target optimization.
AI Limitations Important Limitations Lack of depth guarantee in skill design: There is a systematic tendency to ‘skip over’ in the designs of Phases 4-6. This was discovered and fixed only after users reported that ‘progress too quickly’. Phase 0 did not actively consider scenarios with multiple intentions until explicitly indicated by users. Insufficient security code generation: When writing guard-check.py, the risk of shell injection was not considered. It took a professional code review sub-agent to discover this issue. This indicates that AI lacks security awareness when generating code involving external commands. Lack of proactive comparison between planning documents and actual code: It was not actively reported that ccusage is only marked as verified in the planning document but has not been implemented in the code. This was revealed only after users asked questions. This indicates a lack of initiative in comparing planning status with actual code. Lack of product intuition: The floating ball product positioning requires more than three rounds of clarification to determine whether it is ‘a secondary entrance rather than the main channel’. The floating ball clarification options did not cover the behavior that users actually want, such as ‘吸附缩入边框’, forcing users to manually add None of the above. Default analysis granularity for tools invocation: The system reminder tag returned by WebSearch can interrupt the ccplan workflow. AI only discovered and fixed this issue after users reported it. This indicates a lack of pretection against context amnesia risk when handling tool invocation scenarios. Incomplete evaluation of UI side effects: When recommending an overlay fix to eliminate flicker, it did not actively evaluate scenarios where content would be occluded. When implementing the horizontal layout of the floating ball, it did not anticipate the need to leave the screen edge after expansion. This was only discovered after users provided feedback after actual use. General Limitations Omissions in HPC planning: The network accessibility of HPC nodes to Figshare was not pre-verified, resulting in UCE being blocked. Verifying the availability of model checkpoints should be a necessary item during planning. Bias in judgment regarding research scenarios: It is recommended to use smaller models (UCE 4-layer, Geneformer V1) to quickly run through the process. However, researchers require ensuring academic comparability according to paper/HuggingFace default settings. AI prioritizes engineering feasibility over experimental reproducibility standards. Incomplete understanding of Tauri/Win32 API details: decorations:false cannot completely eliminate Windows borders (it requires calling DwmSetWindowAttribute separately). The way Win32 return values is different in different versions of the windows crate (comparing SetWindowRgn return values was incorrect). It took multiple failed API import attempts to find an available path.
