summary: The process is carried out concurrently across three devices: DCC HPC, Tianhe Supercomputer, and TzJsDesktop. On DCC, the MIHD-QueST cross-sample query protocol has been finalized, along with the construction of a benchmark framework for 8 gene encoders. At Tianhe, the full task rollout evaluation of Pi0.5 LoRA has been completed, along with the migration to the BOSS environment and a systematic reconstruction of the Error Recovery Benchmark. TzJsDesktop has undergone multiple system upgrades to improve ccplan skills (Prompt Calibration, multi-intention decomposition, Feature Guard), and TokenMonitor has undergone significant feature improvements (full lifecycle of floating balls, Windows native UX, code refactoring, integration with ccusage).
title: 错误日志 2026-03-26

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-25 Today’s Overview Done: Multi-device collaboration across six directions: Tianhe server focuses on the entire pipeline of VLA robot research (Git migration to BOSS project → training scripts → LeRobot data pipeline fixes →改造 OpenPI evaluation client). TzJsDesktop completed the refactoring of research_scout.py’s 7 modules, TokenMonitor Phase E cross-platform adaptation and Windows .exe building, as well as fixing 3 Windows bugs. Optimize skill extended to four languages. How it was done: Structured process for verifying ccplan skill-driven assumptions → generating ideas → adversarial review by Critic → persistent ECL documentation ensures continuity across sessions. Parallel Explore agents accelerate code analysis in six directions; #[cfg] conditional compilation handles platform differences; NSIS toolchain packages Windows installer; three parallel agents’ code reviews cover reuse, quality, and efficiency. What it means: Complete the training-evaluation pipeline for BOSS projects. Research_scout.py is broken down into maintainable sub-packages from 2934 lines of heavy code. TokenMonitor generates the first distributable Windows .exe (NSIS 3.2MB). All three tools improve cross-platform or multilingual capabilities. ECL documentation fully records architectural decisions to ensure continuity across sessions. TzJsDesktop Done: Completed the refactoring of research_scout.py’s 7 modules, TokenMonitor Phase E cross-platform migration (Rust backend completed, front-end glass cleanup pending), Windows .exe building, fixing 3 Windows runtime bugs, cleaning up about 1300 dead code lines, extending optimize skill to four languages, creating ccusage dependency installation tool, and reorganizing the global skill library. How it was done: ccplan conducted structured planning, Critic agents performed adversarial reviews, three parallel agents’ code reviews were carried out, Rust+MSVC Build Tools was installed to build Windows targets using cmd.exe, NSIS makensis compiled the installer package, and shell scripts automated skill directory operations. What it means: All compilations passed with zero warnings (Rust 191 tests + 175 front-end tests all passed). The generated TokenMonitor_0.5.0_x64-setup.exe and TokenMonitor-PrereqSetup.exe (72KB) are distributable. ECL documentation ensures continuity across sessions. Tianhe Done: Generated OVERVIEW.md for error_recovery_benchmark, assisted Chenjunye in migrating BOSS project Git, comparing training code and creating training scripts for openvla-oft, fixing LeRobot data pipelines, and transforming OpenPI evaluation clients. Discussed cross-domain migration from CrossFormer to battery foundation models with Chenlu, and helped Mozihao locate RoboTwin rendering resolution. How it was done: Parallel Explore agents deeply analyzed the project architecture, compared openvla/openvla-oft finetune.py functions line by line, fixed LeRobot→RLDS data conversion script field filtering logic, and transformed evaluation code according to OpenPI client examples. What it means: Complete technical documentation for error_recovery_benchmark. The BOSS project’s Git workflow can be reproduced. The complete training-evaluation pipeline for openvla-oft is established, and data conversions from LeRobot to RLDS are fixed. Tianhe server users completed the entire VLA/robot research stack (Git migration, training scripts, data pipeline fixes, OpenPI evaluation clients). TzJsDesktop completed the modular refactoring of research_scout.py, TokenMonitor cross-platform adaptation and the first Windows .exe building, optimize skill extended to four languages, and detailed configuration of Claude Code toolchain.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy 🔄 TokenMonitor Phase E Cross-platform Migration and Windows Building — Completely removed 4 objc2 crates and macos-private-api features from Cargo.toml, deleted about 250 lines of glass/transparent Rust code. Updated CI to build on three platforms (macOS+Windows+Ubuntu), installed Rust+MSVC Build Tools to build Windows .exe (NSIS 3.2MB, MSI 4.6MB, bare exe 14MB). Rust backend completed; front-end glass cleanup (E-3+E-9) is pending. ✅ TokenMonitor Code Cleaning and Windows Runtime Bug Fixes — Three parallel agents reviewed and fixed: removed 4 dead MCP modules (~1300 lines), removed mod declarations, restored #[cfg] for set_dock_icon_visible function backtracking, cleaned Phase A comments; fixed 3 Windows bugs (TrayBottomCenter window alignment, CREATE_NO_WINDOW to prevent terminal pop-up, progress bar scaled 1.5x in Windows); changed bootstrap.ts serial IPC to Promise.allSettled(); changed sort+reverse to sort_unstable_by. ✅ Research_Scout.py Modular Refactoring — Broken down 2934-line single-file heavy code into scout sub-packages (config/prompts/project/search/evaluate/report/cli, totaling 7 modules). Reduced research_scout.py to about 80 lines of thin shim to ensure zero changes to mcp_server.py. Added SSRF protection and externalized configuration values, validated all six items. ✅ Openvla-oft Training Code Comparison and Script Creation — Deeply compared openvla vs openvla-oft finetune.py: action representation (discrete tokens vs L1 regression/Diffusion), architectural differences (FiLM, Proprio, Action Chunking), training configuration differences (LR scheduling, breakpoint resumption), data interfaces (OFT additional support for wrist cameras and proprioception). Created run_openvla_oft.sh (torchrun, L1 regression, dual-input, proprioception, 150K steps). ✅ BOSS OpenPI Evaluation Client Transformation — Converted eval_skills_unaffected_by_oss.py into a WebsocketClientPolicy client, retaining BOSS benchmark loading and init_states reproduction logic. Adapted to openpi image preprocessing (180° flip+resize+pad), state vector assembly (ee_pos+axisangle+gripper), and action chunking (recquired every replan_steps steps). ✅ Optimize Skill Four-Language Extension (Hub+Spoke) — Extended Python-only optimize skill to support Python/Swift/Rust/TypeScript. Hub+spoke architecture (SKILL.md 133 lines + 4 languages, each with 56-57 lines, totaling 359 lines). ≥7 checks per language and dimension, combined with single-language focused instructions to prevent prompt dilution. ✅ lerobot2rlds.py Data Interface Fixes — Fixed missing joint_state fields in observation.state filter. Changed matching to startswith+exclusion combinations, added dones field skipping logic, fixed v[’names’] KeyError, ensuring LeRobot→RLDS correctly includes state/joint_state/double-image. ✅ Error_Recovery_Benchmark OVERVIEW.md Generation — Analyzed core modules of the framework using six parallel Explore agents (core.py/recovery_types/error_skills/Phoenix), generating about 450 lines of technical overview documents with actual output statistics (pick_place 4995 scenarios, code library 9011 lines, data 211MB). ✅ CrossFormer to Battery Foundation Model Cross-Domain Migration Analysis — Identified motivations for battery modeling from CrossFormer’s segment-based modeling/double-attention/router routing/multi-scale prediction. Proposed four mapping directions: charge-discharge cycles=segmented units, multiple sensors=dimensional attention, large-scale battery packs=router requirements, fine-to-medium-coarse three-level granularity=multi-scale prediction, and cycle-aware attention. Discussion remains at the conceptual level without code output. ✅ Claude Code Skill Library Reorganization and Toolchain Configuration — Installed 31 awesome-claude-skills, moved 11 coding/documentation/research-related skills to gadget project levels, removed 36 irrelevant ECC skills (logistics/energy/investment/social), rewritten tutorial.md; updated summarize tutorial to python -m summarize format and added weekly report section. Implementation and Fixes ✅ ccusage Prerequisite Dependency Installation Tool Creation — Compiled TokenMonitor-PrereqSetup.exe (72KB) using NSIS, automatically installed Node.js and @ccusage/mcp; created macOS/Linux shell script versions simultaneously. ✅ BOSS Project Git Migration and Repository Organization — Moved BOSS project from YY-GX/BOSS to Junye-Chen/boss, configured project-level git user, resolved global proxy unreachable issues (switching to localhost:9977), removed openvla-oft/.git nested repository into a regular directory, successfully pushed 84 files. Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. Risk of broken import when mcp_server.py directly imports 15 functions after research_scout.py refactoring (CRITICAL level) Solution: Reduced research_scout.py to about 80 lines of thin shim, ensuring zero changes to mcp_server.py through re-export. No modifications were needed in the calling party.
Key Insight: The risk of MCP import breakage discovered by Critic agent is the highest-risk point in modular refactoring; shim re-export is the only non-invasive migration path.
2. Code review found ~1300 lines of dead MCP modules declared in lib.rs but never called externally, wasting compilation time and causing confusion Solution: Removed 4 mod declarations and related Phase A comments from lib.rs. The files were retained for future integration.
Key Insight: Three parallel agents’ code reviews detected “self-consistent but isolated” module groups—internal dependencies without being referenced by any external modules. Single-linear reviews could not detect this.
3. macOS-specific APIs (Keychain, /bin/zsh path, set_dock_icon_visible) cause compilation issues on Windows/Linux during cross-platform migration Solution: Used #[cfg(target_os)] conditional compilation to separate platform code; macOS Keychain returned Err instead of panic on other platforms for graceful degradation; set_dock_icon_visible was restored using cfg on macOS, kept noop for non-macOS.
Key Insight: “Deleting macOS code” and “changing macOS code to noop” are two different operations that must be decided function by function. macOS-only APIs can return Err gracefully on other platforms without completely removing features.
4. Three runtime issues with Windows tray applications: windows not aligned with taskbar, CLI pop-up window, progress bar too short Solution: Used TrayBottomCenter instead of TrayCenter; added .creation_flags(0x0800_0000) to all Command calls; defined 1.5x progress bar width for Windows/Linux using #[cfg].
Key Insight: tauri_plugin_positioner already has TrayBottomCenter built-in; tokio::process::Command exposes creation_flags() natively in Windows, no additional trait import needed (different from std::process::Command).
5. In lerobot2rlds.py, ‘observation.state’ missed joint_state fields, causing RLDS data to lack joint states Solution: Changed it to startswith(‘observation.’)+exclusion of image/dones exact combinations, while handling v[’names’] KeyError.
Key Insight: String containment matches can lead to unexpected results (‘observation.state’ is a substring of ‘observation.joint_state’); using startswith+exclusion combination is more precise.
6. Optimize skill single-file solution estimated 500-700 lines. Critic found that multi-language prompts dilute Claude’s attention when handling single language Solution: Adopted hub+spoke architecture: placed common frameworks (133 lines) in the hub, with each of the 4 languages having ≤80 lines in the spoke, combined with single-language focused instructions.
Key Insight: In multi-language prompt design, physical file isolation (speech files) is more effective than section separation to prevent prompt dilution.
7. resizeDebug tool was considered simple to archive, but actually contained 8 files called 100+ times in depth Solution: Adjusted the plan after a full grep scan: moved to lib/debug/ directory instead of archiving completely, avoiding large-scale call cleanup.
Key Insight: Grep must be used to evaluate actual call depth before archiving. Fewer files does not mean less impact; ccplan Review Gate requires this evaluation step.
8. Global skill library has over 50 irrelevant skills that load about 15000-20000 tokens per session Solution: Divided by project granularity: moved Python/research/coding-related skills to gadget project levels, removed 36 irrelevant skills.
Key Insight: Claude Code loads all skill indexes per session, should be managed according to proximity principles. Only truly general skills should remain in the global library.
General Issues 9. Loading LeRobot Parquet files with datasets 3.6.0 reported ValueError: Feature type ‘List’ not found Solution: The new version of datasets removed List type and replaced it with Sequence; guided use of fix_parquet_metadata.py to batch replace metadata using positional parameters.
Key Insight: LeRobot dataset is tightly coupled with the datasets library version. Cross-version upgrades require checking Parquet metadata feature type definitions.
10. In Git Bash environment, /usr/bin/link overwrites MSVC link.exe, causing Rust MSVC target linking failures Solution: Install Visual Studio 2022 Build Tools, activate MSVC environment via cmd.exe before running cargo build.
Key Insight: Git Bash path priority can override Windows native linker. Rust MSVC targets must bypass Git Bash environment using cmd.exe.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level#### Complementarity of Runtime Bug Awareness (Users) vs Static Code Analysis (AI) Role Approach Humans Users perceive 3 specific bugs during real Windows testing: incorrect window position, terminal pop-up, short progress bars. The descriptions are precise at the phenomenon level. AI AI identifies issues such as dead modules, feature regressions, and redundant IPC calls during code review on /simplify. This is a static analysis rather than runtime awareness. Difference Analysis: The two perspectives complement each other. Users provide runtime bug reports, while AI helps identify code structure quality issues. In cross-platform scenarios, the user’s real-world experience cannot be covered by AI’s static analysis.
Productization Thinking: Users’ Perspective Beyond AI’s “Function Completion” Role Approach Humans Users directly propose creating separate .exe/.dmg installation packages to handle dependencies automatically, without requiring users to manually execute commands. AI AI initially focuses on listing commands that users need to perform manually, transferring the installation responsibility to users. Difference Analysis: Users consider productization experience for packaging and distribution earlier than AI. AI only focuses on functional implementation while neglecting distribution convenience.
AI Proactively Identifies Technical Risks (Prompt Dilution, MCP Breakage, Archiving Dangers) Role Approach Humans Users prefer single-file solutions. They intuitively think that resizeDebug can be simply archived without evaluating technical boundaries beforehand. AI AI identifies the risk of prompt dilution through the Critic agent. By using grep to scan, it discovers over 100 calls to resizeDebug. It also identifies MCP import breakage as a critical issue. Difference Analysis: The adversarial review mechanism allows AI to proactively identify destructive risks before execution, preventing unexpected regressions caused by human intuition. In this case, AI identified technical risks earlier than users.
Cross-domain Research Ideas Come from Users, with AI Responsible for Systematization Role Approach Humans Users actively provide the core ideas of the CrossFormer paper and propose ideas to transfer it to the battery modeling field. This gives a clear sense of research direction. AI AI systematically expands 4 mapping directions based on user frameworks, proposing specific innovations such as cycle-aware attention. Difference Analysis: Cross-domain ideas come from users’ domain knowledge and intuition. AI cannot generate cross-domain research ideas independently but can only structure and specify them within given frameworks.
AI Provides Incorrect Project Status Assessments Based on Outdated Documentation Role Approach Humans Users directly ask about Windows/Linux adaptation solutions, predicting that there are feasible paths. AI AI claims without first reviewing the ECL documentation that TokenMonitor is macOS-only and cannot be run directly. However, actual cross-platform work has already been partially completed (phase-9-A+E). Difference Analysis: Users’ intuitive judgment of project status is more accurate than AI’s improvised retrieval. AI should read the latest ECL/planning documents before providing project status assessments.
AI Limitations Major Limitations Providing outdated project status assessments without reviewing existing planning documents (ECL/CLAUDE.md): Claiming TokenMonitor is macOS-only ignores the cross-platform progress recorded in ECL (phase-9-implementation-A+E), which requires users to correct. Cross-platform code changes cannot be locally run on the target platform (Windows) using cargo build/check for real-world compilation verification. It relies on user confirmation offline, increasing the friction of cross-platform migration verification. During Phase E implementation, no active tracking was done regarding the completion status of front-end glass cleanup (E-3+E-9). This task remains pending after multiple sessions, and users need to ask questions to realize the omission. General Limitations Inaccurate understanding of API differences between tokio::process::Command and std::process::Command: First, unnecessary CommandExt trait imports are added. Only after the compiler reports an unused import is the issue corrected. The default commit behavior does not align with user preferences (automatically adding Co-Authored-By lines; incorrect reference to old SSH keys). This needs to be corrected after users notice it. Today’s Takeaways Key Takeaways ECL (Evolving Constraint Language) documentation can preserve the complete architecture decision context across sessions (assumption verification history, reasons for scheme selection, Critic feedback, dependency DAG), allowing subsequent agents to continue without re-analysis. This is a key persistence mechanism for large multi-session refactoring. The Critic agent and parallel multi-angle Agent review (reuse/quality/efficiency) are effective mechanisms to prevent destructive changes. They can systematically identify key dependencies missed by humans (MCP import breakage, prompt dilution, 100+ calls to resizeDebug), and adversarial reviews should be enforced at every architecture decision. The difference between OpenVLA-OFT and OpenVLA: OFT uses independent Action Head (L1/Diffusion) to output continuous actions rather than discrete tokens. It also supports FiLM language conditions, ontology awareness, Action Chunking, and multi-image inputs. Both have RLDS data formats, but OFT’s RLDSBatchTransform additionally processes wrist camera and ontology fields. The Hub+spoke architecture is suitable for designing multi-language prompt skills: The hub maintains a common framework and philosophy, while the spoke focuses on language-specific checks (≤80 lines). This helps prevent attention dilution when Claude handles single languages. Physical file isolation is more effective than section separation. When migrating across platforms, “deleting macOS code” and “changing macOS code to noop” are two different operations that must be decided function by function. The macOS-only API can gracefully downgrade in other platforms without completely removing features. The core ideas of CrossFormer (time segmentation, time×dimension dual attention, Router routing, multi-scale prediction) can be mapped to battery foundation models: Charge-discharge cycles = segmented units, multiple sensors = dimension attention, large-scale battery packs = routing requirements, fine-to-medium-thick three-gradation = multi-scale prediction. Rust’s cross-platform conditional compilation #[cfg(target_os] is a clean solution for handling platform-specific APIs. tokio::process::Command has native support for creation_flags() in Windows, eliminating the need for additional trait imports (different from std::process::Command). The hierarchical management rules of Claude Code skills: ~/.claude/skills/ is loaded globally, and project-level .claude/skills/ is only loaded for that project. The more skill index entries there are, the more context tokens are consumed. They should be managed according to proximity principles. Unrelated skills should be moved to project-level or removed. The ccplan Review Gate is valuable in tasks like file reorganization: Before making archiving/deletion decisions, one must use grep to evaluate the actual call depth. “Small number of files” does not equal “small impact range”. This avoids incorrectly archiving frequently called tools. Practical Takeaways RLDS data format uses is_first/is_last/is_terminal to represent episode boundaries. It should not contain explicit dones fields. When converting from LeRobot to RLDS, the dones fields need to be skipped, and the episode boundaries should be used to derive them automatically. Session Summary TokenMonitor 🔄 Phase E cross-platform migration, Windows .exe build, code cleanup, and Windows bug fixes 00:48:26.474 | claude_code Four main sessions with TokenMonitor: (1) In Phase E, 4 objc2 crates and ~250 lines of glass code were removed. The CI changed to a three-platform matrix. MSVC Build Tools was installed, and Windows .exe (NSIS 3.2MB) was successfully built through cmd.exe. (2) Users actively proposed packaging dependency installation tools and created an NSIS-based TokenMonitor-PrereqSetup.exe of 72KB. (3) /simplify’s three parallel agents reviewed and fixed ~1300 lines of dead MCP modules, set_dock_icon_visible regression issues, etc. All tests were passed with 191+175 successes. (4) Three Windows runtime bugs (TrayBottomCenter/CREATE_NO_WINDOW/progress bar 1.5x) were fixed, /optimize’s concurrent IPC optimization was done, and /ccplan planned file reorganization was created (ECL documentation has been prepared, implementation is pending). Front-end glass cleanup (E-3+E-9) is still incomplete.
gadget Research ✅ research_scout.py: 2934 lines of massive modular refactoring into scout sub-packages (7 modules) 00:55:52.333 | claude_code Through /ccplan, assumption verification and scheme divergence were completed. The scout/ sub-package solution was chosen based on responsibilities. The Critic agent found a CRITICAL risk in mcp_server.py’s direct import of 15 functions, and a shim re-export was designed to mitigate this issue. Seven modules (config/prompts/project/search/evaluate/report/cli) were created in parallel. All six verifications (imports, backward compatibility, SSRF protection, etc.) were passed. The ECL documentation recorded the complete decision chain.
chenlu Research Discussion 🔍 Analysis of Motivation for Transferring CrossFormer Core Ideas to Battery Foundation Model 06:52:35.576 | claude_code User chenlu introduced the core ideas of the CrossFormer paper (time segmentation, time×dimension dual attention, Router routing, multi-scale prediction). AI systematically proposed 4 battery modeling mapping directions, including potential innovations like cycle-aware attention and cross-battery routing. The discussion remained at the conceptual level without producing code.
BOSS (chenjunye) ✅ Git migration, training code analysis and script creation, data pipeline debugging, OpenPI evaluation client modification 06:09:02.365 | claude_code Four sessions related to BOSS: (1) Git migration to Junye-Chen/boss solved the proxy unreachable problem. The openvla-oft nested .git was cleaned up, and 84 files were pushed. (2) A detailed comparison of openvla vs openvla-oft finetune.py was done, and a run_openvla_oft.sh training script was created for torchrun, L1 regression, dual images, and ontology awareness. (3) Debugging issues with uv PATH and datasets 3.6.0 List compatibility was done. Fix_parquet_metadata.py was used to guide the process. (4) The evaluation code was modified into a WebsocketClientPolicy client, retaining the BOSS benchmark logic while adapting to openpi image preprocessing and action chunking.
error-recovery-benchmark ✅ Generating a paper-style technical overview OVERVIEW.md for robot error recovery benchmarks 01:02:00.964 | claude_code Using the /summarize skill, the framework core modules were deeply analyzed through 6-way parallel Explore agents (ErrorSpec/EnvWrapper/13 ErrorSkills/5 RBG recovery strategies/Context Replay/Phoenix). A total of about 450 lines of six-paragraph OVERVIEW.md was generated, including actual output statistics (pick_place with 4995 scenarios, code library of 9011 lines, data size of 211MB).
gadget Skills & Toolchain Configuration ✅ Optimize skill expansion for four languages, global skill library reorganization, update of summarize tutorial 01:44:07.472 | claude_code Three configuration-related sessions: (1) The optimize skill was expanded into a hub+spoke four-language architecture through /ccplan (359 lines). The Critic agent adjusted the plan after finding a risk of prompt dilution in single files. (2) 31 awesome-claude-skills were installed, with 11 moved to the gadget project-level and 36 irrelevant ECC skills removed. tutorial.md was rewritten. (3) The summarize tutorial.md was updated to python -m summarize format, with a weekly report section added. Tianjin servers synchronously installed ccplan/summarize/optimize custom skills.
RoboTwin (mozihao) ✅ Searching for the location where RoboTwin’s rendering resolution is defined 09:14:39.642 | claude_code AI search returned: The main configuration task_config/_camera_config.yml defines 4 types of cameras (D435:320×240, Large_D435:640×480, etc.). Camera resolutions for Observer and World cameras are hard-coded in envs/camera/camera.py.
sysu_gbli2xy_1 (codex) ✅ Automatically generating AGENTS.md contributor guidelines for the repository 07:11:13.564 | codex Codex generated AGENTS.md according to the standard template, covering project structure, build/test commands, coding standards, and Commit/PR specifications. The document is concise, ranging from 200-400 words.

title: 错误日志 2026-03-25

content:
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking### Strategic Level Tool/Skill Design Decisions (Single Responsibility vs Thesis-style Narrative Framework) Role Approach Human Humans clearly state that summarize and optimize should be separated (single responsibility). They also propose describing code in the format of academic papers—highlightings to Future Work. This is a cross-disciplinary analogy, aligning software engineering documentation with scientific paper structures. Why and impact take precedence over what. AI AI’s current skills involve flat four-dimensional technical summaries, focusing on descriptive facts about code (what/how). They lack narrative motivation and an evolutionary perspective. AI does not actively judge whether tools should be separated; instead, they tend to describe possibilities without making choices. Difference Analysis: Humans have a clearer understanding of single responsibility in tool design and can innovate with narrative frameworks. AI provides implementation details (chapter adjustments, downgrading strategies, scale adaptation). Humans provide the framework—architectural intuition guides tool design.
Workflow Problem Diagnosis (ccplan Multi-Phase Interruption) Role Approach Human Based on user experience: “Many processes end before they are completed.” This is directly classified as a workflow problem. AI After analyzing SKILL.md, three structural root causes were identified: lack of transition instructions at phase boundaries, absence of global constraints, and incomplete multi-turn protocols. Difference Analysis: Humans provide symptoms at the user experience level, while AI provides root cause analysis at the system structure level. The two complement each other to form a complete diagnostic chain.
Architecture Choice (Reconstruction Scheme vs ccusage Integration Architecture) Role Approach Human Approving scheme A (minimum splitting + packaging) and demanding a one-time solution to three problems (more aggressive than AI’s default phased approach). In the ccusage architecture, the MCP server scheme was ultimately chosen. AI During reconstruction, scheme A was recommended consistent with human judgment but with a default phased approach. In the ccusage scenario, sub-process calls were initially recommended. After studying the Explore Agent, it was found that the MCP server is better and the recommendation was updated. Difference Analysis: Humans’ requirement for “one-time solution” is more aggressive than AI’s default phased suggestion. AI needs to actively explore the understanding of ecological tools to complete them. After two iterations, a better scheme was achieved.
BOSS Evaluation Framework OSS Design Understanding Role Approach Human Directly asking AI to compare the differences between two files without preset expectations. AI Identifying core design concepts: The affected version maps modified tasks back to the original training model through mapping.json, achieving “using the original model to evaluate robustness in a modified environment” (OSS testing paradigm). Difference Analysis: AI can extract high-level design intentions from code differences, while human open-ended questions guide AI output that is valuable for research.
Feature Selection and Dead Code Awareness Role Approach Human Quickly deciding to remove the rate limit feature (significantly simplifying the architecture). After asking about change_stats/subagent_stats, it was decided to keep them and complete integration—indicating a lack of understanding of existing dead code features in their own project. AI Presenting three options during planning: keeping, removing, or using ccusage blocks. It tends to prefer keeping some rate limit view. Modules like change_stats have clear functions but were not actively explained during planning. Difference Analysis: Human pragmatism and simplification (removing non-core features to reduce complexity) vs AI’s preference for preserving features. AI needs to actively explain the value of existing features during planning, rather than defaulting to users understanding their code base.
AI Limitations Major Limitations Unable to verify its own modifications: ccplan workflow fixes can only statically confirm structural existence; it cannot verify actual effects by running multi-phase tasks in the same session. In Windows Git Bash, cargo is not in the PATH. All Rust code modifications in Phase A and Phase E (four new modules + multiple file modifications) have not been compiled to verify potential type errors or API incompatibilities. Key constraints that require specialized Agent discovery: During the initial planning of cross-platform projects, the fixed square 16-32px size constraints for Windows/Linux trays were not actively considered. This was discovered by the Feasibility Agent. Understanding the ccusage ecosystem requires specialized exploration agents to complete (discovering MCP server issues), resulting in iterative updates during option evaluation. General Limitations Code generation has risks of redundancy and incompleteness: Duplication of the parse_date() function occurs when extracting daily.py and cli.py modules. The weekly summary import in mcp_server.py may be incomplete (_resolve_output_dir source across modules), requiring additional testing to confirm the correct import chain. API connections rely entirely on external network infrastructure. When ConnectionRefused/FailedToOpenSocket occurs, work cannot be downgraded, affecting user efficiency on the entire device for about 6 hours. Today’s Takeaways Key Takeaways Migration to Smoke Testing Priority Mode: Before refactoring large files, write all external import contracts as tests (47). After refactoring, verify backward compatibility immediately, exposing problems during development rather than production. AI Workflow CONTINUOUS EXECUTION MANDATE Design Pattern: The phase boundaries of multi-phase tools must be clear → NEXT: mandatory transition (not suggestive text), and each phase requires an independent multi-turn protocol. Otherwise, AI will “politely stop” at the phase boundary. Counteractive Critic/Review High ROI in Planning Phase: Critic identified 12 problems (2 critical). Feasibility Agent discovered Windows tray size constraints, both of which were found before implementation, saving a lot of rework costs. Parallel Critic + Red Team + Feasibility multi-Agent mode can systematically identify constraints missed by single-thinking. Cross-platform tray display requires a mixed strategy with platform awareness: macOS menu bars are scalable (set_title effective), while Windows/Linux trays have fixed square icons (16-32px). The most natural UX mode should be adopted on each platform (set_title vs tooltip vs short numbers within icons) rather than forced visual uniformity. Thesis-style code documentation structure (six chapters: Highlights/Introduction/Architecture/Implementation Results/Conclusion & Future Work) is more effective at conveying the why and impact of code. It has the greatest developer value when reading one’s own project months later. ccusage MCP server is better than CLI sub-process calls: Resident processes have no cold startup delay (vs 1-5 seconds),已实现多provider路由, standard JSON-RPC protocol is easy to integrate. Existing solutions within the ecosystem should be prioritized for research rather than implemented independently. ECL YAML documentation is an effective mechanism for solving context rot in multi-session complex projects: It persists verified requirements, architectural decisions, counteractive review results, and current status. Any Agent can continue working from this point. In BOSS, the affected evaluation script’s OSS design: Mapping modified tasks back to the original training model achieves robustness assessment under offset observation space. Differences in video recording timing (before vs after step) reflect different focuses on “original observations” in OSS research. Practical Takeaways ccplan Phase 0 code library scanning is a high-value investment: Identifying all macOS dependencies (objc2 four crates, NSVisualEffectView, etc.) in advance allows for precise targeting of locations requiring modifications during Phase E implementation. .skill files are in ZIP format (zipfile.ZipFile can be decompressed). After exporting from Claude.ai, they need to be adapted to Claude Code format (adding origin:custom, removing reference to upload path); the local installation path is ~/.claude/skills//SKILL.md Session Summary Gadget (Comprehensive Summarize Upgrade) ✅ Reconstruct summarize module (2930 lines → 8 modules + 72 tests) + /code-summarize command addition + redesigned skill with six chapters 20:28:25.974 | claude_code Throughout the day, three upgrades were made to gadget summarize: (1) Planning through ccplan, identified 12 problems by Critic (2 critical issues like MCP server import breakage), refactoring 2930 lines → 8 modules, first writing 47 import smoke tests for safety net, all 72/72 tests passed, updates to MCP server + monthly + weekly import chains completed; (2) Adding /code-summarize command (supporting default./ directory, recursive scanning, intelligent output); (3) Upgrading skill from flat four-dimensional to thesis-style six-chapter format, with scale adaptation and Results-level downgrading strategy, creating ECL planning documents.
Gadget (Skills Toolchain) ✅ Renaming ccplan + workflow interruption repair + installation of code-summarizer/optimizer 19:58:03.000 | claude_code Renamed cchelper to ccplan, fixed root causes of workflow interruptions (CONTINUOUS EXECUTION MANDATE + 10 → NEXT: transition instructions + 9 multi-turn protocols); decompressed and adapted two.skill ZIP packages for code-summarizer and code-optimizer (added origin:custom, removed upload path reference); installed all skills in ~/.claude/skills/ and verified that 4 files are in place.
TokenMonitor ✅ Improvement of CLAUDE.md + complete architecture planning (cross-platform + ccusage MCP + SSH) + user tutorial 21:32:04.982 | claude_code Deeply analyzed the TokenMonitor code library (Tauri v2+Svelte 5+Rust) and added missing key content to CLAUDE.md; verified three major changes through multiple rounds of ccplan planning. Key paths: Explore Agent found MCP server is better than sub-process calls, Feasibility Agent discovered Windows tray size constraints, determined mixed tray scheme and five-phase migration plan, ECL documents fully record all decisions; created docs/tutorial.md for complete user guide (installation/interface/Settings/fault tolerance).
🔄 Phase A MCP Bridge four-module implementation + Phase E cross-platform code cleanup 21:32:04.982 | claude_code Created four Rust modules in Phase A (detect/mcp_process/mcp_client/mcp_adapter), covering cross-platform detection, process lifecycle, high-level client, and adaptation layer. lib.rs has been updated; completed removal of objc2 series dependencies from Cargo.toml in Phase E, added Win/Linux configuration to tauri.conf.json, removed ~350 lines of glass code from commands.rs, cross-platform transformation of tray_render.rs, and added set_tooltip() on all platforms. Both phases have not been compiled due to cargo not being in the PATH.
BOSS (Robot Benchmark) ✅ Creation of BOSS robot benchmark code library CLAUDE.md, dataset error repair, comparison of two evaluation scripts 03:18:28.244 | claude_code Created CLAUDE.md for BOSS on tianhe server (training/evaluation commands, three-level challenges, RAMG data augmentation); identified errors caused by the coexistence of libero_10 and libero_90 folders in form_boss_44_dataset.py and provided fixes; compared four types of differences between the two versions of evaluation scripts, identifying the core design intention of affected versions to achieve OSS robustness assessment through mapping.json.
LiPM (Battery Model) ✅ Logical review of LiPM battery model trainer.py, identified 5 potential bugs 13:41:51.723 | claude_code After multiple connection failures (interruptions for about 6 hours from 07:14 to 13:37), reconnection was attempted. Reviewing trainer.py revealed 5 issues: repeated batch_cuda call on line 74, variable name error on line 147 (test_datasets → test_dataset), net.train() overriding backbone.eval(), lack of KeyError protection for ‘mae’ key, unclear conditional judgment semantics. Each issue had specific locations and repair suggestions provided, pending user confirmation before execution.
Gadget (rclone Synchronization) ✅ Synchronizing research data to Google Drive using rclone 19:54:55.000 | claude_code Executed sync.py to push research cache/projects/reports and configuration files to gdrive:gadget. Other directories were skipped, and the operation was completed quickly without errors.

title: bug journal 2026-03-24

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-23 Today’s Overview What was done: Completed gadget project data sync, cchelper skill installation, and GPU resource survey on the DCC HPC cluster, while systematically documenting the core challenges of the MIHD spatial transcriptomics multimodal clustering project into a persistent problem summary document. How it was done: Used rclone + sync.py to pull data from Google Drive, queried cluster resources with SLURM commands (sinfo/sacctmgr), manually installed the cchelper skill directory to ~/.claude/skills/, and integrated the full picture of issues by reading plans.md/RESULTS.md/diagnostic reports. Why it matters: The new DCC machine went from zero to a fully operational environment, and the MIHD project’s historical issues were consolidated into PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md for the first time, providing a structured entry point for future ablation experiments and debugging. Completed environment initialization on the DCC HPC cluster (rclone sync, GPU node survey, skill installation), and documented current issues for the MIHD research project.
Today’s Tasks Architecture & Strategy ✅ MIHD project progress and issue review — Reviewed the current state of the MIHD project (best ARI 0.546, root cause of cross-slice retrieval failure, list of fixed bugs) and generated a structured problem summary document at docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md. Implementation & Fixes ✅ DCC environment initialization: rclone sync of Google Drive data — Created ~/.config/gadget/sync.json on the DCC cluster and used sync.py pull to sync the four data categories (summarize/research/test/website) from gdrive:gadget to local storage. Successfully synced 11 directories and 3 file groups. ✅ DCC GPU node survey — Used sinfo/sacctmgr to identify GPU partitions available to user account (yizhanglab): common (RTX 2080), gpu-common, scavenger-gpu (A6000/RTX 5000-6000 Ada), scavenger-h200 (H200×8). Account TRES limits are cpu=100, mem=500G. ✅ Install cchelper skill on DCC — Installed the project’s skills/cchelper directory to ~/.claude/skills/cchelper/, containing 4 files: SKILL.md, helper.md, ecl-schema.md, methods-catalog.md. Issues & Solutions Critical Issues 1. MIHD cross-slice retrieval almost completely failed (10 out of 14 query directions had SL@50=0) Solution: Diagnosis confirmed the root cause is that PCA/STAIG processes each slice independently, producing incomparable embedding spaces. The fix is to use scGPT embeddings directly (shared space, average SL@50=0.416, 100% hit rate).
Key insight: Embedding space comparability (whether a shared coordinate system exists) is more fundamental than embedding quality. Cross-slice tasks and single-slice clustering should use different encoders.
2. MIHD single-slice ARI gap vs. SOTA (current 0.546 vs. target 0.60+) Solution: A 4-phase ablation experiment plan was drafted (clustering ablation → batch effect analysis → retrieval probing → 151676 targeted diagnosis), but not yet fully executed.
Key insight: Zero-variance embeddings on slice 151676 cause mclust to fail and fall back to KMeans, which is the key factor dragging down the average. Root cause still unknown.
General Issues 3. Missing ~/.config/gadget/sync.json on DCC, preventing sync.py from running Solution: AI read the existing summarize config to obtain the rclone_remote setting, manually created sync.json, verified the remote directory structure, then executed the pull.
Key insight: Config files are not automatically migrated to new machines and must be inferred from existing configs and rebuilt.
4. cp -r flattened the cchelper directory into ~/.claude/skills/ instead of preserving the subdirectory structure Solution: After discovering the issue, used mkdir -p ~/.claude/skills/cchelper to create the subdirectory, then moved the scattered files into it.
Key insight: When the target directory already exists, cp -r expands the source directory’s contents directly into it. The target path must be confirmed beforehand.
Human Thinking vs. AI Thinking Strategic Level Persisting problem summaries as documentation Role Approach Human After the AI verbally summarized the issues, the user proactively requested to “write the problems in detail to a .md file,” driving the externalization of knowledge. AI The AI defaulted to answering in conversational form and did not proactively suggest writing the analysis to a file. Analysis: Humans have an instinct to externalize session content into documents; AI tends to deliver answers within the conversation without proactively persisting them.
Implementation Level Clarifying an ambiguous personal file sync request Role Approach Human The user requested to “sync personal files on gdrive,” which was vague — no target directory or specific folders were specified. AI The AI listed the directory structure on Google Drive, then asked the user to clarify their specific needs, making no assumptions before executing. Analysis: When requirements are unclear, the AI chose to confirm rather than guess — the correct approach — but the session ended without completing the actual sync as a result.
AI Limitations Significant Limitations When reviewing the MIHD project issues, the AI’s summary relied entirely on existing documentation (plans.md/RESULTS.md/diagnostic reports) and could not independently infer undocumented implicit progress or judge the priority of ablation experiments. General Limitations Overlooked the behavior difference of cp -r when the target already exists, causing files to be flattened rather than keeping the subdirectory structure. The user needed to notice this before it was corrected (in practice the AI itself discovered and corrected it). Glob search for rclone config files timed out (20 seconds) on the large HPC filesystem, requiring a fallback to a more targeted path search strategy. Today’s Takeaways Core Insights In cross-slice spatial transcriptomics tasks, the comparability of the embedding space (shared coordinate system) is more critical than local embedding quality. Foundation Models (e.g., scGPT) are naturally suited for cross-slice retrieval because they output a fixed representation space, whereas PCA/STAIG processes each slice independently, making coordinate systems incomparable. The optimal method for single-slice clustering (STAIG) and the optimal method for cross-slice retrieval (scGPT) are different. Different encoders must be chosen for different task objectives rather than seeking a single unified optimal solution. Practical Insights The DCC cluster’s scavenger-h200 partition provides H200×8 nodes (preemptive), which is an important resource for training tasks requiring large GPU memory. The account TRES limits of cpu=100/mem=500G are hard constraints when submitting large jobs. Session Summaries Gadget (DCC) ✅ Pulled full gadget project data from Google Drive per sync.py configuration 19:27:27.829 | claude_code User requested syncing Google Drive content to DCC local storage per sync.py’s config. AI found sync.json was missing, inferred the remote path as gdrive:gadget from the summarize config, created the config file, ran a dry-run preview, then executed the actual pull. Successfully synced 11 directories across summarize/research/test/website; 6 new directories that didn’t yet exist were skipped.
✅ Queried available GPU nodes and partition info on the DCC SLURM cluster 20:57:23.648 | claude_code User asked which GPU nodes could be requested on the DCC cluster. AI used sinfo/sacctmgr to confirm the cluster runs SLURM; user account yizhanglab has access to four partitions: common (RTX 2080), gpu-common, scavenger-gpu (various high-end GPUs), scavenger-h200 (H200×8). TRES limits are cpu=100/mem=500G. AI also provided example srun commands for each partition.
✅ Installed the cchelper custom skill to ~/.claude/skills/ on DCC 21:03:52.648 | claude_code User requested loading the project’s skills. AI found the skills/cchelper directory (Evolving Constraint Planning skill) and installed it to ~/.claude/skills/. During the process, cp -r flattened the files due to a path issue; AI immediately detected this and corrected it via mkdir+mv, ultimately confirming all 4 skill files were correctly installed under the ~/.claude/skills/cchelper/ subdirectory.
🔄 Initial exploration of rclone config on DCC to sync personal Google Drive files 19:20:48.829 | claude_code User requested syncing personal Google Drive files on the DCC cluster using an existing rclone config. AI confirmed rclone was available and the gdrive: remote was configured, then listed the cloud directory structure (ADnet/ZLibrary/gadget/summarize/tmp). Since the user did not specify a target folder or local path, AI asked for clarification and the session ended without performing an actual sync.
MIHD ✅ Reviewed MIHD project progress and core issues, written to PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md 21:08:23.848 | claude_code User asked where the MIHD project stood and what issues had been encountered. After reading plans.md/RESULTS.md and two diagnostic reports, AI compiled: the completed framework and evaluation of 10 fusion strategies; four unresolved issues (ARI gap / cross-slice retrieval failure / 151676 crash / scGPT paradox); and 5 previously fixed historical bugs. The user then requested writing the issues to a file, and AI created docs/PROBLEM_SUMMARY.md with a structured summary organized into 4 major sections.
