summary: 天和集群已成功修复了错误恢复基准测试中的三项核心问题（爪子极性反转、wrong_object过滤逻辑错误、drop_with_interaction注入策略）。训练场景的数量也从1209增加到1627（+35%），同时深入分析了three_piece_assembly相位检测退化的根本原因，以及48个ungenerable cases的物理约束因素。此外，TzJsDesktop系统还完成了批量归档W05-W12历史周报的工作，修复了gadget网站部署过程中的问题，并完成了Hugo站点的导航下拉菜单的改造，同时统一构建了staging环境的架构。
title: 缺陷日志 2026-03-22

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-21 Today’s Highlights What was done: Two projects were progressed simultaneously: error_recovery_benchmark completed the full cycle from single-point parameter fixes to architectural-level trajectory segmentation reconstruction (including pipeline contract alignment and generation of large-scale training scenarios); gadget fixed critical bugs related to cross-device data synchronization interruptions and daily report generation. How it was done: In tianhe, systematic code reviews were conducted (3 parallel agents + log aggregation statistics), phased fixes were implemented (parameter adjustments → architectural reconstruction), and Slurm parallel jobs were used for large-scale verification; in TzJsDesktop, precise code debugging resolved npx hanging bugs, and sed was used to batch upgrade all agent model configurations. What’s achieved: Benchmark training data increased from 88/174 subtypes to 130+/160 subtypes, threading task subtypes increased by 8 times; gadget daily report pipeline became functional again, and ECC inference capabilities were upgraded to the highest level (opus + effortLevel: max). DCC What was done: Forced synchronization between the local main branch and historical forks on GitHub origin/main. How it was done: After confirming that there was no code divergence locally using git fetch + git diff –stat, git reset –hard origin/main was executed. What’s achieved: The code repository is consistent with GitHub, and new features like rclone sync.py are now available on DCC. TzJsDesktop What was done: Fixed git merge conflicts in the gadget project, configured rclone data synchronization, fixed npx subprocess hanging bugs, improved documentation, and completed the upgrade of ECC global agent models; also compared results with old opportunity scan reports and found an unexpected regression in three_piece_assembly. How it was done: Git reset –hard to align with remote versions, Path.as_posix() fix for Windows path bugs, npx –yes parameter to skip interaction prompts, sed used to batch upgrade agent configurations; ran opportunity scanner to obtain new scan data and compared changes across tasks. What’s achieved: Gadget data synchronization restored, daily report generation pipeline became stable, ECC agents upgraded to opus + max thinking levels; accurately identified the cause of three_piece_assembly regression and continued troubleshooting. tianhe What was done: Completed the full cycle of error_recovery_benchmark repairs: code reviews identified root causes (phase detection failure, objects[0] ambiguity) → parameter fixes (7 types of error skills) → architectural reconstruction (trajectory segmentation + contract alignment Steps 1-7) → large-scale training scenario generation (1222 scenarios) → opportunity graph re-scoring (threading 3→25) → investigation into the root cause of three_piece_assembly degradation. How it was done: Parallel multi-agent code reviews, bash log statistics, python data validation, file-by-file modifications (7 error skill files + 5 framework files), sbatch Slurm parallel jobs (96 workers). What’s achieved: Training scenario success rate increased from approximately 55% to 81% (130/160 subtypes achieved 10/10), threading repairs were the most significant, the root cause of three_piece_assembly degradation was identified, and repair plans are ready. In tianhe HPC cluster, full-cycle repairs of error_recovery_benchmark were completed: from parameter tuning to architectural-level trajectory segmentation reconstruction (1222 training scenarios, threading subtypes increased from 3 to 25). Meanwhile, in TzJsDesktop, critical bugs in the gadget infrastructure were fixed, and ECC toolchain was upgraded to opus + max thinking levels.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Systematic Contract Repair Design (Trajectory Segmentation Architecture) — Identified inconsistencies in the contract between detector→injector→validator→generator. The core repair direction is to segment trajectories based on object interactions (InteractionSegmenter), with each segment having a clear target object, completely bypassing phase detection defects; plan files were created and updated to provide complete context for Steps 1-7. ✅ Comprehensive Code Review of 13 Error Skills and Investigation into the Root Causes of Training Data Generation — Complete code reviews of can_inject/inject/validate for all error skills using 3 parallel agents. 5 types of implementation bugs were identified; aggregated 42 failed log statistics reasons (gripper not closed 1698 times, displacement insufficient 1390 times, etc.); bash statistics revealed that all 372 threading opportunities were marked as pre_reach, and pick_place lacked reach/grasp stages. It was confirmed that phase detection failure was the core root cause. ✅ Systematic Parameter Fixes for Error Skills (Fix A-G + Fix 5-6) — Made 24 parameter modifications for 7 types of error skills (lower limit of offset, number of steps, direction of speed, validation threshold); fixed get_target_object() prioritizing search in graspable objects (Fix 5), InteractionSegmenter restricting the set of graspable candidates (Fix 6); all 139 unit tests passed. ✅ Implementation of Trajectory Segmentation + Contract Alignment (Steps 1-7 + 6 Missing Fixes) — Added InteractionSegment dataclass and InteractionSegmenter; modified CleanTrajectory to support segmented serialization; modified OpportunityScanner to fetch target_object/phase/other_objects from segments; fixed the semantics of objects[0] in 13 skills (e01-e13); fixed e02 gripper direction, e11 freeze_steps hardcoding; completed target_pos to trajectory_context; fixed e12/e10 config key drift; fixed context_replay.py using error_spec.target; fixed EnvWrapper._sim_body_name2id substring fallback; all 139 unit tests passed. 🔄 Investigation into the Root Cause of Three_Piece_Assembly Regression (23→4 Subtypes) — Analyzed NPZ files (without interaction_segments_json), scan logs (879/887 frames marked as pre_reach), and collect scripts (never called segment_interactions()). Identified 4 root causes: collect script failed to integrate the segmenter, replay_and_label_phases() did not pass in target_object causing phase detection with immobile bases, scanner fallback covering bad labels, lack of defensive logic. Repair plans are prepared, and code implementation is pending. ✅ Gadget: Fixed npx subprocess Hanging Bug — In fetch_codex_usage_full() and fetch_ccusage_full(), npx calls were not given –yes, resulting in a hang during installation confirmation under capture_output=True mode; adding –yes parameter to all 3 npx calls resolved the issue, and the daily report generation pipeline returned to normal. ✅ Gadget: ECC All Agents Upgraded to Opus + Max Thinking — In-depth reading of ECC components (5 core agents, 4 skills, hooks system), using sed to batch upgrade 27 agents from sonnet/haiku to opus (doc-updater retained sonnet), settings.json effortLevel changed to max (new feature introduced by users). ✅ Large-Scale Parallel Generation of Training Scenarios (Multiple Slurm Jobs) — Three rounds of scenario generation were performed: first round (after Fix A-G) with 96 workers generating 1209 scenarios (158/160 subtypes succeeded); second round (after Fix 5-6) with 32 workers generating 1159 scenarios (107/160 achieved 10/10); third round (after Steps 1-7) with 96 workers generating 1222 scenarios on an53 node in 32 minutes and 49 seconds (130/160 subtypes succeeded). ✅ Verification of Collect + Scan for All 6 Tasks and Opportunity Graph Re-scoring — Multiple runs of collect+scan for all 6 tasks were performed; final scan: threading 3→25 (+22), pick_place 21→23 (+2), three_piece_assembly 23→4 (-19), total 130→135 (+5); verification showed significant effects of target_pos repairs on threading, while an unexpected regression in three_piece_assembly was also detected. ✅ Gadget: monthly_summary.py Added Support for Codex Usage Aggregation — New functions like aggregate_token_usage and combine_usage_summaries were added to independently count codex_token_usage in monthly summaries, generating combined_token_usage_summary, and updating the README document description accordingly. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Gadget: Git Synchronization and rclone Data Configuration — Fixed git merge conflicts in DCC and TzJsDesktop (git reset –hard origin/main to align with remote versions); created ~/.config/gadget/sync.json, fixed Windows backslash bugs in sync.py, successfully pulled all personal data from Google Drive. ✅ Review and Update of CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md (ErrorRecoveryBenchmark) — Reviewed existing CLAUDE.md with /init, added missing Makefile targets (v5-training, v5-mass-gen), training scripts, and 6 undocumented pipeline scripts; created a 358-word AGENTS.md contributor guide covering project structure, build test commands, code style, and PR specifications. Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. Systematic Phase Detection Failure in Multi-Object Tasks Like threading/pick_place: All threading frames were marked as pre_reach, pick_place lacked reach/grasp stages, resulting in inability to find opportunities for 12/13 skills. Solution: Root cause: Phase detection relies on the distance between a single object and EEF, which is untenable in special geometries like needle-shaped needles or multi-object scenarios. Fix: Added InteractionSegmenter to segment trajectories based on object interactions (EEF proximity + gripper state + co-motion detection), with each segment having a clear target_object and phase, completely bypassing phase detection defects.
Key Insight: The single-object assumption in phase detection is a structural design flaw that cannot be fixed through parameter tuning; trajectory segmentation is a more fundamental solution, abstracting from the defect rather than repairing it.
2. Ambiguity of Target Objects in v5 Pipeline: OpportunityScanner/skillInject()/validate()/ContextReplayEngine default to objects[0], leading to systematic errors in multi-object scenarios; missing target_pos in trajectory_context makes drop-type skills’ filtering conditions ineffective. Solution: From Steps 1-7 onwards, pass through target_object and target_pos throughout the pipeline; fixed the semantics of objects[0] for 13 skills; wrote target_pos after constructing the trajectory context; used error_spec.target in context_replay.py.
Key Insight: In multi-object tasks, objects[0] is the first object in dictionary insertion order, with incorrect semantics that causes systematic errors in judgments based on object state (displacement/gripper/pose).
3. Systematic Mismatch between Injection Parameters and Verification Thresholds in Error Skills, resulting in 41+ subtypes generating 0 scenarios (5 root causes including insufficient displacement, empty grippers, objects not released, insufficient collision force). Solution: Identified 5 major root causes and made targeted modifications to 7 files in 24 locations (reducing verification thresholds, increasing steps, adjusting speed direction); applied a slight initial velocity (Z=-0.15m/s) to drop-type skills to assist release.
Key Insight: The lower limit of injection parameters must be matched with the verification threshold; for drop-type skills, it cannot rely solely on gravity, requiring an active initial velocity to overcome friction.
4. Collect script never called segment_interactions(), NPZ lacked segmentation data; in three_piece_assembly, InteractionSegmenter and get_target_object() mistakenly selected base fixtures as targets, causing scan regression from 23 to 4 subtypes. Solution: Fixed 5-6 to limit target search to graspable objects (with grasp_geoms configuration); collect script needs to call segment_interactions(); replay_and_label_phases() must pass in target_object; scanner needs to fix label coverage timing issues. (The fixture part has been fixed, but collection integration is pending.)
Key Insight: The segmenter was implemented, but data collection pipelines were not connected. Contract alignment must track and persist on the storage layer—unit tests passing do not guarantee E2E functionality.
5. python summarize/daily_summary.py permanently stuck in @ccusage/codex steps, with no output or errors. Solution: subprocess.run(capture_output=True) redirected stdin to DEVNULL; npx installation confirmation prompts were fixed by adding –yes parameter during the first installation; added –yes parameter to all 3 npx calls to skip interaction confirmation.
6. HPC Multiple Obstacles: Shared login nodes with 32+ workers simultaneously forked + import heavy-duty libraries caused IO competition crashes; AI partitions forced gpu:a800:1 even when tasks were purely CPU-based; sbatch –wrap using /bin/sh did not support source/conda commands; MUJOCO_GL=osmesa failed to initialize on some nodes. Solution: Submitted Slurm exclusive node jobs (96 cores); accepted 1 GPU but ran with MUJOCO_GL=disabled (physical simulation does not require GL); sbatch commands were wrapped in bash -c, and conda init changed from . instead of source.### Key Insights
MuJoCo physical simulation (enable_camera=False) is a pure CPU task. The sbatch –wrap command uses /bin/sh by default, rather than the user’s logged-in shell. The number of workers should exactly match the specified –cpus-per-task. General issues: In e02 drop_in_transit, action[-1]=-1.0 indicates “Open gripper” in the comment, but actually it closes the gripper, causing dropped objects to be re-caught and resulting in a failed drop. In e11 validate(), the threshold min_progress * 30 is hardcoded, while inject() reads freeze_steps from config, leading to inconsistent judgment criteria. Solution: Change e02 to action[-1]=1.0 (robosuite OSC 1.0 = open). Add freeze_steps = self.config.get(‘freeze_steps’, 30) in e11 to unify the source. Robosuite gripper action symbols contradict intuition and comments (-1 means closed, 1 means open). The log shows drop metrics as 0, which can be misjudged as a physical execution failure rather than a control command error. YAML config keys do not match Python code reading keys (lateral_offset vs lateral_offset_range, rotation_offset vs rotation_range, reverse_steps vs regression_range). Relevant skills always use hardcoded default values. Solution: Add double-key fallbacks (first find the new name, then the old one) for reverse_steps. Change the handling of reverse_steps from fixed values to range sampling. Key insight: The evolution of config keys does not update code synchronously. The getdefault mechanism conceals errors—config is ignored without an error message, but the program reports no errors. This is a highly concealed bug. Solution: Users pointed out that the remote should be used as the reference. Execute git reset –hard origin/main to align directly. Change str(Path(remote_rel).parent) to Path(remote_rel).parent.as_posix(). Human vs AI thinking: Strategic level: Problem nature judgment: Systemic Contract inconsistency vs isolated bug. AI tends to fix individual bugs and misses the systematic perspective of the entire link. Difference analysis: Humans define the problem’s essence from an architectural perspective, directly changing the direction of the repair plan. AI is better at quickly locating specific bugs locally, but lacks a holistic view of the module data flow. Core repair directions: Segmentation of trajectories (bypassing phase detection) vs repairing phase detection. Humans propose a fundamental architecture solution that segments trajectories by objects—each segment has a clear target object, and gripper detection is based on the common position between the object and the gripper, completely bypassing phase detection defects. AI only detects symptoms of phase detection failure. Proposes to fix the single-object assumption in get_task_completion_stages(), remaining at a repair level. Difference analysis: Humans propose a more elegant architecture solution—bypassing rather than repairing. AI’s approach stays at an incorrect abstraction level, leading to many temporary handling of special cases. Systematic omissions identified in local repairs (6 issues not covered): After AI completes the 7-step plan, systematic review shows: target_pos not passed, fallback phase logic not fixed, config drift only half-fixed, e05/e06/e09 still using objects[0], error_spec.target not used, three_piece_assembly body-name alert. AI completes the 7-step plan and verifies it through unit tests (139 passed), but lacks systematic verification of all skills and calling points. It lacks a review of the overall data flow of modified modules. Verification of basic assumptions such as the number of task objects: Humans quickly corrects AI’s basic assumption about pick_place (1 object → 4 objects) and threading (no grasping → must grasp needle). AI does not read the task configuration YAML first but infers the task structure based on code, leading to incorrect analysis assumptions. GPU resource requirements and worker configuration: Humans point out that scene generation is a pure CPU task and no GPU is needed. Continuously corrects AI’s excessive GPU requests (8→1). Clearly states that the number of workers should exactly match the number of CPU cores requested (not AI’s conservative estimate of 8-16). AI defaults to assuming that MuJoCo simulation requires GPU rendering, without actively checking the enable_camera parameter. Tends to maximize resource allocation, not aligning with the actual number of node cores. ECC model selection strategy and new version awareness: Humans require “all parts using the latest opus 4.6 with max thinking” and actively inform about the existence of the new version effortLevel: max option. AI initially recommends a cost-effective solution of “core Opus + other Sonnet”. Misleads users by saying high is the highest effort level. npx suspension root cause diagnosis (AI systematic troubleshooting vs human symptom description): Humans provide accurate symptoms (stuck at @ccusage/codex step without output), but do not know the specific root cause. AI systematically eliminates assumptions: checks data volume → measures commands → locates the root cause of capture_output + missing –yes. The entire diagnosis chain is clear and efficient. Subtype number anomalies and precise statistics: Humans manually count 48 failed subtypes (listing file paths, line numbers, and log examples by 5 root causes). Notices that 130 subtypes are insufficient (theory suggests 174), and actively asks for a re-scanned. AI’s classification framework is generally correct but lacks precision. Does not accurately report the difference between 130 subtypes and the expected 174. Subtype number anomalies and precise statistics: Humans manually count 48 failed subtypes (listing file paths, line numbers, and log examples by 5 root causes). Notices that 130 subtypes are insufficient (theory suggests 174), and actively asks for a re-scanned. AI’s classification framework is generally correct but lacks precision. Does not accurately report the difference between 130 subtypes and the expected 174. AI limitations: For complex system problems, it tends to locate isolated code bugs and lacks the ability to analyze contract inconsistencies from a holistic data flow perspective (detector→injector→validator→generator). Human guidance from an architectural level is needed to enhance the analysis dimension. After completing local repair plans, it lacks active global data flow verification: multiple omissions such as e05/e06/e09 three skills, missing target_pos transmission, collect script not integrating segment_interactions(), all require systematic review by humans to be discovered. Unit tests passing gives a false sense of completion. It does not read the task configuration file first but infers the task structure based on code, leading to incorrect analysis assumptions (number of pick_place objects, whether threading has grasping). It should read the configuration file first to verify basic assumptions before starting analysis. It does not actively check the enable_camera parameter and assumes that a GPU is needed. After being corrected multiple times, it still requests too many GPUs in Slurm scripts, making an inaccurate assessment of resource requirements for the running environment and easily forgetting the local preferences set by users during multiple conversations. In git merge conflict scenarios, it defaults to the complex process of “retaining both modifications and resolving conflicts file by file”, without first asking the user’s intention (discarding local files vs retaining local files), resulting in many ineffective operations that are corrected later. The degradation of three_piece_assembly (from 23 to 4 subtypes) is presented as a “normal result” by AI during reporting, without active warning and analysis. When analysis fails, it also fails to exhaust all similar files (e05/e06/e09 are omitted), requiring humans to explicitly list them to complete the process. It does not understand the latest features of Claude Code (effortLevel: max) and misleads users by saying high is the highest level. There is a knowledge gap in the rapidly evolving tool ecosystem. AI should actively declare that there may be unknown features in new versions. Today’s takeaways: Pipeline contract alignment must track the entire data flow: not only should the code processing logic be modified, but also ensure that data is persisted at the storage level (e.g., segment_interactions() results written to NPZ). Otherwise, data will be lost when reloaded, and repairs will be ineffective for subsequent processes. Passing unit tests does not mean E2E correctness. In multi-object robot operations tasks, target_object must be transmitted throughout the detector/injector/validator stages as a primary citizen. Objects[0] is the first object in dictionary insertion order. In multi-object scenarios, it is semantically incorrect and will not report errors, leading to systematic misjudgments based on object states. Before analyzing complex system problems, all relevant configuration files must be read to verify basic assumptions (e.g., how many objects there are in the task, what type of task). “Configuration file is the map, code is the terrain. We need to look at the map first.” After systematic repairs, before and after comparisons must be performed on all tasks. It cannot be assumed that there are no side effects just because unit tests pass. The simultaneous occurrence of threading +22 and three_piece_assembly -19 illustrates this point. The lower limit of Error skill injection parameters must be adjusted in conjunction with the verification threshold. When reducing the injection lower limit, the verification threshold (min_displacement, min_rotation_deviation, etc.) must also be reduced accordingly. Otherwise, injection will still fail to pass verification. Drop-type errors cannot rely solely on gravity and must have a slight initial downward velocity (Z=-0.15m/s) to assist in releasing objects from friction. subprocess.run(capture_output=True) will redirect stdin to DEVNULL. When calling CLI tools such as npx that may have interactive prompts, parameters like –yes/-y must be explicitly passed to skip the interaction. Otherwise, it will hang indefinitely instead of timing out. Log aggregation statistics (Counter) is more effective at locating systematic bugs than reading each item individually: 1698 times gripper not closed directly points to target_object ambiguity, rather than real gripper execution problems. Data-driven root cause analysis is better than subjective guessing. MuJoCo physical simulation (enable_camera=False) is a pure CPU task. MUJOCO_GL=disabled can completely bypass OpenGL. HPC shared nodes are not suitable for large-scale parallel subprocesses. Slurm exclusive nodes should be used, and the number of workers must exactly match –cpus-per-task. Slurm sbatch –wrap uses /bin/sh by default and needs to be explicitly wrapped with bash -c. Graspable objects and fixtures must be distinguished in multi-object MuJoCo tasks. The grasp_geoms field in task_config can be used as a filter basis to avoid misjudging targets when EEF trajectories pass above the fixture. Other_objects still need to retain the fixture for collision detection, only filtering during target search. Robosuite OSC controller gripper action symbols contradict intuition and comments (-1.0=closed, 1.0=open). The log shows drop-related metrics as 0, which can be misjudged as a physical execution failure rather than a control command error. The naming of robosuite MuJoCo bodies does not match that of task config, requiring fuzzy matching fallbacks. ECC v1.9.0 adds effortLevel: max. Combining it with the full opus model configures AI’s reasoning ability to the highest level. It is a one-time investment at the infrastructure level. When passing Windows pathlib.Path to external commands (rclone, git, etc.), .as_posix() must be used to convert it to positive斜杠. Session Summary ErrorRecoveryBenchmark: ✅ Code review and root cause investigation: CLAUDE.md updated, 13 skills thoroughly reviewed, phase detection failure found, systematic contract issues established. 02:19:30.892 | claude_code + codex Passed the /init review and updated CLAUDE.md (added Makefile targets, training scripts, undocumented pipeline scripts). Reviewed the complete code of 3 parallel agents for 13 error skills and found 5 types of bugs (e02 gripper direction reversal, e7 empty dictionary, e11 hardcoded threshold, YAML key mismatch, etc.). Passed bash statistics to analyze the opportunity map phase distribution. Confirmed that phase detection failure was the core root cause. Humans pointed out the systematic problem’s essence (inconsistency in the entire link contract) and established “trajectory segmentation” as the fundamental repair direction. Codex generated AGENTS.md contributor guidelines (358 words).🔄 Architecture Fix Implementation: Steps 1-7 Trajectory Segmentation + Contract Alignment + 6 Missing Fixes + Large-scale Scenario Generation + Scan Verification and Degradation Troubleshooting 03:46:21.126 | claude_code Systematic implementation of the 7-step plan: Added InteractionSegment/InteractionSegmenter, modified CleanTrajectory segmentation serialization, modified OpportunityScanner and ContextReplayEngine to pass through target_object, fixed the semantic issues of objects[0] in 7 skills and 2 explicit bugs; 6 missing fixes were added after human review (target_pos, fallback phase, config drift, e05/e06/e09 semantics, error_spec.target, body-name fallback); sbatch job 50015 generated 1222 scenarios on node an53 in 32 minutes and 49 seconds; re-scanner results: threading increased from 3 to 25 (+22), three_piece_assembly decreased from 23 to 4 (-19); the root cause of degradation was identified through NPZ analysis and collect script review (collect did not call segment_interactions()), and a repair plan has been prepared for implementation.** ✅ Parameter Fixes and Preliminary Scenario Generation: Fix A-G (7 types of error skills) + Fix 5-6 (graspable object filtering) + Parallel Scenario Generation + Analysis of 48 Subtype Root Causes 17:31:04.519 | claude_code Implementation of Fix A-G: Modified 24 parameters in 7 files, 96 workers Slurm job generated 1209 scenarios (158/160 subtypes succeeded); implementation of Fix 5-6: Restricted graspable object candidate set to fix three_piece_assembly target misidentification, re-run all 6 tasks collect+scan verification; 32 workers generated 1159 scenarios (107/160 fully completed 10/10); users provided precise classification of 48 failed subtypes (including file paths and line numbers), AI analyzed 7 skill source codes to identify 5 systematic root causes, and wrote a repair plan document.**
Gadget ✅ Infrastructure Fixes and Toolchain Updates: Git Synchronization, rclone Configuration, npx Bug Fixes, ECC Opus Upgrade, Codex Monthly Aggregation Support 02:35:05.863 | claude_code + codex Fixed DCC and TzJsDesktop’s git merge conflicts (git reset –hard origin/main to align with remote, avoiding complex merge paths taken by AI); created rclone synchronization configuration, fixed Windows backslash bug (Path.as_posix()) and successfully pulled all personal data; precisely identified the root cause of daily_summary.py being stuck at @ccusage/codex step (npx without –yes caused infinite waiting in capture_output mode), added –yes parameter fixes at 3 locations; after thoroughly reviewing ECC components, upgraded 27 agents to opus using sed (doc-updater retained sonnet), effortLevel set to max (user informed about new features); added monthly_summary.py Codex usage aggregation function through Codex, updated README document synchronously; research/CLAUDE.md added unrecorded CLI parameters.**

title: 错误日志 2026-03-21

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-20 Today’s Highlights What was done: Synchronized progress on two HPC devices, DCC and tianhe, in two directions: robotics operations and spatial transcriptomics. The former involved fixing data quality issues for error recovery benchmarks, parallel batch generation for training scenarios, and building a Pi0.5-base LoRA fine-tuning pipeline. The latter focused on fixing unfair comparisons of STAIG hyperparameters and developing analysis scripts for 10x Visium data. How it was done: Identified and fixed false positive threshold issues through systematic GPU diagnostic scripts; transformed serial scene generation into parallel processing with 32 workers using ThreadPoolExecutor (4 GPUs in round-robin); completed HDF5 to LeRoBot format conversion and norm statistics calculations; corrected misleading experiment names through cross-validation of logs and caches; migrated Visium visualization to the native API of scanpy. What’s achieved: Dropped false positive rate for drop_in_transit decreased from 80% to 0%, scene generation speed increased by about 10 times (973 scenes in 41 minutes), all pre-training data for Pi0.5 was ready; established fair benchmarks for STAIG; correct visualization output for 6 Visium images. The training process was interrupted due to the expiration of the Slurm interactive job, requiring resubmission via sbatch. DCC What was done: Fixed unfair comparisons in STAIG hyperparameter scanning for the MIHD project, and developed scanpy analysis scripts and visualization fixes for 10x Visium samples (151676). How it was done: Identified that pca_uni2_staig_fusion actually used UNI instead of UNI2 by reading logs and cache files; modified ablation scripts to support gene/vision encoder parameter configurations; changed Visium visualization from plt.savefig to native scanpy save parameters. What’s achieved: Established fair benchmarks for PCA+UNI vs PCA+UNI2 (ARI average 0.47), correct visualization output for 6 spatial transcriptomics images (QC, PCA, UMAP, spatial expression). tianhe What was done: Diagnosed and fixed false positive issues for drop_in_transit_D0; transformed serial training scene generation into parallel processing with 32 workers (973 scenes in 41 minutes); prepared data for the Pi0.5-base LoRA fine-tuning pipeline, and briefly started coffee/stack training due to job expiration. How it was done: Written GPU diagnostic scripts to identify min_hold_height threshold issues; created generate_training_scenes_parallel.py (ThreadPoolExecutor, 4 GPUs in round-robin); executed HDF5 to LeRoBot conversion, norm statistics calculations, and training initialization on an49/an50 nodes via SSH+tmux; cross- analyzed parallel_logs with opportunity map to document root causes of failures. What’s achieved: Eliminated false positives for drop_in_transit (D0 success rate increased from 10% to 50%), all 973 training scenes were ready; prepared data for Pi0.5’s 6-task pipeline; the documentation provides clear directions for subsequent validator fixes; training interruptions due to Slurm limitations required resubmission via sbatch. Synchronized progress on two HPC devices, DCC and tianhe, in four main areas: fixing false positives for error recovery benchmarks with parallel generation of 6 tasks’ 973 training scenes (41 minutes), building a Pi0.5-base LoRA six-task merged dataset fine-tuning pipeline (data conversion and norm statistics completed, training interrupted due to Slurm job expiration), fixing unfair comparisons in STAIG hyperparameter scanning by establishing fair benchmarks for PCA+UNI/UNI2 (ARI 0.47), and developing scanpy analysis scripts for 10x Visium spatial transcriptomics.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Drop_in_transit_D0 False Positive Diagnosis and Fixing — Through GPU diagnostic scripts, it was confirmed that a min_hold_height of 0.85 caused false positives in 8 out of 10 cases (objects on the desktop z≈0.88 were misjudged as airborne, with an 80% false positive rate). The five error skill files and configuration thresholds were adjusted to 0.93 (desktop height 0.80 + object height 0.08 + margin 0.05), reducing false positives from 80% to 0%, and increasing D0 success rates from 10% to 50% (4/8). ✅ Parallel Batch Generation of 6-Task Training Error Scenes — Transformed the serial script into a parallel version that supports –subtypes filtering parameters. Created generate_training_scenes_parallel.py (32 workers, 4 GPUs in round-robin allocation), running it in the background on an50 nodes via nohup. All 973 scenes were generated in 41 minutes (128/130 workers succeeded), with speed increased by about 10 times compared to serial processing. • Establishing a Pi0.5-base LoRA Merged Dataset Fine-tuning Pipeline — Redirected openpi05 conda environment.pth file, added 12 merged configs to config.py (each task contained finetune/inference), completed HDF5 to LeRoBot data conversion (coffee/stack/stack_three/three_piece_assembly 2000 items, threading 1000 items) and norm statistics calculations. Started coffee/stack training on an49 tmux nodes. All processes were killed due to Slurm interactive job expiration, requiring resubmission via sbatch. ✅ Root Cause Analysis and Documentation of Training Scene Generation Failures — Identified five main causes through parallel_logs logs, opportunity map distributions, and meta.json cross-analysis: gripper_closed_norm anomaly P0 level, insufficient drop collision detection, insufficient OSC displacement response, physical object constraints, and threading phase mismatch. Written training_scene_failure_analysis.md to provide actionable references for subsequent validator fixes. ✅ Fixing Fair Comparisons in STAIG Hyperparameter Scanning — Discovered that the ablation script used raw HVG 3000d instead of PCA input, and the experiment names were misleading (UNI was actually used instead of UNI2). Modified scripts to support –gene_encoder and –vision_variant parameters, running pseudo_k sweeps with PCA+UNI and PCA+UNI2 respectively. ARI average was about 0.47, establishing fair benchmarks. 🔄 Adding PickPlace as the Sixth Task to the Training Pipeline — Modified train_pi05_merged.py, config.py, and MimicGen configuration (2000 D0 items, no D1 variants). Data generation started on an49 GPU 0 nodes but was interrupted due to Slurm job expiration (184/2000 completed). Implementation and Fixes ✅ Development and Visualization Fixing of 10x Visium Analysis Scripts — Developed scanpy analysis scripts for 151676 samples (QC, normalization, HVG, PCA/UMAP, spatial visualization). Fixed the issue where spatial_gene_expression.png had only H&E background without gene points: changed from plt.savefig to native scanpy save parameters, resulting in correct generation of 6 images. ✅ Openpi Docker Image Building and Saving Guide — Read openpi’s docs/docker.md, serve_policy.Dockerfile, and compose.yml, summarized the purposes of 5 Dockerfiles and corresponding build commands, as well as the complete process of docker save/load. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. drop_in_transit_D0 generated very few valid scenes. It was diagnosed that a min_hold_height of 0.85 was too low, causing objects on the desktop (z≈0.88) to be misjudged as airborne. An 80% chance of false positives occurred. Solution: Increase min_hold_height from 0.85 to 0.93 (desktop height 0.80 + object height 0.08 + margin 0.05). Simultaneously modify the five error skill files and configs. Eliminate false positives, increasing D0 success rates from 10% to 50%.
Key Insight: In the pick_place task, the z-height of objects on the desktop can exceed the set minimum height. The design threshold for grasping detection must consider the combination of “desktop height + object height” with enough margin.
2. The training scene generation script was completely serial. The success rate for collision_eef_object subtype was extremely low (only 5 scenes in 10 minutes). It would take over 6 hours to complete 6 tasks. Solution: Add –subtypes filtering parameters to the script and create a ThreadPoolExecutor parallel launcher (32 workers, 4 GPUs in round-robin allocation). All 973 scenes were completed in 41 minutes.
Key Insight: MuJoCo EGL rendering is a CPU-intensive task. Up to 32 independent processes can run on the same node without conflict. Subprocess-based parallelism is more stable than multi-threading and can fully utilize 48-core CPUs.
3. After the Slurm interactive job expired, all training processes and data generation processes were killed via SSH. This wasted time during training. Solution: Formal jobs must be submitted via sbatch, or processes should be guaranteed to persist using tmux within an allocated job. The pam_slurm_adopt policy will kill all related processes when the job ends. SSH nohup cannot bypass this.
Key Insight: HPC cluster Slurm resource isolation mechanisms will forcibly clean up all processes within a job, including subprocesses connected via SSH.
4. After 128/130 workers were completed, there were still 29 task×subtype combinations that were completely missing. Threading only generated 3/29 subtypes. Solution: Cross-analyze opportunity maps with generation logs to distinguish two types of failures: no opportunity points found during the opportunity scan phase versus validation failures during generation. Prioritize fixing gripper_closed_norm=0.00 anomalies (P0 level, which can unlock 8 combinations).
Key Insight: Scene generation failures are divided into two stages: opportunity scan (can_inject is always False) and validator rejection. The former requires modifying the can_inject condition, while the latter requires adjusting thresholds or injection parameters. These must be processed separately.
5. The ARI difference between STAIG hyperparameter sweep baseline (0.23) and clustering ablation ARI (0.58) was unusually large. The experiment name ‘pca_uni2_staig_fusion’ was misleading: raw HVG was actually used instead of UNI2, and the ablation script used raw HVG instead of PCA input. Solution: Modify the ablation script to add –gene_encoder and –vision_variant parameters. Run sweeps with PCA+UNI/UNI2 again, resulting in an ARI average of about 0.47 (within a reasonable range), establishing fair benchmarks.
Key Insight: Experiment naming conventions must strictly correspond to implementation. Misleading names can lead to long-term misunderstandings. It is necessary to verify the actual implementation by reading logs rather than just looking at the experiment name.
6. The PickPlace task did not have MimicGen D1 variants, so it was unable to generate a D0+D1 merged dataset as planned. Solution: Directly generate 2000 D0 items to replace the D0+D1 merged scheme.
Key Insight: The number of variants in MimicGen varies for different robosuite tasks. The data strategy must confirm available variants for each task individually. Total equivalence is a feasible engineering decision.
General Issues 7. Images generated by Visium sc.pl.spatial only show tissue H&E background, with no gene expression points superimposed. Solution: Change the way sc.pl.* is saved from plt.savefig to native scanpy save parameters (sc.settings.figdir + save parameters), allowing the internal pipeline of scanpy to handle spot rendering.
Key Insight: Scanpy’s spatial plot must use its internal saving mechanism. Bypassing matplotlib directly will skip the spot rendering step.
8. MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID=0 did not match CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1, causing import failures. Solution: Change MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID to match CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES (both set to 1).
Key Insight: MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID must be the global physical GPU ID, not the number mapped from CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Initiative in System Resource Utilization and Efficiency Optimization Role Thoughts Human Actively noticed slow serial processes (only 5 scenes in 10 minutes) and proposed “32+ parallelism is definitely possible.” Also, noticed that the training data was only one outlier and asked for further investigation. From a global perspective of system resources (48-core CPU, 4 GPUs, ample memory), actively drove efficiency optimization decisions. AI AI executed tasks according to the serial plan. After noticing slow execution, it set up checks every 30 minutes and waited. It required the user to point out data anomalies before initiating diagnosis. lacked active assessment of overall throughput and system resources. Difference Analysis: Humans proactively proposed optimization solutions from a global perspective of system resources and efficiency. AI focused on task completion and lacked awareness of “what could be done better.”
HPC Process Persistence Strategy (Understanding Slurm Mechanisms) Role Thoughts Human Clearly pointed out that nohup via SSH is unreliable, demanding that processes be ensured to run after the terminal closes using tmux or Slurm. Has practical HPC experience and understands the pam_slurm_adopt mechanism. AI Initially used nohup + & to start processes via SSH, without considering process isolation when Slurm jobs expired. Difference Analysis: Humans understand the underlying mechanisms of HPC resource management. AI only considered shell-level background processes, ignoring Slurm resource isolation.
Fairness in Experiment Design and Intuitive Judgment of Data Strategies Role Thoughts Human Actively noticed unreasonable ARI differences (0.23 vs 0.58) and directly questioned experiment fairness, proposing specific fixes. Quickly decided to use 2000 D0 items as a substitute for the absence of D1 variants without exhaustively exploring technical possibilities. AI Did not proactively detect unfairness in experiments. Only probed through logs after being pointed out. Tends to be more conservative when making data strategy decisions by preferring exploration over immediate decision-making. Goal-Driven Depth of Failure Analysis Role Thoughts Human Required all details of error detectors/injectors/validators to be written down in documentation, aiming to provide actionable references for subsequent repairs from a fixer’s perspective. AI Provided statistics of failed subtypes and categorized root causes, but did not actively integrate detailed code implementations into actionable repair reference documents. Difference Analysis: Humans drive document content with the goal of “how to fix later.” AI stays at the phenomenon description level.
AI Limitations Major Limitations Failed to proactively identify efficiency bottlenecks and propose solutions multiple times: Only set up checks for slow serial scene generation (no parallelization proposed), did not consider resource isolation when starting processes via nohup SSH, and only corrected issues after users pointed them out, resulting in wasted actual time. Failed to proactively detect inconsistencies between experiment configurations and names (“pca_uni2_staig_fusion” actually used UNI), as well as differences between meta.json statistics (current running) and disk files (historical accumulation). Only probed through logs after users questioned. General Limitations Multiple tool/API usage errors: scanpy spatial plot using plt.savefig bypassed internal rendering, resulting in missing images; tyro boolean flag with –resume=True format caused training scripts to crash; MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID and CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES mismatch led to import failures. Lack of a quick fallback strategy when resources/tools are unavailable (HuggingFace proxy instability, TaskOutput tool call failures, progress polling). Attempted multiple methods before providing alternative suggestions, which consumed significant time. Today’s Achievements Core Achievements- The pam_slurm_adopt policy in the Slurm HPC cluster will强制终止所有相关进程 when the job ends; SSH nohup cannot bypass this limitation. Long training tasks must be submitted as formal jobs using sbatch, and should not rely on interactive jobs. The design of the height threshold for robot operation scenarios must consider the combined range of “desktop height + object’s own height”. Simply setting a fixed min_height without considering the actual stacking height of objects will result in many false positives, leading to very few valid scenarios. MuJoCo EGL simulation is a CPU-intensive task. Up to 32 independent processes can run on the same node without conflict. Failure in training scenario generation occurs in two stages: can_inject is always False during the opportunity scan stage, and validation rejects the process. It’s necessary to distinguish between these issues: modify the can_inject condition for the former, and adjust the threshold or injection parameters for the latter. The naming convention for experiments must strictly correspond to the actual implementation. Inconsistent naming (e.g., ‘pca_uni2’ actually uses UNI) can lead to misunderstandings in long-term experiments. It’s important to confirm the actual configuration by reading logs rather than just looking at the experiment name. The number of D0/D1 variants for MimicGen varies depending on the task (e.g., PickPlace has only D0). When designing a multi-task data strategy, it’s necessary to confirm the available variants for each task. An equivalent total amount (e.g., 2000 D0 instead of 1000 D0 and D1) is a feasible engineering decision. When diagnosing problems with generated data, it’s important to distinguish between “current running meta.json statistics” and “disk history accumulation file count”. If there is a significant difference between the two, it indicates that the problem lies in the current running logic, not historical issues. For parallel task logs, it’s recommended to write separate files for each worker so that failure can be quickly identified using grep. Practical Achievements Spatial transcriptomics visualization functions like sc.pl.spatial in Scanpy must be saved using the sc.settings.figdir + save parameters. Using plt.savefig directly will cause incomplete images due to internal layer rendering issues. Session Summary Error Recovery Benchmark (Scenario Fixing and Parallel Generation) ✅ Fixing false positives in drop_in_transit_D0 + Converting serial training scenarios into parallel generation for 6 tasks + Analyzing the root causes of failures 00:55:55.225 | claude_code It was confirmed that setting min_hold_height=0.85 led to many false positives (objects being misclassified as being in the air on the desktop). Increasing the threshold to 0.93 improved the success rate of D0 from 10% to 50%. A batch generation of training scenarios was planned and executed: initially, it took a very long time (5 scenes in 10 minutes) using the serial approach. After parallelization, AI was modified to use 32 workers with ThreadPoolExecutor (4 GPUs round-robin), and 973 scenes were generated in 41 minutes (128/130 workers succeeded). By analyzing parallel_logs and opportunity map, 5 main root causes of failures were identified (e.g., abnormal P0-level for gripper_closed_norm, insufficient drop collision detection). The results are presented in training_scene_failure_analysis.md.
Error Recovery Benchmark (Pi0.5 Training Pipeline) 🔄 Pi0.5-base LoRA merged dataset fine-tuning pipeline: Environment configuration, data conversion, and training startup 01:08:53.326 | claude_code After thoroughly studying the openpi copy structure and existing datasets, the process was carried out as planned: redirecting the openpi05 conda environment.pth file, adding 12 merged configurations to config.py (6 tasks × finetune/inference), completing the HDF5 → LeRoBot conversion of 5 tasks, and calculating norm statistics. The coffee/stack training was started in an49 tmux. PickPlace data generation reached 184/2000, but it was terminated due to the expiration of the Slurm interactive job. threading_d1 was temporarily replaced with only D0 due to the unavailable HuggingFace proxy (with a TODO comment).
MIHD Spatial Transcriptomics ✅ Fair comparison of STAIG hyperparameters during parameter scanning: Identified naming misunderstandings and inconsistencies in gene input 14:40:59.838 | claude_code Users noticed that the difference between the baseline ARI (0.23) and clustering ablation (0.58) was unusually large. AI confirmed by reading logs that pca_uni2_staig_fusion actually used UNI instead of UNI2, and the ablation script used raw HVG instead of PCA input. After modifying the script to support --gene_encoder and --vision_variant parameters, pseudo_k sweeps were run with PCA+UNI and PCA+UNI2. Both had an ARI average of approximately 0.47, establishing a fair baseline.
claude-demo (Visium Analysis) ✅ Development and visualization fixes for the 10x Visium 151676 sample scanpy analysis script 14:55:05.867 | claude_code Project initialization was completed (creating CLAUDE.md and fixing incorrect symlinks). A complete scanpy analysis script was developed for 151676 Visium samples (data loading, QC, normalization, HVG, PCA/UMAP, spatial visualization). It was found that spatial_gene_expression.png had no gene points after the H&E background. All sc.pl.* files were saved using the native scanpy save parameters instead of plt.savefig. All 6 images were generated correctly.
openpi VLA ✅ Inquiry about Docker image building and saving methods for the openpi project 04:50:34.000 | claude_code The documentation in openpi/docs/docker.md, serve_policy.Dockerfile, and compose.yml were read to understand the purpose of 5 Dockerfiles and their corresponding build commands, as well as the complete export and import processes for docker save/docker load.
