title: Haiyong Wang — 研究者分析报告

content:
Problems and Solutions### Key Issues 1. Fundamental flaws in MIHD’s cross-sample embedding methodology: AI concludes that “PCA is worse than STAIG” based on the wrong assumption that “input features themselves are weak,” without actively questioning the validity of the experimental design. Solution: After users questioned the effectiveness of cosine similarity, it was discovered through import tracing that both PCA and STAIG exhibit double-incomparability due to independent HVG selection per-section and independent PCA fitting. Comparing the two is ineffective; instead, using the raw_shared scheme with shared HVG intersection (1137 genes) as the correct baseline is recommended.
Key Insight: The effectiveness of cross-sample embedding comparisons depends on shared feature spaces. Embeddings fitted independently per-section have no mathematical meaning in terms of cross-sectional cosine similarity, regardless of the model used.
2. Drop skill objects do not actually fall: After opening the gripper and setting the object’s initial velocity, the OSC controller in env.step() re-applies clamping force to “pull” the object back. Solution: After opening the gripper and setting the object’s initial velocity, call mujoco.mj_step() to run 15 physical steps (completely bypassing the OSC controller) to complete the initial separation before entering the standard control loop.
Key Insight: sim.forward() only updates the kinematic state without advancing the dynamics. Only mujoco.mj_step() actually advances the MuJoCo physics engine, thereby bypassing all higher-level controllers. There is a fundamental conflict between direct state operations and feedback controllers. Simulation injection design must clearly choose one path.
3. Stack task body name parsing fails silently: stack.yaml uses cubeA/cubeB, while MuJoCo’s actual names are cubeA_main/cubeB_main. The _sim_body_name2id returns -1, and Python negative index body_xpos[-1] reads the last body. Task phase detection misjudges all as pre_reach. Solution: Fix the body_name field in stack.yaml. Add a fallback logic with the main/_body0 suffix in env_wrapper._sim_body_name2id. Output a WARNING instead of silently returning -1 when a failure occurs.
Key Insight: The negative index of body_xpos[-1] always returns the same position in both cubes. This silent error is extremely difficult to detect. Any parsing failure should trigger an alarm rather than returning a sentinel value.
4. VLA context replay architecture assumption error: AI designed a complete N-1 frame replay mechanism, believing it was necessary for providing correct observation history for VLA. At the same time, multiple data sources (demo+VLA rollout+BC rollout) were designed, ignoring the differences in controllability of each source. Solution: Users pointed out that most VLAs do not have a context window, and context replay is a useless overhead. Reconstruct ContextReplayEngine into InjectionEngine to directly restore the injected frame sim state. Limit data sources to MimicGen demo data (higher controllability).
Key Insight: The general benchmark should be efficient for models without context (BC-RNN, ACT, etc.). It cannot be over-engineered only for a few VLAs that support historical inputs. Users have a better understanding of the actual model range than AI’s theoretical inferences.
5. CalendarPro intent misclassification: Semantic routing has no confidence threshold (0.52 is also considered a valid classification). Sentences with time expressions are routed incorrectly due to keyword matching errors. The short confirmation word ‘ok’ lacks context understanding. The system prompt is too long (530 lines), and the estimation of Chinese tokens is three times off. Solution: Add a per-routing threshold (0.40-0.60). Cases below the threshold are handed over to the LLM. Introduce a keyword scorer (time regular boost schedule) combined with an embedding of 70/30 ratio. Split SYSTEM_PROMPT into BASE (~50 lines) and 11 intent-specific fragments for injection as needed. Change the estimation of Chinese tokens to chinese_chars × 1.5 + other_chars / 4.
Key Insight: Embedding’s nearest-neighbor routing lacks the ability to express “I’m not sure.” Keyword scoring precisely compensates for the embedding’s blind spot in dealing with time/digital expressions. The two complement each other. Chinese character tokens account for about six times that of English characters. Without correction, the context length will be systematically underestimated.
6. S2 co-author analysis is completely ineffective for top researchers like Levine/Abbeel/Finn (all depth-2 sections are empty), and there is a serious issue of name ambiguity (Xiaolong Wang matches veterinary/geological researchers, Shuran Song has only 2 papers in 2025). Solution: Reconstruct it as a homepage-first strategy: Prioritize extracting student lists from researchers’ homepage/laboratory pages. Co-authorship should be used as a supplement. Use multiple strategies for URL discovery (S2 homepage field + LLM suggestions + –homepage parameter). Warn of name ambiguity and suggest using S2 authorId for precise querying.
Key Insight: Academic homepages explicitly list students, which is a level one order of reliability compared to inferring from co-authorship relationships. Top researchers publish over 500 papers, and the first-author signal is diluted by many collaborators. There are fundamental applicability issues with co-author analysis methodology.
7. Research toolchain fragmentation: The functions of paper scout and researcher profiler overlap, and commands are scattered. There is complete duplicate code in New feature/directory. The dimension of citation relationships is missing in the toolchain. Solution: Use research_scout.py as a unified CLI entry point. Import the modular profiler as a library through lazy loading. Add a new profile/citations subcommand. Remove the New feature/directory and add the Semantic Scholar citation graph API. The three-stage report automatically runs a citation analysis on top 5 papers.
Key Insight: Retain the original CLI entry point and integrate new modules through lazy loading, rather than completely rewriting them. This ensures backward compatibility. Citation relationships (forward citations + backward references) are a core feature that is underestimated in the research toolchain.
8. Demo video scripts using action replay lead to cumulative open-loop errors in later frames. Phase detection in later frames is completely incorrect. Solution: Use set_sim_state() to directly restore the MuJoCo state vector for each frame, bypassing the accumulation of open-loop errors.
Key Insight: The stored clean trajectory contains a complete sim state vector. Restoring the state directly is much more accurate than replaying actions. Action replay is suitable for real-time control, while state restoration is suitable for offline analysis.
9. trajectory_regression cannot find any injection opportunities: can_inject() requires prev_phases to be at least 10 in length, but the pipeline never calls replay_and_label_phases(), and phase_labels is always None. Solution: Pass the –label_phases flag by default in Step 0 of run_v5_all_tasks.py.
Key Insight: Implicit dependencies (certain skills require a specific step of the pipeline to be explicitly activated) can only be exposed when that skill fails to run. End-to-end integration testing is more effective at detecting such pipeline-level defects than unit testing.
10. pi05 training error: The CheckpointWeightLoader structure does not match. The new progress layer (progress_mlp_in/out/cond_proj) is not in the checkpoint and does not match the hard-coded ‘..lora.’ regular expression. Solution: Add a configurable missing_regex field to CheckPointWeightLoader (defaulting to ‘..lora.’ for backward compatibility). Change the 4 progress experiment configurations to ‘..lora.|.progress.’.
Key Insight: Loading pre-trained weights with a new experimental module is a high-frequency scenario. The missing_regex should be designed as a configurable parameter. This is exactly the same requirement as LoRA fine-tuning, which is a common design pattern for transfer learning.
11. Aux_targets shape assumption error in pi0.py: AI inferred that LeRobot would maintain the shape of (b,1) after loading the shape of (1,) and modified the code accordingly. In reality, LeRobot will squeeze it to be a scalar (b,), resulting in a shape mismatch during training. Solution: Run the training and observe the log (‘aux_targets[…]: (32,)@float32’) to confirm the actual shape. Restore the original original[:, None] and jnp.stack operation.
Key Insight: LeRobot automatically squeezes the scalar feature of shape=(1,?) during DataLoader processing. This is a framework-level behavior. Hypotheses about the internal behavior of third-party frameworks must be verified through actual running and cannot be purely inferred.
General Issues 12. adata_hvg cache bug: The HVG AnnData var_names in section 151673 were reset to integer indices (‘0’,‘1’,‘2’…), resulting in a zero intersection of gene names. Solution: Abandon reliance on the adata_hvg cache and load directly from the original HDF5 data (via load_dlpfc_data). Manually perform normalization and HVG selection.
Key Insight: Silent transformations may occur when writing key fields in cached data. A soundness check should be performed before use (e.g., whether var_names are gene symbols rather than integers).
13. When the LLM generates Chinese long-form JSON, Chinese quotation marks ("") mix into the JSON string values, leading to parsing failures. This issue is repeated in the profiles of many researchers such as Chelsea Finn, Yuke Zhu, and Mingyu Ding. Solution: Resubmit the malformed JSON to Claude, requesting only a corrected pure JSON. Automate processing through the repair_json_with_llm mechanism. Plan to explicitly require English quotation marks in the prompt as a long-term fix.
Key Insight: Generating JSON containing Chinese content should explicitly require English quotation marks in the prompt. Alternatively, perform format verification immediately after generation and fix the JSON as a fixed pipeline step, which is more reliable than relying on generation quality.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Methodological questioning of cross-sample cosine similarity effectiveness (MIHD) Role Thought Human After AI concludes that “PCA is worse → input features themselves are weak,” humans instinctively ask, “Could it be that the cos similarity of cross-sample embedding is problematic?” This directly points to methodological flaws rather than numerical results. AI AI tends to attribute problems directly from experimental numbers and does not actively question the validity of the experimental design itself (whether the feature space is comparable). Difference Analysis: Humans have a prior ability to doubt methodologies and can ask, “Is the experiment designed correctly?” when they see abnormal results. AI is better at analyzing data under given assumptions, but requires external triggering for reflection on the assumptions themselves.
Necessity of VLA context window and design of error scene data sources Role Thought Human Users actively point out that most VLAs do not have a context window. Context replay is a useless overhead. They also explicitly require using only MimicGen demo data and prohibit the use of VLA/BC-RNN rollout data (randomness is uncontrollable). AI AI designs a complete N-1 frame replay mechanism as a necessary step. It also plans to retain multiple data sources, believing diversity to be beneficial. Both lack understanding of the actual model range and data controllability. Difference Analysis: Users identify over-engineering based on the characteristics of the actual models and data controllability. AI starts from theoretical correctness and requires user practical experience to correct it.
Semantic splitting of E2 and design of CalendarPro optimization plan Role Thought Human Users actively split E2 into three independent skills according to semantic differences in the recovery strategy. Similarly, they independently diagnose problems (analyzing the four root causes of misclassification) and design 7-stage specifications for CalendarPro, using the complete plan as input. AI In the benchmark, AI parameterizes different situations with the same injector. It does not actively propose semantic subdivision. In CalendarPro, it mainly plays a role in implementation and verification. Difference Analysis: The most valuable design work (semantic classification, scheme design) is completed by humans. AI contributes value in parallel execution and handling boundary cases. User domain experience is irreplaceable, and AI’s parallel execution ability significantly accelerates implementation.
Citation relationships as the core feature of the research toolchain Role Thought Human Users actively point out that the citation links between papers are very important. High citations represent popular directions. It is necessary to analyze “why they are popular” and “what has been done subsequently.” At the same time, it is clear that the number of citations is suitable for sorting but should not affect the relevance score. AI Initially, AI focuses on integrating the functions of two tools into one (unifying the CLI). It regards citation function as an optional extension and does not actively make the citation graph a core feature. It needs to wait for user decision-making regarding the separation of evaluation. Difference Analysis: Users have a clearer research methodology perspective—citing relationships are a core tool for understanding the evolution of research influence, rather than metadata. “Relevance” and “popularity” are different dimensions. Humans are more aware of their different uses in the research workflow.
Student discovery strategy: Debugging S2 co-author logic vs using professor’s homepage Role Thought Human Users directly propose not to debug the existing co-author analysis logic but to directly extract student lists from professors’ personal homepages because homepage information is more direct and authoritative. AI After failing in depth-2, AI starts to deeply debug the scoring logic of student_discovery.py and the threshold settings. It tries to fix it within the existing framework. Difference Analysis: AI tends to find bugs or adjust parameters within the existing scheme. Users can identify the applicability boundaries of methodologies more quickly and propose more efficient alternative paths, bypassing the fundamental limitations of S2 data quality.
Identification of coffee machine component disassembly issues Role Thought Human By visually observing screenshots, users can identify specific phenomena such as lid floating and base deviation. They directly propose three diagnostic directions: missing joint definitions, coordinate deviation, and loading logic errors. AI AI only focuses on fixing contact parameters (margin/solimp). It does not actively check whether the model’s kinematic tree is correctly assembled. Difference Analysis: Humans can identify new problems that AI has not actively discovered through visual intuition and provide a higher-level structural diagnostic framework. AI’s fixes only address “contact being too soft,” but do not solve the problem of “components not connected together.”
Delegation mode for VLA training debugging Role Thought Human Adopt a goal-driven delegation strategy: “Execute the training command yourself, fix all errors until no more errors occur, and then stop.” This gives clear termination conditions without interfering with specific steps. AI AI follows a scientific method: run → observe errors → read source code to locate the problem → make minimal changes → re-run. However, it makes an error in the shape assumption of LeRobot and needs actual running logs to correct it. Difference Analysis: Human delegation mode allows AI to debug independently. The wrong shape assumption is exposed through natural running. It is correct for humans to choose not to intervene in specific decisions—the error correction mechanism is built into the iterative loop.
AI Limitations Important Limitations Insufficient ability to reflect on experimental conclusions: After conducting the MIHD PCA diagnostic experiment, it directly draws wrong conclusions from surface numerical results without actively checking the validity of the experimental design itself (the comparability issue of independent per-section PCA). It requires external intervention by users to be corrected. Silent failure modes lead to serious bugs being hidden for a long time: When parsing body names in stack.yaml fails, it silently returns -1. No warnings or assertions are designed, resulting in Python negative index bugs being completely invisible. Similar problems (integerization of var_names in adata_hvg cache) are also not discovered due to lack of soundness checks. Over-engineering and wrong architectural assumptions: V5 context replay is over-designed based on the wrong assumption that “all VLAs require a context window.” It makes incorrect assumptions about the internal behavior of third-party frameworks (LeRobot), assuming that shape=(1,) features will be squeezed, and modifies the code accordingly. Both need to be corrected by users or verified through actual running. Insufficient ability to actively question the applicability boundaries of methodologies: When S2 student discovery is completely ineffective for top researchers, AI continues to deeply debug the code logic (reading student_discovery.py, analyzing threshold settings), without actively questioning the applicability boundaries of the methodology itself. It requires user guidance to switch to the homepage solution. Insufficient entity disambiguation ability in Semantic Scholar: For common Chinese-English translations such as Xiaolong Wang, Shuran Song, and Ming Yu, it almost always matches the wrong researchers. The LLM analysis cannot automatically identify “this is not the same person,” and can only issue warnings afterward. It lacks the ability to actively disambiguate entities. General Limitations## Today’s Achievements Key Achievements Necessary prerequisite for cross-sample embedding comparison: The feature spaces must be shared. Independent HVG selection per section + independent PCA fitting results in double irreliability; effective cross-sample baselines must use the intersection of shared HVGs + joint PCA, or a foundation model with fixed pre-trained weights. Standard method to bypass the upper-layer controller in MuJoCo: It is necessary to directly call mujoco.mj_step() (propagation dynamics) instead of sim.forward() (only updating kinematics), so as to complete the physical state change before the OSC controller intervenes. There is a fundamental conflict between direct state operations and feedback controllers. The simulation injection design must clearly choose one path. Composite objects generated by MuJoCo’s CompositeBodyObject usually have a _main suffix in their body names (e.g., cubeA_main instead of cubeA). The body name parsing function of the env wrapper needs to include a backtracking logic for multiple candidate names ({name}→{name}_main→{name}_body0). When searching fails, it should output WARNING instead of silently returning -1. The semantic split of error types should be based on ‘whether the recovery strategy is the same’ rather than ‘whether the injection mechanism is the same’: The detection conditions and recovery logic for drop_in_transit/drop_at_wrong_place/drop_with_interaction are completely different. Even if the injection actions are the same, they must be modeled separately. This is more meaningful for curriculum design during the training phase. Architectural defect of the Semantic Router: The nearest neighbor always produces one result, unable to express ‘I’m not sure’. A combination of confidence thresholds + alternative LLMs + keyword scorers is the most practical repair pattern and can be applied to all vector retrieval-based classification systems (RAG routing, tool selection, etc.). For top scholars who have published over 500 papers, the analysis of S2 co-author frequencies cannot reliably identify students—the first-author signal is diluted by a large number of collaborators. Listing students explicitly on the professor’s homepage is an order of magnitude more reliable than inferring reliability from collaborative relationships. This is the correct method for discovering students of top scholars. Citation graphs (forward citations + backward references) are undervalued core features in the research toolchain: By analyzing ‘who cited this paper’, we can understand the evolution of research influence and popular follow directions; in the scoring system, ‘relevance’ and ’number of citations/popularity’ should be handled separately—citing count is used for ranking rather than scoring, preventing papers with high citations but low relevance from interfering with project direction filtering. Mentor pedigree of embodied AI scholars: Mingyu Ding ← Jitendra Malik (Berkeley), Ruoshi Liu ← Carl Vondrick (Columbia), Xiaolong Wang ← Abhinav Gupta (CMU), Shuran Song ← Thomas Funkhouser (Princeton), Yunzhu Li ← Antonio Torralba (MIT), Yuke Zhu ← Li Fei-Fei (Stanford) — presenting a systematic student output pattern from top perception/robotics mentors to embodied AI. In offline trajectory analysis, directly using set_sim_state() to restore the complete state vector for each frame is much more accurate and reliable than action replay, completely avoiding the accumulation of open-loop errors. Saving clean trajectories with states at the same time is a correct design decision. The LeRobot dataset framework automatically squeezes single scalar features of shape=(1,) into (batch_size, ) when using DataLoader. In the model code, it is necessary to explicitly raise the dimension using[:, None]. When storing data, use np.float32(scalar) instead of np.array([scalar]). This is a framework-level behavior that must be verified through actual logs rather than inferred. On-demand injection strategy in prompt engineering: Splitting the system prompt into a base (always included) + intent-specific fragment (dynamically injected based on classification results) can reduce token consumption by 40-60%. The token density of Chinese characters is about 6 times that of English characters (1.5 tokens/character vs 0.25 tokens/character). Without correction, the context length will be systematically underestimated. The hack of sys.path.insert is a fragile cross-module dependency method: Any function renaming will cause a ImportError at runtime. The correct approach is to use common/packages + pip install -e..; the Python re-export shim pattern (the module only contains from x import y; all=[…]) is an elegant migration method that maintains backward compatibility. In multi-tool coexistence projects, organizing output directories ‘by file type priority’ rather than ‘by tool name priority’ (outputs/reports/summarize/ instead of summarize/reports/) can reduce the.gitignore from multiple scattered rules to a single line outputs/, making it more friendly for CI/CD and disk quota management. Verifying the feasibility of the simulation pipeline in small batches (100 total) is the correct iterative strategy: 207 scenarios exposed 5 systematic defects. If you directly rush to 2900 scenarios, you will waste a lot of GPU time on injections that are doomed to fail. End-to-end integration testing can discover implicit dependency defects at the pipeline level better than unit testing. Flow matching is becoming the mainstream action decoding architecture for robot VLA: Models like π₀ converge to a combination of ‘pre-trained VLMs and flow-matching action heads’, which outperforms diffusion models in multi-modal modeling of continuous high-dimensional action spaces. Shuran Song’s Im2Flow2Act and UMI are two major breakthroughs in the field of robot data efficiency in 2024. Session Summary MIHD 🔄 MIHD Cross-sample Embedding Comparability Diagnosis: PCA Baseline Implementation, Per-Section Double Irreliability Root Cause Identification, Raw Shared Scheme Design 19:33:45.000 | claude_code Starting from confirming the project status (current best ARI=0.546, PCA+UNI2+STAIG), three-mode support for –embedding_source was implemented. PCA analysis showed that all 14 combinations of SL@50 were zero. After the preliminary AI error conclusion was questioned by users, tracking through the import chain revealed that both PCA and STAIG had per-section independent processing defects (double irreliability). It was found that there was a bug in the data_adata_hvg cache regarding integerization of gene names. Finally, the raw_shared scheme was adopted to load HVG intersections from the original HDF5 file (1137 genes) was implemented and is being diagnosed. At the same time, it was revealed that STAIG had layer-specific phenomena where SL@50=0.94-1.0 at Layer_1/Layer_5 and all intermediate layers failed.
ErrorRecoveryBenchmark ✅ v4 Archive, E2 Semantic Split, v5.1 Architecture Planning, 5 Skill Fixes, D0 Scenario Generation, Three Bug Fixes, Stack Demo Video, Root Cause Diagnosis of Failures 20:20:54.000 | claude_code Major progress was made in the benchmark framework throughout the day: ①v4 code was fully archived in archive/v4/ (19 framework modules, policy_adapter cross-dependency fixed), 94 v5 tests passed ②E2 drop was split into drop_in_transit/drop_at_wrong_place/drop_with_interaction according to semantics (13 skills/29 subtypes, 105 tests passed) ③v5.1 technical planning completed (removed context replay, speed limit, human example collection, training goals clear before April 1st) ④Fixed 5 failed skill errors (mujoco.mj_step() bypassing OSC controller/gripper steps/phase tags/target_object transfer chain), generated 231 scenarios and 231 MP4s in an53 ⑤Three bug fixes (coffee contact parameters/render_fn passing 17 files/output path cleanup), and a coffee demo video was generated to verify the effect ⑥The first round of D0 generated 207 scenarios exposing 5 systematic failure roots ⑦Stack body name bugs fixed + 11 demo videos generated (action replay changed to state restore)
VLA-RoboTwin ✅ Progress Prediction Experiment Training Pipeline Full-Link Debugging and Verification 01:40:13.000 | claude_code Continuing from the previous session, the shape adaptation of five progress fields in the HDF5→LeRobot format conversion script was completed. Three independent problems were fixed: CheckpointWeightLoader’s missing_regex did not support the progress layer (a configurable field was added), an error occurred in aux_targets shape processing in pi0.py (it was found through actual logs that LeRobot would squeeze(1,) to a scalar, restoring[:, None]), and the action_loss/aux_loss log was invisible (has_aux=True+logging.info). Verification showed that at step 100, action_loss=0.37, aux_loss=0.22, and the loss curve decreased normally. All four experiment configurations were ready.
UniVLA 🔄 CALVIN Data Format Research and Evaluation Script Container Single-Card Compatibility Fixes 12:34:04.000 | claude_code Clarified the data usage differences between training scripts (DiskCalvinDataset directly reads CALVIN npz format) and evaluation scripts (through calvin_env online rollout, using only validation/initiation scenarios). Fixed multiple problems in run_calvin_eval_ddp.py: Added a –single_gpu mode to bypass Kubernetes container IPv6 DNS resolution issues, fixed GenerateConfig missing window_size field, MAPBloc spelling errors, braceexpand missing dependencies, evaluate_policy hardcoding others’ absolute paths, and adjusted the GIF frame rate to 120fps. The script can now be started, and debugging is still ongoing.
CalendarPro ✅ Open Source Ecosystem Research + 7-Stage Optimization Plan Design + Full Implementation (230 Tests Passed) 21:29:45.000 | claude_code Three-phase work: ①Review of CLAUDE.md conclusions was accurate and comprehensive, no modification needed ②Online search revealed a lack of complete implementations for energy-aware scheduling + integrated life management in the open source ecosystem. Using 4 real misclassification records as root evidence, a 7-stage comprehensive optimization plan was designed ③Parallel sub-agents were fully implemented for Phases 1-7 (semantic routing confidence threshold + mixed routing, prompt simplification + Chinese token correction, Provider retry, scheduling scoring configuration, automatic threshold tuning, ThoughtStore cache optimization), all 4 misclassification scenarios were fixed, and 230 tests passed entirely.
gadget/Research Toolchain Architecture ✅ common/Package Extraction Reconstruction, Outputs/Unaligned Consolidation, Comprehensive Updates to CLAUDE.md/README.md/TUTORIAL.md, MCP Server Bug Fixes 21:11:57.000 | claude_code Two major architectural reconstructions: ①Implementation of common/packages (6 modules), simplifying summarize/llm_backends.py from 516 lines to 25 lines re-export shim, eliminating the sys.path hack in research_scout.py, approximately 400 lines of repetitive LLM/IO code were unified and extracted ②Consolidated output directories for each tool into outputs/{reports,logs,cache,data}/, created common/paths.py, modified over 10 files, .gitignore simplified to a single line outputs/ ③Fixed the MCP server’s old function name load_known_arxiv_ids → _load_known_paper_ids ④Multiple rounds of updates to CLAUDE.md, README.md, TUTORIAL.md (expanded from 10 chapters to 13 full-chinese chapters)
gadget/Research Profiler ✅ Homepage Student Discovery Implementation, New Research Section in Hugo, CLI Integration (Citation Graph + Three Backends LLMs), Batch-depth Images of 7 Embodied AI Scholars 20:53:14.000 | claude_code Four core tasks: ①Implementation of a new module in homepage_discovery.py, refactoring discover_students into a four-stage strategy for homepage-first, modifying 9 files to solve the problem that S2 co-author analysis was completely ineffective for top scholars ②Hugo added a research section, separating scholar portraits from bugJournal, implementing deploy_to_hugo(), adding –deploy parameter ③Using research_scout.py as a unified CLI entry point, removing duplicate directories for new features, adding semantic_scholar citation graph API (get_paper_by_id/citations/references), adding profile/citations sub-command, llm.py supports three backends, three-stage reports automatically run top-5 paper citation analysis ④Batch analysis of Mingyu Ding/Ruoshi Liu/Xiaolong Wang/Shuran Song/Yunzhu Li/Yuke Zhu/Chelsea Finn/Sergey Levine/Pieter Abbeel, identifying complete mentor relationships networks, completing VIN/TrajOpt/MineDojo award recognition; warning and giving suggestions for de-duplication for S2 homonym歧义(Xiaolong Wang等)

title: バグレポート 2026-03-15

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-14 Today’s Highlights What was done: Improved the interface of the GPU monitoring tool nvitop, implemented batch visualization of HDF5 camera data as MP4 videos, and designed and implemented the architecture for four sets of experiments using the pi05 model to predict operation progress. How it was done: Optimized the monitoring tool with a terminal buffer and adaptive column width settings. Decoded JPEG frames in batches using OpenCV to create 2×2 grids and write them into MP4 videos. Added auxiliary MLP prediction headers, stop_gradient isolation strategies, and experimental configuration switches across six files under the JAX/Flax NNX framework. What’s achieved: The GPU monitoring tool has been improved to the level of nvitop; 50 demonstration videos have been generated for data quality inspection; the four sets of experiments using pi05 are ready, and only the lerobot data format conversion is needed to start training. Improved the interaction experience of the GPU monitoring tool, completed visualization of four cameras for 50 robot demonstration data, and designed an experimental framework for four sets of operations progress prediction tasks in the pi05 VLA model.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy 🔄 Implementation of four sets of experiments using pi05 to predict operation progress — Implement auxiliary prediction headers for manip_progress_time/distance in the pi05 model (four sets of experiments: last_token vs special_token × time vs distance). Modify six files: pi0_config.py, model.py, tokenizer.py, robotwin_policy.py, config.py, and pi0.py. Add ProgressConfig configuration switches and four one-click experiment configurations. Training was delayed due to data not being converted to lerobot format. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Batch visualization script for HDF5 camera data — Created script/visualize_hdf5_cameras.py to read JPEG frames from front/head/left/right cameras in 50 episodes under place_dual_shoes/demo_clean/data. Written these frames into 2×2 labeled grids and saved them as MP4 videos at 640×480@30FPS. All 50 videos (about 2.3MB each) were successfully generated. ✅ gpumon.py: Buffer and adaptive layout — Added a nvitop-style buffer for the GPU monitoring script (\033[?1049h to switch to a separate screen, and Ctrl+C to return to normal). Adjusted the GPU and process tables to adapt to the actual COLUMNS/LINES of the terminal. Fixed an issue where os.get_terminal_size() couldn’t read COLUMNS in child processes. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Training failure for pi05: Missing fields like manip_progress_time, manip_progress_distance_left/right, target_endpose, and target_joint in the dataset. Solution: Need to modify ~/HDD_POOL/mozihao/VLA/convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py to add these fields before converting the dataset again. Key insight: Before designing the training code, it’s important to ensure that the data pipeline supports all required fields to avoid discovering missing data after the code is written.
General Issues 2. os.get_terminal_size() cannot read COLUMNS/LINES in pipeline/subprocess environments, resulting in tables not being fully expanded during 120-column tests. Solution: Modify _get_term_size() to prioritize reading COLUMNS/LINES from the environment variables. If this fails, revert to os.get_terminal_size(). Key insight: Terminal width detection needs to handle both real tty and environment variable scenarios.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Scientific design for four sets of experiments using pi05 Role Thoughts Human Humans proposed a complete set of comparative experiments: two types of feature extraction methods (last_token vs special_token), two types of prediction targets (time vs distance), and the mechanism to inject predicted results as conditioning tokens into the action expert. AI After receiving the experimental design, AI analyzed the feasibility of the architecture and proposed engineering details such as MLP size (2048→256→out), loss weight λ=0.1, stop_gradient strategy, and configuration switches. Difference analysis: Human-led scientific hypotheses and experimental designs were used by humans, while AI was responsible for architectural analysis and engineering implementation. Humans contributed more to the core research direction.
Implementation Level Interface specifications of the GPU monitoring tool Role Thoughts Human Humans specified the interaction style similar to nvitop (restoring the command window after exit) and specific requirements for adaptive width and height. AI AI implemented a buffer mechanism, but the table width didn’t change according to the terminal during the initial implementation. Issues were discovered during debugging. Difference analysis: Humans had clear expectations for the UX experience, while AI had omissions in implementing details (environment variables vs tty). User testing was needed to address these issues.
AI Limitations Major Limitations Before implementing the pi05 training code, no verification was done to ensure that the data pipeline was complete (fields like manip_progress were written into the lerobot dataset). This led to discovering missing data during training after the code was implemented, wasting engineering time. General Limitations gpumon.py’s initial adaptive implementation overlooked an issue where os.get_terminal_size() couldn’t read COLUMNS in child processes. This issue was discovered and fixed through user testing. Today’s Achievements Core Achievements Using stop_gradient to isolate the gradients of the main task and auxiliary tasks in VLA is a safe starting point—ensuring that action prediction isn’t interfered with first. If the results aren’t good, then compare the gradients. When adding auxiliary task headers in JAX/Flax NNX, use GT values for teacher forcing during training and predict values for injection during inference. Both paths need to be implemented separately in compute_loss and sample_actions while maintaining consistent interfaces. Practical Achievements The terminal buffer (\033[?1049h to enter, \033[?1049l to exit) combined with signal.SIGINT can achieve a full-screen UI similar to nvitop. It automatically restores the original terminal content after exiting. Session Summary RoboTwin GPU Monitor ✅ gpumon.py added a nvitop-style buffer and adaptive terminal layout 09:24:23.170 | claude_code Users requested that the GPU monitoring script be transformed into an nvitop-style interface: switching to a separate screen when starting, restoring it after exiting, and changing from fixed column width to adaptive one. AI implemented the \033[?1049h buffer mechanism, fixed the issue where os.get_terminal_size() was ineffective in pipeline environments, and adjusted the width of the GPU/process tables proportionally. Tests with 80 columns and 120 columns were successful, and the number of rows in the process table was dynamically truncated according to the terminal height.
RoboTwin HDF5 Visualization ✅ Implemented a batch visualization script for HDF5→MP4 and successfully processed all 50 episodes 13:21:34.636 | claude_code Users submitted a planning document requesting this. AI created script/visualize_hdf5_cameras.py, using cv2.imdecode to decode JPEG frames and assemble them into 2×2 labeled grids, which were then written as MP4 videos at 640×480@30FPS. The script successfully processed all 50 episodes, generating 50 video files of about 2.3MB each. Both the number and size of the files were verified to be correct.
🔄 Planning for HDF5 camera visualization (exploring Plan Mode) 13:18:03.922 | claude_code Users re-initiated the visualization request. AI used Explore agent to analyze the data collection pipeline and HDF5 file structure, confirming the format of JPEG-encoded camera data for 50 episodes (240×320, four cameras). It read the existing parse_hdf5.py tool to understand the decoding mode and finally generated a detailed implementation plan (2×2 grid, 640×480@30FPS MP4) before exiting Plan Mode.
❌ HDF5 camera visualization request interrupted due to 403 Forbidden error 13:16:50.918 | claude_code Users requested a script for visualizing HDF5 camera data. AI encountered a 403 Request not allowed error when trying to read the file for the first time (session token was invalid and required relogging in). The session was immediately interrupted, and users then re-initiated the same request in a new session.
RoboTwin pi05 VLA 🔄 Discussion on the architecture of four sets of experiments using pi05 to predict operation progress and implementation across six files 14:21:07.908 | claude_code Users proposed four sets of comparative experiments (last_token vs special_token × manip_progress_time vs distance_left/right). AI analyzed the JAX/Flax NNX architecture and proposed engineering details such as MLP size (2048→256), loss weight λ=0.1, stop_gradient strategy, and configuration switches. All were confirmed by users. AI completed implementation across six files, including ProgressConfig configuration classes, special token registration, Observation.aux_targets field, auxiliary loss calculation, and four one-click experiment entrances. Importing and configuration verification tests all passed. Training was started with issues where the dataset lacked progress-related fields, requiring modification of the lerobot conversion script first.

Additionally, we have designed and implemented an experimental framework for predicting operational progress in four scenarios within the pi05 VLA model.
title: 缺陷记录表 2026-03-14

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-13 Today’s Overview What was done: Added five per-frame variables such as operation progress and target pose in the RoboTwin simulation platform Place Dual Shoes task, and removed critical_region. How it was done: Adopted a post-processing approach: obtained the end-state from the simulator after each move() execution, and retrieved the pickle file for that frame to avoid issues with knowing future states during online collection. What’s useful: Provided richer supervisory signals such as operation progress and target end pose for VLA model training, thereby improving dataset quality and model learning capabilities. Added five new per-frame data variables for the RoboTwin Place Dual Shoes robot task, and fixed two data collection quality bugs.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Design a new variable data collection architecture — After analyzing the code library, determined the post-processing approach: since target_endpose/target_joint requires the end-state at the end of move(), we used the method of retrieving the patch pickle file after each move() execution. The general recursive design of pkl2hdf5.py does not need to be modified. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Implement target_endpose and target_joint variables — Retrieved the end-state of both handenders’ poses and joint states from the simulator after each move(), and used these as the target state for that move, written into all corresponding frames. ✅ Implement manip_progress_distance_left/right variables — Calculated the operation progress of both hands based on formula 1 - |current-final| / |start-final|, and clamped it to [0,1] to avoid out-of-range values caused by curved paths. ✅ Implement manip_progress_time variable — Generated a linear interpolation time progression variable from 0 to 1 during each move(), setting it to 0 at the start of the move and 1 at the end. ✅ Remove critical_region variable — Finally, explicitly pop(‘critical_region’, None) was used during the pickle patch phase to remove this field. Simply removing the subclass override was not enough because the base class get_obs()无条件 writes this field. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. Variables like target_endpose/target_joint require the end-state obtained at the end of move(), but this cannot be known during frame collection. Solution: Adopted a post-processing architecture: retrieved the end-state from the simulator after each move() execution, and retrieved the pickle file for all frames during that move.
Key Insight: Frame-level data collection and action execution are in a pipeline relationship. Variables that depend on future states must be post-processed rather than collected online. The general recursive design of pkl2hdf5.py is naturally compatible with the new keys and does not need to be modified.
General Problems 2. After removing the subclass get_critical_region_label() override, the critical_region field still appears in HDF5. Solution: Explicitly called pkl_data.pop(‘critical_region’, None) during the pickle patch phase of move() to remove this field.
Key Insight: The base class _base_task.py’s get_obs() calls get_critical_region_label() and writes it unconditionally at line 510. Whether the subclass method is overridden or not does not affect the appearance of this field. It must be removed actively after data writing.
3. manip_progress_distance_left/right shows negative values in some frames. Solution: Used np.clip to force the calculation result to be within [0.0, 1.0].
Key Insight: When the robot end-effector moves along a curved path, the distance between intermediate frames and the final point may be greater than that between the starting frame and the final point, resulting in negative values in the progress formula. The linear progress indicator based on Euclidean distance has fundamental limitations when dealing with non-linear paths.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Semantic Definition of New Variables and Calculation Formulas Role Thinking Human Humans clearly specified the names, semantics, and precise calculation formulas for 5 variables, including the formula for manip_progress_distance as 1 - AI AI was responsible for technical implementation, identified the ‘future knowledge’ problem, proposed a post-processing architecture, and analyzed the versatility of pkl2hdf5.py to determine the minimum modification scope (only one file needs to be modified). Difference Analysis: The semantics of variables and formulas were completely designed by humans. AI’s contribution was in selecting the architecture and engineering implementation. AI predicted the implementation obstacles and found elegant ways to bypass them.
Implementation Level Discovered a residual bug with critical_region Role Thinking Human Humans discovered that critical_region still exists through actual data collection and checking HDF5 files. AI AI located the root cause (line 510 of get_obs()) by analyzing the base class source code, and provided a definite solution. Difference Analysis: Humans discovered problems through experimental verification. AI found the root cause through code analysis. At the initial design stage, AI did not realize that the base class wrote this field unconditionally.
Discovered a bug where manip_progress_distance shows negative values Role Thinking Human Humans observed abnormal negative values by examining the actual collected data. AI AI explained the geometric reasons for negative values caused by curved paths and proposed a clamping solution. Difference Analysis: Humans discovered boundary situations through data analysis. AI provided theoretical explanations. AI missed analyzing boundary situations for non-linear paths during the initial design stage.
AI Limitations General Limitations Did not anticipate that the base class get_obs() would write critical_region unconditionally. Mistakenly thought that removing the subclass override would remove this field, resulting in a secondary fix. Did not consider the boundary situation where intermediate frame distances exceed the starting distance due to curved paths when designing the manip_progress_distance calculation formula. Missing the [0,1] clamping treatment. Today’s Gains Core Gains When data variables depend on the end-state after the action sequence is completed, post-processing (retrieving pickle files) is a more reliable architecture choice than online collection, as long as the downstream HDF5 converter is sufficiently versatile. Practical Gains Before modifying data output, it is necessary to check whether the base class unconditionally calls/writes relevant fields. Simply overriding the subclass method may not be enough to prevent the field from appearing in the output. The progress indicator calculated based on the ratio of Euclidean distances (1 - dist_current/dist_start) will produce out-of-range values under non-linear paths. It is necessary to explicitly clamp it to a valid range. Conversation Summary ✅ Designed and implemented a new per-frame variable scheme for Place Dual Shoes task 03:39:55.636 | claude_code Users requested the addition of five variables: manip_progress_time, manip_progress_distance_left/right, target_endpose, target_joint, and removal of critical_region. AI deeply explored the code library, identified the ‘future knowledge’ problem, designed a post-processing architecture (retrieving patch pickle files after each move). Ultimately, only envs/place_dual_shoes.py was modified to complete the implementation, and it was verified that pkl2hdf5.py’s versatility did not require additional changes.
✅ Fixed two data quality bugs related to critical_region remaining and negative progress values 15:34:27.123 | claude_code After running data collection, two problems were discovered: the critical_region field still appeared in HDF5, and manip_progress_distance showed negative values. AI identified the root causes: the former was unconditionally written by the base class get_obs() (popped during the patch phase), and the latter was a boundary situation caused by curved trajectories (clamped to [0,1]). Both fixes were completed directly through the Edit tool.
❌ Activated the conda environment (interruption) 03:18:46.380 | claude_code Users attempted to activate the RefineVLA conda environment but immediately interrupted it without generating substantial work content.

title: 缺陷记录表 2026-03-13

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-12 Today’s Overview What was done: Fixed issues with mismatched torch/torchvision versions in the VLA/RoboTwin evaluation environment and ABI compatibility problems with curobo CUDA extensions. Also improved the terminal width and process display logic of the GPU monitoring tool gpumon.py. How it was done: Traced down dependency errors layer by layer (upgrade to torchvision → fix curobo JIT compilation path → verify checkpoint path). By setting CUDA_HOME+CPATH to point to the conda internal CUDA header file directory, curobo was recompiled from source. What’s achieved: Resolved a critical dependency conflict in the context of torch 2.7.1. The evaluation script can now run normally until the model loading stage, clearing environmental obstacles for subsequent robot strategy evaluations. Debugged multi-layer dependency issues in the VLA robot evaluation environment on Tianhe servers while optimizing the display logic of the GPU monitoring tool
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy 🔄 VLA eval.sh running environment fix — Fixed an issue where bash eval.sh place_dual_shoes demo_clean pi05_robotwin2 demo_clean 0 2 failed to run. Tried to solve the problem by fixing mismatched torchvision versions (0.21.0→0.22.1) and curobo CUDA extension ABI incompatibility (recompiling from source). The remaining issue of the checkpoint_id=5000 path not existing needs to be resolved (available checkpoint: 15000/25000/29999). Implementation and Fixes 🔄 gpumon.py display logic optimization — Fixed issues with overly wide output (over 100 columns) and repeated process displays of the GPU monitoring tool. AI achieved global deduplication, but users corrected it to only display each process once per GPU in a session, resulting in interrupted sessions and incomplete tasks. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. curobo precompiled .so files are incompatible with torch 2.7.1 ABI (undefined symbol: torchInternalAssertFail). JIT recompilation failed due to lack of ninja and CUDA header files. Solution: Install ninja, set CUDA_HOME to the conda environment root directory, set CPATH to envs/RefineVLA/targets/x86_64-linux/include/, and execute pip install -e . to recompile curobo from source. Key insight: The CUDA toolkit headers installed via conda are located in /envs//targets/x86_64-linux/include/ instead of /usr/local/cuda/include/. When compiling CUDA extensions, CUDA_HOME and CPATH must be set to this path.
2. torch 2.7.1+cu126 is not compatible with torchvision version 0.21.0. The torchvision::nms operator registration failed. Solution: Upgrade torchvision to 0.22.1+cu126 (pip install torchvision==0.22.1 –index-url [[[HYMTPH_0]]). Key insight: Torch 2.7.x must be paired with torchvision 0.22.x. The link in the torchvision .so file refers to the torch C++ ABI, and a major version upgrade requires a corresponding update.
General Issues 3. The checkpoint path policy/pi05/checkpoints/pi05_robotwin2/demo_clean/5000/assets/ does not exist. The default deploy_policy.yml checkpoint_id is 5000, but the actual available values are 15000/25000/29999. Solution: The interrupted session was not resolved. The checkpoint_id in the deploy_policy.yml needs to be changed to an available value (e.g., 29999) or passed as a parameter to eval.sh. Key insight: The checkpoint_id parameter passed by eval.sh must correspond to the actual training output. The default value of 5000 in the yml is just a placeholder.
4. The output width of gpumon.py exceeds the terminal width, and processes appear repeatedly on multiple GPUs. Solution: Rewrite the process table logic to limit the output to 80 columns and filter out noise from sub-processes like multiprocessing worker/wandb-core. Key insight: AI understood the requirement as global deduplication (once per process), but the actual need is to deduplicate for each (process, GPU) pair (each process appears once per GPU).
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Understanding of gpumon.py process deduplication granularity Role Thinking Human Humans clearly distinguish between “multiple-to-multiple relationships between processes and GPUs”: We expect to display each (process, GPU) pair only once, not globally once per process. AI AI achieved global deduplication—only one instance appears per process. The GPU columns are connected by commas with all used card numbers (e.g., 0,1,5,7). Difference analysis: AI’s solution lost the correspondence between processes and specific GPUs. Humans removed redundant rows while preserving multiple cards.
Implementation Level Dominance over dependency environment debugging Role Thinking Human Humans adopted an iterative strategy: Rerun the script after fixing one error to observe new errors, actively rejecting AI’s tools (find command, ExitPlanMode) to control the pace. AI AI was responsible for analyzing root causes and executing technical solutions layer by layer, able to identify the complete dependency chain of torch→torchvision→curobo and propose fixes. Difference analysis: Humans controlled the execution pace and scope, while AI provided diagnostic and implementation capabilities. The division of labor was clear, but human intervention prolonged the debugging cycle.
AI Limitations Important Limitations AI misunderstood gpumon.py’s deduplication requirement, implementing “global deduplication per process” instead of “deduplication for each (process, GPU) pair”. Users had to explicitly correct it to discover the error. General Limitations It took multiple attempts to trace CUDA header file paths (pip nvidia package path → system /usr/local/cuda → conda targets directory). AI failed to locate the correct path based on the environment structure at once. When paths were uncertain, AI tended to use find for global search, which was rejected by users. It should prioritize using the known environment structure (conda env layout) to infer paths. Today’s Gains Core Gains When installing the CUDA toolkit in a conda environment, the header files are located in /envs//targets/x86_64-linux/include/ (not /usr/local/cuda/include/). When compiling CUDA extensions, set CUDA_HOME to <conda_env_root> and CPATH to <targets_include>. After a major version upgrade of torch, all CUDA extensions that depend on the torch C++ ABI (precompiled .so files like torchvision and curobo) need to be recompiled or upgraded. Torch 2.7.x corresponds to torchvision 0.22.x. When curobo’s precompiled .so file is not compatible with the current torch, deleting the .so and then using pip install -e . to rebuild from source is a feasible quick fix. The key is setting the correct CUDA compilation environment. Session Summary RoboBrain GPU Monitor 🔄 gpumon.py terminal width limitation and process display deduplication optimization 15:48:28.705 | claude_code Users reported issues with overly wide output and repeated processes in gpumon.py, requesting fixes. AI rewrote the process table logic, limiting the width to 80 columns and implementing global process deduplication. Users then corrected it: The correct requirement was to display each process once per GPU, not global deduplication. The session was interrupted, and the second fix was not completed.
VLA RoboTwin pi05 🔄 eval.sh dependency chain fix: torchvision upgrade + curobo source recompilation + checkpoint path issue discovered 02:34:02.614 | claude_code After successfully upgrading torchvision to 0.22.1 as planned, an incompatibility error with curobo CUDA extension ABI occurred. AI installed ninja, deleted the old .so file, and found the CUDA header files in the conda environment targets/x86_64-linux/include/ directory. After setting CUDA_HOME+CPATH, it successfully recompiled curobo from source. When running the script again, it was found that the checkpoint_id=5000 path did not exist (the actual available values are 15000/25000/29999). The session was interrupted while analyzing parameter mapping.
🔄 First error analysis of eval.sh: Diagnosis and fix plan for torch/torchvision version mismatch 02:23:18.758 | claude_code Users ran eval.sh and encountered an error where the torchvision::nms operator was not found. AI diagnosed that there was a version mismatch between torch 2.7.1 and torchvision 0.21.0, and planned to upgrade torchvision to 0.22.1. Users chose the upgrade option but rejected AI’s direct execution of ExitPlanMode, and the session ended waiting for user instructions.
