content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-09 Today’s Overview Done: Three devices are working parallel to advance four main tasks: (1) MIHD spaceomics – completed visualization of cross-sample RM-IDEAL evaluation, design and implementation of CrossModalEnhancer, literature review on scGPT, and encountered GPU compatibility issues; (2) Robot error recovery benchmark – completed bug fixes, re-labeling scenarios, quota generation system, online collection of excessive exposure to architectural defects, established a new direction for offline injection; (3) VLA engineering – analysis of UniVLA/CALVIN evaluation dependencies, interpretation of Pi0 stream matching, data conversion for RoboTwin; (4) AI infrastructure – MCP transformation of gadget tools, integration with CalendarPro, multi-source enhancement by research_scout. How done: In DCC, systematic binary debugging was carried out on cuBLAS large tensor bugs in cuBLAS, and the bug was bypassed by “projecting first then aggregating neighbors”; tianhe identified CALVIN configuration defects through code depth reading and compressed evaluation data requirements. The quota generation system exposed natural capture of imbalance issues after running on A800; TzJsDesktop built an MCP service using FastMCP+capture_stdout+asyncio.to_thread, cross-environment calling with conda run, and zero-dependency access to bioRxiv/PubMed through urllib.request. What’s useful: MIHD cross-sample retrieval pipeline is in place. CrossModalEnhancer has been integrated into the fusion framework (CPU testing passed). The discovery of scGPT’s questionable value is strategic for gene encoder selection; 1,029 re-labeling scenarios have been completed. Offline injection of architectural decisions provides a direction for dataset construction; CALVIN eval file requirements were compressed from 1.3GB to 600KB; the gadget was upgraded to an AI Agent service layer. CalendarPro implements daily automatic research paper discovery, and research_scout covers three sources: arXiv/bioRxiv/PubMed. DCC Done: Full-stack advancement of MIHD project: completed cross-sample RM-IDEAL evaluation from 151673 to 151508 (Layer_1 ρ=0.62, Layer_4 ρ=0.66) and generated 7 space heat maps. The CrossModalEnhancer module was implemented, and the cuBLAS large tensor bug on RTX 2080 Ti was overcome. A visualization script for cross-slice patch queries was created (due to 151676 embedding collapse + GPU environment issues). Literature review on scGPT was completed. How done: The benchmark script was run in the conda General environment. Systematic binary debugging identified the trigger point of cuBLAS N>3500 bugs, and the bug was bypassed by “projecting to hidden_dim first then indexing neighbors” and mini-batch contrast loss. Comprehensive literature search combined multiple benchmark conclusions. What’s useful: Verified STAIG fusion embedding’s ability to capture cross-sample spatial topology. CrossModalEnhancer passed CPU three-mode tests. It was found that scGPT zero-shot is inferior to PCA. Cross-slice visualization was blocked due to GPU environment issues and needs further resolution. TzJsDesktop Done: Completed design and implementation of the gadget MCP Server (9 tools, FastMCP framework). Reorganized tool output to content return mode (added save parameter), implemented an asynchronous integration layer between CalendarPro and gadget (conda run across environments, two background services: research + daily report). Enhanced the research_scout log system with multi-source support for bioRxiv/PubMed. Created a multi-project workspace CLAUDE.md.
How done: Built a stdio MCP server using FastMCP+capture_stdout+asyncio.to_thread; used async subprocess+conda run for zero-invasion cross-environment calling; RotatingFileHandler (5MB×3) with dual output logs; zero-dependency access to bioRxiv API and PubMed esearch→efetch two-step XML APIs through urllib.request.
What’s useful: The gadget tool was upgraded from a standalone CLI to an AI Agent callable service layer. CalendarPro automatically triggers research discovery at 8AM/11PM every day and summarizes daily reports (13 unit tests passed). Research_scout covers three major sources of papers. The content return mode of the MCP tool allows Claude Code to directly consume full text content.
tianhe Done: Advanced two types of work: Error recovery benchmark – fixed two key bugs in monitor.update() and taxonomy labels. Re-labeling 1,029 historical scenarios. Implemented a quota generation system (127 unit tests passed). A800 GPU operation exposed natural capture of severe overcapacity issues. Users proposed an offline architecture injection. VLA engineering – analysis of UniVLA CALVIN evaluation dependencies (4 issues), eval file extraction script (1.3GB→600KB), in-depth analysis of training data pipeline, interpretation of Pi0 stream matching, conversion of 50 episodes from RoboTwin to LeRobot format.
How done: Deep exploration of the code library + planning. Task scheduling and real-time monitoring on A800 GPU nodes. Reading the code layer by layer to identify CALVIN hard-coded paths and missing parameters. Python scripts parsed HDF5/NPZ dimensions, reusing existing conversion scripts to rewrite directory mode.
What’s useful: Error recovery benchmark system was fixed. Quota generation operation exposed online architectural limitations. Offline architecture injection decisions established a subsequent direction. CALVIN eval data was significantly compressed, reducing storage requirements. The principle of Pi0 stream matching was clear, laying the foundation for subsequent model modifications. 50 episodes from RoboTwin were successfully converted for training use.
Parallel advancement on three devices: DCC, tianhe, TzJsDesktop. MIHD cross-modal enhancement module implementation and RM-IDEAL cross-sample evaluation, robot error recovery benchmark quota generation (exposing online architectural limitations and establishing a new direction for offline injection), VLA robot framework engineering (data pipelines for UniVLA/Pi0/RoboTwin), and upgrading the gadget tool to an AI Agent callable MCP service and completing CalendarPro integration.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy 🔄 Design and implementation of CrossModalEnhancer cross-modal enhancement module — AI identified the core problem of single spot KV degradation (each spot has only one vector, directly cross-attention degrades to linear projection). Proposed a solution for constructing KV sequences from spatial neighbors. Implemented CrossModalAttentionBlock (including symmetric InfoNCE training) and integrated it into 5 files. CPU three-mode tests were passed. GPU-side bugs on RTX 2080 Ti cuBLAS large tensors (N>3500) were overcome through architectural restructuring (projecting first then indexing neighbors + mini-batch contrast loss), but the complete pipeline evaluation is not yet completed. 🔄 Implementation of error recovery benchmark quota generation system and GPU operation — Created 3 new scripts (1d_quota_generation.py with three-stage scheduling, 1f_relabel_scenes.py, 1g_check_quota_progress.py) and type_feasibility.yaml. After A800 GPU node operation, pick_place generated 21,001 items but naturally captured severe overcapacity (7,233 items premature_release, 0 for 7 types). Users stopped and proposed an offline architecture injection: collecting complete trajectories, detecting injectable points and creating indexes, then injecting in batches according to quotas. ✅ Design, implementation, and restructuring of Gadget MCP Server — Used FastMCP+capture_stdout+asyncio.to_thread to encapsulate summarize/research/benchmark into 9 MCP tools (mcp_server.py + pyproject.toml + .mcp.json). Reorganized 5 tools from “writing file return path” to “returning complete content” and added a save parameter. Determined the distribution scheme for pip install -e .+console entry point (uvx is not suitable for scenarios with local data directories). All 9 tools were registered and passed functional verification. ✅ Implementation of CalendarPro gadget integration layer — Created src/tools/package (protocol/runner/gadget_tools), implemented ResearchScoutTool and DailySummaryTool (async subprocess+conda run across environments), registered research_scout_service (8 AM daily) and gadget summary_service (11 PM每晚) to BackgroundCoordinator. Added 12 configuration items to config.py. After fixing the compatibility issue with conda –no-banner version, all 13 unit tests passed. ✅ MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark evaluation and space heat map visualization — Completed PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion evaluation on bidirectional evaluations from 151673 to 151508. Layer_1 (ρ=0.62) and Layer_4 (ρ=0.66) performed best, while Layer_3 (ρ=-0.21) was the worst due to high internal heterogeneity. Generated 2×3 space heat maps for 7 niche labels comparing ground truth with retrieval results. ✅ Literature review on scGPT/scGPT-spatial performance — Core findings: Independent evaluation in Genome Biology 2025 confirmed that scGPT zero-shot is inferior to PCA/scVI. scGPT-spatial was only compared with weak baselines (SpaGCN/stLearn, ARI≈0.30-0.40). The true SOTA (GraphST, ARI≈0.55-0.63) was not included, and it was not captured by any independent third-party benchmark—the value of using scGPT as a gene encoder in the MIHD project is questionable. ✅ Bug fixes for error recovery benchmark and re-labeling scenarios — Fixed two key bugs: (1) The return value of monitor.update() was discarded, causing step-by-step error detection to fail; (2) _generate_labels() used validator names instead of taxonomy types. Added map_to_taxonomy_type() to achieve correct mapping. Written 1f_relabel_scenes.py to re-label 1,029 historical scenarios into valid taxonomy types. All 127 unit tests passed. 🔄 Analysis of UniVLA CALVIN evaluation dependencies and eval file extraction — Analyzed the complete dependency chain in run_calvin_eval_ddp.py, identified 4 issues that needed fixing (CALVIN_ROOT hardcoding, missing window_size field, MAPBloc typo, uncompressed dataset). Key finding: CALVIN evaluation only requires merged_config.yaml (no episode data read), and extract_eval_files.py compressed 1.3GB to 600KB. Dataset uncompression is not completed. ✅ In-depth analysis of UniVLA CALVIN training data pipeline — Analyzed the complete data flow in finetune_calvin.py+DiskCalvinDataset: auto_lang_ann.npy index construction, 12-frame sliding window.npz loading, dual-stream input (VLA visual stream+LAM encoder stream), online VQ-VAE encoding to generate latent action supervision signals, three-module joint training architecture. Each step relied on online LAM inference, with significant computational overhead. ✅ Research_scout log system with multi-source support for bioRxiv/PubMed — Introduced RotatingFileHandler with dual output logs (5MB×3 backups, DEBUG-level files+INFO-level terminal), migrated ~77 print calls. Added warning for empty field counts in Stage1/Stage2, added try-except in _eval_with_anthropic. Zero new dependencies implemented search_biorxiv() and search_pubmed() (esearch→efetch XML, 0.4s limit). Generalized paper_id/source fields maintained backward compatibility with arxiv_id. Final file had 2,654 lines. ❌ MIHD cross-slice patch visualization script — The script for UNI2+PCA+STAIG fusion nearest-neighbor visualization from 151673 to 151676 was completed. However, it was found that the STAIG embedding in 151676 was completely zero (model collapsed). GPU retraining failed due to conflict between PyTorch 2.9.0+cu129 and PyG scatter CUDA deterministic, causing task blockage. ✅ Understanding of Pi0 stream matching implementation — Parsed pi0.py conditional stream matching: Beta(1.5,1) time sampling (t=1 with pure noise, t=0 target action), linear interpolation path, constant velocity vector field (u_t = noise - actions) MSE loss, Euler’s method inference, KV cache optimization. Compared Pi0 (concatenated time encoding) vs Pi0.5 (adaRMS conditionalized) architecture variants. ✅ Conversion of RoboTwin demo_clean to LeRobot format — Rewrote the conversion script to adapt to 14DOF action space and input without zip directory. Successfully converted 50 episodes (11,459 frames). Fixed the issue where the import of the HF_LEROBOT_HOME module was invalid after loading (used root parameter instead). Users manually moved the generated dataset to the target path simultaneously. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. RTX 2080 Ti + PyTorch 2.9.0+cu129 triggers cuBLAS CUBLAS_STATUS_EXECUTION_FAILED for high-dimensional large tensors (3D tensors with N>3500), and conflicts with PyG scatter’s CUDA deterministic mode, leading to CUDA illegal memory access Solution: Dual architectural modifications: (1) For full embeddings, first project them to hidden_dim(128) using Linear, then index neighbors using idx_tensor to avoid high-dimensional large tensors entering cuBLAS; (2) Replace full InfoNCE with mini-batch contrast loss (batch_size=512) to avoid N×N matrix backward triggering bugs
Key Insight: “Projecting to low dimension first and then aggregating neighbors” is not only a GPU compatibility workaround but also a general best practice for high-dimensional embedding cross-modal fusion. Mini-batch contrast loss is the standard approach for large-scale contrast learning. It should be fixed to verified version combinations (PyTorch 2.1-2.4).
2. The paper on scGPT-spatial claims it outperforms the baseline, but its actual value is difficult to evaluate Solution: System literature research revealed that its baseline was weak methods from 2021-2022 (SpaGCN/stLearn, ARI≈0.30-0.40). SOTA methods (GraphST, ARI≈0.55-0.63) were not compared, and it was not included in any independent third-party benchmark
Key Insight: The paper avoids direct competition by choosing a weak baseline. Evaluating new methods requires verifying whether their baseline is the current SOTA. The significance of “outperforming the baseline” depends entirely on the level of the baseline.
3. Natural capture of severe overcapacity in online quota generation: Some error types (premature_release 7,233 items) are extremely frequent, while 7 types have completely zero occurrences Solution: Users proposed an offline architecture injection—let the strategy run a complete trajectory, detect injectable points and create indexes offline, then selectively inject according to quotas. Skip types that have reached their limit
Key Insight: Online natural capture is highly influenced by the distribution of strategy behavior. It’s impossible to control type balance. The offline architecture decouples “exploring injectability” from “executing injection,” making it the correct approach for constructing balanced error scenario datasets.
4. MCP Server tools default only write files and return paths, so Claude Code cannot directly read the content Solution: Reorganized the tool to bypass the cmd_* wrapper and call underlying functions directly, returning complete markdown/JSON through a save parameter. This allows the file writing to be controlled via a save parameter
Key Insight: The primary purpose of MCP tools is to deliver content to AI consumers. File writing is an optional side effect rather than a main function—when designing tools, one should consider who the consumer will be.
5. CALVIN evaluation script has multiple hard-coded issues (CALVIN_ROOT path, missing window_size field, import typo), and the 1.3GB dataset transfer cost is high Solution: Identified and fixed these issues one by one. Key finding: CALVIN evaluation is purely online simulation, without reading any episode data. It only requires validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml. The 1.3GB dataset can be compressed to a 600KB eval-only version
Key Insight: Evaluation scripts often have surprising storage requirements: Pure online simulation doesn’t read historical data, significantly reducing storage and transmission costs.
6. When the LLM returns incomplete JSON, the motivation/innovation_point static fields are empty. No logs are available for debuggingSolution: After Stage1/2 evaluation, add a warning for the count of missing fields. Add try-except+logger.error in _eval_with_anthropic, and record the original response as DEBUG log in _try_repair_result. Key Insights: Silencing.get() fallback掩盖LLM response quality issues. Structured logs are crucial for troubleshooting LLM integration failures, and log埋点 should be designed from the beginning of integration.
7. For STAIG fusion embedding with ID 151676, all values were zero, resulting in model collapse. This cannot be used for cross-slice queries. GPU retraining failed due to compatibility issues between PyTorch 2.9.0 and CUDA 13.1 drivers. Solution: Attempted GPU retraining but was blocked due to environmental compatibility issues, causing the task to pause. The fundamental solution needs to address GPU environment version issues.
Key Insights: The statistical characteristics of cached embeddings should be verified before use (norm range). Zero values are a clear sign of model collapse, and health checks should be added during caching write operations. Older PyTorch versions may introduce regression issues ahead of driver versions.
8. In the injection scenario, the validator names used (drop/tip_over/stuck) instead of the 24 taxonomy types led to inconsistencies in the label system. This caused errors in distribution statistics and quota calculations. Solution: Add _map_to_taxonomy_type() in generates_labels(), mapping from (validator, task_phase) to taxonomy types. database._classify_scene() retains existing valid labels without overwriting them.
Key Insights: The label system must be correct from the moment it is generated. Dependence on post-mapping will掩盖 real type distribution issues.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Identification of Key Challenges in CrossModalEnhancer Architecture Role Thinking Human Users provide functional requirements and constraints (cross-attention, residual connection, contrastive loss, no annotation, per-section), but do not mention the problem of single spot KV degradation. AI After deeply exploring codebases, AI identifies core architectural challenges: each spot has only one vector, and direct cross-attention degenerates into linear projection. AI proposes a solution to build KV sequences using spatial neighbors. Difference Analysis: AI contributes real architectural insights (not implementation-level solutions) and identifies key technical defects that users may not be aware of while proposing solutions—this is an architectural-level contribution from AI.
Data Generation Architecture: Online Quotas vs Offline Injection Role Thinking Human Users immediately stop when they observe excessive data in real-time, proposing a completely different offline architecture: first collect complete trajectories, build indexes for injectable points offline, then inject in batches according to quotas. AI AI designs an online quota system, mixing exploration and collection. It does not anticipate that uneven distribution of strategic behavior will lead to severe skewness in types; it also does not actively suggest stopping during runtime. Difference Analysis: Humans have the ability to stop immediately when the direction is wrong and can propose more elegant architectural solutions. AI tends to execute the established plan and relies on user intervention to change directions.
MCP Tool Design: Content Return vs File Writing Role Thinking Human Users explicitly state that summarize and research are Claude Code skill enhancement tools. The tool results should be directly presented to AI consumption, with file writing being an optional side effect. AI AI’s initial design focuses on file writing, only returning paths and summaries, following the traditional CLI tool approach. It does not consider the scenario where ‘consumers are AI itself’. Difference Analysis: Humans design from the perspective of tool usage scenarios (AI consumes content), while AI designs from the implementation path (existing cmd_* functions). This is a difference at the architectural level.
Strategic Questions in scGPT Literature Research Role Thinking Human Users actively question the value of scGPT as a gene encoder, driving external verification research. This is a strategic questioning of the project’s core assumptions. AI AI searches and synthesizes multiple papers, providing specific quantitative data (AvgBIO metrics, DLPFC ARI comparison), converting directional issues into concrete evidence. Difference Analysis: The strategic judgment of research direction comes from humans, while AI is responsible for information collection and quantitative synthesis. Together, they complete conclusions that have a significant impact on the project.
GPU Error Handling Strategy: Fast Fallback vs Root Cause Diagnosis Role Thinking Human Users repeatedly point out that when encountering CUDA errors, one should not immediately switch to CPU. Instead, system diagnosis of the root cause should be carried out first. In terms of embedding collapse issues, retraining is also required rather than accepting bad results. AI After encountering CUDA errors, AI tends to quickly fall back to CPU or change architecture, considering this a safer solution. The root cause was not quickly identified in the early stage. Difference Analysis: Humans have a stronger intuition of ’not giving up easily’ and require understanding the problem before changing strategies. AI tends to be conservative and prefer quick fallback.
AI Limitations Major Limitations System design phase failed to anticipate data distribution issues: When designing an online quota generation system, it was not predicted that uneven distribution of strategic behavior would lead to severe skewness in natural capture. When observing 21,001 scenarios and excessive data during runtime, no proactive alerts were issued, and direction changes required user intervention. A better offline injection architecture did not propose this solution proactively. MCP tool design did not consider the perspective of consumers: It did not take into account that the tool consumer is AI itself. The initial design followed the traditional CLI tool pattern of file writing and path return, which only needed user explicit indication to be corrected. This reflects a lack of proactive reasoning ability regarding ‘who consumes the tools’. Too quick fallback when encountering hardware compatibility issues: After encountering CUDA errors, it tended to directly switch to CPU instead of systematically diagnosing the root cause of PyTorch version compatibility issues. GPU debugging experienced multiple attempts with inaccurate directions. The initial failure to quickly identify the mini-batch was the core strategy for solving N×N matrix problems. General Limitations Not verifying signatures before using APIs/libraries: When using FastMCP, it was not checked whether the version parameter existed first (only discovered during runtime crashes). When using the conda –no-banner parameter, the local version was not detected (only discovered when the program failed to run). When handling LeRobot output paths, the Python module-level import timing issue was not anticipated. It should be inspected with inspect.signature before using unfamiliar APIs. Some literature is not accessible in full (bioRxiv PDF returns 403), and related data relies on abstracts and secondary information. The complete GPU pipeline evaluation of CrossModalEnhancer has not been completed, and the actual effects of the modules have not yet been verified. Today’s Achievements Core Achievements scGPT zero-shot clustering was less effective than PCA/scVI in independent benchmarks (Genome Biology 2025). scGPT-spatial only compared with a weak baseline and had no independent third-party verification. The value of using scGPT as a zero-shot gene encoder for the MIHD project is questionable, and the strategy for choosing a gene encoder needs to be re-evaluated. The offline injection architecture (first collecting complete trajectories → detecting injectable points and building indexes → injecting in batches according to quotas) is more suitable for creating balanced error scenario datasets than the online quota system. It decouples ’exploring injectability’ from ’executing injections’, allowing precise control of the number of each type of error. The online natural capture based on the BC-RNN strategy will strongly favor high-frequency errors, and some types are almost impossible to trigger naturally. CALVIN evaluation is a completely online simulation that does not read episode .npz frame data at all. Only validation/.hydra/merged_config.yaml is needed to initialize the simulation environment. The 1.3GB dataset can be compressed into a 600KB eval-only version, significantly reducing storage and transmission costs. In MCP server, the capture_stdout() context manager is a key security design issue: All legacy code that depends on print() and sys.exit() must execute within this context. Otherwise, any print output will contaminate the JSON-RPC stdio transmission, leading to protocol errors. MCP tools should return content rather than file paths. File writing is an optional side effect. RTX 2080 Ti + PyTorch 2.9.0+cu129 has a cuBLAS large tensor bug (N>3500 high-dimensional operations) and a CUDA deterministic compatibility issue with PyG scatter. General strategy: first project to low-dimensional hidden_dim, then aggregate neighbors. Use mini-batch instead of the full N×N matrix for contrast loss. It should be fixed to verified version combinations (PyTorch 2.1-2.4). Pi0 flow matching time convention: t=1 corresponds to pure noise, t=0 corresponds to target action (opposite of some literature directions). The Beta(1.5,1) distribution has a higher weight for noise terms, making training more stable. Pi0.5 uses adaRMS normalization for injecting time conditions, with stronger expression ability than simple concatenation. UniVLA’s three-module joint training: LAM online encoding (initial frames, target frames) → VQ-VAE discrete coding as a supervisory signal; VLA main branch predicts latent action tokens; ActionDecoder decodes continuous actions from the VLA hidden state. Each step requires 12 consecutive frames and depends on online LAM inference, with significant computational overhead. PubMed esearch→efetch two-step E-utilities API can freely index subscription journals like Nature/Cell/Science. It is the best free alternative to obtain metadata for these journals. The bioRxiv API(api.biorxiv.org/details/biorxiv) is also open, requiring no new dependencies (urllib.request). uvx creates a temporary environment that is not suitable for MCP servers that rely on local data directories. pip install -e .(editable install)+console entry point should be used to make the server run within the repo directory to maintain stable data paths. The standard way to call scripts across conda environments is: conda run -n python script.py, and setting cwd to the script working directory is key. DLPFC cross-sample RM-IDEAL results: Layer_1 (ρ=0.62) and Layer_4 (ρ=0.66) have unique structures with clear boundaries, and the fusion embedding performs well. Layer_3 (ρ=-0.21) has high internal heterogeneity, making it a major challenge for cross-sample retrieval. Error labels should be correct from the moment they are generated (use taxonomy type names instead of validator names). Dependence on post-mapping will掩盖 real type distribution issues. The cached embedding should have its statistical characteristics verified before writing (norm range). Zero values are a clear sign of model collapse. Conversation Summary MIHD 🔄 Implementation and debugging of CrossModalEnhancer, visualization of RM-IDEAL benchmark results, review of scGPT literature, visualization script for cross-slice patch queries 23:07:33.887 | claude_code Completed four tasks on DCC: (1) CrossModalEnhancer cross-modal enhancement - AI identified the core problem of single spot KV degradation and proposed a solution using spatial neighbor KV sequences. Implemented CrossModalAttentionBlock and integrated 5 files. CPU three-mode tests passed, and systematic dichotomy debugging confirmed the RTX 2080 Ti cuBLAS large tensor bug on GPU side. Bypass by ‘project first then index + mini-batch loss’ was achieved, but the complete pipeline evaluation is not completed; (2) Completed bidirectional RM-IDEAL evaluations for 151673↔151508 (Layer_1 ρ=0.62, Layer_4 ρ=0.66, Layer_3 ρ=-0.21), generating 7 niche label space heat maps; (3) Review of scGPT literature revealed that its zero-shot performance is inferior to PCA/scVI. scGPT-spatial only compared with a weak baseline, which is strategic for the project’s gene encoder strategy; (4) Visualization script for cross-slice patch queries was completed, but issues with 151676 STAIG embedding being all zeros and GPU environment compatibility caused blockages.
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Bug fixing, scene re-labeling, implementation of quota-based generation system, and GPU running excessive problems 21:58:42.068 | claude_code Completed on tianhe A800 GPU node: Updated CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md documents, fixed two key bugs (monitor.update() returning discarded values, taxonomy label using validator names), created 3 new scripts (quota scheduling/re-labeling/progress checking), re-labeled 1,029 historical scenes, all 127 unit tests passed. After the GPU quota generation process exposed serious problems: premature_release had extremely skewed data (7,233 episodes, 0 for 7 types), users stopped and proposed a new offline injection architecture (first collecting complete trajectories → detecting injectable points and building indexes → injecting in batches according to quotas), establishing direction for dataset construction.
UniVLA 🔄 Initialization of CLAUDE.md, analysis of CALVIN evaluation dependencies, script for extracting eval files, deep analysis of training data pipeline 03:35:00.014 | claude_code Created CLAUDE.md for the UniVLA repository on tianhe; systematically analyzed CALVIN ABC→D evaluation dependencies, identified 4 issues that must be fixed (hardcoded path/缺失window_size/import typo/data not unzipped), solved flash-attn installation across file systems (directly installing pre-compiled wheels); key discovery: CALVIN evaluation only requires merged_config.yaml. Writing a script to extract eval files compressed 1.3GB into 600KB; deeply analyzed the complete training data pipeline from auto_lang_ann.npy to dual-stream batch processing; K8s cluster DNS resolution was interrupted before confirmation due to proxy at localhost:9997.
Pi0 VLA ✅ Complete interpretation of pi0.py conditional flow matching implementation 11:37:19.597 | claude_code Detailed analysis of the core components of pi0.py, including training (Beta(1.5,1) time sampling, linear interpolation paths, constant velocity vector field MSE loss) and reasoning (Euler method integration, KV cache optimization). Compared Pi0 (concatenated time encoding) and Pi0.5 (adaRMS conditioned), laying the theoretical foundation for subsequent model modifications.
RoboTwin VLA ✅ Successfully converted 50 episodes from demo_clean to LeRobot format 16:18:03.597 | claude_code Created convert_robotwin_democlean_to_lerobot.py (adapting 14DOF action space and directory input), successfully converting 50 episodes (11,459 frames). Found an issue where the HF_LEROBOT_HOME module import setting was ineffective, fixed by using the root parameter. Users manually moved the generated dataset to the target path to complete the task. The initial Plan mode session was abandoned due to user interruption, and in the new session, it was implemented directly.
Gadget ✅ Complete redesign of MCP Server design implementation: research_scout multi-source enhancement, update of CLAUDE.md 22:12:24.330 | claude_code TzJsDesktop completed a comprehensive upgrade of the gadget: (1) Created 9 tools for MCP Server (FastMCP+capture_stdout+asyncio.to_thread, fixed version parameter incompatibility issues), redesigned tool output to content return mode (added save parameter), confirmed pip install -e . distribution plan; (2) Three enhancements to research_scout: RotatingFileHandler logging system (migrating ~77 prints), Stage1/2 empty field warnings, multi-source support for search_biorxiv()+search_pubmed() (zero new dependencies, paper_id/source fields kept backward compatible); final file size was 2,654 lines; (3) Created a root directory CLAUDE.md covering 5 independent projects.
CalendarPro ✅ Design and implementation of gadget’s asynchronous integration layer: tools/package+background service+unit testing 18:59:53.270 | claude_code Users requested CalendarPro to run research_scout daily and process daily reports. AI explored two codebases and designed an async subprocess + conda run zero-invasiveness solution, confirming three constraints (cond| Metric | Value | |——|——| | Total Tokens | 46,850,173 | | Input Tokens | 42,593 | | Output Tokens | 208,297 | | Cache Creation | 3,389,424 | | Cache Reads | 43,209,859 | | Cache Hit Rate | 92.7% | | Total Cost (USD) | $32.1709 |
Model Details Model Input Output Cache Creation Cache Reads Cost Percentage claude-opus-4-6 28,241 84,605 1,853,902 28,636,125 $28.1613 87.5% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 14,352 123,692 1,535,522 14,573,734 $4.0096 12.5% Usage by Device Device Total Tokens Input Output Cost DCC 16,764,319 8,203 67,914 $12.0631 tianhe 5,528,054 7,669 27,990 $3.5028 TzJsDesktop 24,557,800 26,721 112,393 $16.6049
summary: The process is being carried out concurrently on three devices: DCC, tianhe, and TzJsDesktop. The MIHD cross-modal enhancement module is being utilized for cross-sample evaluation with RM-IDEAL. Robot error recovery benchmarks are being generated using quota-based data generation methods (these approaches expose limitations in online architectures and provide a new direction for offline injection). Additionally, the VLA robot framework is being engineered (using UniVLA/Pi0/RoboTwin data pipelines). Finally, the gadget tool is being upgraded to an AI Agent-accessible MCP service, and it has been integrated with CalendarPro.
title: 错误日志 2026-03-09

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W10 (2026-03-02 ~ 2026-03-08) This week, we have been working across three devices: DCC, Tianhe Supercomputer, and TzJsDesktop. We have been advancing four main areas simultaneously: space transcriptomics research, robot operation training/assessment, AI personal assistants, and paper management tools. In the MIHD project, we completed the full set of experiments for scGPT+UNI2 (QFormer avg ARI=0.370, +117% compared to scGPT-only). We also established a zero-shot cross-sample evaluation framework. The Pi0.5 LoRA training achieved an overall success rate of 58.9%, significantly outperforming BC-RNN (0%). Quantitative verification confirmed the advantages of the VLA model. CalendarPro completed its transition from passive response to active decision-making, achieving 321 fully green tests. Meanwhile, we identified and fixed critical integration defects such as BackgroundCoordinator never being started. Gadget Research Scout was developed from scratch to a production-ready state, completing a two-stage LLM paper evaluation pipeline in a single day and providing three research direction suggestions for the first time. error_recovery_benchmark completed the cleanup of 65 symlinks and built infrastructure for visualizing error scenarios in MP4 format. The most important engineering experience this week is that test passing does not mean system availability (integration layers must be verified separately), and it is essential to study the architecture patterns of mature projects before designing an agent-based system.
Weekly Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-03-02 ~ 2026-03-08 Active Days 7 / 7 Total Conversations 31 Projects Involved 18 Tasks Completed 51 Ongoing Tasks 11 Total Tokens 436,851,629 Total Cost $306.71 Claude Code Tokens 424,137,873 Claude Code Costs $302.72 Codex Tokens 12,713,756 Codex Costs $3.99 Average Daily Cost $43.82 Project Progress error_recovery_benchmark (Robot Error Recovery Benchmark) (5 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Full evaluation of BC-RNN (5 tasks × 50 rollouts). The root cause of SR=0% was identified as an observation key bug, not a model capability issue. Project panorama summary.md was systematically aligned (M14 evaluation data increased from 726 episodes to 6474 episodes. The Pipeline diagram included two paths: VLA Rollout injection and natural failure capture). Cleanup of 65 symlinks (4-way parallel Agents covering 9 stages). All import paths were moved to the error_benchmark framework. 127 unit tests were all green. Visualization of error scenarios in MP4 format for three tasks in coffee/stack/three_piece_assembly. 3 GPUs worked together to produce 9+ videos. Implementation of a pre-error trajectory playback storage layer (action_history + initial_state stored in NPZ), laying the foundation for playback within the first 5 seconds of an error. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The visualization logic for pre-error playback was not completed due to context exhaustion. ⚠️ Formal fixes for BC-RNN’s observation key bug have not been implemented yet. ⚠️ The three_piece_assembly injection mode had zero effective scenarios (the base was too stable). Adjustments were needed in the impact parameters. MIHD (Multimodal Space Transcriptomics Fusion) (4 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Completed the full set of experiments for scGPT+UNI2 fusion strategies. QFormer avg ARI=0.370 (+117%), followed by LLaVA-MLP (0.316). Both were significantly better than scGPT-only (0.170). Three-layer code tracing revealed that STAIG actually did not use gene encoder output (using the original HVG as GCN input). Improvements should start with the GNN structure. Implemented a cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark script (781 lines), added zero embedding runtime detection, and completed a 7-stage evaluation from 151673↔151508. Layer_1/5 performed well (Spearman 0.42-0.66). Negative correlations were found in the intermediate layers (Layer_3/6), indicating limitations in the embedding space of the transition layer. Established a zero-shot differential narrative framework (contrasting with STAIG’s training method). Preparation for Monday’s report was completed. Analyzed QueST’s cross-sample evaluation method (WWL Graph Kernel constructed pseudo ground truth), and wrote documentation for the Cross-Sample Patch Query method (459 lines). Pi0.5 LoRA Training and Evaluation (Tianhe Cluster) (4 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
BatchedVLAServer completed 9 tasks in parallel evaluations (44 minutes). Overall SR=58.9% (Stack_D0 100%, Stack_D1 95%, StackThree 80-90%). Three-layer evidence confirmed that pi05_base weights were correctly used for all training sessions (config.py weight_loader + training logs + comparison groups). Fixed several key issues in the evaluation pipeline: websocket ping_timeout=None (JIT compilation timeout), JIT concurrency crashes (starting at wrong times). Quantitatively confirmed that VLA inference GPU utilization was only 10% due to action chunking. Multi-client concurrency was 24% slower than serial processing. Continued training on two nodes (an49/an53) for 9 tasks, recovering from the highest step of 18000 without loss. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The optimization plan for BatchedVLAServer batch inference has been designed but not implemented. ⚠️ The rendering of demonstration videos will be executed after confirming the final evaluation results. CalendarPro (Personal Intelligent Butler) (3 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Designed according to OpenClaw/GSD architecture patterns, implementing 16 new service files (GapAnalyzer/AutonomousExecutor/SituationMonitor, etc.). 321 tests were all green. Fixed a critical integration defect where BackgroundCoordinator.start_all() was never called (all background services never run in production). Completed the development of Discord Bot Handlers for 9 IntentTypes, eliminating intent routing gaps. Eliminated 16 silent exceptions (except: pass), removed dead code in executor.py and implemented real distribution logic. Externalized 452 utterances to JSON files, enabling UtteranceAugmenter to automatically learn from mismatch logs (run every day at 2 AM). Compiled a complete Chinese tutorial of 1674 lines, identifying three root causes of context-aware bugs in Discord bots. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The solution for context-aware bug fixes has been designed (general_keywords simplification + LLM passing conversation history + semantic routing reset), but has not been implemented. Phoenix/FLARE/zhaoganlong Framework (Robot Training Infrastructure) (3 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
zhaoganlong completed the 4-step pipeline for 9 tasks data preparation (5Hz annotation → speed dataset → LLaVA JSON with 1.3 million entries → RGB rendering of approximately 1 million images). Diffusion Policy (single GPU) and Pi0.5 (2+3 FSDP GPUs) were successfully started training on an53. The Phoenix/FLARE code base was separated from the mixed library into a standalone project, reducing its size from 155GB to 37MB (symbolic links shared for large datasets). Developed 6 full-task training scripts, adding 3 missing OpenPI warmup configurations. Fixed potential issues with norm_stats/prev_actions normalization in Openpi-moe (apply_tree silently skipped). Blocked Items:
⚠️ LLaVA MPM training is blocked due to no network access in the cluster. The local copy of liuhaotian/llava-v1.5-7b is missing. It needs to be confirmed whether the cxp2 user has a complete local copy. gadget/Research Scout (Paper Management System) (2 active days) — ✅ Completed Completed:
Developed Research Scout from scratch, including 6 command-line interfaces, multi-source search on arXiv, and a two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline. First completed the full run, generating three actionable research directions: generative digital twin scenarios, restoring human videos to their original language, and document-guided operations. Implemented a four-level priority configuration for resolve_param(), specialized search for conference papers (arXiv comment fields), and author search (au: query). Implemented early stopping for search deduplication (threshold set at 5 known papers). The init –from-overview option automatically extracts project information from existing documents. VLA-RoboTwin (Robot Operation Data Collection) (2 active days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Fixed RoboTwin’s Vulkan rendering failures in headless Docker environments. Extracted the user-mode GL library from the official.run package and injected it through VK_ICD_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH, without requiring root privileges. Developed a system for marking critical regions for 10 robot operation tasks. After finding that critical_region was always zero, changed to recording original ee_target_distances. Blocked Items:
⚠️ The threshold for critical_region needs to be calibrated based on actual distance data before re-collection (τ=0.10m estimate is too low). Key Tasks ✅ Complete Design and Implementation of CalendarPro Personal Butler System Phases 1-3 (2026-03-05) — Designed according to OpenClaw/GSD architecture patterns, implemented 16 new services, with 321 tests all green. Identified and fixed a critical integration defect where BackgroundCoordinator never started. ✅ Complete Implementation and Verification of Research Scout’s Two-Stage Paper Evaluation Pipeline (2026-03-07) — Light filtering in Stage 1 → Deep analysis in Stage 2 (up to 20 papers). First run produced three actionable research directions, supporting conference search, author search, and four-level parameter configuration. ✅ MIHD scGPT+UNI2 Full Set of Fusion Experiments (5 Strategies) (2026-03-02) — QFormer avg ARI=0.370 (+117%), confirmed that STAIG does not use gene encoder output, established a zero-shot narrative framework. ✅ Pi0.5 LoRA Batch Evaluation (9 Tasks, Overall SR=58.9%) (2026-03-03) — BatchedVLAServer completed 9 task evaluations in 44 minutes. Stack tasks were almost perfect (95-100%), BC-RNN was completely green, and VLA advantages were quantitatively proven. ✅ Verification of Pi0.5 LoRA Base Model and Continued Training After 9 Tasks (2026-03-03) — Three-layer evidence confirmed that both sides used pi05_base. Continued training from the highest step of 18000 without loss; prevented false starts by covering existing checkpoints. ✅ MIHD Cross-Sample RM-IDEAL Benchmark Implementation (781 Lines) (2026-03-03) — Two-way cross-section evaluation, added zero embedding runtime detection and automatic recalculation. Layer_3 Spearman r≈0.44, established a reusable evaluation framework. ✅ Determination of the Root Cause of SR=0% in BC-RNN Normal Tasks (2026-03-04) — Code exploration identified an object observation key bug in baseline accuracy evaluation, clearly distinguishing between Normal SR (interrupted by the bug) and Error Recovery SR (core argument of the benchmark). ✅ Fixing Issues with Normativity in Openpi-moe norm_stats/prevactions (2026-03-05) — Found an apply_tree(strict=False) silently skipped missing keys. VAE showed scale mismatch issues between normalized and unnormalized action combinations. Modified compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect and write statistics. ✅ Externalization of Utterances in CalendarPro and Automatic Augmentation by UtteranceAugmenter (2026-03-05) — 452 hard-coded utterances were migrated to JSON files, enabling online augmentation based on automatic learning from mismatch logs, running every day at 2 AM. ✅ Fixing RoboTwin’s Headless Docker Vulkan Rendering Failures (2026-03-06) — Extracted the user-mode GL library from the NVIDIA official 535.104.12 .run package and injected it through VK_ICD_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH, without requiring root privileges. ✅ 65 Symlinks Cleanup in error_recovery_benchmark and Code Migration (2026-03-08) — 4-way parallel Agents covered 9 stages. All paths were moved from the error_framework to the error_benchmark framework, with 127 unit tests all green. 🔄 Implementation of Critical Region Marking System in VLA-RoboTwin (10 Tasks) (2026-03-08) — Implemented critical_region marking at every frame. After finding that the threshold τ=0.10m estimate was too low, changed to recording original ee_target_distances for data-driven calibration. 🔄 Design of GPU Utilization Optimization Plan for VLA Evaluation Pipeline (2026-03-02) — Confirmed that action chunking was the root cause of only 10% GPU utilization. Designed a BatchedVLAServer batch inference plan, but it has not been finalized. 🔄 Analysis and Fixing Specifications for Context-Aware Bugs in CalendarPro Discord Bots (2026-03-06) — Identified three root causes: general_keywords pre-check truncation following messages, _llm_classify not passing conversation history, and GENERAL routing containing confirmation words. The solution has been designed but not implemented. 🔄 9-Task Data Preparation Pipeline for zhaoganlong and Two-Link Training Startup (2026-03-05) — Completed the 4-step pipeline. Diffusion Policy and Pi0.5 FSDP were successfully started; LLaVA MPM was blocked due to lack of a base model. 🔄 Development of Pre-Error Trajectory Playback Storage Layer (2026-03-08) — Added action_history collection and initial_state storage to rollout_generator. NPZ storage expanded, but the visualization script playback logic was not completed due to context exhaustion. Issues and Solutions 1. STAIG’s two-stage approach actually completely ignores gene encoder output, using the original HVG as GCN input, resulting in scGPT+UNI2+STAIG≡PCA+UNI2+STAIG [MIHD] (2026-03-02) Solution: Three-layer code tracing (Fusion.py→runner.py→STAIGTrainer.py) confirmed the architectural fact: gene encoder was decoupled from GNN training. Improvements should start with the GNN structure rather than replacing the gene encoder.
2. Pi0.5 evaluation client reported keepalive ping timeouts, with all tasks having SR=0%; multi-client concurrency was 24% slower than serial processing [Pi0.5/VLA Evaluation] (2026-03-02) Solution: Add ping_timeout=None to connect() to disable the 20-second timeout (JAX JIT compilation took 30-60 seconds, exceeding the default value). Confirmed that VLA inference was strictly serial, and multi-client concurrency was ineffective. The correct solution is BatchedVLAServer batch inference.
3. STAIG’s fusion embeddings failed in some sections during training, resulting in a NaN Spearman r in cross-sample evaluation [MIHD] (2026-03-03) Solution: Add zero embedding detection in load_fused_embeddings() (norm<1e-6), automatically triggering –recompute_fusion to retrain STAIG for about 50 seconds.
4. Pi0.5 LoRA training configuration names contain ‘pi05_libero’, and users suspect that the initialization weights come from libero rather than pi05_base [Pi0.5/VLA Training] (2026-03-03) Solution: Three-layer evidence (config.py weight_loader field + training log Restoring checkpoint path + comparison group configuration) confirmed that both sides used pi05_base correctly. The configuration name describes the data loading configuration; weight_loader is the only basis.
5. BC-RNN had SR=0% on all normal tasks, which did not match the training rollout [error_recovery_benchmark] (2026-03-04)Solution: The code is exploring ways to fix the bug in the baseline_accuracy evaluation for object observation keys. Strategies that accept empty observations are being implemented. In M14, having an Error SR close to 0% is a core argument of the benchmark (the current strategy lacks error recovery capabilities). Comparing it with Normal SR can help clarify the issue. 6. In the training feature norm_stats.json for Openpi-moe, there is a missing prev_actions key without any errors. In VAE, directly concatenating hist_actions (unnormalized) and actions (normalized) causes scale mismatch [zhaoganlong/openpi] (2026-03-05) Solution: Modify compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect prevActions and write them into the statistics. The fundamental mechanism is to silently skip missing keys when apply_tree(strict=False). New training features must force a synchronized normalization script.
7. All background services of CalendarPro have never been run in production environments. Despite passing all 321 tests, this integration flaw has not been discovered [CalendarPro] (2026-03-05) Solution: Add await coordinator.start_all() in the on_ready() method of discord_bot.py. The root cause is that the registration mode and lifecycle management are decoupled. Unit tests cannot detect silent failures at the integration level.
8. The headless Docker container SAPIEN reports a Render Error. The bare except hides the real error. The apt driver version does not match the kernel module [VLA-RoboTwin] (2026-03-06) Solution: Expose the real error. Confirm that there is a missing NVIDIA Vulkan ICD. Extract the user-mode GL library that matches the kernel version from the official 535.104.12 .run package. Inject it through the VK_ICD_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variables (no root required).
9. The initial version of Research Scout performs a single-stage deep evaluation on all 50 papers. There is a significant waste of tokens, and low-relevance papers consume a lot of analysis resources [gadget/Research Scout] (2026-03-07) Solution: Reconstruct it into a two-stage pipeline: Stage 1 for lightweight filtering; Stage 2 for in-depth analysis (up to 20 high-relevance papers). This is based on the reading habits of real researchers.
10. In error_recovery_benchmark, 65 symlinks cause an indirect layer during code migration. After removing the symlinks, the depth of the sys.path parent chain needs to be recalculated [error_recovery_benchmark] (2026-03-08) Solution: The four-way parallel Agents clean up all symlinks according to a nine-stage plan without overlap. The main thread handles Makefile/documents. Fix two unexpected sys.path depth errors (Path.resolve() no longer folds symlinks).
11. In VLA-RoboTwin, the data collected for critical_region is always zero in key areas. The τ=0.10m threshold is purely based on estimates, and it does not match the actual working space scale [VLA-RoboTwin] (2026-03-08) Solution: Do not adjust parameters directly. Instead, record the original ee_target_distances (4-8 distance variables per task). Analyze the real data distribution before calibrating the threshold based on the data.
Learning Outcomes Architecture Three criteria for large-scale implementation: 1) Whether new services are connected to the startup link; 2) Whether cross-layers (IntentType→routing→Handler→service) are updated consistently; 3) Whether silent exceptions (except: pass) mask runtime errors. Passing the test is a necessary condition, not a sufficient condition. Integration layer defects can only be discovered through end-to-end verification. Before designing an Agent-based system, actively study mature projects in the same field. The EventBus/CronScheduler/Plugin registration patterns of OpenClaw and GSD’s STATE.md persistent memory/multi-Agent context isolation are highly reusable architecture patterns. Related fixes should be merged into a single change rather than implemented stage by stage to avoid code rework. apply_tree(strict=False) is an implicit risk in ML training pipelines. If the norm_stats calculation script that needs normalization is forgotten, it will cause a silent scale mismatch that affects training quality. The mismatch records of AI systems are free annotation data. Automatically回流为utterance增广 can achieve low-cost continuous self-improvement. Two-stage information processing (crude screening + fine evaluation) is better than single-stage full-processing in terms of token efficiency and analysis depth. Stage 1 focuses on problem relevance/innovation/relevance to the source (judged within 30 seconds). Stage 2 focuses on key insights that drive results rather than engineering details, and critically examines the baseline and metrics chosen by the author. Debugging Analyze the root cause of low GPU utilization in VLA inference: action chunking makes the inference calls sparse (about 8 times per trial × 2.5s/200s total time = 10%). Multi-client concurrency cannot improve the throughput of strict serial inference. The correct optimization direction is BatchedVLAServer batch inference + multi-GPU independent deployment. Identify the diagnostic path for BC-RNN SR=0%. First, check whether the observation key name/dimension matches the training data. Then, distinguish between “evaluation bug” and “training fails due to defects” based on historical training logs. Avoid misdiagnosing it as a model capability problem. The bare except is the most common debugging obstacle in rendering/simulation frameworks. It should be changed to except Exception: traceback.print_exc(). Parameter settings should be driven by experiments rather than intuition. First, collect a small amount of data to verify the actual distribution before setting thresholds. Incorrect estimates of key parameters (such as distance threshold, driver version) can result in invalid entire batches of data or installation failures. Tools Four-way parallel sub-Agents + the main thread handles non-paralleling parts. The large-scale code migration of 65 symlinks can be completed within a single session. The key is to divide the agent tasks without overlap according to file collections. BatchedVLAServer parallel evaluation (44 minutes/9 tasks) saves about 80% of time compared to serial evaluation. Fix the standard solution for headless HPC nodes with Vulkan/EGL rendering: Extract the user-mode GL library that matches the kernel version from the official NVIDIA .run package. Inject it through VK_ICD_FILENAMES/__EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES/LD_LIBRARY_PATH without rooting. EGL/Vulkan device configuration is independent of CUDA. If CUDA works, Vulkan can also work. The arXiv comment field is actually an area for conference acceptance announcements. A special search for conference papers can be achieved by searching the full text with all:“CVPR 2025” and filtering after finding comments. au:“Author Name” AND (kw1 OR kw2) is an effective way to track a specific researcher’s latest work. Domain Knowledge Learned fusion (QFormer) is significantly better than simple concatenation: scGPT 512d representation itself is valuable, but it needs non-linear projection to be fully activated (+117% vs scGPT-only). The gene encoder of STAIG and the GNN training are decoupled. Differences between documents and code can only be discovered through code tracing. Zero-shot ability is a core competitive point in differentiation with methods that rely on training. Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning can achieve 95 - 100% for Stack-type tasks (total 58.9%). BC-RNN fails completely (0%). The complexity of the task (multiple steps, delicate operations) is the main factor determining the success rate difference between VLA and traditional sequence modeling. The Pi0.5 LoRA model also requires at least 2 80GB GPUs (FSDP is a prerequisite). AI Usage Notes Effective Modes:
✓ Multi-layer code tracing (3 - 4 call stacks) to accurately identify the facts of STAIG architecture, providing evidence at the line level. This is more reliable than document analysis. ✓ Four-way parallel Agents are divided into tasks without overlap according to file collections. The large-scale migration of 65 symlinks can be completed within a single session. ✓ sacct step timestamps systematically analyze and correctly exclude misjudgments caused by AI operations, establishing an evidence chain of cause and effect for NODE_FAIL. ✓ Actively clarify vague requirements (whether the author’s search needs LLM evaluation) through AskUserQuestion. This accurately transforms product requirements into engineering solutions. ✓ It performs better than intuition in systematically verifying the correctness of experimental configurations regarding the source of the base model. Limitations:
✗ After implementing new functions, it has not been verified whether they are connected to the system startup link. Only unit tests are relied on, and integration layer checks are omitted (BackgroundCoordinator is not started). ✗ When designing an Agent-based system, mature implementations in the industry were not actively studied. It needs to be referenced after users clearly specify them, such as OpenClaw/GSD architecture patterns. ✗ Parameter settings lack experimental verification. The τ=0.10m is purely estimated, resulting in all critical_region label data being zero for an entire batch. ✗ The description of research contributions tends to focus on technical details rather than competitive advantages. The differentiated positioning of zero-shot needs to be clearly specified by users. ✗ The initial task arrangement tends to be conservative and serial. Opportunities for long-term GPU tasks and lightweight visualization tasks that can be executed parallelly are not actively identified. ✗ There is a lack of necessary resume/fresh-start intention confirmation before resource-intensive operations (GPU training). This may lead to the launch of fresh start covering checkpoints. Next Week’s Plans Next week’s core priorities: 1) Fix three bugs in CalendarPro’s context awareness. Remove confirmation words/chronological words from general_keywords, pass the last 3 conversation histories to _llm_classify, and reset GENERAL routing utterances. Complete the final loop from architecture upgrade to actual usability of the system. 2) Complete the visualization replay logic for pre-error trajectories (the storage layer is ready. Complete the initial state playback and frame rendering parts in the visualization script). 3) Monitor and evaluate zhaoganlong training results: The Diffusion Policy is expected to be completed in 2 - 4 days. Pi0.5 FSDP still requires about 31 hours. Start batch evaluation immediately after training completion. 4) Unlock LLaVA MPM training: Confirm the integrity of cxp2 user local llava-v1.5-7b. Start training by pointing to the local path through –model_name_or_path. 5) Calibrate the threshold of key areas in VLA-RoboTwin: After analyzing the actual distribution of ee_target_distances, re-set the τ value and collect critical_region label data again. 6) Officially fix the observation key bug in BC-RNN and run normal tasks for evaluation again. 7) Render demonstration videos (compare success/failure rates of Pi0.5 high SR tasks). Research Scout is ready for production and can start configuring regular paper discovery processes for active projects such as MIHD and error_recovery_benchmark.
