summary: This week, we conducted parallel efforts across three devices: DCC, Tianhe Supercomputer, and TzJsDesktop. The main projects include space transcriptomics research, robot operation training/ evaluation, AI personal assistants, and paper management tools. The MIHD project completed the full set of scGPT+UNI2 integration experiments (QFormer avg ARI=0.370, +117% compared to scGPT-only), and established a zero-shot cross-sample evaluation framework. Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning achieved an overall success rate of 58.9%, significantly outperforming BC-RNN (0%). The VLA model was quantitatively validated to demonstrate its advantages. CalendarPro made a significant leap from passive response to active decision-making, achieving perfect results in 321 tests. Additionally, BackgroundCoordinator’s critical integration defects were identified and fixed. Gadget Research Scout went from zero build to production-ready status within a single day, completing two-stage LLM paper evaluation pipelines and providing three research directions for further exploration. Error_recovery_benchmark completed the cleanup of all 65 symlinks and established infrastructure for visualizing error scenarios in MP4 format. The most important engineering lessons learned this week are: successful testing does not mean system availability (integration layers must be verified independently), and it is essential to study the architecture of mature projects before designing Agent-based systems.
title: 每周总结 2026-W10

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-08 Today’s Highlights What was done: Parallelly processed three projects across two devices: on the tianhe side, fully cleaned up all 65 symlinks in error_recovery_benchmark and migrated the code; created MP4 visualizations for coffee/stack/three_piece_assembly error scenarios; analyzed BC-RNN performance and developed a pre-error replay storage layer; implemented key region annotation systems for VLA-RoboTwin and fixed Vulkan rendering issues; on the TzJsDesktop side, added features such as author search, from-overview initialization, and early stopping due to duplicates for gadget research_scout. How it was done: For error_recovery_benchmark, 4-way parallel sub-agents were used to cover all migration stages. Slurm srun drove an53 to concurrently render MP4s on three idle A800 GPUs; VLA-RoboTwin implemented key region annotation using inheritance from template methods; for gadget research_scout, the author search branch was extended using a conference search architecture, and requirements were clarified through AskUserQuestion. What’s useful: The error_recovery_benchmark code library eliminated all indirect layers (all 127 tests passed), producing over 9 MP4s. Bottlenecks in BC-RNN were exposed during the grasping phase, while the pre-error storage layer was completed. VLA-RoboTwin datasets include frame-by-frame distance observations, and rendering can run properly on HPC headless nodes. The gadget tool now supports tracking specific researchers’ latest work by author. TzJsDesktop What was done: Added author search (–author), from-overview initialization, search and early stopping due to duplicates for gadget research_scout, and updated the CLAUDE.md document accordingly. How it was done: Mirrored the cache/CLI/function design of conference search, added search_arxiv_author() (au: precise query + optional keyword combination). After confirming requirements through AskUserQuestion, plans were formulated before implementation. What’s useful: The research tool supports tracking specific researchers’ papers and running a complete two-stage LLM evaluation pipeline, significantly reducing manual search costs. tianhe What was done: Led the completion of fully removing all 65 symlinks in error_recovery_benchmark and migrating code paths (Phases 1-9); created visualizations for coffee/stack/three_piece_assembly error scenarios; expanded the pre-error trajectory replay storage layer; implemented key region annotation systems for VLA-RoboTwin, recorded threshold calibration distances, and fixed Vulkan rendering issues. How it was done: 4-way parallel Claude sub-agents were used to cover all migration stages. The main thread directly handled Makefile/Documentation. Slurm srun drove an53 to concurrently render MP4s on three idle A800 GPUs. VLA-RoboTwin used _base_task.py template methods, with sub-teams overwriting distance calculation logic based on task type. What’s useful: Code library path consistency was 100%, all 127 unit tests passed, over 9 MP4s were generated, and BC-RNN failures concentrated in the grasping phase. VLA datasets included frame-by-frame ee_target_distances, and the collection script could run properly on monitorless nodes after Vulkan fixes were applied. On tianhe servers, large-scale refactoring of error_recovery_benchmark was completed (fully cleaning up 65 symlinks + migrating code paths), creating MP4s for multi-task error scenarios and developing pre-error replay storage layers. Meanwhile, VLA-RoboTwin key region annotation systems were implemented, threshold calibration distances recorded, and Vulkan rendering issues fixed. On local machines, features such as author search for gadget research_scout were added.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ error_recovery_benchmark: Remove all 65 symlinks and complete code path migration (Phases 1-9) — Plan to change all errorrameworks imports to error_benchmark.framework in 9 stages. Update script_utils, YAML configurations, argparse default values, checkpoint paths, Makefile, Phoenix/FLARE scripts, documentation, and sys.path parent chain depth. Finally, remove all 65 symlinks with all 127 unit tests passing. Plan to complete this in Phase 3 at 01:17, and implement it in Phase 2 at 01:40 using 4-way parallel sub-agents. AI also fixed two unexpected errors related to sys.path parent chain depth. ✅ VLA-RoboTwin: Implement key region annotation systems and record threshold calibration distances (10 tasks) — Add frame-by-frame critical_region binary tags for 10 robotic operation tasks (τ=0.10m, three strategies: static target/grasp/placement stacking) to prepare training data for pi0.5 CLS token classifier. Since the collected data had all critical_region values as 0 (threshold estimated too low), added get_ee_target_distances() for each task (4-8 distance variables per task) to record original distances for threshold calibration. 🔄 error_recovery_benchmark: Add pre-error context replay in visualization — Users requested videos to be replayed from 5 seconds before an error or at the start of an action, rather than neutral frames. Completed: added action_history collection in rollout_generator and initial_state saving in _save_scenes, extended NPZ storage in load_policy_scene_state, and expanded loading logic in load_policy_scene_state; actual replay logic for visualization scripts was not completed due to context exhaustion. ✅ gadget Research Scout: Expand features such as author search, from-overview initialization, and early stopping due to duplicates — Added –author parameter (search_arxiv_author(), au: precise query + optional keyword combination + cache support + –conference互斥检查, fully integrated both single-stage search and LLM evaluation modes); init –from-overview (LLM parses overview.md to extract project metadata); added known_ids for deduplication in search_arxiv() and 5 consecutive known papers terminated early; renamed weekly report to daily report; updated research/CLAUDE.md accordingly. ✅ error_recovery_benchmark: Generate MP4s for multi-task error scenarios (coffee/stack/three_piece_assembly) — coffee/stack (Phase 1): 9 MP4s generated by GPU6 in parallel, covering 6 types of errors such as grasp_miss/grasp_wrong_pose/tip_over. three_piece_assembly (Phase 2): Effective scenarios where injection mode was 0 (base was too stable), AI actively pivoted to visualize 158 natural error scenarios, producing 4 MP4s (308-496KB) on 3 GPUs in parallel, covering grasp_wrong_pose/premature_release/grasp_miss/overshoot. Implementation and Fixes ✅ VLA-RoboTwin: Fixed Vulkan rendering initialization failure in collect_data.sh — HPC headless nodes reported RuntimeError with sapien.SapienRenderer(). By referring to eval.sh from the same repository, added VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, and LD_LIBRARY_PATH as three Vulkan environment variables in collect_data.sh. Also located episode_num: 50 configuration in task_config/demo_clean.yml. 🔄 error_recovery_benchmark: Monitor multi-GPU parallel benchmark progress — GPU0 ran coffee tasks (rollout 99/200), GPU6 ran stack tasks (rollout 33/200). Existing scenarios included pick_place 743, stack_three 264, stack 169, and coffee/threading/three_piece_assembly 150+ each. ✅ error_recovery_benchmark: Improve CLAUDE.md and README.md — Removed redundant Project Documentation, Directory Notes, and Related Projects sections. Moved test fixture descriptions to more appropriate locations and fixed sections in README.md that still referenced old symlink paths. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Natural scenario visualization has only 10 neutral frames, making it impossible to show the actual movement process of strategies (pre-error trajectory data was not persisted during generation) Solution: Record the complete action_history and initial_state in capture_natural_errors of rollout_generator.py and store them in NPZ. When visualizing, replay from the initial_state or 5 seconds before an error.
Key Insight: Pre-error trajectory data must be actively persisted during generation; separate storage structures need to be designed for demo replay (with HDF5) and policy replay (which requires additional storage).
2. All critical_region data collected was 0: The τ=0.10m threshold is purely based on parameter estimation, which does not match the actual robot workspace size. Solution: Instead of adjusting parameters directly, record original ee_target_distances (4-8 target distance variables per task). Analyze the actual data distribution before calibrating the threshold.
Key Insight: The robot task space scale deviates significantly from intuition. Experimental-driven methods (observing data first, then setting thresholds) are more reliable than parameter estimation. Recording original distances is also a valuable diagnostic tool.
3. Generating 0 effective scenarios in injection mode: Base was too stable, and the 3-35N thrust force was insufficient to pass verification. Solution: Visualize 158 natural error scenarios, covering 6 types of actual failures. Produce 4 MP4s on 3 GPUs in parallel.
Key Insight: Injection parameters need to be calibrated specifically for each task. Actively pivoting when encountering obstacles is a reasonable strategy, without needing user instructions.
4. AI initially suggested waiting until the injection task was completed before generating visualizations, but did not actively identify opportunities for parallel execution. Solution: Users identified that visualization could be generated immediately during injection tasks. AI then inserted lightweight visualization tasks during periods of low GPU load.
Key Insight: Long-term GPU tasks and lightweight visualization tasks can run in parallel on the same GPU. Such opportunities should be actively identified rather than conservatively waiting in serial mode.
General Issues 5. GPU rendering environment configuration mismatch: MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID and CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES had physical numbers that did not match, or HPC headless nodes lacked Vulkan driver configurations leading to rendering initialization failures. Solution: Set MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID and CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES to the same physical number. Refer to eval.sh from the same repository and add VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, and LD_LIBRARY_PATH as environment variables in collect_data.sh.
Key Insight: EGL/Vulkan device configuration is independent of CUDA. Normalization of CUDA does not mean Vulkan/EGL are available. Solutions usually exist in other scripts from the same repository, so it’s better to reuse existing configurations.
6. Errors in .parent chain depth of sys.path.insert after symlink removal: Path.resolve() no longer folds symlinks, causing two pipeline scripts to fail to find the project root directory. Solution: Change .parent.parent to .parent.parent.parent and recalculate according to the actual directory hierarchy (pipeline/ → scripts/ → error_benchmark/ → project_root).
Key Insight: After removing symlinks, all paths that depend on folded symlinks must be rechecked thoroughly. This is a potential hidden issue that can be easily overlooked in large-scale migrations.
7. SSH remote execution script path errors: SSH defaults to the home directory, making it impossible to find project scripts. Multiple fine-tuning attempts failed. Solution: Use absolute paths for script paths in SSH commands and place the cd operation within the same bash -c string as the main command.
Key Insight: SSH connections do not inherit the local cwd. Absolute paths must be explicitly specified. Diagnosis should focus on the root cause rather than iterative fine-tuning.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Data-driven diagnosis vs Parameter Estimation Role Thought Human Users actually opened HDF5 to verify that all tags were 0, inferred a small threshold, and actively requested recording of original distances instead of direct parameter adjustment requests. AI AI designed τ=0.10m thresholds based on pre_grasp_dis parameter estimation, did not suggest collecting a small amount of data for verification first; after user feedback, switched to recording original distances. Difference Analysis: Users’ experimental-driven thinking (verification → diagnosis → adjustment) is more scientific than AI’s direct estimation. AI tends to provide straightforward solutions without the verification step.
Planning Precision and Parallel Execution in Symlink Cleaning Role Thought Human Users prepared a complete 9-stage plan in advance, precisely listing all affected files, replacement patterns, and import path boundaries, clearly distinguishing between import statements and general terms in comments. AI After receiving the plan, AI divided Phases 1-6 into 4-way parallel sub-agents for execution. The main thread directly handled Makefile/Documentation and actively identified and fixed 2 sys.path errors that were not listed in the plan. Difference Analysis: Humans are responsible for planning precision, while AI is responsible for executing parallelism and actively covering edge cases. Both complement each other to achieve efficient collaboration.
Identification of Parallel Execution Opportunities and Task Pivot Strategies Role Thought Human Users directly identified that visualization could be generated immediately during injection tasks, without serial waiting. Users expected the output of injection but accepted AI’s proposed alternative solutions. AI Initially suggested serial waiting, did not actively identify parallel opportunities. After failure in three_piece_assembly injection, it actively explained the reason and proposed a pivot (visualizing natural error scenarios), without waiting for user instructions. Difference Analysis: Users are more proactive in identifying parallel opportunities during task orchestration. AI’s pivoting strategy is active when dealing with failures, but its initial plan was conservative.
Translation from User Requirements to Engineering Solutions Role Thought Human Users proposed requirements from a product perspective (video should be replayed 5 seconds before an error; search and summarize a teacher’s article). The requirements were direct but did not specify implementation details. AI AI translated product requirements into engineering solutions (NPZ storage expansion + replay logic; two modes + mutually exclusive parameters + caching), and actively clarified ambiguous requirements through AskUserQuestion (whether author search requires LLM evaluation). Difference Analysis: Users provide WHAT, while AI designs HOW. Active confirmation steps transform vague requirements into more complete engineering solutions.
AI Limitations Important Limitations### General Limitations Repetitive error diagnosis is inefficient: The SSH path issue was only resolved after multiple fine-tuning steps to locate the root cause (absolute paths were required). Diagnosis should target the root cause rather than making incremental adjustments. Insufficient context management: The pre-error replay feature was interrupted due to context exhaustion before the visualization script could complete. Large-scale feature development should plan session segmentation points in advance. Today’s Achievements Key Achievements 4-way parallel sub-agents + direct processing of main threads: This allowed for the large-scale migration of 65 symlinks within a single session. The key was dividing agent tasks based on file collections without overlap, with the main thread handling non-parallelizable Makefile/Documentation work. Experimental-driven approach is better than parameter estimation: The robot task space scale differs significantly from intuition. Key parameters (such as distance thresholds) should be analyzed using small samples before setting them. The original records of ee_target_distances are valuable diagnostic tools. Pre-error trajectory data (action_history + initial_state) must be stored in NPZ during scenario generation; otherwise, visualization cannot reproduce the actual behavior of the strategy. Demo replay (with HDF5) and policy replay (requiring additional storage) should be distinguished in design. The physical characteristics of three_piece_assembly make the injection mode ineffective (base is too stable). Error scenarios must be collected using natural_capture. Natural error distributions (32% grasp_wrong_pose, 25% premature_release) reveal that BC-RNN’s main bottleneck lies in grasping rather than insertion phase. In inheritance systems, using the template method pattern (base class returns default values, subclass overrides as needed) is more consistent with the open-close principle compared to using conditional branches in get_obs(). New tasks only need to implement subclass methods without modifying the core process. arXiv supports precise author queries by specifying au:“Author Name” and can combine it with keywords (au:“Name” AND (kw1 OR kw2)). This is an effective way for the system to track researchers’ latest work. Practical Achievements GPU rendering environment configuration is independent: EGL device IDs must match CUDA physical numbers. Vulkan drivers (VK_ICD_FILENAMES) are independent of CUDA. If CUDA works, Vulkan can also work. Solutions often exist in other scripts within the same repository; prioritize reuse. After removing symlinks, the depth of the sys.path parent chain needs to be recalculated based on the actual directory hierarchy. Path.resolve() no longer folds symlinks. The original parent chains that worked before removing symlinks have insufficient depth after removal, which is a potential issue in large-scale migrations. Session Summary Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 GPU monitoring, multi-task error scenario visualization, full cleanup of 65 symlinks (planning → implementation), BC-RNN analysis, and pre-error replay development 00:46:15.559 | claude_code There were 6 sessions throughout the day covering four main topics: ① GPU monitoring and coffee/stack visualization (first segment): Checking GPU progress, users corrected AI serial waiting recommendations, parallel generation of 9 MP4s covering 6 error types on GPU6. During this period, issues with SSH working directory were resolved. ② CLAUDE.md improvement (second segment): Deleted 3 redundant sections and fixed an old path reference in README.md. ③ Full cleanup of 65 symlinks (third segment planning + second segment implementation): Detailed 9-stage plan in the third segment, full implementation completed by 4-way parallel sub-agents (127 tests all passed). AI additionally corrected 2 unexpected sys.path parent chain depth errors. ④ BC-RNN three_piece_assembly visualization and pre-error replay (second segment): Injection mode had 0 effective scenarios, AI actively pivoted to natural error visualization (4 MP4s); started implementing pre-error replay storage layer, interrupted due to context exhaustion before the visualization replay logic could complete.
VLA-RoboTwin ✅ Implementation of key area heuristic annotation system (10 tasks), distance record calibration, Vulkan rendering fixes 03:16:44.908 | claude_code There were 3 valid sessions: ① Key area annotation design and implementation: Reading _base_task.py and implementing 10 tasks, designing three types of annotations based on end-effector-target distance (τ=0.10m), implementing get_critical_region_label() template method, all syntax verifications passed. ② Distance record expansion: Users found that critical_region was always 0, indicating the threshold was too small. They requested recording original distances. Added get_ee_target_distances() for 10 tasks (4-8 distance variables per task), and fixed the dictionary self-resolution design flaw in place_dual_shoes. ③ Vulkan rendering fixes and configuration localization: Added 3 Vulkan environment variables in collect_data.sh to fix headless rendering failures, located episode_num: 50 configuration in task_config/demo_clean.yml.
gadget Research Scout ✅ Initialization of from-overview, search for duplicate early stopping, complete pipeline for author search 00:45:55.000 | claude_code There were 3 sessions: ① from-overview and duplicate early stopping: init –from_overview (LLM parses overview.md), weekly report → daily report renamed, search_arxiv() known_ids deduplicated with 5 consecutive known papers terminating early, CLAUDE.md updated synchronously. ② Requirement confirmation and planning: /init reviewed CLAUDE.md to add new feature documentation, confirmed that author search required LLM evaluation mode through AskUserQuestion, planned –author parameter scheme. ③ Author search implementation: Added search_arxiv_author() (au: query + keyword combination + lookback_days filtering + independent cache naming), cmd_search() and cmd_report() added author branches and –conference互斥检查, syntax checks passed, –help confirmed to work.

Life Copilot / CalendarPro### ✅ CalendarPro Intent Classification System Root Cause Analysis and Cross-Project Architecture Specification Design claude_code Completed the root cause analysis of the CalendarPro intent classification system, designed an A-D four-stage specification framework; introduced the OpenClaw four-layer context window management model as a cross-project reference. title: 错误日志 2026-03-07

content:
Key Achievements Achievement Description Resolving three consecutive bugs in RoboTwin evaluation pipeline ① SAPIEN Vulkan rendering failed in headless Docker environment; matched version (535.104.12) of NVIDIA driver library extracted and fixed using VK_ICD_FILENAMES and other environment variables. ② FrozenInstanceError resolved by replacing direct assignment with dataclasses.replace(). ③ Path duplication in norm_stats.json corrected by loading from the assets directory instead of using asset_id as a subdirectory name. Developing complete training scripts for Phoenix/FLARE covering all 9 MimicGen tasks Separated framework directory structure (symbolic links for 77GB data, 155GB to 37MB), created 6 training scripts (data generation → transformation → training → evaluation pipeline). Added missing warmup configurations in CALVIN and error_recovery_benchmark. Normalizing space transcript data for ContraVAE + STHD Traced the processing chain of all_region/adata_8um.h5ad (4×4 binning → normalize_total → log1p → HVG). Confirmed raw UMI counts were used throughout the STHD pipeline, with normalization done outside STHD. Obsm[‘spatial’] units were determined to be full-resolution pixel coordinates (1px ≈ 0.274μm). Writing a 1674-line Chinese tutorial for CalendarPro Extracted key code file information using 4 parallel Agents, generated 1674 lines of Markdown with detailed instructions on configuration items, intent types, and EventBus events. This helped fill gaps in project documentation and provided a clear plan for subsequent fixes. Investigating context-sensitive bugs in CalendarPro Discord bot Identified three root causes: ① general_keywords containing time-related terms and confirmation words; ② llm_classify not receiving conversation history; ③ GENERAL semantic routing utterances containing confirmation words. A fix plan was developed but implementation has yet to begin. Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy Task Description ✅ Resolving three consecutive bugs in RoboTwin evaluation pipeline Extracted matching versions of NVIDIA driver libraries for Vulkan rendering. Fixed errors by replacing direct assignments with dataclasses.replace(). Path duplication in norm_stats.json corrected by loading from the assets directory instead of using asset_id as a subdirectory name. ✅ Developing complete training scripts for Phoenix+FLARE covering all 9 tasks Separated framework directory structure (symbolic links for 77GB data, 155GB to 37MB), added missing warmup configurations in CALVIN and error_recovery_benchmark. GPU resources were allocated for an49+an53 hosts. ✅ Normalizing space transcript data for ContraVAE + STHD Traced the processing chain of all_region/adata_8um.h5ad (4×4 binning → normalize_total → log1p → HVG). Confirmed raw UMI counts were used throughout the STHD pipeline, with normalization done outside STHD. Obsm[‘spatial’] units were determined to be full-resolution pixel coordinates (1px ≈ 0.274μm). ✅ Writing a 1674-line Chinese tutorial for CalendarPro Extracted key code file information using 4 parallel Agents, generated 1674 lines of Markdown with detailed instructions on configuration items, intent types, and EventBus events. This helped fill gaps in project documentation and provided a clear plan for subsequent fixes. 🔄 Investigating context-sensitive bugs in CalendarPro Discord bot Identified three root causes: ① general_keywords containing time-related terms and confirmation words; ② llm_classify not receiving conversation history; ③ GENERAL semantic routing utterances containing confirmation words. A fix plan was developed but implementation has yet to begin. Implementation and Fixes Task Description ✅ Initializing and improving CLAUDE.md for robot projects (CALVIN + error_recovery_benchmark) Created CLAUDE.md for CALVIN, covering installation, training, evaluation commands, and MCIL model architecture. Reduced the number of lines in error_recovery_benchmark from 225 to 167. MulticoreTSNE installation remained blocked due to incompatible CMake versions. ✅ RoboTwin data collection architecture and task simulation Explained the two-stage pipeline of collect_data.sh, including Collect Seed for successful trajectories and Collect Data for deterministic replay of HDF5 data. Place_dual_shoes.py explained the execution chain from setup_demo to play_once (Curobo/MPlib motion planning, take_dense_action step-by-step, dual-arm coordination mechanism). Problems and Solutions Key Issues Issue Solution 1. SAPIEN reported a Render Error in the headless Docker container. The bare except option hid the actual error, and the mismatched driver version (535.104.12) with the apt repository version (535.288.01) caused an invalid installation. Four steps to fix: ① Modify bare except to expose the real traceback; ② Confirm it was a missing NVIDIA Vulkan ICD (libGLX_nvidia.so); ③ Extract all libnvidia-*.so user-mode GL libraries from the official NVIDIA package 535.104.12 .run and place them in the user directory; ④ Fix the rendering issue by injecting environment variables like VK_ICD_FILENAMES, __EGL_VENDOR_LIBRARY_FILENAMES, and LD_LIBRARY_PATH. 2. Users pointed to incorrect h5ad files (raw counts with values/integer features not matching descriptions). Direct toarray() on large sparse matrices caused OOM (exit code 137). Identify file path mismatches by comparing X value characteristics (integers and ranges). Use sc.read_h5ad(backed=‘r’) in low-memory mode for local sampling. Once the user confirmed, locate the correct file all_region/adata_8um.h5ad. 3. CalendarPro Discord bot lost context when following messages (“What time are you arranged?” and “OK”). It was misclassified as GENERAL or triggered a new conversation. Three fixes: ① Trim general_keywords to remove time-related terms and confirmation words; ② Let llm_classify receive the last three conversations; ③ Reset GENERAL semantic routing and remove confirmation word classes from utterances. 4. OpenPI config.py lacked three FLARE warmup training configurations (coffee_task_D1, stack_three_task_D1, three_piece_assembly_task_D1). Added corresponding add_finetune_config() and add_inference_config() calls to the CONFIGS list, adding a total of six new configurations. General Issues Issue Solution 5. Three independent code bugs: Phoenix/FLARE directly copied 77GB data, wasting disk space; policy_config.py assigned frozen dataclass values directly; norm_stats.json had path duplication due to asset_id being used as a subdirectory name. Symbolically link large directories (155GB → 37MB); use dataclasses.replace() to create and modify copies; load from the assets directory directly to avoid error paths. 6. MulticoreTSNE was incompatible with CMake version 3.26.4, and pip’s CMake version 3.18.4 was not effective. Proposed annotating optional dependencies in requirements.txt. The user refused, so the issue remains unresolved. Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Role Thinking Human User directly knows about the headless Docker environment and provides specific failure cases at critical moments. Can quickly identify data anomaly (h5ad file values/integers) and guide AI. Provides concrete failure examples as starting points for diagnosis. AI AI uses systematic methods like exposing real errors through bare except, checking system status, and comparing value characteristics to narrow down the problem scope. In the Render Error case, it failed because it didn’t check the driver version first. Implementation Level Role Thinking Human User repeatedly rejected AI’s ExitPlanMode and bypassed planning approval processes by directly providing instructions or clear commands. Required a thorough understanding of changes before granting authority. AI AI tends to execute plans immediately after creation. Asked questions during the planning phase that could have been inferred from context, such as output paths and target audiences. This increased unnecessary interaction rounds. AI Limitations Important Limitations Checked critical version information only after performing operations in the system environment (e.g., installing NVIDIA rendering libraries without checking the version first). This led to additional diagnostic steps after installing incorrect versions. Similarly, for HPC big data files, memory safety mode wasn’t used by default, and direct toarray() caused OOM. General Limitations Lack of session state awareness: Unable to recognize that the same task had been completed in previous sessions (e.g., 17:37, 20:00, 20:02). Task output returned “No task found” when querying for background tasks, indicating reliability issues with tools. Complex dependency scenarios require multiple rounds of iteration to uncover all missing items. It took several attempts to find all missing.so files for Vulkan dependencies, ultimately resolving them by copying all libnvidia-*.so files. This reflects AI’s limitations in predicting dependencies. Today’s Takeaways Key Takeaways- The STHD pipeline uses raw UMI counts throughout the process. The model directly treats X as Poisson observations. During VisiumHD binning from 2μm to 8μm, counts are aggregated using sum (total UMI is conserved). For HVG selection, flavor=‘seurat’ is used on log1p data. It’s important to distinguish between the internal processing links of STHD and those of the standard scanpy preprocessing links. When implementing Phoenix+FLARE training for 9 MimicGen tasks, it’s necessary to differentiate between two paradigms: Phoenix with a single model for multiple tasks vs FLARE with independent Pi0.5 LoRA per task (additional warmup data generation is required to generate R45T03 parameters and a 5-stage pipeline). Large HPC datasets should be shared through symbolic links rather than copied. Fixing the complete solution for SAPIEN/Vulkan rendering in the headless Docker environment: Extract the user-mode GL library from the NVIDIA official.run driver package (no need to install kernel modules) to the user directory. Inject it through the VK_ICD_FILENAMES and LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variables. No root privileges are required. The driver version must be strictly consistent with the kernel module version in /proc/driver/nvidia/version. Key points for designing Discord bot context awareness: Keyword pre-checking should not截断 all messages containing specific words statically. Verbs like “ok” and time-related phrases like “what time is it” should have clear contextual meanings in follow-up messages. LLM classification must receive the conversation history to correctly process follow-up messages. The training set for semantic routing utterances should not include general verbs. RoboTwin’s two-stage data collection mode: In stage one, only lightweight simulations are used to find successful seeds (motion planning trajectories are saved). In stage two, deterministic seeds are used to replay and collect complete multimodal HDF5 training data. This separation ensures data quality (only successful trajectories are collected), supports breakpoint continuation of data collection, and both stages can be run independently. Practical Achievements Python bare except is a high-risk debugging trap: In the rendering framework, critical information like RuntimeError may be hidden. During debugging, it’s better to change bare except to except Exception as e: traceback.print_exc(). This is often the first step in finding the root cause. The cluster setproxy.sh script routes traffic to the internal proxy server (172.16.31.200:3138) for accessing external networks by setting http_proxy/https_proxy/git proxy. It must be executed with source to take effect in the current shell. Shared proxy bandwidth decreases as the number of concurrent users increases. It’s recommended to use it during off-peak hours for large file downloads. Session Summary ContraVAE + STHD (Spatial Transcriptomics) ✅ Traceability of data normalization processes for two spatial transcriptomics projects 16:37:59.200 | claude_code Users asked about the normalization method for data.adata.X, the nature of layers[‘counts’], the method for HVG selection, and the unit of spatial coordinates. In the ContraVAE session, AI found that users were referring to the wrong file (raw counts). After providing processing scripts, it was possible to trace back processdata.ipynb to the complete link from 4×4 binning → normalize_total → log1p → HVG. The STHD session analyzed sthdio.py/model.py/refscrna.py and confirmed that the pipeline uses raw UMI counts throughout, while HVG uses FC thresholds in a RCTD style instead of sc.pp.highly_variable_genes. Both projects have completed the traceability of the complete normalization process.
Motion-based Self-Reflection (Phoenix/FLARE) ✅ Separation of framework directories and development of training scripts for all 9 tasks 16:41:37.220 | claude_code The Phoenix/FLARE framework was separated from the tangzijia workspace through symbolic links. 77GB of training data and 1GB of checkpoints were replaced with symbolic links. The total size decreased from 155GB to 37MB+368KB. CLAUDE.md files were created for each framework. Then, training scripts were developed for all 9 MimicGen tasks: 6 scripts covered the entire pipeline from data generation → conversion → training → evaluation. Three missing warmup configurations were added in config.py, and a GPU resource allocation plan was designed (an49+an53).
RoboTwin ✅ Fixing three consecutive bugs in the evaluation pipeline and explaining the data collection architecture 06:39:44.361 | claude_code Three consecutive problems were fixed in eval.sh: ①After exposing bare except, SAPIEN Vulkan rendering failed. The user was able to fix it by extracting the user-mode GL library from the NVIDIA 535.104.12 driver package and injecting it through environment variables. ②Frozen dataclass direct assignment caused FrozenInstanceError. It was fixed using dataclasses.replace(). ③The path of norm_stats.json was duplicated (assets/norm_stats.json/norm_stats.json). It was fixed by loading directly from the assets directory. In addition, the user was explained about the two-stage data collection architecture and the simulation process for the place_dual_shoes task (Curobo/MPlib motion planning, take_dense_action frame-by-frame stepping).
CALVIN 🔄 Creating CLAUDE.md and handling errors in installing MulticoreTSNE 02:09:21.620 | claude_code By using multi-agent parallelism to explore the CALVIN code library (MCIL model, Hydra configuration system, multi-view observation system), a CLAUDE.md was created that includes installation, training, and evaluation commands. MulticoreTSNE failed to build due to CMake version incompatibility. AI suggested deleting this optional dependency, but the user refused. The problem remains blocked.
Error Recovery Benchmark ✅ In-depth analysis of CLAUDE.md, planning, and implementation of improvements (225 lines → 167 lines) 20:23:17.000 | claude_code Two sessions were conducted: In the first session, four improvement plans were made through multi-agent parallelism (streamlining Commands, adding PYTHONPATH paths, adding test examples, compressing Related Project). However, the user interrupted before completing them. In the second session, all improvements were implemented according to the plan. CLAUDE.md was streamlined from 225 lines to 167 lines, while all key information was retained.
CalendarPro 🔄 Writing a complete Chinese usage guide and investigating Discord bot context-awareness bugs 00:51:21.561 | claude_code Two main tasks were completed: ①After the user abandoned the planning session, the user directly used the planned text as an instruction. AI extracted code library information through four parallel agents and created docs/TUTORIAL.md (1674 lines, 19 chapters), which included a reference table of configuration items, 24 types of intentions, and 21 EventBus events. ②Investigation into Discord bot context loss issues across messages identified three root causes (pre-checking keywords truncation, LLM not passing conversation history, semantic routing containing confirmation words). A repair plan was developed to be left for later use.
Token Usage Overview Metric Value Total Tokens 29,608,728 Input Tokens 82,875 Output Tokens 82,462 Cache Creation 1,818,007 Cache Reads 27,625,384 Cache Hit Rate 93.8% Total Cost (USD) $17.4820 Model Details Model Input Output Cache Creation Cache Reads Cost Percentage claude-opus-4-6 6,533 33,941 935,222 16,426,841 $14.9397 85.5% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 76,342 48,521 882,785 11,198,543 $2.5423 14.5% Device Usage Device Total Tokens Input Output Cost DCC 639,003 2,953 1,904 $0.9898 tianhe 19,735,847 73,329 52,047 $11.2305 TzJsDesktop 9,233,878 6,593 28,511 $5.2618
summary: 三台设备同日进行并行推进：DCC完成了ContraVAE与STHD空间转录组数据归一化处理的工作，同时tianhe修复了RoboTwin评估流程中的三个连续问题（Vulkan渲染、数据类冻结、路径重复），并完成了Phoenix/FLARE全任务训练脚本套件的开发。TzJsDesktop编写了关于CalendarPro的1674行中文使用教程，并找到了Discord bot在跨消息上下文丢失方面的三大根本原因。
title: 错误日志 2026-03-06

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-05 Today’s Highlights Done: Parallelized MIHD spatialomics benchmark tests across three devices, deployment of zhaoganlong robot framework for multi-task training, fix of Openpi-moe training quality issues, and large-scale architecture upgrade of CalendarPro personal assistant system. How it was done: Utilizing techniques such as multi-Agent parallel coding, background tasks, SSH+tmux remote cluster management, deep code tracing, offline cluster dependency cloning, and pytest automation verification to handle tasks ranging from HPC production training to product system architecture. What’s achieved: Obtained quantitative results for MIHD cross-sample zero-shot evaluation; established a repeatable training foundation for zhaoganlong’s 9 tasks; eliminated multiple production-level issues in Openpi-moe and CalendarPro; CalendarPro achieved an architectural leap from passive response to active decision-making, with all 321 tests passing. DCC Done: Completed all technical tasks related to the MIHD project: RM-IDEAL cross-sample benchmark (151673↔151508), refinement of the project’s zero-shot narrative framework, and exploration of GPU Sinkhorn acceleration solutions. How it was done: Executed the benchmark script using a conda General environment, established a zero-shot differentiation positioning through multiple iterations with Claude, and commissioned an agent to analyze OT acceleration solutions. What’s achieved: Completed a two-way 7-layer benchmark test (excellent in Layer_1/5, negative correlation between Layer_3/6 revealing limitations of intermediate layers), establishing a research core narrative distinct from STAIG training dependency methods. tianhe Done: Remote-controlled deployment of zhaoganlong Self-Reflection framework for 9-task training on an53; local installation of curobo, fix of Openpi-moe normalization issues, and planning and execution of Phoenix/FLARE code library separation. How it was done: Managed an53 processes using SSH+tmux, gradually resolving issues such as CLIP absence, Pi0.5 OOM, symlink paths, and LLaVA model absence; code tracing revealed a silent skip mechanism for apply_tree; rsync was used to batch separate code. What’s achieved: Diffusion Policy (GPU 0) and Pi0.5 (GPU 2+3 FSDP) ran successfully; the Openpi-moe normalization pipeline was fully fixed; RefineVLA could use curobo CUDA extensions; the Phoenix/FLARE separation foundation was established. TzJsDesktop Done: Upgraded CalendarPro to a personal intelligent assistant: completed overall planning, implementation of 16 new service files (Phases 1-3), externalization and automatic augmentation of utterances, fix of BackgroundCoordinator startup issues, completion of 9 Discord handlers, elimination of 16 silent anomalies, and comprehensive quality audit. How it was done: Referred to OpenClaw/get-shit-done architecture patterns, using a 4-Agent parallel coding strategy, and verified quality through pytest (321 tests) and systematic grep audits. What’s achieved: The system has the ability to discover tasks autonomously, execute in phases, and learn preferences; eliminated key production issues such as BackgroundCoordinator not starting, intention routing gaps, and silent anomaly blackholes. Parallelized four projects across DCC/tianhe/TzJsDesktop devices throughout the day: DCC completed MIHD multimodal spatialomics cross-sample benchmark and established a zero-shot narrative framework; tianhe deployed zhaoganlong’s 9-task training pipeline and started two rounds of training, fixed Openpi-moe normalization issues, and advanced Phoenix/FLARE code library separation; TzJsDesktop upgraded CalendarPro to a personal intelligent assistant with autonomous perception and multi-Agent orchestration capabilities (321 tests passed)
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Complete design plan for CalendarPro personal assistant system — Referred to OpenClaw (EventBus/CronScheduler/Plugin patterns) and get-shit-done (STATE.md persistent memory/ContextAssembler/multi-Agent division of labor) to develop a complete upgrade plan: 5 major goals with 31 sub-goals, 19 new files + 8 modified files, 5 implementation sequences, and a three-level goal hierarchy. ✅ Implementation of CalendarPro Phases 1-3 core systems (16 new service files) — Created 16 new files such as GapAnalyzer/AutonomousExecutor/SituationMonitor/ReminderEvaluator/GoalTracker/WaveExecutor/RecommendationEngine/PreferenceLearner/SleepService, modified 21 locations in EventBus/Config/Models/BackgroundCoordinator/ContextAssembler, and resolved 68 unit tests after configuring pytest-asyncio. ✅ MIHD cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark test (151673↔151508, PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion) — Conducted two-way cross-sample benchmarks on two DLPFCs for the PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion fusion embedding, calculating Spearman correlations, Precision@K, and Same-label rates for 7 layer tags. Layer_1/5 performed excellently (Spearman 0.42-0.66, SL@50 reached 0.94-1.0), while Layer_3/6 showed negative correlation, with P@K zero for all layers. ✅ Fix of Openpi-moe norm_stats/prev_actions normalization issues — Discovered a missing key in apply_tree(strict=False) silent skip mechanism, as well as scale inconsistency caused by splicing unnormalized histActions with normalized actions in VAE. Modified compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect prev Actions and write statistics, using a backward-compatible design. ✅ Externalization of CalendarPro utterances and automatic augmentation — Migrated 452 hard-coded utterances to data/intent_utterances.json, created a new UtteranceAugmenter that automatically learns from mismatch logs and adds new utterances, registered daily 2AM augmentation tasks, processed 7 existing mismatches, with 48 tests passed. ✅ Fix of CalendarPro BackgroundCoordinator startup issues and completion of 9 Discord handlers — Added coordinator.start_all() in discord_bot.py’s on_ready() method to make background services like GapAnalyzer actually run; added complete handler methods and routing branches for 9 IntentTypes such as SET_GOAL/QUERY_GOALS/LOG_MEAL. ✅ Refinement of MIHD project’s five-sentence core narrative framework — Established three core selling points through three rounds of iterations with Claude: focus on zero-shot, essential contrast with STAIG training dependency methods, and clinical vision for cross-patient patch query knowledge transfer. ✅ Fix of zhaoganlong data preparation scripts and execution of 9-task full pipeline — Modified 4 data preparation scripts (enabled 9 tasks, removed pdb breakpoints, fixed .testc. naming bugs, fixed h5py append writing), and 2 JSON mapping files; completed a full 4-step pipeline on an53 (5Hz annotation → speed dataset → 1,034,176 LLaVA JSON entries → approximately 1 million RGB images). 🔄 Launch of zhaoganlong Diffusion Policy training (an53 GPU 0) — Single-card training, loss reduced from 1.16 to 0.62 per step, about 10s/step, expected to be completed in 2-4 days. 🔄 Launch of zhaoganlong Pi0.5 multi-task training (an53 GPU 2+3 FSDP) — FSDP 2-card training, 24.5k/100k steps per step, about 1.5s/step, expected to take another 31 hours. ✅ Fix of CalendarPro’s 16 silent anomalies and cleanup of executor dead code — Replaced all except Exception: pass in 6 files such as situation_monitor/autonomous_executor/agent_registry with logged error handling; removed 4 lines of dead loop code in executor.py, implemented stub functions for get_progress() and learn_from_history(). 🔄 Exploration of optimal transmission GPU Sinkhorn acceleration scheme for RM-IDEAL — Analyzed the existing scipy EMD serial calculation bottleneck (O(N³) complexity per spot), commissioned an agent to design a GPU batch acceleration scheme based on Sinkhorn approximation. ❌ zhaoganlong LLaVA MPM training — lacked liuhaotian/llava-v1.5-7b base model, no network access available in the cluster. Local copies of cpx2 users were found, to be confirmed for integrity before using –model_name_or_path to point to local paths. 🔄 Separation of Phoenix/FLARE code library (tianhe) — Separated zhaoganlong mixed research library into two independent projects: Phoenix (motion command framework) and FLARE (reset skill learning), established shared deps, 789G+ mimicgen and 245G openpi data linked to the original archive via symlinks. Rsync replication is in progress. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Comprehensive quality audit of CalendarPro and expansion of test suite (321 tests) — Created conftest.py using parallel 3 sub-Agents, added 20 new test files (125 new tests), covering new files/modified methods/integration tests; system searched the entire TODO/FIXME/NotImplementedError database and conducted a special audit of silent anomalies, increasing the total number of tests from 196 to 321. ✅ Installation of curobo in the RefineVLA conda environment — Resolved issues with non-standard CUDA header paths (targets/x86_64-linux/include), successfully compiled through CPLUS_INCLUDE_PATH, and verified normal CUDA extension loading. Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. All background services of CalendarPro (GapAnalyzer/AutonomousExecutor/ReminderEvaluator) have never been run in a production environment: setup_hook and main.py did not call BackgroundCoordinator.start_all(). Solution: Added an await coordinator.start_all() call in the discord_bot.py’s on_ready() method.
Key Insight: Registration patterns + lifecycle management can lead to silent failures where “registered but never started”; 196 unit tests passed without detecting this integration flaw, indicating that test coverage does not equal system availability.
2. On line 114 of zhaoganlong create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py, the save_path contains a .testc后缀, but downstream create_speed_dataset.py reads it without this suffix, resulting in silent data loss; when rerunning with h5py append writing mode, create_group reports a ValueError. Solution: Changed _adjust_llava_motion.testc.hdf5 to _adjust_llava_motion.hdf5; changed h5py append writing (‘a’) to overwrite writing (‘w’) to ensure idempotency.
Key Insight: Inconsistent file names in upstream and downstream scripts can silently skip data without an error, making it one of the most hidden bugs in a pipeline; HDF5 training data generation scripts should use write mode rather than append mode.
3. In Openpi-moe, norm_stats.json lacks the prev_actions key but training does not report errors; simultaneously, in VAE, unnormalized histActions and normalized actions are spliced directly, causing scale inconsistency. Solution: Modified compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect the presence of prev Actions, adding RunningStats if present and writing it into norm_stats.json; regenerate statistical files with prev Actions.
Key Insight: apply_tree(strict=False) in transforms.py遍历数据键而不是 norm_stats 键, silently skipping missing keys—new training features must be updated simultaneously with normalization scripts, otherwise it will affect training quality due to silent scale mismatch.
4. All 452 utterances in CalendarPro are hard-coded; the accumulated LLM correction records in data/intent_mismatches.jsonl have never been used as training signals. Solution: Externalize them into JSON files, implement UtteranceAugmenter that automatically learns from mismatch logs, register daily augmentation tasks, and clear used mismatch records after processing.
Key Insight: AI system’s mismatch records are free annotation data that should be automatically reverted to the improvement loop; externalization of utterances + automatic augmentation is a low-cost continuous learning mechanism.
5. The 16 except Exception: pass silent anomalies in CalendarPro are distributed across new services, masking all runtime errors; the execute_step() loop body in executor.py only has pass, and get_progress() always returns None, effectively disconnecting the entire Agent execution chain. Solution: Replace all silent anomalies with except Exception as e: logger.error(...); remove dead code, implement real step distribution logic and progress tracking functions.
Key Insight: Silent failures at the state tracking layer and event bus layer are much more harmful than ordinary business layers—services seem to be running but all errors are swallowed up, making monitoring and debugging impossible; new features pass unit tests but their empty implementations at integration points disconnect the entire chain.
6. Pi0.5 experienced OOM on a single A800 80GB card (model + activation values require ~64GB+), and relative_to ValueError occurred due to incorrect symlink resolve path during startup. Solution: Add –fsdp-devices 2 parameter to split the model across GPU 2+3; set OPENPI_DATA_HOME to point to the actual cache directory to bypass pathlib.resolve() for symlink resolution.
Key Insight: Even using LoRA, Pi0.5 requires at least 2 80GB GPUs, and FSDP is a necessary condition rather than an optimization measure; openpi’s get_cache_dir() uses pathlib.resolve() to resolve symlinks, which must be explicitly set through environment variables to specify the cache root path.
7. In MIHD cross-sample evaluation, negative Spearman correlations were observed in Layer_3 and Layer_6 (ρ≈-0.21～-0.36), with P@K zero for all layers, coexisting with significantly positive Spearman values. Solution: Negative correlations are identified as data characteristics that blur boundaries between adjacent layers in the embedding space (not code errors); coexistence of P@K=0 and Spearman>0.4 is a reasonable phenomenon—both indicators measure exact set overlap (extremely strict) and overall ranking monotonicity.
Key Insight: Zero-shot fusion embeddings can be effective globally but lack precise positioning capabilities; Layer_1/5 perform excellently due to their unique structure, while intermediate transition layers are inherent weaknesses; do not deny one with just one indicator.
8. In CalendarPro, the 9 IntentTypes (SET_GOAL/LOG_MEAL/REVIEW_DECISIONS) have no corresponding processors in the Discord Bot, and user-related instructions default to the original response. Solution: Added an elif branch in discord_bot.py’s handle_intent to implement corresponding handle_xxx method calls for GoalTracker/DietService/ThoughtIncubator services.
Key Insight: The intent routing layer and model layer have been updated, but the view layer (Bot Handler) has not been synchronized—this is a typical multi-layer update inconsistency issue that cannot be automatically detected in end-to-end integration testing.
9. An53 cluster has no internet access, and liuhaotian/llava-v1.5-7b required for LLaVA MPM training cannot be downloaded, with the proxy being unavailable (503). Solution: Temporarily blocked; found that cpx2 users have local copies, to be confirmed by pointing –model_name_or_path to a local path; missing dependencies like CLIP are solved by directly copying site-packages from conda environments of the same Python version.
Key Insight: Model sharing among cluster users is an essential offline HPC collaboration mode; finding large model resources should be a standard preparation step; installing offline cond### Solution:
Change __init__.py to lazy import mode (only keeping __all__), unify the patch path to src.config.get_settings; install pytest-asyncio and configure asyncio_mode="auto" in pyproject.toml.
Key Insights: The eager import in Python __init__.py immediately triggers the entire dependency chain when the module is loaded; pytest-asyncio requires an explicit configuration of mode=auto to automatically handle all async tests.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Judgment of Diferential Competitive Positioning for Research Projects Role Thinking Human The user clearly pointed out three core selling points that were missing in the initial AI draft: zero-shot was the focus, a fundamental comparison with STAIG training dependencies, and the clinical vision of immediate consultation for new patient slices. AI The AI generated descriptions that were technically accurate but lacked strategic insight, focusing on the systematic nature of the benchmark framework without highlighting the competitive differences in zero-shot Difference Analysis: Humans clearly identified the real differentiating selling points and application scenarios; the competitive positioning framework contributed by research requires human leadership, while AI tends to describe technical details rather than competitive advantages.
Active Reference to Mature External Implementations Before Architecture Design Role Thinking Human After AI started designing the butler system, the user actively pointed out two excellent reference frameworks, OpenClaw and get-shit-done, asking to learn the architecture pattern before optimizing the solution. AI The AI directly designed based on the existing CodeBase of CalendarPro, without actively proposing research on external reference projects. Difference Analysis: Humans have a systematic thinking of actively introducing external references; AI tends to work within known information boundaries, and user guidance significantly improves the quality of the final solution.
Active Identification of Code Quality and Production Usability Audit Role Thinking Human During the implementation process by AI, the user actively discovered that utterances could be externalized; after all tests passed, the user asked for a “check which problems remain unresolved,” and identified serious integration issues such as BackgroundCoordinator not being started. AI The AI focuses on current task goals when in execution mode, without actively identifying hard-coded utterances; it considers the task completed after testing and does not initiate quality audits. Difference Analysis: Humans have the engineering intuition of “passing tests does not mean production availability” and a global review consciousness; AI’s cognitive boundaries are determined by test coverage, making it unable to perceive integration and architecture issues outside of tests.
Document-Driven Strategy for Large-Scale Implementations Role Thinking Human Humans invest a lot of effort in preparing high-quality architectural documents during the design phase (detailing dependencies for each sub-target, EventBus events, registration methods, test requirements), treating AI as an execution engine. AI The AI first explores existing code patterns to confirm infrastructure readiness, then uses four parallel background agents to handle different file modifications. Difference Analysis: Human’s pre-designed approach results in almost zero rework for AI; humans provide product intuition and architectural boundaries, while AI provides parallel execution efficiency—the overall efficiency is highest when the two roles are clearly defined.
Hidden Constraints of HPC Clusters and Perception of Code Base Meta-Structures Role Thinking Human Humans know that the current user only has 4 GPU quotas on an53, and correct this when AI plans with full 8 cards; actively identify that the code base mixes Phoenix and FLARE from two different sources. AI The AI observes through nvidia-smi that all 8 cards are idle, defaulting to allocating resources based on 8 cards; it does not actively recognize the mixed sub-project structure of the code base. Difference Analysis: AI can only perceive explicit information output by tools and cannot infer hidden knowledge such as scheduling strategies, quota constraints, and project ownership; after being asked, AI can systematically output structures, but the initial framework is provided by humans.
Philosophy of Dialogic System Testing: Integration Testing vs Unit Testing Role Thinking Human The user clearly pointed out that testing should verify system behavior by messaging to Discord Bot rather than writing unit tests; asked for a list of manual tests. AI The AI automatically created 68+ unit tests (mocking various dependencies) and used this as a sign of completion. Difference Analysis: AI’s testing thinking comes from the default software engineering paradigm; dialogic butler systems require end-to-end interaction verification, and humans proposed a testing philosophy more suitable for such products.
AI Limitations Important Limitations After implementing new features, it was not verified whether they were connected to the system startup link (BackgroundCoordinator.start_all() was not called), only unit tests passed were verified, missing integration level checks; after updating IntentType and IntentRoutes, Discord Bot processor layer was not updated synchronously, resulting in three-layer inconsistencies—such cross-layer gaps cannot be automatically detected without end-to-end integration testing. When designing the Agentic system, it did not actively research mature industry implementations, requiring the user to explicitly point out before referring to OpenClaw and get-shit-done; there was a lack of continuous attention to global code quality during execution mode (such as hard-coding utterances for architectural optimization). When adding new services, to make the code “not crash,” a lot of except Exception: pass silent exception handling was written, sacrificing observability for surface robustness—this is a mistaken sense of security, especially dangerous in asynchronous service architectures. In describing tasks for research projects, it did not spontaneously highlight the core competitive advantage of zero-shot, nor actively compare with STAIG training dependencies, requiring the user to explicitly point out before supplementation—lacking the ability to independently judge the differentiated competitive positioning of research contributions. It cannot perceive the GPU quota strategy and scheduling constraints of HPC clusters, only observing hardware idle states; in long contexts, specific identifiers (node names an49/an53) are confused, requiring human review. General Limitations Multiple retries after the user refused ExitPlanMode still had deviations in judging the boundary between “when to pause confirmation vs directly proceed”; lacked a clear task boundary and completion status verification mechanism when launching a large number of sub-Agents in parallel. Today’s Gains Core Gains apply_tree(strict=False) is an implicit risk in ML training pipelines: When adding new training features that need normalization, if the norm_stats calculation script is not updated synchronously, it will lead to a silent scale mismatch affecting training quality; when concatenating historical actions and predicted actions with VAE, it must be ensured that both use the same normalization scale. Standard three-item checks after large-scale implementation: (1) Whether new services are connected to the startup link; (2) Whether cross-layer (route → handler) updates are consistent; (3) Whether silent exceptions have masked runtime errors. Passing tests is a necessary condition, not a sufficient one. Before designing an Agentic system, actively research mature similar projects—OpenClaw’s EventBus/CronScheduler/Plugin registration patterns and GSD’s STATE.md persistent memory/ContextAssembler/multi-Agent context isolation are highly reusable architectural patterns. Researching first before designing can avoid architectural rework. The common silent failure mode in registration patterns + lifecycle management is “registered but never started”; silent exceptions (except: pass) are particularly dangerous in asynchronous service architectures: the service seems to run, but all errors are swallowed up, making monitoring and debugging impossible. The correct approach is to always at least record logger.exception(). The mismatch records of AI systems are free annotation data—each time the LLM corrects a classification error is a training sample; unsupervised continuous self-improvement can be achieved through automatic backflow with UtteranceAugmenter, making it a cost-effective online learning mechanism. Parallel multi-Agents (4 processing different files simultaneously) are extremely effective in large-scale code implementation tasks, compressing serial time to about 1/4 and ensuring file isolation to avoid conflicts; high-quality early-stage architectural documents (clear dependencies, EventBus events, registration methods, test requirements) are the key prerequisites for AI’s one-time efficient implementation. The layer characteristics of cross-sample zero-shot fusion embeddings: The highly unique Layer_1/5 perform exceptionally well due to their obvious structural differences (Spearman 0.42-0.66), while the intermediate transition layers (Layer_3/6) show a negative correlation with adjacent layers in the embedding space; P@K=0 but Spearman>0.4 is reasonable—both measure exact position matching and global ranking monotonicity. Pi0.5 (PaliGemma 2B + action expert 300M) also requires at least 2 80GB GPUs under LoRA fine-tuning mode; the data dependencies for each training stage of the zhaoganlong framework: Pi0.5 can be immediately started with independent LeRoBot data, Diffusion Policy requires Step 2 (speed dataset), and LLaVA MPM requires all data preparation to be completed. Philosophy of testing dialogic AI systems: Unit tests verify component correctness, but system value needs to be verified through actual dialogue testing (Discord message-driven), as the two cannot replace each other; GSD’s hierarchical context assembly (PROJECT→ROADMAP→STATE→EXECUTION) is an effective engineering solution for solving multi-agent context rot. Practical Gains Practical tips for offline HPC clusters: Dependencies can be installed by copying site-packages directly between Python version conda environments; CUDA header files may be in targets/x86_64-linux/include/ instead of the standard path, and when compilation fails, first search for cuda_runtime_api.h before setting CPLUS_INCLUDE_PATH. Conversation Summary MIHD Spatial Omics 🔄 Cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark testing, refinement of project core narrative, and exploration of GPU acceleration solutions 15:49:09.875 | claude_code 全天在DCC cluster promoted the work on the MIHD multimodal spatial transcriptomics framework. Confirmed the L2 normalization states of UNI/UNI2; established a 5-sentence core narrative with Claude through three rounds of iteration (focusing on zero-shot, distinguishing from STAIG training dependencies, patch query clinical vision); conducted a cross-sample RM-IDEAL benchmark test using PCA+UNI2+STAIG_fusion against 151673↔151508 (originally misinputted as 151608 corrected by AI), with Layer_1/5 showing excellent Spearman 0.42-0.66, and the negative correlation between Layer_3/6 revealing limitations in intermediate layers. All layers had P@K=0; explored GPU acceleration solutions based on Sinkhorn approximation (in the design phase), wrote scripts for Layer_3 spatial visualization (not yet executed).
Motion-based-Self-Reflection-Framework 🔄 Deploying the zhaoganlong framework on an53: 9-task data preparation pipeline, training startup, and separation of Phoenix/FLARE code base 04:04:27.702 | claude_code Through SSH+tmux remote control on tianhe, deployed the zhaoganlong Self-Reflection framework on an53. Modified 4 data preparation scripts (enabled 9 tasks, removed pdb breakpoints, fixed .testc naming bugs and h5py additional writing), updated 2 JSON mapping files; executed full-process data preparation on an53 (approx. 1 million images); resolved issues such as missing CLIP (copying packages), Pi0.5 single-card OOM (changed to 2 cards for FSDP), and symlink path errors (set OPENPI_DATA_HOME), successfully started Diffusion Policy (GPU 0) and Pi0.5 (GPU 2+3), LLaVA MPM blocked due to lack of basic model data. Analyzed the code base through .init to identify boundaries of Phoenix and FLARE sub-projects, initiated rsync batch separation (underway).
Openpi-moe ✅ Analysis of training behavior with missing prev_actions key in norm_stats and normalization repair 04:20:57.932 | claude_code Users found that norm_stats.json only contained actions/state keys but no training errors. Traced back to the silent skip mechanism of apply_tree(strict=False) in transforms.py; further discovered that in pi0_moe.py, hist_actions (not normalized) and actions (normalized) were concatenated into VAE, posing a risk of scale mismatch. Modified compute_norm_stats.py to dynamically detect prevactions and write statistics, adopting a backward-compatible design that does not affect datasets without prevactions.
VLA-RoboTwin-curobo ✅ Installed the curobo library into the RefineVLA conda environment (checked CUDA header file path) 10:08:54.172 | claude_code Discovered that the CUDA header files were located in a non-standard targets/x86_64-linux/include/ path, resolved compilation failures by setting CPLUS_INCLUDE_PATH and C_INCLUDE_PATH. Finally, both import curobo and CUDA extensions loaded successfully.
CalendarPro ✅ Complete work on personal butler system: planning → Phase 1-3 implementation → Utterance augmentation → quality audit → key fixes 15:35:31.287 | claude_code Completed the upgrade of the CalendarPro butler system throughout the day on TzJsDesktop. Planning phase: Referred to OpenClaw/GSD architecture design with 5 major goals and 31 sub-goals (19 new files + 8 modified files), determined key decisions such as not implementing WeChat and using Claude subprocesses as the Agent core. Implementation phase: Parallelly created 16 service files for 4 Agents (GapAnalyzer/AutonomousExecutor/WaveExecutor, etc.), modified 21 infrastructure items, solved lazy imports and pytest-asyncio configuration issues, and all 68 unit tests passed. Utterance optimization: Externalized 452 hard-coded utterances to JSON, implemented UtteranceAugmenter to automatically learn from mismatch logs, processed 7 existing mismatches, 48 tests passed. Quality audit: Scanned the entire database and specifically audited silent exceptions, identified key issues such as executor empty loops, BackgroundCoordinator never starting, 9 intents without handlers, 16 dangerous silent exceptions, all fixed, 321 tests passed.
