summary: Over the entire day, three devices—DCC/tianhe/TzJsDesktop—were utilized to simultaneously carry out four projects: DCC completed the MIHD multimodal dataset benchmark and established a zero-shot narrative framework; tianhe deployed the zhaoganlong framework with a nine-task training pipeline, initiating two parallel training processes. Additionally, issues related to Openpi-moe normalization were resolved, and the separation of the Phoenix/FLARE code base was finalized. On TzJsDesktop, CalendarPro was fully upgraded to become an intelligent personal assistant capable of autonomous perception and multi-agent orchestration (with 321 successful tests).
title: バグレポート 2026-03-05

content:
Error Recovery Benchmark What was done: Parallel work on two devices: DCC analyzed QueST paper to develop cross-sample query designs for the MIHD project; Tianhe focused on aligning error recovery benchmark documentation, diagnosing performance issues, and building training infrastructure. How it was done: DCC obtained HTML versions of papers through multiple WebFetch/WebSearch calls and extracted technical details; Tianhe explored code libraries using parallel agents, compared document implementations with actual code, updated documents using Edit/Write tools, and planned training orchestration scripts. What’s useful: The structured understanding of QueST’s evaluation methods can be directly applied to MIHD’s evaluation design. The improved alignment in error recovery benchmark documentation provides a basis for subsequent tuning. The root cause analysis of zero success rates in BC-RNN is essential for future optimizations, and the completion of the an49 training pipeline lays the groundwork for multi-task training. DCC What was done: Read the analysis paper on QueST (arXiv:2410.10652v3): a comprehensive methodology and evaluation design for cross-sample transcription niche query frameworks. How it was done: Initially, PDF parsing failed, so an HTML version was used to obtain architectural details. WebSearch and Moonlight/GitHub were utilized to supplement the v3 updates (as there was no HTML version in v3). A structured summary of the self-encoder, contrastive learning, adversarial batch removal modules, and WWL Kernel evaluation scheme was created in Chinese. What’s useful: This clarified QueST’s method of using the WWL Graph Kernel to construct pseudo ground truth for cross-sample niche query evaluations. It provided a reference for designing evaluation metrics in the MIHD project. Tianhe What was done: Completed multiple parallel tasks related to error recovery benchmarks: updated the project overview.md (aligned with implementation details and corrected M14 evaluation data), created a training guide document for zhaoganlong, investigated the root cause of zero success rates in BC-RNN, planned an all-task training schedule for an49, and modified data preparation scripts to enable all 9 tasks. How it was done: Used multiple parallel agents to explore code libraries (53 error_framework files, 22 scripts) and compare them with documentation. Directly read summary.json to obtain actual evaluation data, checked GPU utilization, dataset status, and checkpoint existence, and precisely edited large Markdown documents using Edit tools. What’s useful: The document accuracy was significantly improved (from 726 ep to 6474 ep, with detailed implementation from blank to comprehensive). The root cause of BC-RNN Normal SR=0% was identified as an observation key bug (not a model capability issue). The completion of the an49 training plan and modification of data preparation scripts to enable all 9 tasks laid the foundation. DCC conducted in-depth analysis of QueST’s cross-sample query and evaluation methods for space transcription papers. Tianhe completed multiple project document updates (error recovery benchmark overview.md + zhaoganlong training guide), investigated the root cause of zero success rates in BC-RNN, and planned the all-task training infrastructure for an49.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Update the project overview.md — align documents with actual code — Add detailed descriptions of detectors/injectors/validators/classifiers/core modules in Section 3.2; correct VLA support models (Pi0-FAST → Phoenix/Flare); update code statistics (scripts 18→22, test cases 109→122); update M14 evaluation data (726 ep→6474 ep, actual SR/RP); add database by_phase and by_severity statistics. ✅ Investigate the root cause of zero success rates in BC-RNN normal tasks — Users reported that the results of BC-RNN training rollouts were completely different from those in evaluations. After AI exploration of the code, it was found that an observation key bug object led to a baseline_accuracy evaluation SR=0%; Pi0.5 Per-Task LoRA performed well on normal tasks (Stack_D0~95%), which contrasted sharply with BC-RNN’s 0%. ✅ Analyze QueST’s cross-sample query methods and evaluation design — Read arXiv:2410.10652 paper, extract the graph self-encoder architecture for cross-sample niche queries, contrastive learning design, adversarial batch removal, and the evaluation method using WWL Graph Kernel to construct pseudo ground truth. Users asked for an evaluation section and requested access to version 3. ✅ Create a zhaoganlong training guide document — Create docs/zhaoganlong_training_guide.md, covering an overview of the MPM→MCM closed-loop framework, complete commands and hyperparameters for 6 training phases, file system verification of all checkpoints (Diffusion Policy/OpenPI Base/Pi0.5 Phoenix), three modes of inference pipeline, and a complete path reference. ✅ Plan an all-task training schedule for an49 (zhaoganlong 6 stages) — Write the training plan: GPU allocation (GPU 0 uses Diffusion Policy, GPUs 1-4 use LLaVA MPM/MCM, GPUs 2-5 use Pi0.5), 4-step data preparation pipeline, tmux session management, all 9 MimicGen tasks, checkpoints stored in the tangzijia directory. ✅ Update the core pipeline diagram to include three parallel inputs — Users pointed out that the original pipeline diagram had only one input for the Demo Dataset, missing two paths: VLA/Policy Rollout injection and natural failure capture. AI updated Section 3.1 to include all three parallel paths converging into ErrorSceneDatabase. Implementation and Fixes 🔄 Modify data preparation scripts to enable all 9 tasks — Need to modify 4 scripts (create_5hz_dataset_new_motion.py, create_speed_dataset.py, generate_llava_json_dataset.py, generate_render_llava_dataset.py) by uncommenting all 9 tasks and removing pdb breakpoints. Also copy 6 missing HDF5 files to origin_datasets/. The plan is complete, but actual execution was interrupted by users. 🔄 Generate Pi0.5 Visualization videos — Users requested visualization videos for Pi0.5 policy rollouts. AI confirmed that the outputs/pi05_eval_results/videos/ directory was empty and needed to run visualize_policy_rollout.py after starting VLA server in an49. Execution was interrupted and not completed. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. BC-RNN had SR=0% on all normal tasks, which did not match the training rollout results Solution: Through code exploration, it was identified that an observation key bug in baseline_accuracy evaluation led to empty observations being received by the strategy. This was not a model capability issue but a bug in the evaluation script.
Key insight: When comparing training results with evaluations, it is necessary to check whether the observation space is consistent (key names, dimensions) rather than assuming that the model did not learn anything.
2. There are multiple discrepancies between the documented pipeline and the actual code implementation (VLA model type errors, outdated code statistics, M14 data being only early results) Solution: Systematically launch 3 parallel agents to explore various aspects of the code library, collect actual values (number of files, functions, evaluation results), and precisely edit the document line by line.
Key insight: Large project documents tend to drift from code. Regular alignment between “document” and “code” is crucial for maintaining readability, and the code should be used as the authority.
General Issues 3. The data preparation script still has pdb.set Tracy() debugging breakpoints activated for only one task, coffee_d1, making it impossible to run all 9 tasks in batch mode Solution: By reading 4 scripts line by line, identify the location of pdb calls and the list of commented-out tasks, then use Edit tools to uncomment them and remove breakpoints.
Key insight: Debugging breakpoints left behind in shared code libraries can cause silent hangs in automated pipelines. This should be checked as a priority during implementation planning.
4. arXiv PDFs cannot be directly parsed (binary stream returned), and version 3 has no HTML version available (404 error) Solution: First, use WebSearch to find the v1 HTML version to obtain the core architecture, then use Moonlight literature review site and GitHub repository to supplement with differences in version 3.
Key insight: Not all arXiv papers have HTML rendering. Using secondary literature review platforms can help access updates to summaries.
5. The project overview.md contains over 25,000 tokens, and a single reading tool cannot fully capture the content Solution: Use offset+limit segmentation for reading, combine with Grep to precisely identify lines that need modification, avoiding full loading.
Key insight: For large documents, segmented reading combined with precise Grep is more efficient than full loading. Key changes should be identified by Grep before editing.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Interpretation of BC-RNN Evaluation Results Role Thought Human Users observed that their rollout results varied, questioning the credibility of BC-RNN’s uniformly 0% in M14 evaluations. Later, they clarified that they were referring to Normal SR, not Error Recovery SR AI Initially, AI explained that M14 specifically evaluated error recovery scenarios, so a SR≈0% was normal. However, it did not actively distinguish between Normal SR and Error SR. Users needed to ask questions to clarify this Difference analysis: Users maintained skepticism about the results (“I’ve seen different outcomes”), driving their exploration of the Normal rollout baseline. They eventually identified a observation key bug in BC-RNN. AI tended to accept rational explanations for existing results.
Discovery of Pipeline Documentation Missing VLA Rollout Injection Path Role Thought Human Users pointed out that the core pipeline diagram in the documentation only had one input, the Demo Dataset, which did not match their actual idea. It was necessary to support injecting errors during VLA model rollouts and verify them. All three sources should be fed into ErrorSceneDatabase AI AI described the existing three paths (Demo injection, Policy Rollout injection, natural capture), but only one path was drawn in the documentation diagram. It did not actively identify discrepancies between the document and user intentions Difference analysis: Users identified systematic deviations from the documentation at the system level, while AI simply reflected the current state of the documentation. This is a difference between high-level architectural understanding and text accuracy.
Implementation Level QueST Paper Switching from v1 to v3 Role Thought Human After AI cited v1 analysis, users directly pointed out “don’t use v1, there’s a v3 version with updates” AI AI did not actively check for updated versions and used the first HTML link (v1) found by Google search instead Difference analysis: Users have stronger awareness of managing literature versions. AI should default to checking for updated versions when retrieving papers.
Scope and Goals of zhaoganlong Training Plan Role Thought Human Users explicitly selected all 6 stages, multi-task data, and stored checkpoints in the tangzijia directory. These are specific engineering decisions AI AI started designing after asking UserQuestion questions and adjusted the parallel strategy based on the actual state of the code library (GPU usage, data preparation status) Difference analysis: Users provided target constraints, while AI was responsible for converting these into executable detailed plans. They worked well together.
AI Limitations Major Limitations When interpreting M14 evaluation results, it tends to find rational explanations for “all results being 0%” (natural SR for error recovery scenarios), rather than actively asking users about their reference systems (Normal SR vs Error SR). Users need to correct this to clarify the issue. It failed to actively identify systematic discrepancies between documentation and code. Users needed to point out these discrepancies one by one before alignment could begin. Ideally, it should systematically scan all values and descriptions for consistency with code when modifying documents. General Limitations Multiple parallel agent tasks timed out in the same session (timeout 1122s). This might be due to remote file system I/O delays. Agents should split large exploration tasks into smaller reading intervals during planning. When retrieving papers, it defaults to using the first link in the search results (v1). It did not actively check for updated versions (v3) and only switched when prompted by users. Additionally, when v3 HTML was unavailable, it could only be supplemented through third-party channels. PDF files cannot be directly parsed (binary stream returned), resulting in a failed first WebFetch and requiring user confirmation. For arXiv PDF URLs, it should default to trying the /abs/ page or HTML version. Today’s Takeaways Core Takeaways QueST’s core idea for cross-sample evaluation: When there is no niche-level ground truth, use cell type annotation + WWL Graph Kernel to construct pseudo ground truth. Then, use Pearson correlation coefficient to quantify similarity between the two rankings - this is a general method for establishing comparable benchmarks in unsupervised embedding space. The performance gap between Pi0.5 Per-Task LoRA and the Global Model is significant (Stack_D0: ~95% vs ~24%). This indicates that the Foundation Model requires task-level fine-tuning to be effective on specialized tasks. The 58.9% average SR of the Global Model masks severe task imbalance. A clear distinction between Normal SR and Error Recovery SR is a key design element in error recovery benchmarks. M14’s close to 0% Error SR does not indicate model failure but rather a core point that current strategies lack error recovery capabilities. This can be demonstrated by comparing it with Normal SR. The three-module design of the zhaoganlong framework: The Motion Prediction Module (MPM) predicts future motion directions and encodes them into a 37-dimensional codebook → The Motion-Conditioned Diffusion Policy receives the codebook to generate actions → The Motion Correction Module (MCM) detects execution deviations and generates corrective instructions, forming a closed-loop self-reflection mechanism. Practical Takeaways Large project documentation alignment strategy: Use code as the authority. Use parallel agents to collect actual status from multiple dimensions (error frameworks, scripts, outputs), then systematically edit documents section by section - this is 3-5 times more efficient than manual line-by-line comparison. Conversation Summary MIHD ✅ Analyzed QueST paper: Cross-sample transcription niche query methods and evaluation design 22:21:12.826 | claude_code Users requested analysis of how cross-sample queries were implemented in arXiv:2410.10652v3 (QueST). AI first encountered PDF parsing failures and obtained complete technical details through HTML v1 version (GIN encoder + adversarial batch removal + cosine similarity retrieval). Users further asked for an understanding of the evaluation method. AI extracted the design of the WWL Graph Kernel as pseudo ground truth, two evaluation metrics (Best Niche Match Accuracy + Pearson correlation). Users requested access to version 3, but since v3 had no HTML version, they supplemented with a summary using the Moonlight review site.
Error Recovery Benchmark**✅ Updated the project overview summary.md: Aligning document descriptions with actual code implementations** 03:04:05.254 | claude_code By exploring the error_framework (file 53) and scripts/outputs directories in parallel, we identified 5 key differences between the documentation and the actual code. We made 5 precise updates: In Section 3.2, detailed descriptions of detectors/injectors/validators/classifiers were added; VLA support was updated to Phoenix/Flare; code statistics were updated; M14 evaluation data was changed from 726 ep to 6474 ep (including actual SR/RP); and database statistics included distributions for phase and severity.
✅ Investigated the zero success rate of BC-RNN: Identified the observation key bug and summarized the performance of Pi0.5 normal tasks 00:31:43.102 | claude_code Users reported a significant difference between the BC-RNN training rollout results and actual performance. After exploring the AI code, we identified the object observation key bug in the baseline_accuracy evaluation. This was the root cause of BC-RNN Normal SR=0%, not due to insufficient model capacity. Additionally, we found that Pi0.5 Per-Task LoRA performed well (Stack_D0 ~95%, overall ~58.9%), which stood out compared to Global model’s 4.22% and M14 Error Recovery’s 0%. This clearly distinguished between Normal SR and Error Recovery SR metrics.
✅ Created a training guide for zhaoganlong Motion-based Self-Reflection Framework 22:23:48.001 | claude_code Users wanted to know how to train the zhaoganlong framework and what the checkpoint status was like. After exploring the code library with 3 parallel agents, we found that the framework consisted of 3 trainable modules (MPM/MCM/Diffusion Policy) and 6 training phases. We created docs/zhaoganlong_training_guide.md, which includes complete training commands, hyperparameters, and checkpoint file system verification (Diffusion Policy 4×253MB available, LLaVA MPM/MCM missing, Pi0.5 Phoenix 180GB available), as well as three inference Pipeline modes.
🔄 Corrected the core Pipeline diagram: Added VLA Rollout injection and natural failure capture paths 00:53:32.952 | claude_code Users pointed out that the project documentation’s Pipeline diagram only showed the Demo Dataset entry, missing two paths: VLA/Policy Rollout injection and natural failure capture. We updated Section 3.1 to show a three-way parallel input structure all converging into ErrorSceneDatabase. Users further questioned the design of ErrorSceneDatabase. AI analyzed the implementation of database.py and core.py, explained the current design structure and API, and completed a comprehensive code vs documentation alignment work (ExitPlanMode was rejected).
🔄 Learned about zhaoganlong retry/reset framework model training methods and planned documentation 21:24:32.032 | claude_code Users asked about the training method and checkpoint status of the zhaoganlong framework. AI failed due to timeout after starting 3 agents for exploration; it successfully completed the exploration after restarting, and we planned to create docs/zhaoganlong_training_guide.md (ExitPlanMode was rejected), laying the foundation for documentation in the next 22:23 session.
🔄 Implemented an49 full task training plan: Modified data preparation scripts to enable 9 tasks 22:56:14.823 | claude_code Users provided a detailed training plan, and AI started execution: parallelly executed 4 data preparation scripts, confirming that only coffee_d1 was activated with pdb breakpoints; verified the source HDF5 files (all 9 tasks available in tangzijia/mimicgen_datasets/core/); checked origin_datasets for missing 6 files. We began modifying the script to enable all tasks and remove breakpoints, but the execution was interrupted by users.
❌ Generated Pi0.5 visualization video 00:26:04.288 | claude_code Users requested a visualization of Pi0.5 rollout. AI found the videos in the outputs/pi05_eval_results/videos/ directory, but the files were empty (failed to generate). We needed to run visualize_policy_rollout.py –policy vla_pi05 after starting VLA server on an49; due to session interruption, video generation was not completed.
✅ Asked how to download the project overview summary.md to a local machine 22:34:38.641 | claude_code Users asked how to download the project overview summary.md to their local machines. AI provided four methods and specific commands: SCP, SFTP, VS Code Remote Explorer, and FileZilla.

title: 错误日志 2026-03-04

content:
Key Achievements Cross-Section RM-IDEAL Benchmark Implementation and Verification (MIHD) — Created new scripts/benchmark_rm_ideal.py (781 lines), supporting bidirectional cross-section evaluations (151673 ↔ 151676), automatically detecting zero embeddings and recalculating STAIG fusion. The RM-IDEAL score cache and Spearman/Precision@K/Same-label rates were calculated, resulting in a bidirectional r=0.44/0.45 on Layer_3. Pi0.5 LoRA Fine-Tuning Batch Evaluation (an46, 9 tasks) — Ran 9 MimicGen tasks via Slurm job 47209 on an46, with each task having 20 rollouts. BatchedVLAServer was used for parallel execution, taking approximately 44 minutes. The overall success rate was 58.9%: Stack_D0 achieved 100%, Stack_D1 95%, StackThree 80-90%, Coffee_D0 45%, Threading/TPA 30-45%. Pi0.5 LoRA Base Model Verification and an49 Resume Recovery — Users suspected that the training used pi05_libero instead of pi05_base. By checking the config.py weight_loader field and the Restoring checkpoint path in the training log, it was confirmed that both zhaoganlong and tangzijia correctly used pi05_base. After stopping the mis-started fresh-start process, 9 tasks were resumed via SSH in the background (resuming from the highest 18000 steps of the existing checkpoint). BC-RNN Full Evaluation (5 tasks × 50 rollouts) — Created symlinks for 5 task checkpoints, fixed the numpy.float64 type bug in policy_adapter.py, and added –task parameter to support full evaluation. All tasks had a success rate of 0%, confirming that Coffee_D0’s zero SR during 600 epochs indicates a failure in training rather than an evaluation issue. Cross-Sample Patch Query Method Documentation (MIHD) — Created docs/CROSS_SAMPLE_QUERY.md (459 lines), covering chapters on spatial graph construction, k-hop subgraph extraction, WWL graph kernel, Wasserstein distance, cross-section process, embedding similarity comparison, evaluation metrics, and experimental results. an53 Node NODE_FAIL Investigation — Using sacct step timestamps confirmed that AI operations were completed before 05:07. An53 experienced a NODE_FAIL at 08:30 (8 GPUs fully loaded causing system OOM/GPU driver failure), and it was restarted by 09:41. There was no causal relationship between AI operations and the failure. Implementation and Fixes Dual GPU Evaluation Script Creation and Launch (an53) — Modified run_pi05_eval_v5_single_gpu.sh to create a dual-GPU version: VLA server ran on GPU0, MuJoCo rollout on GPU1. After fixing MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID remapping and SSH absolute path issues, it was successfully launched on an53 (PID 492473).
❌ Demo Video Rendering — Waiting for evaluation results to confirm which tasks had high SR before rendering the success/failure videos for BC-RNN and Pi0.5 tasks.
✅ Project Documentation Updates (CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md) — Updated CLAUDE.md with approximately 35 Makefile targets, explanations of parallel VLA evaluation architecture, and coding standards. Created an 390-word AGENTS.md contributor guide covering project structure, build/test commands, code style, and submission guidelines.
Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. Pi0.5 LoRA training configuration name contains ‘pi05_libero’, users suspect the initialization weights came from libero checkpoint rather than pi05_base Solution: Verified through three layers of evidence: ① openpi config.py weight_loader explicitly points to pi05_base/params; ② Training log shows “Restoring checkpoint from …/pi05_base”; ③ zhaoganlong’s configuration also loads pi05_base.
Key Insight: The configuration name in openpi TrainConfig (e.g., pi05_libero) describes data loading configurations, while the weight_loader field is the only basis for model initialization. These two can have different names.
2. BC-RNN had a success rate of 0% across all 5 MimicGen tasks, and Coffee_D0’s SR was always 0 during 600 epochs Solution: Not fully resolved yet; confirmed through training logs that it was an inherent training failure rather than an evaluation issue. Further investigation is needed to determine whether the checkpoint quality and dataset paths were correct.
Key Insight: Historical training logs can distinguish between “evaluation issues” and “training failures,” avoiding wasting time on non-existent evaluation problems. BC-RNN’s complete failure under error recovery framework indicates that traditional sequence modeling strategies lack generalizability.
3. An53 node experienced a NODE_FAIL at 08:30, users suspected AI operations (srun) caused the failure Solution: Using sacct step timestamps systematically ruled out possibilities: AI operations were completed before 05:07, and srun never executed due to node congestion. The most likely cause of the NODE_FAIL was system OOM/GPU driver failure due to 8 GPUs fully loaded.
Key Insight: Sacct step timestamps are effective tools for tracing operation sequences and determining causal relationships.
4. STAIG fusion had all-zero embeddings on 151676, resulting in a NaN Spearman r during cross-section evaluation Solution: Added zero-embedding detection (norm < 1e-6) to load_fused_embeddings(), automatically triggering –recompute_fusion to retrain STAIG (RTX 2080 Ti, 300 epochs, approximately 50 seconds).
Key Insight: STAIG has a risk of training collapse in certain sections. Phase 2 cached embeddings cannot be trusted unconditionally and must be verified at runtime.
5. AI pre-started fresh-start training process when the user did not confirm the intention to resume Solution: The user stopped the process promptly, and AI killed the error process and recreated the resume script (–no-overwrite –resume), restarting it via SSH in the background.
Key Insight: Before performing resource-intensive operations (GPU training), it is necessary to confirm with the user whether to use resume/fresh-start. Relying on pre-determined judgments cannot be trusted.
General Issues 6. P100 GPUs (sm_60) are incompatible with new PyTorch; common SSH remote execution issues (relative paths, uid mapping, variable expansion, multi-line Python heredoc) Solution: Switch to RTX 2080 Ti (sm_75) nodes; explicitly use absolute paths in SSH commands; specify -l username; use single quotes for nohup scripts; convert multi-line Python code into temporary script files.
Key Insight: There are fixed patterns and solutions for SSH remote execution issues. CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES remapping can affect MuJoCo EGL device numbers (physical GPU 1 should have EGL device id set to 0 under CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1).
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Experimental Design and Key Decisions (Evaluation Framework, Base Model Selection, Resume vs Fresh-Start) Role Approach Human Humans led all core experimental decisions: proposed RM-IDEAL + embedding cosine similarity cross-sample evaluation framework; actively identified potential risks of pi05_libero vs pi05_base; chose “immediate evaluation using current checkpoint” to save waiting time; stopped AI from mis-starting fresh-start in time and requested resume | AI | AI was responsible for turning designs into code and systematically verifying the correctness of experimental configurations through multiple layers of evidence (config.py + training logs + comparison groups) |
Difference Analysis: Humans identified key risks based on domain knowledge and intuition, while AI provided systematic verification but sometimes required multiple rounds of correction. Humans’ pragmatic decisions (immediate evaluation, not covering checkpoints) saved a lot of computational resources and waiting time for experiments.
Problem Diagnosis (STAIG zero-embedding, NODE_FAIL responsibility determination, BC-RNN training failure identification) Role Approach Human Humans relied on temporal relevance and intuition to make preliminary judgments (e.g., suspecting srun caused NODE_FAIL), sometimes making mistakes | AI | AI provided more reliable root cause determinations through active debugging (checking embedding norm), structured evidence analysis using sacct, and reviewing historical training logs (confirming that Coffee SR was always 0). This was better than intuitive judgment. |
Difference Analysis: AI had an advantage in diagnosing issues that had already occurred (systematic verification, historical log analysis), but its proactive review of potential experimental design risks was not as effective as domain experts.
Cluster Operation Method Selection (sbatch vs SSH Direct Connection) Role Approach Human Users explicitly requested not to use sbatch and instead used SSH direct connection to an49 in the background, based on a clear understanding of the cluster’s actual architecture (consistent paths, direct connection to compute nodes) | AI | AI defaulted to using sbatch as the standard HPC scheduling method. After creating .sbatch scripts, it was interrupted; switching to SSH made it possible to execute effectively. |
Difference Analysis: This is a typical “general HPC knowledge vs specific cluster environment knowledge” difference. Users’ specific knowledge is more suitable for this cluster, while AI’s general best practices were inefficient in this scenario.
AI Limitations Important Limitations Lack of proactive review of experimental design rationality: Did not actively check whether the Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning base model configuration met expectations. It was only discovered after users raised concerns about potential issues between libero and base. Lack of necessary pre-verification before resource-intensive operations (GPU training): Pre-started fresh-start training scripts before the user explicitly stated their intention to resume/fresh-start. Multiple attempts at ExitPlanMode also showed that it wasn’t accurate enough to determine when confirmation was needed. There were intermediate misjudgments during analysis, requiring multiple rounds of verification to reach correct conclusions: Verified base models went through multiple redundant verifications; visual script debugging experienced 5 failures; auth token expiration caused multiple sub-agents to fail. General Limitations Technical limitations of SSH remote execution: Multi-line Python heredoc escaping, no-upward variable expansion failures ($ts not expanded), process management (pkill being unreliable and requiring specifying PID) repeatedly occurred. CUDA compatibility issues (sm_60 vs sm_70+) were not warned in advance.### MIHD ✅ Cross-Section RM-IDEAL benchmark implementation and method documentation 19:08:35.365 | claude_code Plan and implemented benchmark_rm_ideal.py (781 lines): Explored code libraries through parallel agents to form solutions, fixed STAIG 151676 zero-embedding bug (automatic recalculation) and P100 CUDA compatibility issues (user switched to RTX 2080 Ti), resulting in Layer_3 bidirectional evaluation results of r=0.44/0.45. A spatial heatmap visualization was also generated. Subsequently, docs/CROSS_SAMPLE_QUERY.md (459 lines) was created to fully describe the cross-sample evaluation method chain using WWL graph cores and Wasserstein distance, along with experimental results.
ErrorRecoveryBenchmark ✅ Pi0.5 batch evaluation, BC-RNN baseline, training weight verification, and resume training 00:01:02.845 | claude_code Completed 9 tasks of Pi0.5 LoRA batch evaluation on an46 (total 58.9%, best in Stack category); all 5 tasks for BC-RNN were 0%. It was confirmed that Coffee_D0 training with SR=0 indicates a failure. Users suspected an error in the base model due to the configuration name containing ‘pi05_libero’. AI confirmed through config.py weight_loader fields and training logs that pi05_base was correctly used on both sides (tangzijia/zhaoganlong). After stopping the erroneous fresh-start process, 9 tasks were resumed in the background on an49 via SSH (up to 18000 steps). During this period, an53 NODE_FAIL accident investigation was completed (confirmed by sacct that it had nothing to do with AI operations), a dual-GPU evaluation script was created and started (an53, PID 492473), and documents CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md were updated. Also, the yhbatch command was studied (confirmed as an sbatch wrapper).

title: 错误日志 2026-03-03

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-02 Today’s Overview What was done: Completed the full experimental process of scGPT+UNI2 fusion in DCC and produced visual reports for weekly updates. Managed 9 Pi0.5 LoRA training tasks on Tianhe’s two nodes (an49/an53), including migration, restarting, and progress monitoring. Fixed several critical issues in the evaluation pipeline and designed a solution to optimize GPU utilization. How it was done: In DCC, batch evaluations were conducted using phase2_evaluate.py for 5 fusion strategies. The visualize_from_cache.py script was used to generate cluster visualizations. On Tianhe, SSH+srun –overlap strategy was employed to manage the dual-node processes. Repeated iterations were carried out to fix issues such as WebSocket timeouts and JIT concurrency in the evaluation pipeline. A comparison experiment between 1-client and 2-client concurrency was also conducted. What it means: Prepared complete experimental data for Monday’s report (QFormer avg ARI=0.370, +117% vs scGPT-only). Identified key constraints of the STAIG architecture. All 9 Pi0.5 training tasks were restored to operation, and the evaluation pipeline ran smoothly. The optimization direction for VLA inference was clarified (batch inference instead of multi-client concurrency). A list of external dependencies was created, and cluster access policies were updated. DCC What was done: Fixed a bug in scGPT’s cache metadata. Conducted 5 fusion experiments with scGPT+UNI2, generating 33 single-modal cluster visualizations which were embedded in the weekly report. Through code tracing, it was discovered that STAIG does not use gene encoder for its architecture. How it was done: Fixed the cache_version field in 11 .npz files by batching. Sequential execution ofconcat/mean/attention/llava_mlp/qformer evaluations was performed. When mclust encountered a deadlock, kmeans was used to resolve the issue. Three-layer code tracing (Fusion.py→runner.py→STAIGTrainer.py) verified the STAIG architecture. What it means: scGPT+UNI2+QFormer avg ARI=0.370 (+117% vs scGPT-only 0.170). Confirmed that STAIG does not use gene encoder for its architecture (improvements should start with GNN structure). The weekly report contains complete outputs. Tianhe What was done: Monitored the progress of 6 Pi0.5 training tasks on an49 (about 11%-38%). Migrated 3 queued tasks to an53 and restarted 6 tasks that unexpectedly terminated. Fixed issues such as WebSocket timeouts and JIT concurrency in the evaluation pipeline. Conducted a deep analysis of the root cause of low GPU utilization. How it was done: Monitored progress through Slurm queue queries and log files. Managed the nodes using SSH+srun –overlap strategy. Repeated iterations were carried out to fix evaluation scripts (v3→v4→v5 single-GPU sequential versions). After comparing concurrency experiments, the stable single-GPU solution was chosen over multi-GPU parallel versions. What it means: All 9 tasks were restored to operation. The evaluation v5 single-GPU version ran smoothly. It was confirmed that action chunking is the root cause of low GPU utilization. Cluster node access policies were updated (priority SSH→fallback srun). The optimization plan for VLA pipeline was completed. Completed the full fusion experiment of scGPT+UNI2 in DCC and produced visual reports. Identified that STAIG does not use gene encoder for its architecture. Managed 9 Pi0.5 LoRA training tasks on Tianhe’s two nodes, including migration and restarting. Fixed multiple critical issues in the evaluation pipeline (WebSocket timeouts, JIT concurrency crashes). Conducted a deep analysis of the root cause of only 10% GPU utilization in VLA inference. Completed the design of a batch-concurrency evaluation optimization plan.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Conducted full fusion experiments with scGPT+UNI2 (5 strategies) and compiled weekly reports — After fixing the cache metadata of 11 scGPT .npz files, sequentially ran five fusion strategies: concat/mean/attention/llava_mlp/qformer. QFormer was the best (avg ARI=0.370), followed by LLaVA-MLP (0.316). All were significantly better than scGPT-only (0.170). A comparison table and 3 statistical charts were generated. The visualize_from_cache.py script was used to generate 33 cluster visualizations for PCA/scGPT/UNI2 (kmeans was used after mclust encountered a deadlock). All were embedded in weekly_report_20260301.md. 🔄 Designed a solution to optimize GPU utilization in VLA evaluation pipeline — Conducted research on over 40 related papers and designed a BatchedVLAServer with multi-worker parallel evaluation. The plan was not finalized yet as it requires termination when ExitPlanMode is used. 🔄 Fixed the evaluation pipeline and conducted rollout evaluations of 9 tasks — Fixed multiple bugs such as MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID, WebSocket ping_timeout (with ping_timeout=None), and JIT concurrency crashes. After confirming that multi-clients cannot improve throughput through concurrency experiments, the plan was changed from multi-GPU parallel versions (v3/v4) to single-GPU sequential version (v5). 20 trials were conducted on GPU 3 to evaluate these tasks. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Management of two-node Pi0.5 LoRA tasks (monitoring, migration, restarting) — Monitored 6 tasks on an49 (progress about 11%-38%). Migrated 3 queued tasks to an53 (8 A800s available). Restarted 6 tasks that unexpectedly terminated on an49 due to launcher kill (resumed from step ~4000). Ultimately, all 9 tasks ran in parallel on the two nodes. Evaluation results showed Stack_D0=95%, Stack_D1=100%, Coffee_D0 only 5%. ✅ Updated cluster node access policies — Changed GPU node access policy from “only Slurm” to “priority SSH (when there’s an active job) → fallback srun –overlap”. Updated CLAUDE.md and project memory. ✅ Created a list of external file dependencies for the project — Created docs/external_files_inventory.md, organizing 10 types of external dependencies (4 conda environments, 10 HDF5 datasets, 9 BC-RNN checkpoints, 4 VLA checkpoints, etc.). Each dependency was labeled with complete paths and reference locations. References were added to the project panorama summary.md. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. STAIG’s two-stage architecture completely ignores gene encoder output, using the original HVG expression matrix as input for GCN, resulting in scGPT+UNI2+STAIG≡PCA+UNI2+STAIG Solution: Through three-layer code tracing (models/Fusion.py:1246, pipeline/runner.py:445-446, STAIGTrainer.py), it was confirmed that the staig_gene_feat input path has nothing to do with gene_emb.
Key Insight: STAIG’s gene encoder and GNN training are decoupled designs. Improvements should start from the GNN structure rather than replacing the gene encoder.
2. Pi0.5 evaluation client reports ‘keepalive ping timeout’, causing each episode to fail immediately, with SR always being 0% Solution: Added ping_timeout=None in the connect() call of WebSocket_client_policy.py, disabling the default 20s timeout.
Key Insight: JAX first inferences require JIT compilation (30-60s), exceeding the WebSocket library’s default 20s keepalive timeout. All JAX applications that require long first inferences must explicitly set ping_timeout=None.
3. ’no close frame received’ caused server crashes when 5 JAX servers started simultaneously; 2 clients with one server were actually 24% slower than serial (1771s vs 1428s) Solution: Adopted staggered startup (v4): each server was started one by one, waiting for JIT warmup before starting the next. Abandoned single-server multi-client setup and replaced it with one server per GPU + one client per GPU.
Key Insight: Multiple JAX processes compete for CPU and memory bandwidth during JIT compilation. VLA server inferences are strictly serializable. Multi-client concurrency cannot improve throughput; the only correct parallel approach is independent deployment of multiple GPUs.
4. GPU utilization in Pi0.5 evaluation pipeline was only about 10%, with a lot of GPU time idle Solution: Confirmed that the root cause was action chunking: approximately 400 steps per trial, inference every 50 steps = 8 GPU calls × 2.5s = 20s GPU usage / 200s total time; the optimization direction was batch inference (BatchedVLAServer) rather than increasing concurrent clients.
Key Insight: Action chunking reduces inference calls. Real optimization should focus on aggregating multiple inference requests into batches, rather than trying to concurrency existing serial inference paths.
5. Killing the launcher PID caused all 6 training processes (including those started with nohup) to be terminated by Slurm cgroup together Solution: Used independent background srun –overlap commands to start each training task. Srun processes do not depend on the launcher and can survive after SSH disconnects.
Key Insight: Subprocesses launched within srun by nohup are still terminated by Slurm cgroup even after srun exits. Each long-running task must be treated as an independent srun process.
General Problems 6. SSH to compute nodes was rejected by pam_slurm_adopt; srun to an49 timed out and GPU status could not be directly obtained Solution: Nodes with active jobs (an53) can be logged in directly via SSH. Nodes without active jobs use srun –jobid=XXXXX –overlap. Training logs can also be read directly through the shared file system, bypassing node access restrictions.
Key Insight: pam_slurm_adopt requires an active job on the target node for SSH access. Cluster access policy should be “priority SSH → fallback srun –overlap”. The shared file system can serve as a backup source of information.
7. AI directly created new visualization scripts, ignoring existing visualize_from_cache.py; mclust got stuck on high-dimensional embeddings (512d/1536d) Solution: After the user corrected it, used the existing script. Added –cluster_method kmeans parameter to solve mclust’s deadlock problem.
Key Insight: Before starting a new task, it is better to search for existing project tools. mclust is not suitable for high-dimensional data and should default to using kmeans.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Research innovation direction proposed vs code-level architecture tracing Role Thought Human Teacher Yi actively proposed an innovative direction of cross-sample layer5 patch querying using zero-shot embedding (cross sample query + HD patches). Identified the challenge of batch alignment. Zijia implicitly assumed that STAIG used gene encoder output when asking about STAIG results. AI AI focused on organizing existing experimental data and preparing reports, without actively proposing a research direction. However, through systematic three-layer code tracing, it was discovered that STAIG actually does not use gene encoder output, providing evidence line by line. Difference Analysis: Research innovation directions are driven by mentors. AI has an advantage in systematic code-level tracking, allowing it to reveal architectural details that are difficult to discover from documentation.
Insights on GPU resource constraints & feasibility of parallel evaluation Role Thought Human The user observed that the client had only about 400MB of memory. Actively proposed that a server and client could share one GPU. The user actively asked whether 20 trials could be run in parallel, driving the exploration of concurrent experiments. AI AI defaulted to having one GPU for each server and client. Did not actively calculate the actual memory usage of the client. Designed serial evaluation without considering trial-level parallelism. Difference Analysis: The user proposed key optimizations based on practical resource constraints and goals. AI followed existing design patterns, lacking awareness of active optimization and resource accounting.
Priority of practicality vs over-engineering & limitations of documentation rules Role Thought Human The user decisively stopped the complex parallel plan for GPU 3-7, keeping only GPU 3 for stability. The user pointed out that SSH to nodes should be prioritized, correcting AI’s outdated rule of “never SSH directly”. AI AI tended to pursue more complex parallel solutions (v4 staggered startup). Strictly followed the CLAUDE.md documentation rules until corrected by the user. Difference Analysis: The user focuses on practicality and stability, avoiding over-engineering. AI’s dependence on documentation rules sometimes hinders optimal operations.
AI Limitations Important Limitations Did not check the default ping_timeout configuration of the WebSocket library in advance. Did not anticipate that Slurm cgroup would terminate all background training processes when killing the launcher. Lack systematic testing of the entire evaluation pipeline, resulting in repeated fixes to the same problem across multiple versions (v3/v4/v5). General Limitations Did not search for existing project tools before starting a new task (created new visualization scripts instead of using visualize_from_cache.py), and did not anticipate that mclust might get stuck on high-dimensional embeddings, leading to repeated work. Did not actively calculate the GPU memory usage of the evaluation client. Followed outdated CLAUDE.md node access rules. Did not clearly present the complete plan for users to confirm before starting multiple Agents in parallel. Today’s Achievements Core Achievements STAIG’s two-stage GCN used the original HVG expression matrix instead of gene encoder output, and the selection of gene encoder had no impact on STAIG results. Improvements should start from the GNN structure design. The JIT compilation time for JAX (30-60s) exceeded the WebSocket default ping timeout (20s). Multiple JAX processes competed for CPU and memory bandwidth during JIT compilation. Solution: Set ping_timeout=None in the connect() call + run in parallel after serial warmup. The root cause of VLA inference GPU utilization being only about 10%: action chunking (50-step sequence) reduced each trial to only ~8 GPU calls (8 × 2.5s/200s = 10%). Pi0.5 single-GPU inference is strictly serializable. Multi-client concurrency cannot improve throughput. The correct optimization direction was BatchedVLAServer batch inference + independent deployment of multiple GPUs. Slurm cgroup mechanism: Subprocesses launched within nohup were still terminated by Slurm after the srun command exited. Each long-running task must be treated as an independent srun process. pam_slurm_adopt requires an active job to access nodes via SSH. Cluster access policy should be “priority SSH → fallback srun –overlap”. Learned fusion significantly improved the utilization efficiency of scGPT representation: scGPT+UNI2+QFormer avg ARI=0.370 (+117% vs scGPT-only 0.170), indicating that scGPT’s 512d representation itself is valuable but requires non-linear projection to be fully activated. Practical Achievements Complete openpi benchmark evaluation process: Training completed → Start VLA server (pi05_benchmark_{task}_inference config, openpi05 env) → Run evaluate_mimicgen.py client (mimicgen_env) → Output success rate. Pi0.5 LoRA checkpoints were saved every 1000 steps by default, with max_to_keep=1. Session Summary MIHD (Space Transcriptomics Benchmark)✅ scGPT+UNI2 Fusion Experiment Full Process: STAIG Architecture Discovery, Evaluation of 5 Strategies, Weekly Report Visualization 02:51:19.717 | claude_code
After Teacher Yi expressed satisfaction with the bug fixes, AI discovered through three layers of code tracing that STAIG’s two-stage approach actually does not use a gene encoder (scGPT+UNI2+STAIG≡PCA+UNI2+STAIG, avg ARI≈0.546). After batch-fixing 11 scGPT .npz cache metadata, 5 fusion strategies were executed in sequence (QFormer achieved the best avg ARI=0.370, +117% compared to scGPT-only 0.170), and comparison tables along with 3 statistical charts were generated and included in the weekly report. During the visualization phase, AI accidentally created a new script which was corrected by the user, and visualize_from_cache.py was used instead. When mclust became stuck, kmeans was switched to, successfully generating 33 cluster visualizations for section 6 of the weekly report. Teacher Yi also proposed an innovative research direction: cross-sample zero-shot queries.
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 External Dependency List, Pi0.5 Dual-node Training Management, Evaluation Pipeline Fixes, and GPU Optimization Solutions 03:10:32.470 | claude_code
docs/external_files_inventory.md was created to organize 10 types of external dependencies. The training progress was monitored through log files (6 tasks ranged from 11% to 38%, with 3 tasks in queue). Queued tasks were moved to an53 (8 A800 units available), and the 6 tasks that unexpectedly terminated after killing the launcher were restarted. All 9 tasks were recovered. The cluster access strategy was updated to “SSH priority → fallback srun –overlap”. The evaluation pipeline was repeatedly fixed: websocket ping_timeout (adding ping_timeout=None), JIT concurrency crashes (starting at wrong times), and through concurrent experiments (1-client vs 2-clients, slow by 24%), it was confirmed that multi-clients were ineffective. Eventually, the system stabilized at a single GPU v5 version. Research on BatchBatchedVLAServer optimization solutions from over 40 papers was conducted in plan mode, which was aborted when ExitPlanMode was used.
Token Usage Overview Metric Value Total Tokens 54,883,979 Input Tokens 110,299 Output Tokens 106,931 Cache Creation 2,558,990 Cache Reads 52,107,759 Cache Hit Rate 95.3% Total Cost (USD) $35.8983 Model Details Model Input Output Cache Creation Cache Reads Cost Percentage claude-opus-4-6 63,192 60,737 1,640,537 42,934,009 $33.5547 93.5% claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 47,107 46,194 918,453 9,173,750 $2.3435 6.5% Device Usage Device Total Tokens Input Output Cost DCC 637,595 9 435 $0.4049 tianhe 54,246,384 110,290 106,496 $35.4933
summary: DCC已经完成了scGPT+UNI2全套融合实验以及周报可视化工作，并识别出STAIG在构建关键架构时并未使用gene encoder的事实。此外，我们还成功地在天河双节点上管理了9个Pi0.5 LoRA训练任务，解决了eval pipeline中的多个关键问题（如websocket超时、JIT并发崩溃）。我们进一步分析了VLA推理过程中GPU利用率仅为10%的根本原因，并设计了批量并发eval优化方案。
title: 缺陷记录表 2026-03-02

content:
Weekly Report — 2026-W09 (2026-02-23 ~ 2026-03-01) This week, we continued to work on two main tasks: space transcriptomics (MIHD) and the Error Recovery Benchmark on both DCC and Tianhe clusters. On DCC, we fixed a critical weight loading bug in scGPT, which improved ARI by 44.4%. We also extended the MIHD benchmark to the Visium HD dataset and completed a large-scale code refactoring involving approximately 250K lines of code. On Tianhe, we established the BC-RNN Phoenix baseline training pipeline with 9 tasks running in parallel. We identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in the Pi0.5 evaluation task, and obtained key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation (the learning strategy had an SR of approximately 0% under error scenarios, proving the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning). Additionally, we successfully launched the parallel fine-tuning of Pi0.5 LoRA on 6×A800 with stable performance.
Week Overview Metric Value Date Range 2026-02-23 ~ 2026-03-01 Active Days 5 / 7 Total Conversations 19 Projects Involved 5 Tasks Completed 35 Ongoing Tasks 6 Total Tokens 177,973,151 Total Cost $105.52 Average Cost per Day $21.10 Project Progress Error Recovery Benchmark (Error Recovery Benchmark) (Active for 5 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
M14 three-way evaluation (Pi0/Pi0.5/BC-RNN) completed: BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random was the highest, indicating that the learning strategy failed to generalize well under error injection scenarios. This directly proved the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation: The training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task. Complete implementation of BC-RNN Phoenix baseline: Image mode configuration rewritten to match the MimicGen paper. 9 tasks ran in parallel smoothly, and the Stack D0 achieved an SR of 64% after 22 epochs. Fixed defects in Coffee BC-RNN configuration: Added a missing 57-dimensional object observation key, fixing the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR. Completed the BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline: Fixed a bug in the 4-layer continuous image format, generated 11 tip_over scenarios and MP4 visualizations. Successful launch of Pi0.5 LoRA with 9 tasks running in parallel (6×A800, GPU utilization 100%, 77.7GB per card). The evaluation framework was extended to include 5 MimicGen tasks, with a new task registry and unified management of the –task parameter. Evaluation of the Pi0.5 Phoenix 9-task base model: Total SR=4.2% (Stack D0 24%, others ≤2%). Discovery of a database containing 649 scenarios: ~519 compatible scenarios + ~130 natural_* incompatible scenarios. The actual effective evaluation target was ~519. Blocked Items:
⚠️ Initialization issues in the Coffee environment caused objects to be distorted or stuck (simulator bug). This issue was found after visualization of the BC-RNN rollout and remains unfixed. ⚠️ The effect of Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning (compared to the 4.2% baseline) needs verification after training is completed. MIHD (Multimodal Space Transcriptomics Fusion) (Active for 3 days) — 🔄 active Completed:
Fixed the critical weight loading bug in scGPT: The omission of self.use_fast_transformer in TransformerModel.init caused key remapping to never execute in load_pretrained(). After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and NMI improved by 33.3%. STAIG fusion RM-IDEAL evaluation led the competition (average r=0.396 for 151673, peak r=0.644 for Layer_3). This proves that multimodal fusion is much more effective than single-modal baseline in capturing spatial niches (avg r≈0.06-0.12). Full support for Visium HD crop10large: 8 files were modified, and 4 path adaptation bugs were fixed. Silhouette=0.343 was achieved end-to-end (vs PCA 0.086). Discovery of VisiumHD barcode mapping (r_big//4 pattern), and comparative visualizations of pathologist annotation, STHD cell type, and STAIG fusion clustering. Comparison experiment between Vision Refine: It was found that scan_cluster refine is beneficial for weak fusion but harmful for strong fusion. qformer+no-refine was the best option (ARI=0.4832). Large-scale refactoring of the repository in 6 phases: 41 files/~250K code and 5.3MB images were removed. 6 functional modules were extracted, and 440 experiments passed after dry-run verification. The bug causing logic reversal in BasicContrastive drop_feature was fixed. Three CPU acceleration implementations for STAIG fusion (vectorization of cdist + GPU-based random number generation + batch forward. Expectation is a 20-50x improvement). Blocked Items:
⚠️ There were issues with objects being distorted or stuck during the initialization of the Coffee environment (simulator bug). This issue was found after visualization of the BC-RNN rollout and remains unfixed. ⚠️ The effect of Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning (compared to the 4.2% baseline) needs verification after training is completed. Key Tasks ✅ Fixed the scGPT use_fast_transformer weight loading bug and re-extracted all embeddings (2026-02-28) — The omission of self.use_fast_transformer in TransformerModel.init caused key remapping to never execute in load_pretrained(). All 17.7% attention weight parameters were randomly initialized. After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and all 186/186 parameters matched. ✅ Identified the root cause of OOM in Pi0.5 full fine-tuning and switched to LoRA architecture (2026-02-23) — It was confirmed that JAX FSDP only divides parameter storage but does not reduce activation memory. The complete training state of Pi0.5 requires ~62GB/GPU on A800 or higher. Switching to gemma_2b_lora+gemma_300m_lora reduced memory to ~22.5GB. Job 46553 ran smoothly at 2.0s/step. ✅ Completed the three-way evaluation of M14 and confirmed key conclusions (2026-02-27) — BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random (0.0199) was actually higher than all learning strategies. This proves that the learning strategy failed to generalize well under error injection scenarios, directly supporting the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. ✅ Cleaned and refactored the MIHD repository in 6 phases (2026-03-01) — In Phase1, 41 dead files (~250K code + 5.3MB images) were removed. In Phases 2-4, the run_benchmark.py shared functions were moved to 6 modules and the bug causing logic reversal in BasicContrastive drop_feature was fixed (>= replaced with <). 440 experiments passed after dry-run verification. ✅ Full support for Visium HD crop10large (8 files modified) (2026-02-28) — The data_loader/clustering/run_benchmark/pipeline modules were modified. Automatic clustering without annotations for HD was added, and HD-specific visualization logic and hd_global configuration sections were added. The coordinate space alignment and double preprocessing issues were fixed. Silhouette=0.343 was achieved end-to-end. ✅ Discovered and fixed compatibility issues with M14 scenarios (2026-02-23) — All three-way evaluations crashed on scene 122. AI analysis using EnvironmentMismatchError found that the 649 scenario database contained two types of environments (~519 compatible + ~130 natural_* incompatible). The collector.py added a try-except for scene-level to fix this issue. There were approximately 519 actual effective evaluation scenarios. 🔄 Launched the parallel fine-tuning pipeline for Pi0.5 LoRA with 9 tasks (2026-03-01) — The train_pi05_benchmark.py was created (5 subcommands). 18 configurations were registered to openpi/config.py. Four launch bugs were fixed (JAX_PLATFORMS, norm_stats path, boolean parameters, W&B proxy). It successfully launched on 6×A800 with 100% GPU utilization and 77.7GB per card. 🔄 Completed the script implementation of BC-RNN Phoenix baseline and launched 9 tasks in parallel (2026-02-28) — The train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py was implemented based on the hyperparameters from the MimicGen paper. Three library-level bugs were fixed (mujoco_py import, MimicGen environment registration, get_bounding_box_half_size inheritance chain). 9 tasks ran in parallel on a single GPU (~2.2GB VRAM per task), with 50 rollouts evaluated every 20 epochs. ✅ Located and fixed the missing object observation keys in Coffee BC-RNN (2026-03-01) — By comparing the actual obs key structure of the HDF5 dataset, it was found that Coffee lacked a missing 57-dimensional object state (Pod/Machine/Holder position + relative pose + hinge angle). Extra_low_dim=[‘object’] was added for all 9 tasks. This fixed the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR. ✅ Identified and fixed the root cause of the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation (2026-02-26) — It was confirmed that the training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task. Humans could directly identify this obvious issue through a single question, while AI analysis failed to detect it immediately. ✅ Rewritten BC-RNN image mode configuration (5 tasks, consistent with MimicGen paper) (2026-02-26) — The bc_rnn_configs were rewritten from all low_dim to image mode: ResNet18+SpatialSoftmax encoder, CropRandomizer 76×76, RNN hidden_dim 1000, batch_size 16, epoch_every_n_steps 500. This was consistent with the main results in the MimicGen paper. ✅ Achieved three CPU acceleration implementations for MIHD fusion training (2026-02-23) — scipy cdist vectorization replaced O(n²) nested loops (expected a 100-500x improvement). Random number generation was moved to GPU to eliminate the transmission overhead per epoch. QFormerFusion added a batch forward interface (expectation is a 20-50x improvement). ✅ Developed the BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline (2026-03-01) — The bug in the 4-layer continuous image observation access of robomimic BC-RNN was fixed. 11 tip_over error scenarios and MP4 visualizations were generated. ✅ Completed the full evaluation of Section 151673 RM-IDEAL (27 methods × 7 layers) (2026-02-28) — The RM-IDEAL evaluation with 27 different embedding methods was run. 189 three-dimensional panel visualizations were generated. staig_fusion had an average r of 0.396, ranking first (peak r of Layer_3 was 0.644). This quantified the advantage of multimodal fusion. ✅ Extended the evaluation framework to include 5 MimicGen tasks (2026-02-26) — 5 new task YAML configs and task_registry.yaml were created. The –task parameter was added to the 1/3/4 scripts. The observation dimension error caused by _D0 env suffix stripping was corrected from 65 to 37. run_full_eval.sh was rewritten to support multi-task loops. ✅ Completed the full benchmark test of STAIG fusion (11 DLPFC slices) (2026-02-23) — pca+uni2+staig_fusion and none+uni2+staig_fusion were run on 11 DLPFC slices. The UNI2 patch_size compatibility bug was fixed (256×256 → 224×224). 10/11 slices were successful, with an average ARI of 0.546. Section 151676 had known collapses. ✅ Completed the comparison experiment between MIHD Vision Refine and baseline (2026-02-23) — On slice 151673, a full comparison was run for 8 fusion methods with/without scan_cluster refine. qformer+no-refine was the best option (ARI=0.4832). It was found that refine was beneficial for weak fusion (attention +0.086) and harmful for strong fusion (-0.054). ✅ Fixed key bugs in evaluate_mimicgen.py and collector.py (2026-02-23) — The bug causing in-place array modification of quat2axisangle was fixed (using np.clip+copy instead). The collector.py’s scene-level EnvironmentMismatchError catching was fixed. The reproducibility of env.seed() was ensured, and the state dimension 8D was verified by assertion. Problems and Solutions 1. Pi0.5 full fine-tuning requires ~62GB/GPU. JAX FSDP=4 cannot effectively reduce memory. Repeated OOM in [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Switch to the gemma_2b_lora+gemma_300m_lora architecture, which reduces training parameters by ~90% and reduces memory to ~22.5GB/GPU. Job 46553 ran smoothly at 2.0s/step.
2. All three-way evaluations in M14 crashed on scene 122/649: ~130 natural_* scenario xml_hash incompatibilities with the current mimicgen environment caused EnvironmentMismatchError, leading to a complete evaluation process crashing [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Add a try-except for EnvironmentMismatchError in collect_on_scenes() of collector.py. Skip incompatible scenarios and record warnings. Resume the process with –resume. It was confirmed that there were approximately 519 actual effective evaluation scenarios.
3. Training logs had serious distortions due to stdout buffering: Pi0.5 training showed only ~580 steps, while actually reaching step 3000. This led to misjudgment of training termination and unnecessary restarts [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-23) Solution: Standardize the addition of PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1+stdbuf -oL in the training script and sbatch submission scripts. For long-term training, use sbatch to submit independent batch jobs and decouple them from the interactive session lifecycle.
4. The success rate in Pi0.5 evaluation was 0%, which was misjudged as a model quality issue [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-26) Solution: It was confirmed that the root cause was that the training task (MimicGen 5 tasks) did not overlap with the evaluation task (PickPlace). Instead, it was evaluated on the training task (Coffee/Stack/Threading/Assembly).
5. Observation dimension mismatch in BC-RNN evaluation (Expected 65, got 37) [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-26) Solution: Add import mimicgen to create env.registry during create_env(). Do not strip the _D0 suffix anymore. Keep all MimicGen observables (the _D0 environment provides additional object state observables).
6. In scGPT, TransformerModel.init omitted self.use_fast_transformer, causing key remapping in load_pretrained() to never execute. This led to random initialization of 17.7% attention weights. When strict=False, this was silently ignored [MIHD] (2026-02-28) Solution: Add a line of self.use_fast_transformer = use_fast_transformer to model.py. After fixing this, ARI improved by 44.4%, and all 11 DLPFC slices’ embeddings were re-extracted.
7. WebSocket client always encountered ConnectionRefusedError. Attempting different address formats (localhost/127.0.0.1/0.0.0.0) failed [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-28)Solution: It was found that the http_proxy environment variable was set to 127.0.0.1:10087. The websockets library failed to connect through this proxy. After unsetting all proxy variables at the beginning of run_eval.sh, the connection succeeded. 8. The CoffeeMachineBodyObject lacks the get_bounding_box_half_size() method. An AttributeError was reported during the rollout initialization of the MimicGen Coffee task due to an interface gap between MimicGen and the current robosuite version. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-02-28) Solution: The entire call chain (coffee_machine.py → CoffeeMachineBodyObject → CompositeBodyObject → MujocoXMLObject) was traced back. The get_bounding_box_half_size() method was implemented in each of the three different levels of base classes.
9. In HD data, the uns[‘spatial’] images appear to be full-sized but their coordinates are in the fullres pixel space after cropping. This mismatch leads to visual misalignment. Additionally, since X has been logged transformed, the pipeline will again perform normalize_total + log1p, resulting in a double transformation. [MIHD] (2026-02-28) Solution: In load_hd_data(), replace uns[‘spatial’] images with cropped_fullres.tif and recalculate the scalefactor. Add a skip_log parameter to preprocess_data(). For HD data with skip_log=True, only perform HVG filtering.
10. The Coffee BC-RNN loss converged normally (-7.66→-15.4), but the SR remained at 0% for a long time. Tasks configured with the same parameters achieved 64% after 20 epochs. This performance is highly similar to that of models with insufficient capacity. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: By comparing the actual obs key structure in the HDF5 dataset, it was found that Coffee requires a 57-dimensional object state (required for multi-stage precise operations). The configuration template lacked this field. Add extra_low_dim=[‘object’] to all 9 tasks and regenerate the configuration.
11. There were four consecutive bugs when accessing image observations with robomimic BC-RNN: The logic for enabling the camera, the checkpoint config in JSON string format, issues with obtaining image resolution, and problems with converting from HWC to CHW formats. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: ① Check the rgb modality from the checkpoint config JSON string (which requires json.loads). ② Read the actual 84×84 size from shape_metadata. ③ Manually perform HWC→CHW transposition and conversion from uint8 to float32/255 normalization (this is automatically handled by ObsUtils.process_obs in standard rollouts).
12. There were four consecutive bugs when starting Pi0.5 LoRA training: ① Setting JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu alone was ineffective. ② The norm_stats path was incorrect. ③ The boolean parameter –overwrite=False was ineffective. ④ Access to W&B network interfaces on HPC nodes was blocked. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: ① Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously. ② The actual path for norm_stats is assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json. ③ Use –no-overwrite instead of –flag=False. ④ Preset WANDB_MODE to disabled.
13. Adapting the pipeline path for VisiumHD HD data: Failure occurred when finding the image path for the vision encoder and loading spatial coordinates during the fusion stage, as it assumed the DLPFC directory structure. [MIHD] (2026-03-01) Solution: Add a cropped_fullres.tif pattern recognition to find_spatial_image(). Pass crop_dir.parent as data_root when calling the vision encoder. In the fusion stage, directly load coordinates from data.obsm[‘spatial’] without looking up the path.
14. When calculating norm_stats, the GPU util value was 0, but the memory was fully occupied. The task did not progress for a long time. The root cause was that PaligemmaTokenizer eagerly loaded all visible GPUs when calling get_config().model. [Error Recovery Benchmark] (2026-03-01) Solution: Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously to force the CPU mode (norm_stats does not require model inference at all). Add CPU parallelism and sub-sampling (max-frames 10000) to reduce the calculation time from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes.
Learning Outcomes Architecture JAX FSDP only divides parameter storage, which does not affect the loading of the complete model during initialization or forward activation memory. The full training state for Pi0.5 requires ~62GB/GPU, and a single A800 cannot handle full fine-tuning with 80GB. LoRA (gemma_2b_lora) is the only feasible solution for the A800 cluster, reducing the memory requirement to ~22.5GB. Switch to LoRA when encountering the warning “Can’t reduce memory use below 62.46GiB”. The observation keys configured for BC-RNN must match the task complexity: Tasks with multi-stage precise operations like Coffee require a 57-dimensional object state. A single 84×84 image cannot provide sufficient spatial precision. The performance of configuration bugs (normal loss but SR=0) is highly similar to insufficient model capacity. It is necessary to distinguish between them by comparing dataset structures. Vectorization and batching significantly accelerate the process: O(n²) nested loops can be reduced by about 100-500x through cdist matrix operations. Python’s loop iteration can also be accelerated by about 20-50x through batch GPU forward operations. Norm_stats calculations do not require model inference at all. Forcing the CPU mode, sub-sampling, and parallelism can reduce the calculation time from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes. Methodology for restructuring large repositories: Systematically grep to verify all imported dependencies, then execute in the order of “creating new modules → updating references → deleting old files”, and perform a dry-run verification. Before merging functions with the same name but different variants, carefully compare the boundary conditions (>vs>=vs< can lead to completely opposite results). Debugging PyTorch model.load_state_dict(strict=False) will silently ignore non-matching keys. Keyed weights may run for a long time with random values without causing any errors. Production code should actively print missing_keys/unexpected_keys and verify the statistical values of parameters. There may also be similar bugs in the official open-source repository. It is necessary to audit the weight loading path actively rather than blindly trusting it. Key details of robomimic BC-RNN inference: The checkpoint config is stored as a JSON string (which requires json.loads, not direct dict access). When bypassing the standard rollout process, manual HWC→CHW transposition and uint8→float32/255 normalization are required (this is automatically handled by ObsUtils.process obs in standard rollouts). Domain Knowledge Key conclusions from M14: BC-RNN SR=0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 SR=0%. The Recovery Progress of Random (0.0199) was actually higher than all learning strategies. This directly proves the necessity of LoRA fine-tuning in M15. The 649 scenario database consists of two types of environments: ~519 impulse/augmented compatible scenarios (xml_hash matching) and ~130 natural_* natural capture scenarios (xml_hash different). There were approximately 519 effective evaluation scenarios in M14. All document and target episode numbers need to be updated accordingly. STAIG fusion is overall leading in the RM-IDEAL evaluation (average r=0.396 for 151673, peak r=0.644 for Layer_3). Multimodal fusion has a much better ability to capture spatial niche structures than single-modal baselines (gene/vision average r≈0.06-0.12). The effect of Vision Refine is bipolar: It is beneficial for strong fusion (attention +0.086 ARI), harmful for weak fusion (-0.054 for qformer). Strong fusion already has the ability to handle high-dimensional inputs, and reducing dimensions actually results in a loss of information. Cross-task evaluation is a common pitfall in robot learning: If the training set task does not overlap with the evaluation task, it directly leads to 0% SR. It is necessary to ensure consistency between train/eval tasks during the experimental design phase. The MimicGen _D0 environment provides additional object observables. Removing the _D0 suffix from observations reduces the dimensionality from 65 to 37, disrupting the input consistency during reasoning. VisiumHD coordinate mapping: 2um bin of r_big//4 results in an 8um grid row (similar for col). The visualization size parameters of scanpy spatial are multiples of spot_diameter_fullres. For HD with 8μm diameter (diameter≈7.3), the size should be approximately 4.0. For DLPFC with a diameter of approximately 144, use size=1.0. The Leiden community found that resolution=1.0 is the default clustering strategy for unlabeled HD data (crop10large k=17, Silhouette=0.302). Tools Best practices for Slurm cluster training projects: For long-term training, sbatch should be used instead of srun to avoid session timeout due to SIGTERM. All training scripts should be standardized with PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 + stdbuf -oL. Before starting the job, use fuser /dev/nvidiaX to clean up zombie GPU processes (monitoring tools like nvitop also hold CUDA contexts). MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID corresponds to the physical GPU number, not the logical CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES number. Key configurations for JAX/Pi0.5 training: ① Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously. ② XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION=0.95 is effective for A800 with 80GB (from 61 to 77.7GB). ③ HPC nodes must have WANDB_MODE set to disabled. ④ The argparse boolean flag –no-overwrite cannot be used instead of –flag=False. ⑤ The openpi norm_stats path is assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json. The HTTP/HTTPS proxy environment variable (http_proxy) will be transparently proxied by the Python websockets library to establish WebSocket connections. When debugging local services on HPC clusters, it is necessary to unset the proxy variables. The MimicGen environment registers with robosuite through the side effects of importing mimicgen. It is necessary to explicitly perform this import before calling external tools. It is necessary to trace back the entire inheritance chain and implement methods that are missing in third-party libraries in all base classes. Only fixing the point where it is called most recently will cause other subclasses to still report errors. The pam_slurm_adopt cluster’s GPU nodes do not support direct SSH access. srun –overlap can attach a new step to an existing interactive job to reuse node resources. Robomimic does not have a TTY environment, and will exit with EOFError if it encounters an existing checkpoint directory. It is necessary to completely clean the directory before starting each time. The checkpoint directory is the most reliable evidence of training completion. A SLURM job crashing does not mean that the training has failed. Notes on AI Usage Effective Modes:
✓ Systematic code auditing to identify root causes: By comparing the key values in checkpoints and model state_dicts, it was possible to accurately locate the bug in ScGPT weight loading (just one line of omission). By comparing the actual obs key structure in the HDF5 dataset, a configuration defect in Coffee was discovered independently (without any human intervention). ✓ Complete call chain tracing: Fixed the issue where CoffeeMachineBodyObject lacked a method by tracing the three-level inheritance chain (coffee_machine → CoffeeMachineBodyObject → CompositeBodyObject → MujocoXMLObject). Implement it in all base classes instead of just fixing the point where it is called most recently. ✓ Progressive debugging: Resolved Pi0.5 training OOM issues by analyzing step by step (downgrading dependencies → patching source code → switching architectures → submitting sbatch jobs). ✓ SubAgent parallel monitoring: Monitored multiple long-time experiments through TaskOutput in the background and advanced the different projects across DCC/Tianhe clusters in parallel. Limitations:
✗ Misjudged the memory usage principle of JAX FSDP (thought that FSDP=4 could be reduced to ~16GB). Only switched to LoRA after several OOMs. It was necessary to take immediate action when encountering the warning “Can’t reduce memory use below 62.46GiB”. ✗ Failed to identify the most obvious reason for the 0% success rate in Pi0.5 during diagnosis. Listed secondary factors such as OOM/action clipping first, and was directly questioned by users, hitting the key point. ✗ Lack of active long-term task health checks: Abnormalities such as GPU idling (util=0 but full memory) and processes getting stuck relied on users to discover them actively, rather than AI auditing them. ✗ lacked preventive verification before integrating external tools: Did not check the availability of mclust/rpy2, image format conventions (HWC vs CHW), outbound network restrictions (W&B), and zombie GPU processes before multiple failures were fixed. ✗ Attempted to automatically call ExitPlanMode to proceed after writing the plan several times (was rejected by users 2-3 times). AI failed to understand that a plan is a decision-making checkpoint for users, not an automatic progress signal. ✗ Lacked active verification of outdated dialogue cache information (such as Pi0.5 step 5000 crash records). It was necessary to rely on the actual checkpoint file status rather than dialogue records. Next Week’s Plans Next week’s core tasks: ① Wait and analyze the results of the LoRA 9 task fine-tuning for Pi0.5 (expected to be completed in 53 hours). Compare it with the 4.2% base model baseline to verify the general improvement effect of LoRA on MimicGen tasks. ② Progress the training of BC-RNN 9 tasks to 600 epochs, focusing on the convergence of complex tasks such as Coffee/ThreePieceAssembly D1. ③ Fix the simulator bug in the Coffee environment initialization (object misalignment/crashing), and complete the closed-loop evaluation of Coffee BC-RNN. ④ Start the M15 experiment design: Based on the conclusions from M14 (learning strategy error scenario SR≈0%), plan the data collection strategy for LoRA fine-tuning in error scenarios, and determine the sampling ratio and data volume of error types. ⑤ Expand MIHD: Run multimodal benchmark tests on more VisiumHD slices to verify the statistical stability of STAIG fusion. Also conduct a special investigation into the training collapse of DLPFC 151676 slices (adjust tau/dropout rate/figure construction parameters).
