summary: This week, we have been working concurrently on two projects within the DCC and Tianhe clusters: Space Transcriptomics (MIHD) and the Error Recovery Benchmark. On the DCC side, we fixed a critical weight loading bug in scGPT, resulting in an average improvement of 44.4% for ARI tasks. We also extended the MIHD benchmark to the Visium HD dataset and completed a major refactoring of the code involving approximately 250,000 lines of code. On Tianhe, we established a new BC-RNN Phoenix training pipeline with 9 parallel tasks. We identified and fixed the root cause of the task distribution mismatch that led to zero success rates in the Pi0.5 evaluation. This led to key conclusions from the M14 baseline evaluation: the learning strategy achieved SR≈0% in error scenarios, proving the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Additionally, we successfully stabilized the parallel fine-tuning of Pi0.5 LoRA on 6×A800 hardware.
title: 每周总结 2026-W09

content:
Daily Report — 2026-03-01 Today’s Highlights What was done: Across DCC and tianhe servers, we advanced the space transcriptomics toolchain (STHD/MIHD) and robotics learning benchmarks. DCC focused on restructuring VisiumHD’s multimodal data analysis and repository architecture, while tianhe focused on model evaluation, fixing configuration defects, generating error scenarios, and initializing Pi0.5 LoRA fine-tuning. How it was done: DCC used a security refactoring process involving grep dependency analysis, batch migration, and dry-run validation to complete the multimodal pipeline. Tianhe monitored training using TensorBoard API, identified configuration defects by comparing HDF5 dataset structures, iterated to fix image format adaptations, and gradually resolved four training initialization bugs related to JAX environment variables. What’s achieved: The MIHD repository was significantly streamlined (41 files deleted, ~250K code removed), establishing a clear module structure. Coffee BC-RNN defect root causes were identified and fixed. Pi0.5 LoRA 9-task parallel training ran stably on 6×A800. Error Recovery Benchmark provided the first batch of high-success-rate BC-RNN data and Pi0.5 baseline evaluation. The infrastructure documentation system was fully established. DCC What was done: We deepened the STHD CLAUDE.md, implemented three types of annotation visualization for VisiumHD (pathologist/STHD/STAIG), fixed HD adaptation bugs in the MIHD pipeline, completed end-to-end STAIG fusion operations, visualized scGPT 11-slice KMeans results, and re-engineered the MIHD process into six stages. We also added a report from February 28, 2026. How it was done: We gradually debugged HD data paths (discovered a pattern of r_big//4 mapping), fixed four issues in vision encoder/fusion/mclust. The refactoring process followed the sequence of “grep validation → creating new modules → updating references → deleting old files.” Dry-run validation showed 440 experiments passed. Unusable mclust was replaced with KMeans. What’s achieved: For the first time, MIHD successfully performed pca×uni2×staig_fusion multimodal fusion on VisiumHD (Silhouette=0.343 vs PCA 0.086). The repository deleted 41 files (~250K code), 5.3MB images, and fixed the drop_feature bug. scGPT 11-slice visualization was reduced from expected 6+ hours to 2 minutes. Tianhe What was done: We completed the evaluation of Pi0.5’s 9-task basic model (4.2% SR), fixed Coffee BC-RNN object observation key deficiencies, developed a BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline (11 scenarios + MP4), cleaned up ~530GB GPU VRAM, monitored BC-RNN 9-task training, organized project files v4.17/v4.18, and successfully initialized Pi0.5 LoRA 9-task parallel fine-tuning (fixed four bugs). How it was done: We executed GPU tasks on an49 nodes using SLURM srun –overlap. We identified Coffee configuration defects by comparing HDF5 dataset structures and iteratively fixed four issues in robomimic BC-RNN image observation. We also fixed four initialization bugs related to JAX_PLATFORMS/norm_stats path/boolean parameters/W&B. Using CPU + sub-sampling, we reduced the norm_stats calculation time from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes. What’s achieved: BC-RNN Stack tasks reached 64% SR by epoch 22, verifying data quality. Coffee defect fixes laid the foundation for retraining complex tasks. Pi0.5 LoRA ran stably on 6×A800 (77.7GB per card, with 100% utilization), and the 9-task fine-tuning infrastructure was fully established. Across DCC and tianhe servers, we advanced four projects in parallel: DCC completed VisiumHD’s three types of annotation visualization and STAIG fusion multimodal operations (Silhouette 0.343), and carried out a six-stage large-scale cleanup and restructuring of the MIHD repository (deleting ~250K code). Tianhe completed Pi0.5’s 9-task basic model evaluation (4.2% SR), identified and fixed Coffee BC-RNN object observation key deficiencies, developed a BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline (fixed four image bugs to generate 11 scenarios), and successfully initialized Pi0.5 LoRA 9-task parallel fine-tuning with 100% GPU utilization on 6×A800.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ VisiumHD barcode mapping discovery and three types of annotation visualization — Discovered a pattern of r_big//4 mapping (17502/17502 complete matches), generated pathologist annotations for four tissue types: Neoplasm 48.6%, Connective 29.5%, etc., STHD cell type visualizations with 85 categories reduced to 11 coarse categories by majority vote, achieving 96.3% match rate. We established a baseline for VisiumHD data analysis. ✅ MIHD pipeline HD dataset adaptation and end-to-end STAIG fusion — Fixed four HD path adaptation bugs (adding cropped_fullres.tif mode in find_spatial_image, passing vision encoder data_root, loading spatial coords during fusion phase, mclust→KMeans fallback). We completed the pca×uni2×staig_fusion process for VisiumHD crop10large (5.5 minutes for patch extraction, 5 minutes for UNI2 GPU inference, 6 minutes for STAIG training, with Silhouette of 0.343). ✅ MIHD repository six-stage cleanup and restructuring (deleting ~250K code) — Phase1 deleted 41 dead files (~250K code + 5.3MB images); Phase2 moved run_benchmark.py functions to six modules (staig_utils, vision_extractors, Fusion, clustering, etc.), updated all pipeline/imports; Phase3 removed run_benchmark.py and five dependent models; Phase4 extracted STAIG common code and fixed the drop_feature logic reversal bug in BasicContrastive (>= replaced with <); Phase5 updated all documentation; Phase6 dry-run validation showed 440 experiments passed. ✅ Coffee BC-RNN configuration defect identification and repair (object observation keys) — By comparing actual HDF5 dataset observation key structures, AI independently identified missing object observation keys in Coffee’s configuration template (Coffee requires 57-dimensional object states: Pod/Machine/Holder position + relative orientation + hinge angle). We added extra_low_dim=[‘object’] to all nine tasks, re-generated and validated all configuration files, fixing the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR failure. ✅ BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline development — Fixed four consecutive bugs in robomimic BC-RNN image observation (enable_camera detection, json.loads parsing checkpoint config, automatic detection of 84×84 resolution, HWC→CHW transformation + float32 normalization). We created configs/benchmark_v4_stack.yaml and scripts/batch_visualize_policy_scenes.py, resulting in 11 tip_over error scenarios and 11 MP4 visualization videos. ✅ Pi0.5 LoRA 9-task fine-tuning pipeline setup and successful initialization — Created train_pi05_benchmark.py (five sub-routines), registered 18 configurations to openpi/config.py (nine for fine-tuning, nine for inference), fixed vla_server.py’s IMAGE_KEY_MAP prefix matching defects. We completed HDF5→LeRobot data conversion for nine tasks (avoiding OOM segfault per task), and completed all norm_stats calculations in 2.5 minutes using CPU parallelization + sub-sampling. Fixed four initialization bugs (JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu, assets path, –no-overwrite, WANDB_MODE=disabled). We successfully initialized six tasks with 100% GPU utilization (GPU usage: 77.7GB per card). ✅ Pi0.5 Phoenix 9-task basic model evaluation — Monitored nine MimicGen tasks for 50 rollouts each, achieving total SR of 4.2% (19/450), with Stack_D0 (24%) and Stack_D1 (12%) showing substantial success. The remaining seven tasks had 0-2% success rates. We confirmed that the third training phase reached step 99999 (the previous two failed due to SLURM time limit conflicts). 🔄 Coffee BC-RNN rollout visualization and environment initialization issues troubleshooting — Generated coffee_d0 rollout videos (742KB). Users reported issues with object misregistration or freezing during environment initialization (simulator bugs, not model problems). We began investigating Coffee env kwargs but session truncation prevented further investigation. Implementation and Fixes ✅ GPU VRAM cleanup and BC-RNN 9-task training monitoring — Found and cleaned up about 530GB of idle VRAM on an49 nodes (zhaoganlong Phoenix serve_policy 407GB + two old VLA servers, each with 61GB). Monitored nine BC-RNN tasks: stack_d0 (64%@epoch22), stack_d1 (44%), coffee (0%), stack_three/threading/three_piece_assembly all achieved 58-96%@epoch300-420. ✅ Infrastructure reference documentation creation and project file organization v4.17/v4.18 — Created docs/infrastructure_reference.md (649 scenarios/9 types of errors/6 detectors/4 injectors/full pipeline diagrams, 13 chapters). In v4.17, archived old files in the four-layer directory structure, merged VLM tutorials, and created EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES.md. In v4.18, extracted create_env()/load_task_registry() to script_utils.py (eliminating ~210 lines of duplicate code), updated CLAUDE.md with instructions for M14 evaluation commands. ✅ scGPT 11-slice KMeans visualization — Terminated the time-consuming mclust task (completed 2/11 in 2 hours), and used KMeans to complete all 11 DLPFC slices in 2 minutes, with an average ARI of 0.1695 and NMI of 0.2772. ✅ STHD CLAUDE.md deep improvement — Analyzed all core modules of STHD, added descriptions for six previously omitted modules (frontline.py, qcmask.py, etc.), optimized probability models, detailed Numba JIT operations, and processed patch overlap logic in pdata TSV format. ✅ Supplementary report from February 28, 2026 (MIHD experimental metrics and visualization) — Added a summary of Chapter 5 results and a list of output files for Chapter 6, including 151673 multi-modal benchmark tables, comparisons between Vision Refinement before and after, and over 60 visualizations. Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. VisiumHD HD dataset path adaptation: vision encoder image paths, spatial coords loading, and multiple failures during fusion due to assuming a DLPFC directory structure. Solution: Three fixes were made: add cropped_fullres.tif mode recognition in find_spatial_image(); pass crop_dir.parent as data_root when calling vision encoder; load coordinates directly from adata.obsm[‘spatial’] during fusion phase, bypassing path lookup.
Key insight: The pipeline design assumes a flat DLPFC directory structure (data_root/section_id/), but HD data needs to be mapped at the call level rather than modifying the encoder’s internal logic. The path abstraction layer should provide interfaces for adapting to different dataset structures.
2. Invisibility of long-term task progress: GPU utilization was 0%, but VRAM was fully loaded, making it impossible to determine if it was a CPU bottleneck or model eager loading causing wasted time. Solution: Set PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 and add tqdm for critical loops; set compute_norm_stats.py to require CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu (root cause: PaligemmaTokenizer is eagerly loaded into all visible GPUs during get_config().model call, but norm_stats only requires dataset transforms).
Key insight: GPU utilization of 0% could indicate a CPU preprocessing bottleneck or eager loading without inference calculations. The difference between the two can be distinguished by monitoring VRAM usage with nvidia-smi. Norm stats doesn’t require model inference and should run in CPU mode by default.
3. Coffee BC-RNN loss converged normally (-7.66→-15.4), but SR was always 0%, while Stack achieved 64% at epoch 20 on equivalent configurations. Solution: By comparing actual HDF5 dataset observation key structures, we found that Coffee requires 57-dimensional object states (multi-step precise operations: Pod/Machine/Holder position + relative orientation + hinge angle). The configuration template was missing this information. We added extra_low_dim=[‘object’] to all nine tasks and re-generated the configuration.
Key insight: BC-RNN’s observation keys must match task complexity. Coffee requires multi-step precision operations, and 84×84 images don’t provide enough spatial accuracy information. The performance of configuration bugs (normal loss but SR=0) is highly similar to insufficient model capabilities. This can only be distinguished by comparing dataset structures.
4. Robomimic BC-RNN image observation integration failure: four consecutive bugs (enable camera, checkpoint config format, resolution, image format). Solution: ① Detect rgb modality automatic activation from checkpoint config fields (JSON strings, not dict access); ② Read actual image size (84×84) from shape_metadata and pass it to create_env; ③ Manually perform HWC→CHW transformation and uint8→float32/255 normalization in _prepare_image_obs().
Key insight: Robomimic checkpoint config is stored as a JSON string, which doesn’t follow intuition. When bypassing the standard rollout process, the format conversion automatically completed by ObsUtils.process_obs needs manual supplementation.
5. Pi0.5 LoRA training had four consecutive obstruction bugs: JAX CPU forced failure, norm_stats path error, boolean parameter format issues, and W&B proxy blocking. Solution: ① Set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu for JAX CPU mode (the latter cannot be omitted); ② Output norm stats to assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json, not checkpoints/; ③ Use –no-overwrite for argparse boolean flags, not –overwrite=False; ④ HPC nodes have no outbound network, so set WANDB_MODE=disabled.
Key insight: JAX XLA backend detects independently of CUDA environment variables. Openpi norm stats path is strongly bound to dataset_name and written to assets/. HPC nodes should disable all log systems that rely on external networks by default.
6. MIHD run_benchmark.py was heavily imported by pipeline, making it impossible to delete directly; BasicContrastive.py had reversed drop_feature logic.Solution: Conduct a systematic grep to identify all reference points and migrate in the order of “create new modules → update references → delete old files.” Fix the issue with drop_feature: change >= drop_prob to < drop_prob, and import the corrected version from staig_utils. Key insights: The order of large-scale refactoring migrations is crucial. Functions with the same name may have three variants in three different files. Careful comparison of semantics before merging is necessary (differences in > / >= / < boundary conditions can lead to completely opposite functionality).
General Issues 7. SLURM srun commands hang on nodes with interactive sessions; orbax checkpoint resume conflicts (Destination …/5000 already exists) Solution: Add –overlap to srun to allow shared execution. For orbax resume, set overwrite=True explicitly or clear the existing step directory in advance.
Key insights: SLURM interactive jobs are assigned exclusively by default. Orbax resume=True does not automatically overwrite existing step directories, which is a common pitfall in JAX training frameworks.
8. Bash tools fail multiple times on tianhe nodes (all echo/true/pwd have exit code 1), making it impossible to execute any shell commands. Solution: Use Read/Write/Edit/Glob tools to perform all file operations. For tasks that require a shell, clearly mark them as tasks for user execution.
Key insights: Claude Code toolset can replace most Bash file operations. When Bash fails, switch strategies proactively and inform users instead of waiting in vain.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Active discovery and optimization decisions regarding GPU resources Role Thoughts Humans Actively discover anomalies where GPU util=0 but VRAM is fully utilized (VisiumHD patch causes issues with CPU, norm_stats loads the full model, an49 leaves 530GB VRAM idle). Proactively stop and investigate root causes. Based on knowledge of JAX memory mechanisms, request XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION=0.95, and expand training GPUs from 1-4 to 1-6 (considering global cluster resources). AI Tend to wait for tasks to complete without actively auditing resources. Passively respond to GPU anomalies. In the norm_stats scenario, provide precise root cause analysis through agent-tracking of source code (PaligemmaTokenizer eager loading). Analyze the advantages and disadvantages of memory optimization passively, but do not proactively suggest solutions.** Difference analysis: Humans actively discover system-level anomalies using nvidia-smi and prior knowledge to provide optimization directions. AI has an advantage in root cause analysis depth but lacks proactive monitoring awareness. It fails to detect GPU resources available from 5-6.
Execution permissions and rhythm control (multiple times rejected ExitPlanMode) Role Thoughts Humans Reject AI’s automatic execution requests for irreversible operations such as large-scale file operations, batch submissions of GPU cluster tasks, and training submissions. Require reviewing the plan or starting with a single task for gradual verification.** AI Tend to request execution authorization immediately after planning. Expect to complete all work at once, maximizing parallelism and automation.** Difference analysis: Humans maintain strict review rhythm for irreversible operations. Gradual verification (running a single task first and then batching) is more reasonable in HPC resource-constrained environments. AI systematically underestimates the need for review and gradual verification.
Independent discovery of BC-RNN defects Role Thoughts Humans Discover anomalies where Coffee loss is normal but SR=0%. Submit a diagnostic request without providing specific hypotheses.** AI Systematically compare Coffee/Stack configuration files. Check the actual obs key structure in HDF5 datasets (find object: 57-dimensional). Analyze differences in task complexity and independently conclude that “object keys are missing” (without any human guidance).** Difference analysis: AI has a stronger ability to identify root causes through systematic data/code comparisons compared to human intuition. Humans discover problems based on anomalies, while AI discovers root causes through structured exploration.
Coffee simulator bug vs identification of model learning failures Role Thoughts Humans Immediately recognize that the problem was an object misalignment or freezing issue during rollout video playback, rather than a model problem.** AI Initially attribute the 0% SR to model failure. Tend to attribute problems from data/parameters/task complexity perspectives.** Difference analysis: Human intuition and simulator experience allow humans to quickly distinguish between physical errors and learning failures. AI lacks direct perception of simulator visual anomalies and tends to attribute simulator bugs to model capabilities.
Pragmatic engineering decisions (tool replacement and diagnostic scope) Role Thoughts Humans Directly decide to replace mclust with KMeans under environmental constraints, as it is more efficient than fixing dependencies. For Pi0.5 training failures, choose “only diagnosing without repairing” (since the third attempt has reached 99999 steps). First, evaluate the results before deciding whether to retrain.** AI Tend to repair existing tools (install rpy2) or provide complete diagnostic and repair solutions. Do not prioritize the key constraint of “existing checkpoints are evaluable”.** Difference analysis: Humans make more pragmatic resource prioritization decisions. AI tends to focus on completeness and repair, sometimes ignoring key constraints like “third attempt has been successful.”
AI Limitations Major limitations Lack of active long-term task health checks: Relying on users to discover anomalies such as GPU idling or process freezing instead of regular checks. Set up active monitoring mechanisms for tasks that take more than 5 minutes, rather than waiting for output or responding passively. -Lack of preventive verification before integrating external tools/libraries: No pre-checking the availability of mclust/rpy2, external script CLI interfaces (compute_norm_stats.py parameter format), memory consumption during large-scale operations (LeRobot handling segfaults), or image format conventions (robosuite HWC vs robomimic CHW). This led to multiple failures before fixing problems. The bug in the 4th layer of image observation is a concentrated example of systematic lack of foresight. -Lack of specificity in path and environment assumptions: MIHD pipeline hard-codes the DLPFC directory structure. Script errors assume norm_stats output goes to checkpoints/. JAX CPU forcibly omits JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu. H5 does not actively anticipate that HPC nodes have no outbound network (W&B), both of which led to failures during initial execution before fixing problems. -Assumption bias: Misjudge the Coffee simulator environment initialization bug (object misalignment/freezing) as a lack of model learning ability until users watch videos. Lack of means to perceive simulator visual anomalies. -Tendency to automate batch executions: In large pipelines, do not actively ask if users need gradual verification. Users need multiple interventions to control execution粒度。Killing old processes without realizing that the launcher has restarted child processes with a new PID requires 3 rounds of killing operations. General limitations -Lack of ability to diagnose root causes when Bash tools fail. Can only bypass them passively. SubAgent exploration reports sometimes rely on document inferences rather than actual file system scans, resulting in inferred conclusions that do not match the actual directory structure.
Today’s Takeaways Key takeaways VisiumHD coordinate mapping: 2um bin r_big//4 yields an 8um grid row (similar for col). Annotation barcode format is s_008um_{row:05d}_{col:05d}-1. MIHD on VisiumHD has pca×uni2×staig_fusion Silhouette=0.343, which is significantly better than pure PCA’s 0.086. This verifies the effectiveness of multimodal fusion for HD data. Key configuration for robomimic BC-RNN inference: Checkpoint config stored as a JSON string (needs json.loads, not direct dict access). shape_metadata records actual image sizes. When bypassing standard rollout, manual HWC→CHW transformation and uint8→float32/255 normalization are required (robomimic’s standard rollout automatically handles ObsUtils.process_obs). Key configuration for JAX/Pi0.5 training: ① Force CPU to set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES="" + JAX_PLATFORMS=cpu simultaneously; ② XLA_PYTHON_CLIENT_MEM_FRACTION=0.95 is effective for A800 with 80GB (from 61 to 77.7GB); ③ HPC nodes must preset WANDB_MODE=disabled; ④ argparse boolean flag should use –no-overwrite, not –flag=False; ⑤ openpi norm_stats path is assets//benchmark//norm_stats.json (not checkpoints/). BC-RNN configuration matches task complexity: Coffee requires a 57-dimensional object state (multi-stage precise operations are necessary); Stack only requires simple stacking of images. Orbax resume=True does not automatically overwrite existing step directories; explicit overwrite=True is required (a common JAX ecosystem trap). -Methodology for large repository refactoring: Systematically use grep to verify all imported dependencies, then execute in the order of “create new → update references → delete old”. Carefully compare boundary conditions before merging variants with the same name (> vs >= vs < can lead to completely opposite functionality). -Comparison of BC-RNN and Pi0.5 capabilities: BC-RNN achieves 64-100% SR after 22 epochs for simple tasks (Stack D0/D1, Threading D0); complex multi-step tasks (Coffee, ThreePieceAssembly D1) have clear capacity limits (600 epochs still close to 0%); Pi0.5 base model has a SR of 4.2%, and the effectiveness of LoRA fine-tuning remains to be verified. -Accelerating norm_stats calculations: Only dataset transforms are needed; no model inference required. In CPU mode, with –max-frames set to 10000 and 9 tasks in parallel, execution time is reduced from 10+ minutes to 2.5 minutes (156 batches are sufficient for stable statistics). Practical takeaways -Tips for SLURM HPC debugging: srun –overlap allows additional commands to be executed on nodes with interactive sessions (direct SSH is blocked by pam_slurm_adopt). Nohup bash script child processes do not exit with the parent process. When killing old processes, ensure that the launcher and child processes are handled separately.
Session Summary STHD ✅ Analysis of STHD code library and in-depth improvement of CLAUDE.md 00:06:57.489 | claude_code Read all core modules of STHD, adding explanations for 6 previously omitted modules (frontline.py, qcmask.py, roi.py, sim.py, etc.), probability model optimization objectives, details on Numba JIT parallelization, pdata TSV format, and patch overlap processing logic. Explored the VisiumHD shared data directory and confirmed that both crop10/crop10large have STHD prediction results.
MIHD ✅ Visualization of three types of annotations for VisiumHD + end-to-end STAIG fusion + visualization of scGPT KMeans 00:06:22.389 | claude_code Discovered a 100% match between the r_big//4 barcode mapping rule, generated comparative visualizations for pathologist/STHD/STAIG fusion. After fixing 4 HD path adaptation bugs, completed the entire pca×uni2×staig_fusion process (Silhouette=0.343 vs PCA 0.086). Terminated the mclust task and replaced it with KMeans. The visualization of 11 slices was compressed from 6+ hours to 2 minutes (ARI=0.1695).
✅ 6-stage cleanup and refactoring of MIHD repository + filling in the daily report for February 28, 2026 02:46:18.544 | claude_code Executed according to the 6-stage refactoring plan provided by users: removed 41 dead files (~250K code), moved shared functions of run_benchmark.py to 6 modules, removed monolithic structures and 5 dependent models, extracted common STAIG code and fixed the logical reversal bug in drop_feature. Updated all documents and verified that 440 experiments passed during dry-run. At the same time, filled in the daily report for February 28, 2026, including 151673 multi-modal benchmark tables and over 60 visualization hyperlinks.
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Completion of evaluation for 9 tasks on Pi0.5 + fixing BC-RNN configuration issues + organizing v4.17 files + preparing LoRA data for Pi0.5 00:09:05.761 | claude_code Obtained Pi0.5 evaluation results (total SR=4.2%, with Stack having the highest SR of 24%). Diagnosed two failures in Pi0.5’s first two training attempts (SLURM time limit + orbax conflict), and confirmed that the third attempt has reached 99999 steps. AI independently identified the root cause of Coffee’s 0% SR (missing 57-dimensional object observation keys), fixed it, and verified 9 configuration files. Completed the organization of v4.17 files (archive/four-layer directory, VLM tutorial integration, EXTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES.md). Completed the data conversion for 8/9 tasks using LeRobot, wrote training startup scripts, and ExitPlanMode was rejected.
🔄 BC-RNN Stack_D0 error scenario generation pipeline (fixed 4 bugs) + infrastructure documentation + GPU cleanup + training monitoring 01:10:59.841 | claude_code Iteratively fixed 4 image observation bugs (detecting enable_camera, parsing with json.loads, automatic detection of 84×84 resolution, HWC→CHW transformation). Successfully generated 11 tip_over error scenarios and MP4 videos. Cleaned an49’s idle 530GB VRAM. Created docs/infrastructure_reference.md (649 scenarios/9 types of errors/complete component documentation, 13 chapters). Monitored 7 BC-RNN tasks (Stack was nearly满分, Coffee completely failed). Discovered a simulator bug in coffee rollout video and fixed it before completion.
✅ Initial evaluation of BC-RNN + organization of v4.18 code + verification and successful startup of Pi0.5 LoRA pipeline with 4 bugs fixed 21:48:48.390 | claude_code Obtained first evaluation results through TensorBoard API (Stack_D0=64%@epoch22, Coffee=0%). Implemented v4.18 organization (extracted script_utils.py to eliminate ~210 duplicate lines, updated CLAUDE.md). Verified the complete link for coffee_d0 (1000 demos/2.4GB). Completed task-by-task conversion of 9 datasets (changed to task-by-task after segfault in batch processing). Fixed 4 Pi0.5 training startup bugs (JAX_PLATFORMS/assets path/–no-overwrite/W&B), expanded to GPU 1-6+XLA_MEM=0.95, and finally achieved stable operation with 6 A800 GPUs and 77.7GB per card at 100% utilization for 9 tasks in parallel LoRA fine-tuning.

summary: 在DCC和tianhe这两台服务器上，我们同时推进了四个项目：DCC完成了VisiumHD的三种注释可视化处理以及STAIG融合多模态运行（Silhouette得分0.343），并对MIHD仓库进行了六阶段的大规模清理重构（删除了约250K条代码）；tianhe则完成了Pi0.5基础模型评估（SR率为4.2%），修复了Coffee BC-RNN中对象观测键缺失的问题，开发了BC-RNN Stack_D0错误场景生成流水线（修复了四层图像生成的11个场景问题），并在修复了四个启动错误之后，成功以6×A800 GPU的100%利用率来并行微调Pi0.5 LoRA任务。
title: 缺陷记录表 2026-03-01

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-28 Today’s Overview What was done: Two servers were used to synchronize three main tasks: expanding the MIHD benchmark on DCC to Visium HD data (crop10large, 17502 spots) and fixing a critical weight loading bug in scGPT. Additionally, an automated training script for BC-RNN Phoenix was implemented on tianhe, and the Pi0.5 Phoenix nine-task evaluation deployment was completed. How it was done: On DCC side, code audits were conducted to identify and fix single-line omissions by comparing checkpoints with key values. Full HD support for 8 files and 189 visualizations using RM-IDEAL were also achieved. On tianhe side, challenges such as SLURM constraints, WebSocket proxy interception, and compatibility issues between multiple robosuite libraries were gradually resolved. Finally, 9 BC-RNN tasks were run in parallel on a single GPU. What’s achieved: The MIHD benchmark was extended from DLPFC to HD datasets. ScGPT recovered 17.7% of randomly initialized attention weights. The advantages of staig_fusion multi-modal fusion were quantitatively verified. On tianhe side, a reusable VLA baseline evaluation and BC-RNN training pipeline were established, laying the foundation for comparative experiments with the Phoenix paper. DCC What was done: Fixed the critical weight loading bug in scGPT and extracted embeddings from 11 DLPFC slices. Full HD support for crop10large was implemented, and a complete RM-IDEAL evaluation of section 151673 was completed (27 methods × 7 layers, 189 visualizations). How it was done: By comparing checkpoints with model state_dict keys, an omission in self.use_fast_transformer in model.py was identified. Eight core files (data_loader/clustering/run_benchmark/pipeline) were modified to add HD support without labels. The Leiden community found a way to automatically estimate k and accelerate Wasserstein distance calculations. What’s achieved: ScGPT showed an average improvement of 44.4% in ARI and 33.3% in NMI. Staig_fusion ranked first in RM-IDEAL evaluations (average r=0.396, peak r=0.644). Multi-modal fusion was more effective than single-modal baselines in capturing spatial niche structures (avg r≈0.06-0.12). Tianhe What was done: It was confirmed that Pi0.5 Phoenix had completed 100k steps of training. A nine-task rollout evaluation was started and completed 7/9 tasks out of 9. Meanwhile, train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py was implemented and 5 library-level bugs were fixed. Successful parallel training of 9 BC-RNN tasks on a single GPU was achieved (with 50 rollouts every 20 epochs). How it was done: The training completion status was directly confirmed through the checkpoint directory. Issues such as tyro command syntax errors, WebSocket proxy interception, robosuite API incompatibility, and SLURM account/memory/EGL constraints were resolved. The srun –overlap method was used to reuse GPU nodes from existing interactive jobs. Three library-level defects in mujoco_py import, MimicGen environment registration, and get_bounding_box_half_size were fixed. What’s achieved: Pi0.5 Phoenix completed the evaluation deployment for the first time (24% on Stack_D0, 12% on Stack_D1). BC-RNN nine-task parallel training was stable (each task consumed ~2.2GB VRAM). TensorBoard recorded training curves and success rates. It is expected that 600 epochs of training will be completed in about 35 hours. On DCC, the Visium HD full-link extension for the MIHD project was completed (8 files modified) and the scGPT key weight loading bug was fixed (ARI +44.4%). On tianhe, the BC-RNN Phoenix baseline training pipeline was implemented from scratch, and successful parallel training of 9 tasks was started. At the same time, the Pi0.5 Phoenix evaluation deployment for the first time was completed (7/9 tasks completed).
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Fixed the scGPT use_fast_transformer weight loading bug and extracted full embeddings — The self.use_fast_transformer was omitted in TransformerModel.init, causing the key remapping in load_pretrained() to never execute. As a result, 17.7% of attention weights were randomly initialized. After fixing this issue, all 11 DLPFC slices (151508~151676) were extracted again, and the old cache was backed up to scgpt_buggy_backup/. Batch-generated spatial clustering visualizations were created (outputs/visualization/scgpt_fixed/). ✅ Full HD support for crop10large implementation (8 files modified) — After exploring the HD data structure, it was determined that crop10large regions (17502 spots) should be used. Eight files in the data_loader/clustering/run_benchmark/pipeline were modified to add HD data loading, automatic clustering without labels (estimate_n_clusters_leiden), and skip logic for HD-specific visualizations related to ARI/NMI. Issues such as coordinate space alignment, double preprocessing, and spot size were resolved. End-to-end verification was successful (leiden k=20, Silhouette=0.086). 🔄 Pi0.5 Phoenix nine-task MimicGen rollout evaluation deployment — It was confirmed that Pi0.5 Phoenix (9-task LoRA fine-tuning) had completed 100k steps of training (checkpoint to 99999), but it had never been evaluated. The three Pi0.5 model forms were sorted out: official base, 9-task combined LoRA, and single-task fine-tuning. A nine-task rollout evaluation was started at step 99999 (50 trials per task, totaling 450 trials). After resolving several environmental compatibility issues, 7/9 tasks were completed by the end of the session (24% on Stack_D0, 12% on Stack_D1, ThreePieceAssembly D0/D1 are still running). 🔄 BC-RNN Phoenix baseline script implementation and parallel training of 9 tasks (including rollout evaluation) — Based on the original paper’s hyperparameters for MimicGen and comparing with the 9-task settings in the Phoenix CVPR 2025 paper, train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py was implemented (generate-configs/train/eval/report/status five modes). After fixing three library-level bugs, all 9 tasks were trained in parallel on an an49 node using a single GPU with srun –overlap (Coffee × GPU5, the other 8 tasks × GPU7). Rollout online evaluations were conducted every 20 epochs. It was confirmed that the existing RobomimicPolicyAdapter is compatible with future error injections. ✅ Full RM-IDEAL evaluation of section 151673 (27 methods × 7 layers, 189 visualizations) — A full RM-IDEAL evaluation was performed on DLPFC section 151673 using 27 different embedding methods. The cosine similarity and Spearman r between RM-IDEAL ground truth were calculated. For the first run, only numerical results were obtained, and then 189 three-panel visualizations (niche query + RM-IDEAL + embedding similarity) were generated. The results were saved to outputs/rm_ideal_evaluation/151673/summary.csv. Implementation and Fixes ✅ HD adaptation for visualize_from_cache.py and spot size fixes — HD support was added to visualize_from_cache.py (process_hd_section function, –dataset/–crop_dir parameters). In utils/visualization.py, create_hd_clustering_visualization (H&E + clustering dual panels) was implemented. The sc.pl.spatial size parameter was changed from 1.0 to 4.0. This corresponded to a difference of HD spot_diameter_fullres=7.3 compared to DLPFC ~144 (k=17, Silhouette=0.302). ✅ Pi0.5 training data traceability and sorting of three model forms — The sources and uses of the three Pi0.5 models in the project were clarified: official pre-trained base (zero-shot), 9-task LoRA fine-tuning (4500 episodes, 5 task prompts, 100k steps on 4×A800, phoenix_comparison), and zhaoganlong’s single-task fine-tuning variant. Details of the 9-task training data were confirmed (500 demos per task, 84×84 dual cameras + 8D state → 7D actions). ✅ Updated the project overview.md (v4.13) — Entries for version 4.13, a list of files, and a version history were added to the Error Recovery Benchmark project overview.md. Issues and Solutions Key Issues 1. In scGPT TransformerModel.init, use_fast_transformer was not saved as an instance attribute. As a result, in load_pretrained(), the key remapping never executed, and 17.7% of attention weights were randomly initialized. When strict=False, it silently skipped mismatches without errors. Solution: Added self.use_fast_transformer = use_fast_transformer to line 64 of model.py. After fixing this issue, verification showed that all 186 parameters matched, with an average ARI improvement of 44.4%. Key insight: PyTorch’s strict=False setting has two sides: it allows partial loading while silently ignoring mismatched keys. Key weights may run as random values for a long time. The same bug exists in the official GitHub repository. Weight loading paths in open-source code need to be audited actively, not blindly trusted.
2. The CoffeeMachineBodyObject lacked the get_bounding_box_half_size() method. When MimicGen Coffee task rollout initialization was performed, an AttributeError occurred because this method was called but never implemented in the robosuite base class. This represents a gap in interfaces between MimicGen and current robosuite versions. Solution: Traced the entire call chain (coffee_machine.py → CoffeeMachineBodyObject → CompositeBodyObject → MujocoXMLObject). The get_bounding_box_half_size() method was implemented in each of the three levels of the base class. This calculated the half-size of the bounding box based on the geometric data of each type. Key insight: Fixing missing methods in third-party libraries requires tracing the entire inheritance chain. Simply patching at the nearest call point can lead to errors in other subclasses. Different robosuite forks may have incompatible APIs. Dependency management should be pinned to the same commit as model training.
3. The WebSocket client always reported a ConnectionRefusedError, even though the server process was listening on port 8000. Different address formats (localhost/127.0.0.1/0.0.0.0) were tried during debugging, but none worked. Solution: It was found that the http_proxy environment variable was set to 127.0.0.1:10087. The websockets library failed because of proxy connection. Connecting successfully after unsetting all proxy variables at the beginning of run_eval.sh resolved the issue. Key insight: HTTP/HTTPS proxy environment variables can transparently block WebSocket connections. When debugging local services on HPC clusters, proxy variables should be checked first. Such issues are common on HPC clusters and should be included in standard troubleshooting lists.
4. In env_robosuite.py of robomimic, the mujoco_py was directly imported at the top level (not installed), and mimicgen was not imported. As a result, MimicGen environment variants (Coffee_D0, etc.) were not registered with robosuite, and the rollout environment initialization failed. Solution: Wrapped the import of mujoco_py in try/except blocks. At the same time, importing mimicgen allowed the MimicGen environment to be registered through side effects. It was confirmed that the same version of robosuite as training was used (zhaoganlong’s dependency directory). Key insight: The MimicGen environment is registered through side effects. Any external tool calling this environment must explicitly execute this import. Robomimic itself does not know about this dependency and needs to inject it at the integration level.
5. In theadata_8um.h5ad HD data, the highs-resolution images in uns[‘spatial’] were full-size (6000×3886), while the coordinates were in the cropped fullres pixel space (0-4966, 0-2971). These do not match, causing visualization errors. Moreover, since X has been logged transformed, the original preprocess_data() will execute normalize_total + log1p again, resulting in double transformation. Solution: In load_hd_data(), replace uns[‘spatial’] images with cropped_fullres.tif and recalculate the scaling factor. Added a skip_log parameter to preprocess_data(). When HD data was passed with skip_log=True, only HVG filtering was performed.
6. Robomimic initiated an interactive overwrite confirmation when encountering an existing checkpoint directory in a non-TTY environment. After receiving EOF, it directly exited with EOFError, causing multiple parallel task launches to fail (partially successful runs leaving a directory that triggers the next launch’s overwriting check). Solution: Thoroughly clean the corresponding checkpoint directory before each (re)launch to avoid triggering overwriting checks. Run using nohup + srun –overlap instead of sbatch to ensure the process does not terminate with the shell.
7. Pi0.5 training was considered unsuccessful because SLURM job 46553 crashed. The AI initially judged that the training was incomplete based on previous conversation caches (record of a crash at step 5000). Solution: Directly checked the checkpoint directory and found that complete checkpoints from step 4000 to 99999 were present. It was determined that the training had been recovered after the crash and completed all 100k steps. Key insight: The checkpoint directory is the most reliable evidence of a successful training. It is more authoritative than log files and conversation records. SLURM job crashes do not equal failed training. Checkpoints should be verified directly, not relying on logs.
General Issues 8. In HD data, when the spot_diameter_fullres was set to 7.3 (vs DLPFC ~144), the dots rendered by sc.pl.spatial(size=1.0) were extremely small and almost invisible. Users could detect this issue through visual inspection. Solution: Adjusted the size parameter of visualize_from_cache.py for HD visualization from 1.0 to 4.0, making the dot diameter approximately 29px (≈bin spacing of 29.2 fullres px). Key insight: The size parameter of scanpy spatial is a multiple of spot_diameter_fullres. Different resolution datasets require different multiples. The spot diameter of HD 8µm is about 1/20 of Visium’s, so the size multiple needs to be increased accordingly to fill the bin spacing.
9. Multiple SLURM constraints on Tianhe cluster led to repeated failures in task submission: –account=sysu_gbli2 was missing, –mem was prohibited by the policy, MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID required a physical GPU number instead of the CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES logical number. When allocating GPU memory, insufficient checks were made on the idle status of each card, resulting in coffee_d0 OOM. Solution: Used sacctmgr to add –account by querying the account name. Replaced –mem with –gres=gpu:1. Set MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID to the actual physical GPU number (5 or 7). Real-time checks of compute-apps usage per card were performed, and tasks were reallocated after that. Key insight: EGL device IDs directly correspond to physical GPUs and are not affected by CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES redirection. Before submitting a new cluster for the first time, sacctmgr should be used to confirm the account name and QOS restrictions. Check compute-apps per GPU instead of just the memory summary.
10. In run_benchmark.py, there was a local cluster embeddings copy that duplicated functions with those in utils/clustering.py. After modifying the tool library, the local copy was not synchronized. As a result, when n_clusters=-1, KMeans reported an InvalidParameterError.Solution: The automatic Leiden estimation logic should also be added to the localcluster_embeddings function in run_benchmark.py. This consistency ensures that changes made to the utility library are reflected in this function. Key Insights: Large scripts often contain local functions that duplicate functionality with tools. When modifying the tool library, it’s necessary to check whether there are duplicates within the script or eliminate them through refactoring.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level ScGPT Bug Tracing: Domain Experience (Colleague Guidance) vs System Code Verification Role Approach Human Based on experience with Flash Attention being incompatible with PyTorch, the colleague directly pointed to the root cause of the mismatch in checkpoint key names, significantly narrowing down the problem space. AI AI systematically reads the code, compares the keys of checkpoints and model state_dicts, and writes verification scripts to accurately identify specific lines where errors occurred. Difference Analysis: Humans rely on domain experience and community knowledge to quickly provide direction. AI is responsible for systematic verification and precise identification. The two complement each other: Without human guidance, AI may need more searching; without code verification by AI, human guesses are difficult to confirm.
Experimental Iteration Strategy: Prioritize Verification with Small Proxy Datasets Role Approach Human It was clearly indicated that using the crop10large sub-area (17502 spots) for testing should be done first. Avoiding processing the entire 545K spots directly, priority should be given to verifying the feasibility of the method before expanding it. AI AI’s initial plan was to handle the entire HD data. A complete processing architecture was designed, but it did not propose to start with small-scale iterations. Difference Analysis: Humans have a stronger engineering intuition when it comes to experimental iteration speed and use small proxy datasets for quick verification. AI tends to design complete solutions in one step, lacking the awareness of conservative verification.
HPC Resource Management Global Perspective (GPU Parallelism Strategy, Rollout Activation, Priority Check on Resources) Role Approach Human Based on prior knowledge about memory usage of BC-RNN (~2GB/任务), a strategy of parallel processing 9 tasks on a single GPU was proposed. The colleague actively requested to activate the rollout evaluation during training. Before AI executes commands, it is reminded to check all running jobs and the idle status of GPUs. AI Follows a conservative “one task per GPU” approach. It does not propose high-density parallelism. Configurations are generated using default values (rollout.enabled=false) without optimization. It focuses on solving technical obstacles while ignoring macro-resource status. Difference Analysis: Humans have a stronger systematic awareness of HPC resource management and can translate domain experience into engineering decisions. AI tends to focus on current technical obstacles, ignoring resource constraints and configuration optimization opportunities.
Bug Fixing Methodology: Call Chain Tracing vs Single-Point Patches Role Approach Human Only requiring “fix it” without specifying the location or method of fixing it. AI Systematically traces the entire call chain (coffee_machine.py→CoffeeMachineBodyObject→CompositeBodyObject→MujocoXMLObject). Rational implementations of methods are added in three different base classes. Difference Analysis: AI’s debugging path is more systematic. It correctly identifies that methods need to be added in multiple base classes rather than just patching the nearest call point, demonstrating a complete analysis ability regarding class inheritance structures.
Literature Citation Tracing: Distinguishing between Evaluation Setting Sources and Algorithm Hyperparameter Sources Role Approach Human Clearly stated: Use the Phoenix paper for evaluation settings. However, BC-RNN hyperparameters should be based on the original MimicGen paper (arXiv:2310.17596) rather than the Ph Phoenix paper. AI Attempted to extract BC-RNN hyperparameters from the Ph Phoenix paper. After being blocked by network restrictions, a web search was conducted. It failed to intuitively distinguish between different intentions of citation sources. Difference Analysis: Humans have a clearer awareness of tracing academic literature and can distinguish between “evaluation setting references” and “algorithm hyperparameter references.” AI needs explicit instructions to correctly distinguish the intention of citation sources.
Future Research Perspective: Forward Design for Error Injection Compatibility Role Approach Human Actively proposed that in the future, errors should be injected into trained BC-RNN and scenarios collected. It was required that code consider compatibility in advance, connecting current tool design with future experimental needs. AI Focused on completing immediate training evaluation tasks. After receiving the prompt, it analyzed the existing framework (RobomimicPolicyAdapter) and confirmed that it is naturally compatible. However, it did not actively consider future compatibility. Difference Analysis: Humans have a longer-term research perspective and can foresee the future uses of current tools. AI tends to focus on completing immediate tasks and lacks the awareness of proactive planning for future compatibility.
AI Limitations Major Limitations Dependence on outdated dialogue cache information (such as Pi0.5 step 5000 crash records) lacks active verification awareness. It’s necessary to check the file system to correct incorrect judgments. Over-reliance on outdated memories may lead to misleading conclusions. The actual file state should be used as a reference. HPC environment troubleshooting lacks systematic checklist checks: No proactive checking of proxy environment variables (initial WebSocket connection failures), MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID is hardcoded as a logical ID rather than a physical ID, and multiple launches of large GPU services during debugging may not clean up old processes leading to OOM. These issues need human guidance to be exposed. Improper management of srun process lifecycle: Multiple misjudgments of “task failure” due to empty logs were corrected by cleaning/restarting repeatedly. There was a lack of pre-judgment of “checking the process state before making decisions.” Processes may have been running without producing visible output, which should be combined with status checks. Experiment scale planning is too extensive. No proactive suggestion for iterative verification using small proxy datasets (planning for 545K HD data requires user correction to crop10large). There is a lack of active optimization awareness regarding experiment iteration efficiency. General Limitations Failure to predict consistency issues across file code (local cluster embeddings copies in run_benchmark.py not synchronized) and differences in visualization parameters across datasets (small spot sizes for HD). Users need to observe the output and trigger secondary repairs. Today’s Achievements Core Achievements PyTorch model.load_state_dict(strict=False) silently ignores mismatched keys, causing key weights to be replaced with random values without errors. Such bugs can remain latent for a long time. In production code, it’s necessary to actively print missing_keys/unexpected_keys and verify parameter values after loading. The same bug exists in the upstream GitHub official repository. It’s necessary to audit the weight loading path of open-source code rather than blindly trusting it. staig_fusion is significantly ahead in RM-IDEAL evaluations (average r=0.396 for 151673, peak r=0.644 for Layer_3), proving that learning-based multi-modal fusion has a much better ability to capture spatial niche structures compared to simple fusion (concat/mean avg r≈0.15) and single-modal baselines (gene/vision avg r≈0.06-0.12). MimicGen environment variants (Coffee_D0, Stack_D1, etc.) are registered with robosuite through the side effects of importing mimicgen. Any external tool (such as robomimic) must execute this import before calling these environments. There is API incompatibility between different robosuite forks. Training and evaluation must be pinned to the same commit. HTTP/HTTPS proxy environment variables (http_proxy/https_proxy) are transparently proxied by Python’s websockets library, causing ws://localhost:xxxx to fail through the proxy. When running local WebSocket services on HPC clusters, it’s necessary to unset the proxy variables or set no_proxy=localhost before starting the client. When fixing missing methods in third-party libraries, it’s necessary to trace the complete class inheritance chain and implement them in all involved base classes. Patching only at the most direct call point will cause errors when other subclasses are called. crop10large (17502 spots) is an ideal proxy dataset for verifying the feasibility of HD methods: It has a scale comparable to DLPFC, with supporting cropped fullres images. The entire pipeline can be verified without modifying architectural assumptions. The Leiden community found that resolution=1.0 as an automatic unsupervised clustering number performs well on HD data (k=17-20, Silhouette=0.302), making it suitable for default clustering strategies for unlabeled HD data. Training BC-RNN on the A800 80GB GPU takes about 2.2GB VRAM per task. Multiple tasks can be processed in high density parallel on a single GPU, but this is actually limited by CPU/IO rather than VRAM. The size parameter of scanpy spatial visualization is a multiple of spot_diameter_fullres. For DLPFC (diameter≈144), size=1.0 should be used. For HD with 8µm diameter (diameter≈7.3), size≈4.0 is required to fill the bin spacing. Practical Achievements Special SLURM constraints for Tianhe cluster: It’s necessary to specify –account=sysu_gbli2. The –mem constraint is prohibited by QOS policy, so it needs to be changed to –gres=gpu:1. MUJOCO_EGL_DEVICE_ID corresponds to the physical GPU number rather than the logical CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES number. srun –overlap can be used to attach a new step to an existing interactive job to reuse node resources, without having to submit the job again. Session Summary MIHD ✅ DCC Day Work: ScGPT Bug Fixing + RM-IDEAL Full Evaluation + Visium HD Full Implementation 21:23:19.892 | claude_code Completed three main tasks on DCC: ① Fixed a bug in scGPT-spatial TransformerModel where the use_fast_transformer attribute was omitted. Re-extracted all 11 DLPFC slice embeddings (ARI average+44.4%, NMI+33.3%), generated scgpt_fixed visualization; ② Conducted a full evaluation of section 151673 using 27 different embedding methods. staig_fusion ranked first with an average r=0.396 (peak r=0.644 for Layer_3). Using the cache, quickly generated visualizations of 189 Zhang panels; ③ Expanded MIHD benchmark to Visium HD crop10large (17502 spots), modified 8 core files to support unlabeled HD (Leiden automatically estimates k), fixed issues with HD visualization alignment, double preprocessing, and spot size. End-to-end verification was successful (k=17, Silhouette=0.302).
Error Recovery Benchmark 🔄 Tianhe Day Work: Pi0.5 Phoenix Evaluation Deployment + BC-RNN Baseline Script Implementation and 9 Task Parallel Training 20:53:34.791 | claude_code Completed two main tasks on Tianhe (an49 node): ① Pi0.5 Phoenix evaluation: Confirmed that 100k steps training was completed (checkpoint to 99999) but never evaluated. Organized three forms of Pi0.5 models, debugged and solved syntax errors in tyro sub-commands, HTTP proxy blocking WebSocket issues, robosuite API incompatibility, env.seed() absence issues. Successfully started a nine-task rollout evaluation (step 99999, 50 times per task). By the end of the session, 7/9 tasks were completed (Stack_D0 24%, Stack_D1 12%, ThreePieceAssembly D0/D1 still running); ② BC-RNN baseline: Referred to MimicGen original paper hyperparameters, created train_bc_rnn_benchmark.py (5 modes), fixed SLURM account/memory/EGL constraints, mujoco_py import, MimicGen environment registration, three library-level bugs in get_bounding_box_half_size. Used srun –overlap to start 9-way training on a single GPU (with rollout evaluation attached). Training ran smoothly (about 2.2GB VRAM per task), confirmed that the existing RobomimicPolicyAdapter is naturally compatible with future error injection needs, expected to complete in 35+ hours with 600 epochs.

summary: 在DCC上完成了MIHD项目Visium HD的全链路扩展工作（修改了8个文件），并且修复了scGPT关键权重加载相关的漏洞问题（修复程度达到了44.4%）。同时在tianhe平台上，从零开始实现了BC-RNN Phoenix的基线训练流程，并成功启动了9个任务的并行训练。此外，还完成了Pi0.5 Phoenix的首次评测部署工作（完成了7/9个任务）。
title: 错误日志 2026-02-28

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-27 Today’s Overview What was done: Analyzed the storage distribution of HDD_POOL in the lab, sorted out the application and stable connection methods for Slurm GPU nodes, and queried all results of the M14 baseline evaluation (Pi0, Pi0.5, BC-RNN). How it was done: Scanned the sizes of various user directories through parallel shell commands, checked the partition status and account permissions of the Slurm cluster, and retrieved information from JSON files in outputs/evaluation_logs. What’s useful: Clarified the methods for applying GPU resources to the cluster and the restrictions of pam_slurm_adopt. Confirmed the key findings of M14 (learning strategies suffer from severe distribution deviation in incorrect scenarios), providing a necessary basis for the M15 LoRA fine-tuning experiment. Completed storage analysis on the Tianhe HPC cluster, sorted out the application process for Slurm GPU nodes, and confirmed the conclusions of the M14 baseline evaluation (Pi0/BC-RNN had almost zero success rate in incorrect recovery scenarios).
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Querying M14 baseline evaluation results (Pi0, Pi0.5, BC-RNN) — Retrieved evaluation logs from outputs/evaluation_logs to confirm the full results of M14: BC-RNN had a success rate of 0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 had a success rate of 0%. The Recovery Progress of the Random baseline was actually the highest (0.0199). Implementation and Fixes ✅ Sorting out Slurm GPU node applications and connection methods — Sorted out available Slurm commands for tianhexy-a cluster (srun/salloc/sbatch, etc.), idle nodes in ai and xy-a800 partitions, pam_slurm_adopt restrictions, and the tmux+salloc stable connection method. ✅ HDD_POOL storage analysis — Analyzed the usage of each user under the sysu_gbli2xy_1 directory. Identified the largest user (chenjunye ~5.1TB) and common shared directories (miniconda3, robotics_dataset, VLA_data). The overall file system utilization was 81%. ❌ Searching for historical modifications to “evaluate on training set” — Users remembered requesting that the evaluations of Pi0.5 & BC-RNN be changed to evaluate on the training set. No corresponding modifications were found in the code library, git history, or memory files. It was impossible to continue tracing after the session was interrupted. Problems and Solutions Key Problems 1. Direct SSH access to compute nodes is denied by pam_slurm_adopt (Access denied: you have no active jobs on this node) Solution: You must first allocate resources through salloc to obtain a JOBID, then enter the node using srun –jobid= –pty bash, or use SSH after there are active jobs. It is recommended to use tmux + salloc to maintain a stable session.
Key Insight: This cluster uses the pam_slurm_adopt security policy. Compute nodes completely disable SSH for users without any jobs, which is different from the behavior of ordinary HPC clusters.
General Problems 2. AI cannot find the modifications mentioned by the user regarding “evaluate on training set” Solution: Not resolved. AI suggests that users should further describe the specific content of the modification (is it fixed initial conditions? Or train/test split?). This would help in retracing the process.
Key Insight: Cross-session conversation history is invisible to AI. If the modification does not lie in the code or MEMORY.md, it is completely impossible to trace.
3. The du command repeatedly times out when scanning超大 directories (miniconda3, chenshiyu, VLA_data, etc.), failing to complete within 120 seconds and 300 seconds Solution: switched to using tmpdir + background parallel processes (forking each directory separately), set the timeout to 600 seconds. Eventually, we obtained the sizes of VLA_data (248GB) and other directories. There are still 5 directories that cannot be scanned within a reasonable time due to their large size (>2TB per directory).
Key Insight: The speed of du on shared Lustre file systems is limited by both the number of inodes and network latency.超大 directories can only be estimated using parallel + timeout strategies.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Historical operation memories Role Thinking Human The user remembered requesting that the evaluations be changed to “evaluate on training set” during a previous session, and directly asked for the results. AI AI cannot access cross-session history and can only search for traces in the code library. It ultimately failed to reproduce the user’s memory. Difference analysis: Humans have continuous memories of their past operations, while AI’s memory depends on code implementation or explicit writing to MEMORY.md.
Implementation Level Storage analysis scope Role Thinking Human The user only saw that AI analyzed only their own directory (29GB) and actively requested an extension to the directories of all users in the same group, so as to obtain a global perspective. AI AI defaulted to only analyzing the directories requested by the user (tangzijia), without actively suggesting or extending to other users in the same group. Difference analysis: Humans have a global view and team awareness, while AI tends to perform tasks within a minimal scope.
AI Limitations Important Limitations Missing cross-session memories: The modifications mentioned by the user regarding “evaluate on training set” were not recorded in MEMORY.md or code, resulting in AI being unable to trace back at all. It could only ask the user questions. General Limitations Du scanning strategy is not efficient enough: AI had to make multiple serial attempts (timeouting at 120s→300s→600s) before finally adopting a parallel approach, wasting many interaction rounds. Initial analysis scope was too narrow: AI did not actively suggest analyzing all group directories. It needed user supplements before extending the scope. Today’s Gains Core Gains Key conclusion of M14: BC-RNN had a success rate of 0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 had a success rate of 0%. The Recovery Progress of Random (0.0199) was actually higher than all learning strategies, indicating that learning strategies suffer from severe distribution generalization failures in incorrect injection scenarios. This directly proves the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning. Practical Gains Tianhexy-a cluster uses pam_slurm_adopt, and GPU nodes do not support direct SSH access. GPUs in ai and xy-a800 partitions are not registered with GRES, so no –gres parameter is needed when applying for them. It is recommended to use tmux + salloc to maintain a stable session. The overall utilization rate of the lab’s HDD_POOL file system has reached 81%. The largest user is chenjunye (~5.1TB), with shared miniconda3 and robotics_dataset each accounting for about 1-2TB. Historical checkpoints should be cleaned up regularly. Session Summary ✅ Querying M14 baseline evaluation results (Pi0, Pi0.5, BC-RNN, Random) 05:57:24.584 | claude_code The user asked about the progress of the previous Pi0 and BC-RNN evaluations. AI retrieved the outputs/evaluation_logs directory and summarized the full evaluation results of M14: On 649 PickPlace error scenarios, BC-RNN had a success rate of 0.28%, Pi0/Pi0.5 had a complete success rate of 0%. The Recovery Progress of Random was actually the highest. AI pointed out that this directly proves the necessity of M15 LoRA fine-tuning.
✅ Sorting out the entire process of applying for Slurm GPU nodes and maintaining stable connections 05:18:36.275 | claude_code The user wanted to know about the cluster’s Slurm commands and methods for applying for GPU nodes. AI explored the configuration of tianhexy-a cluster, idle nodes in ai and xy-a800 partitions, account permissions, and found that pam_slurm_adopt directly denied SSH access to compute nodes. Finally, it provided three complete solutions: interactive allocation with salloc, batch processing with sbatch, and stable sessions using tmux. ✅ HDD_POOL storage analysis: Usage of each user directory 04:28:09.784 | claude_code The user requested an analysis of the lab’s shared storage space. AI first analyzed the user’s own directory (29GB), and then expanded it to the directories of all users in the same group at the user’s request. Through multiple rounds of parallel du scanning (switching to tmpdir+parallel strategy after multiple timeouts), we finally obtained the sizes of most directories. We identified chenjunye (~5.1TB) as the largest user. The overall file system utilization was 81%, with the remaining being 1.5PB. 🔄 Searching for modifications related to Pi0.5 & BC-RNN training set evaluations 07:57:48.382 | claude_code The user remembered requesting that the evaluations be changed to evaluate on the training set and asked about the results of this modification. AI did not find any corresponding traces in the code library, git history, or MEMORY.md. After the user interrupted the search, it turned to asking about files like evaluate_mimicgen.py. AI also did not find any related modifications. The session ended with AI反问 the user about the specific modification content, without resolving the issue.
Token Usage General Overview Metric Value Total Tokens 2,398,136 Input Tokens 1,229 Output Tokens 4,345 Cache Creation 161,084 Cache Reading 2,231,478 Cache Hit Rate 93.3% Total Cost (USD) $0.4475 Model Details Model Input Output Cache Creation Cache Reading Cost Percentage claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 1,229 4,345 161,084 2,231,478 $0.4475 100.0%
summary: On the Tianhe HPC cluster, the storage space analysis was completed, and the process of applying for GPUs on Slurm nodes was finalized. The results of the M14 baseline evaluation confirmed that Pi0/BC-RNN achieved almost zero success rates in error recovery scenarios.
title: bug journal 2026-02-27

content:
Daily Report — 2026-02-26 Today’s Overview What was done: Rewrote BC-RNN from low_dim to image mode (consistent with the MimicGen paper), and successfully launched parallel training on 5 MimicGen tasks. Also expanded the evaluation framework to support multi-task evaluations. How it was done: Attached srun to an existing SLURM interactive job (bypassing sbatch permission restrictions). Launched 5 training processes in 8 A800 GPUs; added a task registry and –task parameter to manage task paths and configurations. What’s achieved: Resolved the fundamental contradiction where the evaluation framework only supported PickPlace while training on 5 MimicGen tasks. This laid a solid foundation for subsequent cross-task comparisons between Pi0.5 and BC-RNN. Rewrote BC-RNN training configuration to image mode on the supercomputer cluster and successfully launched parallel training with 5 tasks. Expanded the evaluation framework to support 5 MimicGen tasks. Identified and fixed the problem of task distribution mismatch leading to 0% success rate for Pi0.5.
Today’s Tasks Architecture and Strategy ✅ Rewriting BC-RNN image mode configuration (5 tasks) — Rewrote all 5 JSON configurations under bc_rnn_configs/ from low_dim to image mode: ResNet18+SpatialSoftmax encoder, CropRandomizer 76×76, RNN hidden_dim 1000, batch_size 16, hdf5_cache_mode low_dim. ✅ Launch BC-RNN image mode training (parallel with 5 tasks) — Attached srun –jobid=45628 to an existing interactive job. Launched 5 training tasks coffee/stack/stack_three/threading/three_piece_assembly on GPUs 0-4. All confirmed image_obs=5 for training. ✅ Expand evaluation framework to support 5 MimicGen tasks — Created 5 new task YAML configs + task_registry.yaml; added –task parameter to 1/3/4 scripts; fixed observation dimension errors caused by _D0 env suffix stripping; rewrote run_full_eval.sh to support multi-task loops. ✅ Root cause analysis of Pi0.5 0% success rate — Identified the root cause: Pi0.5 was trained on 5 MimicGen tasks (Coffee/Stack/Threading, etc.), but the evaluation framework only measured PickPlace, which wasn’t in the training set. 🔄 Researching evaluation methods for Pi0.5 on training tasks — Explored pi05_phoenix/evaluate_mimicgen.py and zhaoganlong’s evaluation process, confirmed checkpoint locations, planned to launch VLA server and evaluate on 5 MimicGen tasks. Implementation and Fixes ✅ Modify 3_collect_data.py to support image BC-RNN cameras — Added camera_height/width parameters in create_env; extended camera detection logic to bc_rnn; kept VLA at 256×256, while BC-RNN used 84×84 for training data matching. ✅ SLURM permission bypass — sbatch submission failed (xy-a800 and ai partitions had no permissions). Found the correct srun binary using source set-XY-I.sh and attached it to an existing interactive job to bypass restrictions with –jobid=45628. Problems and Solutions Key Issues 1. Pi0.5 evaluation success rate was 0% Solution: Change to evaluate on training tasks (Coffee/Stack/Threading/Assembly), no longer measure PickPlace.
Key insight: The most fundamental reason is that the training dataset does not overlap with the evaluation tasks, not model quality issues. This is a key discovery that AI initially overlooked and was triggered by human questions.
2. BC-RNN evaluation dimension mismatch (Expected 65, got 37) Solution: Add import mimicgen to _D0 environment in create_env(), no longer strip _D0 suffix, keeping all MimicGen observables.
Key insight: The _D0 suffix provides additional object state observables. Stripping it reduces the observation dimensionality from 65 to 37.
3. BC-RNN configuration was set to low_dim mode, which did not match the main results of the MimicGen paper Solution: Rewrite according to the official generate_training_configs_for_public_datasets.py and config_utils.py using set_learning_settings_for_bc_rnn() function to image mode.
Key insight: MimicGen does not provide pre-trained checkpoints, only datasets. The key difference between image mode and low_dim is RNN hidden_dim (400→1000), batch_size (64→16), epoch_every_n_steps (100→500), and ResNet18 + SpatialSoftmax encoder.
General Issues 4. sbatch submission failed: User’s group not permitted to use this partition Solution: Found /usr/local/slurm.24051/bin/srun using source set-XY-I.sh, attached it to an existing interactive job with –jobid=45628 –overlap on node an49.
Key insight: There were multiple SLURM installations in the cluster. The script needed to be run in the correct environment to use the authorized version. Using the existing interactive allocation was the quickest way to bypass this issue.
Human Thinking vs AI Thinking Strategic Level Root cause analysis of Pi0.5 0% success rate Role Thought Human Directly question: “You wouldn’t mix all 9 task data together to train a model, right?” — The human directly identified the core problem of task distribution mismatch based on common sense. AI AI initially listed 3 reasons (Server OOM, action clipping, action space mismatch), but failed to identify task distribution mismatch as the main cause immediately. Difference analysis: Humans can directly hit the target with a question, while AI needs multiple steps of analysis to reach the same conclusion. Human systematic intuition is better than AI’s symptom attribution.
BC-RNN mode selection Role Thought Human Clearly specified image mode, consistent with the main results of the MimicGen paper. AI The current configuration was low_dim + 600 epochs. AI proposed two options and asked for user input. Difference analysis: Humans focus on paper consistency, while AI tends to follow existing configurations. Human decision-making drove the entire rewriting process.
Implementation Level Granularity of BC-RNN training plan Role Thought Human Provided a very detailed implementation plan, including specific parameter tables, per-task horizons, and key reference file paths. AI Executed the plan, but found additional details during independent exploration (e.g., epoch_every_n_steps 500 vs 100). Difference analysis: Humans provide high-quality planning, while AI is responsible for precise execution and detail completion.
AI Limitations Major Limitations Failed to identify task distribution mismatch as the most obvious reason when diagnosing Pi0.5 0% success rate for the first time. Instead, it listed secondary factors like OOM and action clipping. General Limitations Attempted to call ExitPlanMode without user confirmation twice, which was rejected by the user. AI was too autonomous when entering execution mode and did not wait for the user to review the plan. The Write tool requires Reading before Writing. When AI wrote bc_rnn_stack/stack_three/threading.json, it missed this step, resulting in multiple tool call failures before correction. Today’s Achievements Core Achievements MimicGen does not provide any pre-trained checkpoints, only provides HDF5 demo datasets. BC-RNN is its official benchmark algorithm. The image mode is consistent with the paper. The paper reports 100% success rate for Stack/Coffee, 98% for Threading, and 82% for Assembly. MimicGen _D0 environment (e.g., Coffee_D0) provides additional object observables compared to the basic robosuite environment. Stripping the _D0 suffix reduces the observation dimensionality from 65 to 37, disrupting the consistency of input during reasoning. Cross-task evaluation is a common pitfall in robot learning: If the training set and evaluation tasks do not overlap, it results in a 0% success rate. Consistency between train/eval tasks should be ensured during experimental design phase. Core differences between BC-RNN image mode and low_dim: RNN hidden_dim from 400 to 1000, actor_layer_dims [1024,1024] to [], batch_size from 64 to 16, hdf5_cache_mode all to low_dim, epoch_every_n_steps from 100 to 500. Additionally, add ResNet18Conv+SpatialSoftmax(32kp)+CropRandomizer(76×76). Practical Achievements When not having sbatch permissions, you can attach a new process to the existing interactive allocation using srun –jobid=EXISTING_JOB_ID –overlap. No need to apply for new GPU resources. Session Summary error-recovery-benchmark 🔍 Checking training status of Pi0.5 and BC-RNN, found task distribution mismatch leading to 0% success rate 01:10:41.534 | claude_code The user asked about the results of Pi0.5 and BC-RNN training. AI found that Pi0.5 was trained on 5 MimicGen tasks but evaluated on PickPlace (not in the training set), resulting in a 0% success rate. BC-RNN could not be evaluated properly due to mismatch in observation dimensions. A key turning point: The human’s question “You wouldn’t mix all 9 task data together to train a model, right?” directly identified the root cause. Decision: Change to evaluate on training tasks.
✅ Rewriting BC-RNN image mode configuration + launching parallel training with 5 tasks 04:00:34.795 | claude_code According to the detailed plan provided by humans, rewrite 5 BC-RNN configurations from low_dim to image mode (ResNet18+SpatialSoftmax+CropRandomizer, RNN hidden 1000); modify 3_collect_data.py to enable 84×84 camera rendering for BC-RNN. After SLURM sbatch failed, bypassed permission restrictions using srun –jobid=45628 and successfully launched 5 tasks training on 8 A800 GPUs. All confirmed running in image obs mode. Training lasted about 3 hours, and stack_three/threading/assembly reached epoch ~190/600.
✅ Expanding evaluation framework to support 5 MimicGen tasks and fixing dimension mismatch 02:34:46.032 | claude_code Implemented multi-task evaluation framework expansion: created five task YAMLs + task_registry.yaml; added –task parameter to three evaluation scripts; created BC-RNN training configurations for five tasks; rewritten run_full_eval.sh to support multi-task loops; fixed all _D0 suffix stripping issues in create_env(). All script syntax verifications passed.
🔄 Researching evaluation process for Pi0.5 on MimicGen training tasks 04:10:57.195 | claude_code The user requested testing Pi0.5 on training tasks. AI explored pi05_phoenix/ evaluation scripts, VLA server launch processes, zhaoganlong’s eval_checkpoints_multi.py, confirmed checkpoint location in phoenix_comparison/ (99,999 steps), and understood that it was necessary to start the websocket policy server before connecting to evaluate_mimicgen.py. Evaluation plan research completed but execution not started yet.
Multi-project Testing (X-VLA / mozihao-VLA / HOME, etc.) ❌ Testable Hello connections under multiple project paths 09:46:37.000 | claude_code Conversations containing only hello were initiated under multiple project paths (X-VLA, mozihao-VLA, HOME, etc.), but were interrupted by the user or had no substantial interaction. This might be environmental testing or a false trigger.
